1:1.shloka: धृतराष्ट्र उवाचधर्मक्षेत्रे कुरुक्षेत्रे समवेता युयुत्सवः । मामकाः पाण्डवाश्चैव किमकुर्वत सञ्जय ॥
1:1.transliteration: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच (Dhritarashtra said) / धर्मक्षेत्रे (on the field of duty) / कुरुक्षेत्रे (at Kurukshetra) / समवेता (assembled) / युयुत्सवः (desiring to fight) / मामकाः (my people) / पाण्डवाः (the sons of Pandu) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / किम् (what) / अकुर्वत (did they do) / सञ्जय (O Sanjaya)
1:1.meaning: Dhritarashtra asks Sanjaya what happened when both sides gathered on the battlefield, ready to fight.

1:2.shloka: सञ्जय उवाचदृष्ट्वा तु पाण्डवानीकं व्यूढं दुर्योधनस्तदा । आचार्यमुपसङ्गम्य राजा वचनमब्रवीत् ॥
1:2.transliteration: सञ्जय उवाच (Sanjaya said) / दृष्ट्वा (having seen) / तु (indeed) / पाण्डवानीकं (the Pandava army) / व्यूढं (arrayed in formation) / दुर्योधनः (Duryodhana) / तदा (then) / आचार्यम् (to the teacher) / उपसङ्गम्य (having approached) / राजा (the king) / वचनम् (words) / अब्रवीत् (spoke)
1:2.meaning: Duryodhana sees the Pandava army in battle formation and goes to Drona to speak.

1:3.shloka: पश्यैतां पाण्डुपुत्राणामाचार्य महतीं चमूम् । व्यूढां द्रुपदपुत्रेण तव शिष्येण धीमता ॥
1:3.transliteration: पश्य (look) / एताम् (this) / पाण्डुपुत्राणाम् (of the sons of Pandu) / आचार्य (O teacher) / महतीम् (great) / चमूम् (army) / व्यूढाम् (arrayed in formation) / द्रुपदपुत्रेण (by the son of Drupada) / तव (your) / शिष्येण (by the disciple) / धीमता (wise)
1:3.meaning: Duryodhana points out the Pandava army to Drona and reminds him that the commander is Drona's own clever student.

1:4.shloka: अत्र शूरा महेष्वासा भीमार्जुनसमा युधि । युयुधानो विराटश्च द्रुपदश्च महारथः ॥
1:4.transliteration: अत्र (here) / शूराः (heroes, brave ones) / महेष्वासाः (great bowmen) / भीमार्जुनसमा (equal to Bhima and Arjuna) / युधि (in battle) / युयुधानः (Yuyudhana) / विराटः (Virata) / च (and) / द्रुपदः (Drupada) / च (and) / महारथः (great chariot-warrior)
1:4.meaning: Duryodhana points out the Pandava army's strongest warriors and praises their battle skill.

1:5.shloka: धृष्टकेतुश्चेकितानः काशिराजश्च वीर्यवान् । पुरुजित्कुन्तिभोजश्च शैब्यश्च नरपुङ्गवः ॥
1:5.transliteration: धृष्टकेतुः (Dhṛṣṭaketu) / चेकितानः (Chekitana) / काशिराजः (king of Kashi) / वीर्यवान् (valiant) / पुरुजित् (Purujit) / कुन्तिभोजः (Kuntibhoja) / शैब्यः (Shaibya) / नरपुङ्गवः (best among men)
1:5.meaning: Sanjaya continues listing the mighty warriors in the Pandava army.

1:6.shloka: युधामन्युश्च विक्रान्त उत्तमौजाश्च वीर्यवान् । सौभद्रो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्व एव महारथाः ॥
1:6.transliteration: युधामन्युः (Yudhamanyu) / च (and) / विक्रान्तः (brave, powerful) / उत्तमौजाः (Uttamaujas) / च (and) / वीर्यवान् (valiant) / सौभद्रः (Subhadra's son) / द्रौपदेयाः (the sons of Draupadi) / च (and) / सर्वे (all) / एव (indeed) / महारथाः (great warriors)
1:6.meaning: Duryodhana keeps listing the Pandava side's strongest fighters, showing how formidable Arjuna's army is.

1:7.shloka: अस्माकं तु विशिष्टा ये तान्निबोध द्विजोत्तम । नायका मम सैन्यस्य संज्ञार्थं तान्ब्रवीमि ते ॥
1:7.transliteration: अस्माकं (our) / तु (but/also) / विशिष्टाः (distinguished, chief) / ये (who) / तान् (them) / निबोध (know, note) / द्विजोत्तम (O best of the twice-born) / नायकाः (leaders, chiefs) / मम (of my) / सैन्यस्य (of the army) / संज्ञार्थं (for the sake of recognition, to remind) / तान् (them) / ब्रवीमि (I speak, I name) / ते (to you)
1:7.meaning: Duryodhana tells Drona to notice the main warriors on his own side too. He is naming them so they can be recognised before the battle.

meanings.meaning: for the sake of recognition, to remind

1:8.shloka: भवान्भीष्मश्च कर्णश्च कृपश्च समितिञ्जयः । अश्वत्थामा विकर्णश्च सौमदत्तिस्तथैव च ॥
1:8.transliteration: भवान् (you) / भीष्मः (Bhishma) / च (and) / कर्णः (Karna) / च (and) / कृपः (Kripa) / च (and) / समितिञ्जयः (victorious in battle) / अश्वत्थामा (Ashvatthama) / विकर्णः (Vikarna) / च (and) / सौमदत्तिः (the son of Somadatta) / तथा एव च (and likewise)
1:8.meaning: Duryodhana keeps listing the strongest warriors on his side, naming more of the commanders gathered for battle.

1:9.shloka: अन्ये च बहवः शूरा मदर्थे त्यक्तजीविताः । नानाशस्त्रप्रहरणाः सर्वे युद्धविशारदाः ॥
1:9.transliteration: अन्ये (others) / च (and) / बहवः (many) / शूराः (heroes) / मदर्थे (for my sake) / त्यक्तजीविताः (having given up life) / नानाशस्त्रप्रहरणाः (wielding various weapons) / सर्वे (all) / युद्धविशारदाः (expert in warfare)
1:9.meaning: More warriors stand on Duryodhana's side too. They are fearless, heavily armed, and experienced in battle.

1:10.shloka: अपर्याप्तं तदस्माकं बलं भीष्माभिरक्षितम् । पर्याप्तं त्विदमेतेषां बलं भीमाभिरक्षितम् ॥
1:10.transliteration: अपर्याप्तम् (insufficient) / तत् (that) / अस्माकम् (of us) / बलम् (force) / भीष्म-अभिरक्षितम् (protected by Bhishma) / पर्याप्तम् (sufficient) / तु (but) / इदम् (this) / एतेषाम् (of these) / बलम् (force) / भीम-अभिरक्षितम् (protected by Bhima)
1:10.meaning: Duryodhana judges his own army as weaker and the Pandavas' army as stronger, based on who protects each side.

1:11.shloka: अयनेषु च सर्वेषु यथाभागमवस्थिताः । भीष्ममेवाभिरक्षन्तु भवन्तः सर्व एव हि ॥
1:11.transliteration: अयनेषु (on the battlefronts) / च (and) / सर्वेषु (all) / यथाभागम् (according to each one's place) / अवस्थिताः (standing positioned) / भीष्मम् (Bhishma) / एव (only) / अभिरक्षन्तु (let them protect) / भवन्तः (you all) / सर्वे (all) / हि (indeed)
1:11.meaning: Duryodhana orders all his warriors to hold their positions and guard Bhishma from every side.

1:12.shloka: तस्य संजनयन्हर्षं कुरुवृद्धः पितामहः । सिंहनादं विनद्योच्चैः शङ्खं दध्मौ प्रतापवान् ॥
1:12.transliteration: तस्य (his) / संजनयन् (generating) / हर्षम् (joy) / कुरुवृद्धः (the elder of the Kuru clan) / पितामहः (grandfather) / सिंहनादम् (lion-roar) / विनद्य (having roared) / उच्चैः (loudly) / शङ्खम् (conch) / दध्मौ (blew) / प्रतापवान् (the powerful one)
1:12.meaning: Bhishma blows his conch loudly, and Duryodhana feels encouraged. It is a battle signal, not a teaching verse.

meanings.meaning: the elder of the Kuru clan

1:13.shloka: ततः शङ्खाश्च भेर्यश्च पणवानकगोमुखाः । सहसैवाभ्यहन्यन्त स शब्दस्तुमुलोऽभवत् ॥
1:13.transliteration: ततः (then) / शङ्खाः (conches) / च (and) / भेर्यः (kettledrums) / च (and) / पणवानकगोमुखाः (small drums and trumpets) / सहसा एव (all at once) / अभ्यहन्यन्त (were struck) / सः (that) / शब्दः (sound) / तुमुलः (tumultuous) / अभवत् (became)
1:13.meaning: Conches, drums, and trumpets all sounded together, and the noise became overwhelming.

1:14.shloka: ततः श्वेतैर्हयैर्युक्ते महति स्यन्दने स्थितौ । माधवः पाण्डवश्चैव दिव्यौ शङ्खौ प्रदध्मतुः ॥
1:14.transliteration: ततः (then) / श्वेतैः (with white) / हयैः (horses) / युक्ते (yoked) / महति (great) / स्यन्दने (on the chariot) / स्थितौ (seated) / माधवः (Madhava) / पाण्डवः (the Pandava) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / दिव्यौ (divine) / शङ्खौ (conches) / प्रदध्मतुः (blew)
1:14.meaning: Krishna and Arjuna stand together on their great chariot and sound their conches. It marks their answer to the Kaurava war cries.

1:15.shloka: पाञ्चजन्यं हृषीकेशो देवदत्तं धनंजयः । पौण्ड्रं दध्मौ महाशङ्खं भीमकर्मा वृकोदरः ॥
1:15.transliteration: पाञ्चजन्यं (Panchajanya) / हृषीकेशः (Hrisikesha) / देवदत्तं (Devadatta) / धनंजयः (Dhananjaya) / पौण्ड्रं (Paundra) / दध्मौ (blew) / महाशङ्खम् (great conch) / भीमकर्मा (one of mighty deeds) / वृकोदरः (Bhimasena)
1:15.meaning: Krishna, Arjuna, and Bhima each blew their own conches as the battle began.

1:16.shloka: अनन्तविजयं राजा कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः । नकुलः सहदेवश्च सुघोषमणिपुष्पकौ ॥
1:16.transliteration: अनन्तविजयं (Anantavijaya) / राजा (king) / कुन्तीपुत्रः (son of Kunti) / युधिष्ठिरः (Yudhishthira) / नकुलः (Nakula) / सहदेवः (Sahadeva) / च (and) / सुघोषमणिपुष्पकौ (Sughosha and Manipushpaka)
1:16.meaning: Yudhishthira, Nakula, and Sahadeva blow their conches. The verse adds to the battle scene and names their sacred war horns.

1:17.shloka: काश्यश्च परमेष्वासः शिखण्डी च महारथः । धृष्टद्युम्नो विराटश्च सात्यकिश्चापराजितः ॥
1:17.transliteration: काशीः (the king of Kashi) / च (and) / परमेष्वासः (supreme archer) / शिखण्डी (Shikhandi) / च (and) / महारथः (great warrior) / धृष्टद्युम्नः (Dhrishtadyumna) / विराटः (Virata) / च (and) / सात्यकिः (Satyaki) / च (and) / अपराजितः (invincible)
1:17.meaning: This is a battlefield roll call. More warriors on the Pandava side blow their conches before the war begins.

1:18.shloka: द्रुपदो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्वशः पृथिवीपते । सौभद्रश्च महाबाहुः शङ्खान्दध्मुः पृथक्पृथक् ॥
1:18.transliteration: द्रुपदः (Drupada) / द्रौपदेयाः (Draupadi's sons) / च (and) / सर्वशः (all together) / पृथिवीपते (O lord of the earth) / सौभद्रः (Abhimanyu, son of Subhadra) / च (and) / महाबाहुः (mighty-armed) / शङ्खान् (conches) / दध्मुः (blew) / पृथक् पृथक् (separately each)
1:18.meaning: Drupada, Draupadi's sons, and Abhimanyu each blew their conches. The Pandava side answers the Kaurava side with a full display of strength.

1:19.shloka: स घोषो धार्तराष्ट्राणां हृदयानि व्यदारयत् । नभश्च पृथिवीं चैव तुमुलो व्यनुनादयन् ॥
1:19.transliteration: सः (that) / घोषः (sound) / धार्तराष्ट्राणाम् (of Dhritarashtra's sons) / हृदयानि (hearts) / व्यदारयत् (split) / नभः (sky) / च (and) / पृथिवीम् (earth) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / तुमुलः (terrible, loud) / व्यनुनादयन् (resounding)
1:19.meaning: The loud blast from the Pandava army shook the Kaurava side so deeply that it felt like the sky and earth were trembling too.

1:20.shloka: अथ व्यवस्थितान् दृष्ट्वा धार्तराष्ट्रान्कपिध्वजः । प्रवृत्ते शस्त्रसंपाते धनुरुद्यम्य पाण्डवः ॥
1:20.transliteration: अथ (then) / व्यवस्थितान् (arrayed, standing in order) / दृष्ट्वा (having seen) / धार्तराष्ट्रान् (the sons of Dhritarashtra) / कपिध्वजः (he whose banner bears Hanuman) / प्रवृत्ते (when begun) / शस्त्रसंपाते (the clash of weapons) / धनुः (bow) / उद्यम्य (lifting) / पाण्डवः (the son of Pandu)
1:20.meaning: Arjuna sees the opposing army ready for battle and raises his bow.

1:21.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचहृषीकेशं तदा वाक्यमिदमाह महीपते । सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये रथं स्थापय मेऽच्युत ॥
1:21.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / हृषीकेशं (to Hrishikesha) / तदा (then) / वाक्यम् (words) / इदम् (this) / आह (said) / महीपते (O king) / सेनयोः (of the two armies) / उभयोः (both) / मध्ये (in the middle) / रथम् (chariot) / स्थापय (place) / मे (my) / अच्युत (O Achyuta)
1:21.meaning: Arjuna asks Krishna to stop his chariot between the two armies so he can see who he must fight.

1:22.transliteration: यावत् (until) / एतान् (these) / निरीक्षे (I may inspect) / अहम् (I) / योद्धुकामान् (those desirous of fighting) / अवस्थितान् (standing) / कैः (with whom) / मया (by me) / सह (together with) / योद्धव्यम् (must be fought) / अस्मिन् (in this) / रणसमुद्यमे (war undertaking)
1:22.meaning: Arjuna asks Krishna to hold the chariot still so he can look at the warriors and understand whom he must face.

1:23.shloka: योत्स्यमानानवेक्षेऽहं य एतेऽत्र समागताः । धार्तराष्ट्रस्य दुर्बुद्धेर्युद्धे प्रियचिकीर्षवः ॥
1:23.transliteration: योत्स्यमानान् (those eager to fight) / अवेक्षे (I want to see) / अहम् (I) / ये (who) / एते (these) / अत्र (here) / समागताः (assembled) / धार्तराष्ट्रस्य (of Dhritarashtra’s son) / दुर्बुद्धेः (of the evil-minded) / युद्धे (in battle) / प्रियचिकीर्षवः (wishing to please)
1:23.meaning: Arjuna wants to inspect the warriors gathered on the opposite side, especially those fighting for Duryodhana.

1:24.shloka: संजय उवाचएवमुक्तो हृषीकेशो गुडाकेशेन भारत । सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये स्थापयित्वा रथोत्तमम् ॥
1:24.transliteration: संजय उवाच (Sanjaya said) / एवम् (thus) / उक्तः (spoken to) / हृषीकेशः (Hrishikesha) / गुडाकेशेन (by Gudakesha) / भारत (O Bharata) / सेनयोः (of the armies) / उभयोः (of both) / मध्ये (in the middle) / स्थापयित्वा (having placed) / रथोत्तमम् (the finest chariot)
1:24.meaning: Krishna places Arjuna’s chariot between the two armies, right where the decision must happen.

1:25.shloka: भीष्मद्रोणप्रमुखतः सर्वेषां च महीक्षिताम् । उवाच पार्थ पश्यैतान्समवेतान्कुरूनिति ॥
1:25.transliteration: भीष्म-द्रोण-प्रमुखतः (in front of Bhishma and Drona) / सर्वेषाम् (of all) / च (and) / महीक्षिताम् (of kings) / उवाच (said) / पार्थ (O Partha) / पश्य (behold) / एतान् (these) / समवेतान् (assembled) / कुरून् (Kurus) / इति (thus)
1:25.meaning: Krishna positions Arjuna’s chariot between the armies and asks him to look at the gathered Kurus, including Bhishma, Drona, and the kings.

meanings.meaning: in front of Bhishma and Drona

1:26.transliteration: तत्र (there) / अपश्यत् (saw) / स्थितान् (standing) / पार्थः (Arjuna, son of Pritha) / पितृ़न् (fathers) / अथ (also) / पितामहान् (grandfathers) / आचार्यान् (teachers) / मातुलान् (maternal uncles) / भ्रातृ़न् (brothers) / पुत्रान् (sons) / पौत्रान् (grandsons) / सखीन् (friends) / तथा (and so on)
1:26.meaning: Arjuna looks across the battlefield and recognizes his own relatives and teachers among the enemy ranks.

1:27.shloka: श्वशुरान्सुहृदश्चैव सेनयोरुभयोरपि । तान्समीक्ष्य स कौन्तेयः सर्वान्बन्धूनवस्थितान् ॥
1:27.transliteration: श्वशुरान् (fathers-in-law) / सुहृदः (well-wishers) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / सेनयोः (of the armies) / उभयोः (of both) / अपि (also) / तान् (those) / समीक्ष्य (having seen) / सः (he) / कौन्तेयः (son of Kunti) / सर्वान् (all) / बन्धून् (relatives) / अवस्थितान् (standing)
1:27.meaning: Arjuna sees every kind of relative on both sides of the battlefield and is overwhelmed with grief.

1:28.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचकृपया परयाऽऽविष्टो विषीदन्निदमब्रवीत् । दृष्ट्वेमं स्वजनं कृष्ण युयुत्सुं समुपस्थितम् ॥
1:28.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / कृपया (by compassion or pity) / परया (supreme) / आविष्टः (overcome) / विषीदन् (grieving) / इदम् (this) / अब्रवीत् (spoke) / दृष्ट्वा (having seen) / इमम् (this) / स्वजनम् (my own people) / कृष्ण (O Krishna) / युयुत्सुम् (desiring to fight) / समुपस्थितम् (assembled before me)
1:28.meaning: Arjuna tells Krishna that seeing his own relatives gathered for battle has overwhelmed him with grief and pity.

1:29.shloka: सीदन्ति मम गात्राणि मुखं च परिशुष्यति । वेपथुश्च शरीरे मे रोमहर्षश्च जायते ॥
1:29.transliteration: सीदन्ति (are failing) / मम (my) / गात्राणि (limbs) / मुखं (mouth) / च (and) / परिशुष्यति (dries up) / वेपथुः (trembling) / च (and) / शरीरे (in the body) / मे (my) / रोम-हर्षः (standing of hair) / च (and) / जायते (arises)
1:29.meaning: Arjuna's body is breaking under the pressure of what he sees. His strength, speech, and composure are all slipping away at once.

1:30.shloka: गाण्डीवं स्रंसते हस्तात्त्वक्चैव परिदह्यते । न च शक्नोम्यवस्थातुं भ्रमतीव च मे मनः ॥
1:30.transliteration: गाण्डीवम् (the Gandiva bow) / स्रंसते (slips) / हस्तात् (from the hand) / त्वक् (skin) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / परिदह्यते (burns) / न (not) / च (and) / शक्नोमि (I am able) / अवस्थातुम् (to stand) / भ्रमति (reels) / इव (like) / च (and) / मे (my) / मनः (mind)
1:30.meaning: Arjuna is overwhelmed by fear and grief. His body shakes, his bow slips, and he cannot steady his mind or even stand.

1:31.shloka: निमित्तानि च पश्यामि विपरीतानि केशव । न च श्रेयोऽनुपश्यामि हत्वा स्वजनमाहवे ॥
1:31.transliteration: निमित्तानि (signs) / च (and) / पश्यामि (I see) / विपरीतानि (opposite, inauspicious) / केशव (O Keshava) / न (not) / च (and) / श्रेयः (good, welfare) / अनुपश्यामि (I see) / हत्वा (having killed) / स्वजनम् (my own people) / आहवे (in battle)
1:31.meaning: Arjuna says he sees bad signs and no real good in killing his own relatives in battle.

1:32.shloka: न काङ्क्षे विजयं कृष्ण न च राज्यं सुखानि च । किं नो राज्येन गोविन्द किं भोगैर्जीवितेन वा ॥
1:32.transliteration: न काङ्क्षे (I do not desire) / विजयम् (victory) / कृष्ण (Krishna) / न (nor) / च (and) / राज्यम् (kingdom) / सुखानि (pleasures) / च (and) / किम् (what) / नः (to us) / राज्येन (with kingdom) / गोविन्द (Govinda) / किम् (what) / भोगैः (with pleasures) / जीवितेन (with life) / वा (or)
1:32.meaning: Arjuna says he no longer wants victory, a kingdom, pleasure, or even life itself. The fight has lost its meaning for him.

1:33.shloka: येषामर्थे काङ्क्षितं नो राज्यं भोगाः सुखानि च । त इमेऽवस्थिता युद्धे प्राणांस्त्यक्त्वा धनानि च ॥
1:33.transliteration: येषाम् (for whom) / अर्थे (for the sake) / काङ्क्षितम् (desired) / नः (by us) / राज्यम् (kingdom) / भोगाः (pleasures) / सुखानि (comforts) / च (and) / ते (they) / इमे (these) / अवस्थिताḥ (standing) / युद्धे (in battle) / प्राणान् (lives) / त्यक्त्वा (giving up) / धनानि (wealth) / च (and)
1:33.meaning: The people Arjuna hoped to protect and enjoy life with are standing on the battlefield, willing to risk everything. That makes his refusal to fight even heavier.

1:34.shloka: आचार्याः पितरः पुत्रास्तथैव च पितामहाः । मातुलाः श्चशुराः पौत्राः श्यालाः सम्बन्धिनस्तथा ॥
1:34.transliteration: आचार्याः (teachers) / पितरः (fathers) / पुत्राः (sons) / तथा एव च (and likewise) / पितामहाः (grandfathers) / मातुलाः (maternal uncles) / श्चशुराः (fathers-in-law) / पौत्राः (grandsons) / श्यालाः (brothers-in-law) / सम्बन्धिनः (relatives) / तथा (and so on)
1:34.meaning: Arjuna lists the family members and relatives on both sides of the battlefield. He is overwhelmed because the people he would have to fight are his own kin.

1:35.shloka: एतान्न हन्तुमिच्छामि घ्नतोऽपि मधुसूदन । अपि त्रैलोक्यराज्यस्य हेतोः किं नु महीकृते ॥
1:35.transliteration: एतान् (these) / न (not) / हन्तुम् (to kill) / इच्छामि (I want) / घ्नतः (of the one killing) / अपि (even) / मधुसूदन (O Madhusudana) / अपि (even) / त्रैलोक्य-राज्यस्य (of the rule of the three worlds) / हेतोः (for the sake) / किं (what) / नु (indeed) / मही-कृते (for the earth)
1:35.meaning: Arjuna says he cannot bring himself to kill his own relatives, even for power over all three worlds, let alone for a kingdom on earth.

meanings.meaning: of the rule of the three worlds

1:36.shloka: निहत्य धार्तराष्ट्रान्नः का प्रीतिः स्याज्जनार्दन । पापमेवाश्रयेदस्मान्हत्वैतानाततायिनः ॥
1:36.transliteration: निहत्य (by killing) / धार्तराष्ट्रान् (the sons of Dhritarashtra) / नः (to us) / का (what) / प्रीतिः (joy) / स्यात् (would be) / जनार्दन (O Janardana) / पापम् (sin) / एव (only) / आश्रयेत् (would come upon) / अस्मान् (us) / हत्वा (having killed) / एतान् (these) / आततायिनः (aggressors)
1:36.meaning: Arjuna says that killing his own relatives will bring no happiness and will only stain the killers with wrongdoing.

1:37.shloka: तस्मान्नार्हा वयं हन्तुं धार्तराष्ट्रान्स्वबान्धवान् । स्वजनं हि कथं हत्वा सुखिनः स्याम माधव ॥
1:37.transliteration: तस्मात् (therefore) / न अर्हाः (not fit) / वयम् (we) / हन्तुम् (to kill) / धार्तराष्ट्रान् (the sons of Dhritarashtra) / स्व-बान्धवान् (our own relatives) / स्व-जनम् (our own people) / हि (indeed) / कथम् (how) / हत्वा (having killed) / सुखिनः (happy) / स्याम (we would be) / माधव (O Madhava)
1:37.meaning: Arjuna says the Kaurava side are his own relatives, so killing them would not bring happiness.

1:38.shloka: यद्यप्येते न पश्यन्ति लोभोपहतचेतसः । कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं मित्रद्रोहे च पातकम् ॥
1:38.transliteration: यद्यपि (though) / एते (these) / न पश्यन्ति (do not see) / लोभोपहतचेतसः (those whose minds are overcome by greed) / कुलक्षयकृतं (caused by destruction of the family) / दोषम् (fault) / मित्रद्रोहे (in betrayal of friends) / च (and) / पातकम् (wrongdoing)
1:38.meaning: Greed has made these people blind. They cannot see the harm in destroying their own family or betraying friends.

meanings.meaning: those whose minds are overcome by greed
caused by destruction of the family

1:39.shloka: कथं न ज्ञेयमस्माभिः पापादस्मान्निवर्तितुम् । कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं प्रपश्यद्भिर्जनार्दन ॥
1:39.transliteration: कथं (how) / न (not) / ज्ञेयम् (to be known) / अस्माभिः (by us) / पापात् (from sin) / अस्मात् (from this) / निवर्तितुम् (to turn back) / कुलक्षयकृतम् (caused by family-destruction) / दोषम् (fault) / प्रपश्यद्भिः (by those clearly seeing) / जनार्दन (O Janardana)
1:39.meaning: Arjuna says that because he can see the harm caused by destroying the family, he and his side should turn away from this wrong action.

1:40.shloka: कुलक्षये प्रणश्यन्ति कुलधर्माः सनातनाः । धर्मे नष्टे कुलं कृत्स्नमधर्मोऽभिभवत्युत ॥
1:40.transliteration: कुलक्षये (on the destruction of the family) / प्रणश्यन्ति (are destroyed) / कुलधर्माः (family duties) / सनातनाः (ancient, eternal) । / धर्मे (when duty) / नष्टे (is destroyed) / कुलम् (the family) / कृत्स्नम् (entire) / अधर्मः (wrongdoing) / अभिभवति (overpowers) / उत (indeed)
1:40.meaning: When a family is broken, its long-standing duties disappear. Once duty is gone, wrongdoing spreads through the whole family.

meanings.meaning: on the destruction of the family

1:41.shloka: अधर्माभिभवात्कृष्ण प्रदुष्यन्ति कुलस्त्रियः । स्त्रीषु दुष्टासु वार्ष्णेय जायते वर्णसङ्करः ॥
1:41.transliteration: अधर्माभिभवात् (from the dominance of lawlessness) / कृष्ण (O Krishna) / प्रदुष्यन्ति (become corrupted) / कुलस्त्रियः (family women) / स्त्रीषु (when women) / दुष्टासु (are corrupted) / वार्ष्णेय (O descendant of Vrishni) / जायते (is born) / वर्णसङ्करः (mixed disorder of social order)
1:41.meaning: Arjuna fears that when lawlessness takes over a family, women are harmed and social order breaks down further.

1:42.shloka: सङ्करो नरकायैव कुलघ्नानां कुलस्य च । पतन्ति पितरो ह्येषां लुप्तपिण्डोदकक्रियाः ॥
1:42.transliteration: सङ्करः (mixed lineage) / नरकाय (for ruin) / एव (indeed) / कुलघ्नानाम् (of family-destroyers) / कुलस्य (of the family) / च (and) / पतन्ति (fall) / पितरः (ancestors) / हि (indeed) / एषाम् (of these) / लुप्त (stopped) / पिण्ड (food offering) / उदक (water offering) / क्रियाः (rites)
1:42.meaning: Arjuna says that destroying family order creates ruin for the family and even harms the ancestors, because the rites that support them stop.

1:43.shloka: दोषैरेतैः कुलघ्नानां वर्णसङ्करकारकैः । उत्साद्यन्ते जातिधर्माः कुलधर्माश्च शाश्वताः ॥
1:43.transliteration: दोषैः (by faults) / एतैः (these) / कुलघ्नानाम् (of family-destroyers) / वर्णसङ्करकारकैः (causing mixed social order) / उत्साद्यन्ते (are destroyed) / जातिधर्माः (community duties) / कुलधर्माः (family duties) / च (and) / शाश्वताः (eternal)
1:43.meaning: Arjuna says that when family lines break down, long-standing duties within the family and society are destroyed.

1:44.shloka: उत्सन्नकुलधर्माणां मनुष्याणां जनार्दन । नरकेऽनियतं वासो भवतीत्यनुशुश्रुम ॥
1:44.transliteration: उत्सन्नकुलधर्माणाम् (of those whose family duties are destroyed) / मनुष्याणाम् (of people) / जनार्दन (O Janardana) / नरके (in a lower realm) / अनियतं (for an indefinite time) / वासः (dwelling) / भवति (there is) / इति (thus) / अनुशुश्रुम (we have heard)
1:44.meaning: Arjuna says he has heard that people who destroy family duties end up suffering for a long time in a lower realm.

meanings.meaning: of those whose family duties are destroyed

1:45.shloka: अहो बत महत्पापं कर्तुं व्यवसिता वयम् । यद्राज्यसुखलोभेन हन्तुं स्वजनमुद्यताः ॥
1:45.transliteration: अहो बत (alas indeed) / महत्पापं (great sin / grave wrong) / कर्तुं (to do) / व्यवसिता (resolved) / वयम् (we) / यत् (that) / राज्यसुखलोभेन (by greed for kingdom-pleasure) / हन्तुं (to kill) / स्वजनम् (one's own people / kin) / उद्यताः (prepared / ready)
1:45.meaning: Arjuna sees the war as a terrible wrong. He feels they are driven by greed for power and pleasure, and ready to kill their own relatives.

1:46.shloka: यदि मामप्रतीकारमशस्त्रं शस्त्रपाणयः । धार्तराष्ट्रा रणे हन्युस्तन्मे क्षेमतरं भवेत् ॥
1:46.transliteration: यदि (if) / माम् (me) / अप्रतीकारम् (unresisting, not striking back) / अशस्त्रम् (without weapons) / शस्त्रपाणयः (those holding weapons) / धार्तराष्ट्राः (the sons of Dhritarashtra) / रणे (in battle) / हन्युः (might kill) / तत् (that) / मे (for me) / क्षेमतरम् (more beneficial, better) / भवेत् (would be)
1:46.meaning: Arjuna says that even dying unarmed in battle would be better than fighting his own relatives.

1:47.shloka: सञ्जय उवाचएवमुक्त्वाऽर्जुनः संख्ये रथोपस्थ उपाविशत् । विसृज्य सशरं चापं शोकसंविग्नमानसः ॥
1:47.transliteration: सञ्जय उवाच (Sanjaya said) / एवम् (thus) / उक्त्वा (having spoken) / अर्जुनः (Arjuna) / संख्ये (in battle) / रथोपस्थे (on the chariot seat) / उपाविशत् (sat down) / विसृज्य (casting aside) / सशरम् (with arrows) / चापम् (bow) / शोकसंविग्नमानसः (mind agitated by grief)
1:47.meaning: Arjuna gives up his bow and sits down in the chariot, overwhelmed by grief.

2:1.shloka: सञ्जय उवाचतं तथा कृपयाऽविष्टमश्रुपूर्णाकुलेक्षणम् । विषीदन्तमिदं वाक्यमुवाच मधुसूदनः ॥
2:1.transliteration: सञ्जय उवाच (Sanjaya said) / तम् (to him) / तथा (thus) / कृपया (by pity) / आविष्टम् (overcome) / अश्रु-पूर्ण-आकुल-ईक्षणम् (with eyes full of tears and troubled) / विषीदन्तम् (grieving) / इदम् (this) / वाक्यम् (speech) / उवाच (spoke) / मधुसूदनः (Madhusudana)
2:1.meaning: Sanjaya describes Arjuna in tears and grief as Krishna begins to speak to him.

meanings.meaning: with eyes full of tears and troubled

2:2.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचकुतस्त्वा कश्मलमिदं विषमे समुपस्थितम् । अनार्यजुष्टमस्वर्ग्यमकीर्तिकरमर्जुन ॥
2:2.transliteration: श्री भगवान् उवाच (the divine said) / कुतः (from where) / त्वा (to you) / कश्मलम् (delusion) / इदम् (this) / विषमे (in this crisis) / समुपस्थितम् (has arisen) / अनार्य-जुष्टम् (not followed by noble people) / अस्वर्ग्यम् (not leading upward) / अकीर्ति-करम् (bringing disgrace) / अर्जुन (Arjuna)
2:2.meaning: Arjuna's collapse is called out as misplaced weakness. It is not the response of a noble person, and it only lowers him.

2:3.shloka: क्लैब्यं मा स्म गमः पार्थ नैतत्त्वय्युपपद्यते । क्षुद्रं हृदयदौर्बल्यं त्यक्त्वोत्तिष्ठ परन्तप ॥
2:3.transliteration: क्लैब्यम् (weakness / impotence) / मा स्म (do not) / गमः (go / fall into) / पार्थ (O Partha) / न (not) / एतत् (this) / त्वयि (in you) / उपपद्यते (is fitting) / क्षुद्रम् (petty) / हृदयदौर्बल्यम् (weakness of heart) / त्यक्त्वा (having abandoned) / उत्तिष्ठ (rise) / परन्तप (O scorcher of foes)
2:3.meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna not to sink into weakness. He must drop this small-hearted fear and stand up to fight.

2:4.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचकथं भीष्ममहं संख्ये द्रोणं च मधुसूदन । इषुभिः प्रतियोत्स्यामि पूजार्हावरिसूदन ॥
2:4.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / कथम् (how) / भीष्मम् (Bhishma) / अहम् (I) / संख्ये (in battle) / द्रोणम् (Drona) / च (and) / मधुसूदन (O Madhusudana) / इषुभिः (with arrows) / प्रतियोत्स्यामि (shall fight) / पूजार्हौ (worthy of worship) / अरिसूदन (O destroyer of enemies)
2:4.meaning: Arjuna cannot imagine fighting his own respected teachers and elders. His hesitation is not tactical; it is moral and emotional.

2:5.shloka: गुरूनहत्वा हि महानुभावान्श्रेयो भोक्तुं भैक्ष्यमपीह लोके । हत्वार्थकामांस्तु गुरूनिहैवभुञ्जीय भोगान् रुधिरप्रदिग्धान् ॥
2:5.transliteration: गुरून् (teachers) / अहत्वा (without killing) / हि (indeed) / महानुभावान् (great-souled, noble) / श्रेयः (better) / भोक्तुम् (to eat/enjoy) / भैक्ष्यम् (alms) / अपि (even) / इह (here) / लोके (in the world) / हत्वा (having killed) / अर्थकामान् (those driven by wealth and desire) / तु (but) / गुरून् (teachers) / इह (here) / एव (indeed) / भुञ्जीय (I would enjoy) / भोगान् (pleasures) / रुधिरप्रदिग्धान् (stained with blood)
2:5.meaning: Arjuna says it would be better to live on alms than kill his respected teachers. He sees any victory gained that way as tainted by blood and greed.

meanings.meaning: those driven by wealth and desire

2:6.shloka: न चैतद्विद्मः कतरन्नो गरीयोयद्वा जयेम यदि वा नो जयेयुः । यानेव हत्वा न जिजीविषामस्तेऽवस्थिताः प्रमुखे धार्तराष्ट्राः ॥
2:6.transliteration: न (not) / च (and) / एतत् (this) / विद्मः (we know) / कतरत् (which of the two) / नः (for us) / गरीयः (better) / यत् वा (or which) / जयेम (we would conquer) / यदि वा (or whether) / नः (us) / जयेयुः (they would conquer) / यान् (those whom) / एव (indeed) / हत्वा (having killed) / न (not) / जिजीविषामः (we wish to live) / ते (they) / अवस्थिताः (standing) / प्रमुखे (in front) / धार्तराष्ट्राः (sons of Dhritarashtra)
2:6.meaning: Arjuna admits complete uncertainty. He cannot tell whether fighting or not fighting is the better choice, and he cannot bear the thought of killing relatives who stand before him.

2:7.shloka: कार्पण्यदोषोपहतस्वभावःपृच्छामि त्वां धर्मसंमूढचेताः । यच्छ्रेयः स्यान्निश्िचतं ब्रूहि तन्मेशिष्यस्तेऽहं शाधि मां त्वां प्रपन्नम् ॥
2:7.transliteration: कार्पण्यदोषोपहतस्वभावः (nature overcome by weakness) / पृच्छामि (I ask) / त्वाम् (you) / धर्मसंमूढचेताः (mind confused about duty) / यत् (which) / श्रेयः (the highest good) / स्यात् (may be) / निश्चितम् (certainly) / ब्रूहि (tell) / तत् (that) / मे (to me) / शिष्यः (student) / ते (your) / अहम् (I) / शाधि (instruct) / माम् (me) / त्वाम् (you) / प्रपन्नम् (one who has taken refuge)
2:7.meaning: Arjuna admits he is overwhelmed and confused about what is truly right. He asks Krishna to clearly tell him the best course, as a student seeking guidance.

2:8.shloka: न हि प्रपश्यामि ममापनुद्याद्यच्छोकमुच्छोषणमिन्द्रियाणाम् । अवाप्य भूमावसपत्नमृद्धम्राज्यं सुराणामपि चाधिपत्यम् ॥
2:8.transliteration: न हि (for not) / प्रपश्यामि (I see) / मम (my) / अपनुद्यात् (could remove) / यत् (that which) / शोकम् (grief) / उच्छोषणम् (drying up) / इन्द्रियाणाम् (of the senses) / अवाप्य (having obtained) / भूमौ (on earth) / असपत्नम् (unrivaled, without enemies) / ऋद्धम् (prosperous) / राज्यम् (kingdom) / सुराणाम् (of the gods) / अपि (even) / च (and) / आधिपत्यम् (lordship)
2:8.meaning: Arjuna says that even the greatest possible power and wealth would not remove his grief. His mind is so overwhelmed that nothing outside can fix it.

2:9.shloka: सञ्जय उवाचएवमुक्त्वा हृषीकेशं गुडाकेशः परन्तप । न योत्स्य इति गोविन्दमुक्त्वा तूष्णीं बभूव ह ॥
2:9.transliteration: सञ्जय उवाच (Sanjaya said) / एवम् (thus) / उक्त्वा (having spoken) / हृषीकेशम् (to Hrishikesha) / गुडाकेशः (Gudakesha) / परन्तप (scorcher of foes) / न (not) / योत्स्ये (I will fight) / इति (thus) / गोविन्दम् (to Govinda) / उक्त्वा (having said) / तूष्णीं (silent) / बभूव (became) / ह (indeed)
2:9.meaning: Arjuna tells Krishna he will not fight, then falls silent. The argument pauses in complete refusal.

2:10.shloka: तमुवाच हृषीकेशः प्रहसन्निव भारत । सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये विषीदन्तमिदं वचः ॥
2:10.transliteration: तम् (to him) / उवाच (spoke) / हृषीकेशः (Hṛṣīkeśa) / प्रहसन्निव (as if smiling) / भारत (O descendant of Bharata) / सेनयोः (of the two armies) / उभयोः (of both) / मध्ये (in the middle) / विषीदन्तम् (to the grieving one) / इदम् (these) / वचः (words)
2:10.meaning: Krishna begins speaking to Arjuna, who is overwhelmed with grief between the two armies.

2:11.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचअशोच्यानन्वशोचस्त्वं प्रज्ञावादांश्च भाषसे । गतासूनगतासूंश्च नानुशोचन्ति पण्डिताः ॥
2:11.transliteration: श्री भगवानुवाच (the Blessed Lord said) / अशोच्यान् (for the ungrievable) / अन्वशोचः (you have grieved) / त्वम् (you) / प्रज्ञावादान् (words of wisdom) / च (and) / भाषसे (you speak) / गतासून् (the departed breath) / अगतासून् (the not-departed breath) / च (and) / न (not) / अनुशोचन्ति (grieve) / पण्डिताः (the wise)
2:11.meaning: Arjuna is mourning people who should not be mourned, while speaking as if he understands wisdom. Krishna says the wise do not grieve for either the dead or the living.

2:12.shloka: न त्वेवाहं जातु नासं न त्वं नेमे जनाधिपाः । न चैव न भविष्यामः सर्वे वयमतः परम् ॥
2:12.transliteration: न (not) / तु (indeed) / एव (only) / अहम् (I) / जातु (at any time) / न (not) / आसम् (was) / न (not) / त्वम् (you) / नेमे (nor these) / जनाधिपाः (kings) / न (not) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / न (not) / भविष्यामः (will be) / सर्वे (all) / वयम् (we) / अतः परम् (after this)
2:12.meaning: Krishna says that he, Arjuna, and the kings have always existed in some form, and none of them will simply vanish after death.

2:13.shloka: देहिनोऽस्मिन्यथा देहे कौमारं यौवनं जरा । तथा देहान्तरप्राप्तिर्धीरस्तत्र न मुह्यति ॥
2:13.transliteration: देहिनः (the embodied one) / अस्मिन् (in this) / यथा (just as) / देहे (in the body) / कौमारम् (childhood) / यौवनम् (youth) / जरा (old age) / तथा (so) / देहान्तरप्राप्तिः (attaining another body) / धीरः (the wise) / तत्र (there) / न (not) / मुह्यति (is deluded)
2:13.meaning: The body changes through stages, and the one living in it moves on to another body. A steady person does not get confused by this.

2:14.transliteration: मात्रास्पर्शाः (sense-contacts) / तु (indeed) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / शीतोष्णसुखदुःखदाः (giving cold-heat, pleasure-pain) / आगमापायिनः (coming and going) / अनित्याः (impermanent) / तान् (them) / तितिक्षस्व (endure) / भारत (O Bharata)
2:14.meaning: Pleasure and pain from the senses are temporary. They come and go, so you should learn to bear them without being shaken.

2:15.shloka: यं हि न व्यथयन्त्येते पुरुषं पुरुषर्षभ । समदुःखसुखं धीरं सोऽमृतत्वाय कल्पते ॥
2:15.transliteration: यम् (whom) / हि (indeed) / न (not) / व्यथयन्ति (disturb) / एते (these) / पुरुषम् (person) / पुरुषर्षभ (best among men) / समदुःखसुखम् (equal in pain and pleasure) / धीरम् (steadfast one) / सः (that one) / अमृतत्वाय (for immortality) / कल्पते (is fit)
2:15.meaning: A steady person who stays even through pleasure and pain is not thrown off by changing experiences and becomes ready for inner freedom.

2:16.shloka: नासतो विद्यते भावो नाभावो विद्यते सतः । उभयोरपि दृष्टोऽन्तस्त्वनयोस्तत्त्वदर्शिभिः ॥
2:16.transliteration: नासतः (of the unreal) / विद्यते (exists) / भावः (being) / न (not) / अभावः (nonbeing) / विद्यते (exists) / सतः (of the real) / उभयोः (of both) / अपि (also) / दृष्टः (seen) / अन्तः (limit) / तु (indeed) / अनयोः (of these) / तत्त्वदर्शिभिः (by seers of reality)
2:16.meaning: What is temporary does not truly exist, and what is real cannot be destroyed. Those who see clearly understand the difference between the two.

2:17.shloka: अविनाशि तु तद्विद्धि येन सर्वमिदं ततम् । विनाशमव्ययस्यास्य न कश्चित् कर्तुमर्हति ॥
2:17.transliteration: अविनाशि (imperishable) / तु (indeed) / तत् (that) / विद्धि (know) / येन (by which) / सर्वम् इदम् (all this) / ततम् (pervaded) / विनाशम् (destruction) / अव्ययस्य अस्य (of this imperishable) / न कश्चित् (no one) / कर्तुम् (to do) / अर्हति (is able)
2:17.meaning: Know that the reality behind everything is imperishable. No force can destroy what pervades the whole world.

2:18.shloka: अन्तवन्त इमे देहा नित्यस्योक्ताः शरीरिणः । अनाशिनोऽप्रमेयस्य तस्माद्युध्यस्व भारत ॥
2:18.transliteration: अन्तवन्तः (ending / finite) / इमे (these) / देहाः (bodies) / नित्यस्य (of the eternal) / उक्ताः (are said to be) / शरीरिणः (of the embodied being) / अनाशिनः (of the indestructible) / अप्रमेयस्य (of the immeasurable) / तस्मात् (therefore) / युध्यस्व (fight) / भारत (Arjuna)
2:18.meaning: Bodies are temporary, but the inner being is eternal and cannot be measured or destroyed. Arjuna is told to fight because of that truth.

2:19.shloka: य एनं वेत्ति हन्तारं यश्चैनं मन्यते हतम् । उभौ तौ न विजानीतो नायं हन्ति न हन्यते ॥
2:19.transliteration: यः (who) / एनम् (this) / वेत्ति (knows) / हन्तारम् (as killer) / यः (and who) / च (also) / एनम् (this) / मन्यते (thinks) / हतम् (killed) / उभौ (both) / तौ (those two) / न (not) / विजानीतः (understand) / न (not) / अयम् (this) / हन्ति (kills) / न (not) / हन्यते (is killed)
2:19.meaning: The true self cannot be killed, and it does not kill. Seeing it as either one is ignorance.

2:20.shloka: न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचिन्नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः । अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणोन हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे ॥
2:20.transliteration: न जायते (is not born) / म्रियते (dies) वा (or) कदाचित् (at any time) / नायम् (this one not) / भूत्वा (having become) / भविता (will become) वा (or) न भूयः (not again) / अजः (unborn) / नित्यः (eternal) / शाश्वतः (everlasting) / अयम् (this) / पुराणः (ancient) / न हन्यते (is not slain) / हन्यमाने (being slain) शरीरे (in the body)
2:20.meaning: The true self is never born and never dies. It does not begin with the body, and it is not destroyed when the body ends.

meanings.meaning: will become) वा (or) न भूयः (not again

2:21.shloka: वेदाविनाशिनं नित्यं य एनमजमव्ययम् । कथं स पुरुषः पार्थ कं घातयति हन्ति कम् ॥
2:21.transliteration: वेद (knows) / अविनाशिनम् (the imperishable) / नित्यम् (the eternal) / यः (who) / एनम् (this one) / अजम् (unborn) / अव्ययम् (unchanging) / कथम् (how) / सः (that) / पुरुषः (person) / पार्थ (Arjuna) / कम् (whom) / घातयति (causes to be killed) / हन्ति (kills) / कम् (whom)
2:21.meaning: Anyone who truly sees the inner being as unborn and unchanging cannot think in terms of killing or being killed.

2:22.shloka: वासांसि जीर्णानि यथा विहायनवानि गृह्णाति नरोऽपराणि । तथा शरीराणि विहाय जीर्णान्यन्यानि संयाति नवानि देही ॥
2:22.transliteration: वासांसि (clothes) / जीर्णानि (worn-out) / यथा (as) / विहाय (having discarded) / नवानि (new) / गृह्णाति (takes) / नरः (a person) / अपराणि (other) / तथा (so) / शरीराणि (bodies) / विहाय (having discarded) / जीर्णानि (worn-out) / अन्यानि (other) / संयाति (goes to) / नवानि (new) / देही (the embodied one)
2:22.meaning: The true self passes from one body to another the way a person changes clothes.

2:23.shloka: नैनं छिन्दन्ति शस्त्राणि नैनं दहति पावकः । न चैनं क्लेदयन्त्यापो न शोषयति मारुतः ॥
2:23.transliteration: नैनं (not this) / छिन्दन्ति (cut) / शस्त्राणि (weapons) / नैनं (not this) / दहति (burns) / पावकः (fire) / न (not) / च (and) / एनं (this) / क्लेदयन्ति (wet) / आपो (waters) / न (not) / शोषयति (dries) / मारुतः (wind)
2:23.meaning: The true self cannot be harmed by weapons, fire, water, or wind. It is beyond physical destruction.

2:24.shloka: अच्छेद्योऽयमदाह्योऽयमक्लेद्योऽशोष्य एव च । नित्यः सर्वगतः स्थाणुरचलोऽयं सनातनः ॥
2:24.transliteration: अच्छेद्यः (uncuttable) / अयम् (this) / अदाह्यः (unburnable) / अयम् (this) / अक्लेद्यः (unwettable) / अशोष्यः (undryable) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / नित्यः (eternal) / सर्वगतः (all-pervading) / स्थाणुः (fixed) / अचलः (unmoving) / अयम् (this) / सनातनः (timeless)
2:24.meaning: The true self cannot be destroyed by any physical force. It is permanent, present everywhere, unmoving, and beyond time.

2:25.transliteration: अव्यक्तः (unseen) / अयम् (this) / अचिन्त्यः (unthinkable) / अयम् (this) / अविकार्यः (unchanging) / अयम् (this) / उच्यते (is said to be) / तस्मात् (therefore) / एवम् (thus) / विदित्वा (having known) / एनम् (this) / न (not) / अनुशोचितुम् (to grieve) / अर्हसि (you should)
2:25.meaning: The true self is not something you can see, think through, or alter. Knowing that, Arjuna should stop grieving.

2:26.shloka: अथ चैनं नित्यजातं नित्यं वा मन्यसे मृतम् । तथापि त्वं महाबाहो नैवं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥
2:26.transliteration: अथ (even then) / च (also) / एनम् (this) / नित्यजातम् (always born) / नित्यम् (always) / वा (or) / मन्यसे (you think) / मृतम् (dead) / तथापि (even so) / त्वम् (you) / महाबाहो (mighty-armed one) / नैवम् (not like this) / शोचितुम् (to grieve) / अर्हसि (you should)
2:26.meaning: Even if you see this embodied being as something that keeps being born and dying, grief is still not fitting. Krishna is pushing Arjuna beyond sorrow itself.

2:27.shloka: जातस्य हि ध्रुवो मृत्युर्ध्रुवं जन्म मृतस्य च । तस्मादपरिहार्येऽर्थे न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥
2:27.transliteration: जातस्य (of the born) / हि (indeed) / ध्रुवः (certain) / मृत्युः (death) / ध्रुवम् (certain) / जन्म (birth) / मृतस्य (of the dead) / च (and) / तस्मात् (therefore) / अपरिहार्ये (in the unavoidable) / अर्थे (matter) / न (not) / त्वम् (you) / शोचितुम् (to grieve) / अर्हसि (should)
2:27.meaning: Anything that is born will die, and anything that dies will be born again. Since this cycle cannot be stopped, there is no reason to grieve over it.

2:28.shloka: अव्यक्तादीनि भूतानि व्यक्तमध्यानि भारत । अव्यक्तनिधनान्येव तत्र का परिदेवना ॥
2:28.transliteration: अव्यक्तादीनि (unmanifest at the beginning) / भूतानि (beings) / व्यक्तमध्यानि (manifest in the middle) / भारत (O Bharata) / अव्यक्तनिधनान्येव (unmanifest at the end also) / तत्र (there) / का (what) / परिदेवना (lamentation)
2:28.meaning: All beings are hidden before birth and hidden again after death. Since life is only a brief visible interval, there is no reason for grief.

2:29.shloka: आश्चर्यवत्पश्यति कश्चिदेनमाश्चर्यवद्वदति तथैव चान्यः । आश्चर्यवच्चैनमन्यः श्रृणोतिश्रुत्वाप्येनं वेद न चैव कश्चित् ॥
2:29.transliteration: आश्चर्यवत् (as wondrous) / पश्यति (beholds) / कश्चित् (someone) / एनम् (this one) / आश्चर्यवत् (as wondrous) / वदति (speaks) / तथा (thus) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / अन्यः (another) / आश्चर्यवत् (as wondrous) / च (and) / एनम् (this one) / अन्यः (another) / श्रृणोति (hears) / श्रुत्वा अपि (even after hearing) / एनम् (this one) / वेद (knows) / न (not) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / कश्चित् (anyone)
2:29.meaning: The true self is astonishing to behold, describe, and hear about, yet almost no one truly understands it.

2:30.shloka: देही नित्यमवध्योऽयं देहे सर्वस्य भारत । तस्मात्सर्वाणि भूतानि न त्वं शोचितुमर्हसि ॥
2:30.transliteration: देही (the embodied one) / नित्यम् (always, eternally) / अवध्यः (not to be slain) / अयम् (this) / देहे (in the body) / सर्वस्य (of all) / भारत (O Bharata) / तस्मात् (therefore) / सर्वाणि (all) / भूतानि (beings) / न (not) / त्वम् (you) / शोचितुम् (to grieve) / अर्हसि (you should)
2:30.meaning: The true self in every body cannot be destroyed, so there is no reason to grieve for anyone.

2:31.shloka: स्वधर्ममपि चावेक्ष्य न विकम्पितुमर्हसि । धर्म्याद्धि युद्धाछ्रेयोऽन्यत्क्षत्रियस्य न विद्यते ॥
2:31.transliteration: स्वधर्मम् (one's own duty) / अपि (even) / च (and) / अवेक्ष्य (considering) / न (not) / विकम्पितुम् (to waver) / अर्हसि (you should) / धर्म्यात् (from righteous) / हि (indeed) / युद्धात् (from battle) / श्रेयः (more beneficial) / अन्यत् (other) / क्षत्रियस्य (for a warrior) / न (not) / विद्यते (there is)
2:31.meaning: Arjuna is told not to hesitate once he sees his own duty clearly. For a warrior, fighting for what is right is the best course.

2:32.shloka: यदृच्छया चोपपन्नं स्वर्गद्वारमपावृतम् । सुखिनः क्षत्रियाः पार्थ लभन्ते युद्धमीदृशम् ॥
2:32.transliteration: यदृच्छया (by chance / unbidden) / च (and) / उपपन्नम् (obtained / come upon) / स्वर्गद्वारम् (gate to higher realms) / अपावृतम् (opened) / सुखिनः (fortunate) / क्षत्रियाः (warriors) / पार्थ (O Partha) / लभन्ते (receive / obtain) / युद्धम् (battle) / ईदृशम् (such)
2:32.meaning: A warrior who is given a righteous battle has a rare chance to act with honor and courage. Krishna calls such an opportunity a doorway to a higher realm.

2:33.shloka: अथ चैत्त्वमिमं धर्म्यं संग्रामं न करिष्यसि । ततः स्वधर्मं कीर्तिं च हित्वा पापमवाप्स्यसि ॥
2:33.transliteration: अथ (then) / च (and) / एत् (this) / त्वम् (you) / इमम् (this) / धर्म्यम् (righteous) / संग्रामम् (war) / न (not) / करिष्यसि (will do) / ततः (then) / स्वधर्मम् (one's own duty) / कीर्तिम् (honor) / च (and) / हित्वा (having abandoned) / पापम् (blame / wrongdoing) / अवाप्स्यसि (you will obtain)
2:33.meaning: Refusing a just battle would mean abandoning your duty and honor, and that choice would bring blame.

2:34.shloka: अकीर्तिं चापि भूतानि कथयिष्यन्ति तेऽव्ययाम् । संभावितस्य चाकीर्तिर्मरणादतिरिच्यते ॥
2:34.transliteration: अकीर्तिम् (disgrace) / च (and) / अपि (also) / भूतानि (beings) / कथयिष्यन्ति (will speak) / ते (of you) / अव्ययाम् (everlasting) / संभावितस्य (of one who has been honored) / च (and) / अकीर्तिः (disgrace) / मरणात् (than death) / अतिरिच्यते (is greater)
2:34.meaning: People will keep speaking of your disgrace, and for someone who has been honored, that shame feels worse than death.

meanings.meaning: of one who has been honored

2:35.shloka: भयाद्रणादुपरतं मंस्यन्ते त्वां महारथाः । येषां च त्वं बहुमतो भूत्वा यास्यसि लाघवम् ॥
2:35.transliteration: भयात् (from fear) / रणात् (from battle) / उपरतम् (withdrawn) / मंस्यन्ते (they will think) / त्वाम् (you) / महारथाः (great warriors) / येषाम् (of whom) / च (and) / त्वम् (you) / बहुमतः (highly regarded) / भूत्वा (having become) / यास्यसि (you will go) / लाघवम् (to smallness / low status)
2:35.meaning: If Arjuna stops fighting, the great warriors will assume he fled from fear. People who respected him will now look down on him.

2:36.shloka: अवाच्यवादांश्च बहून् वदिष्यन्ति तवाहिताः । निन्दन्तस्तव सामर्थ्यं ततो दुःखतरं नु किम् ॥
2:36.transliteration: अवाच्यवादान् (unworthy words) / च (and) / बहून् (many) / वदिष्यन्ति (they will speak) / तव (your) / अहिताः (enemies) / निन्दन्तः (mocking) / तव (your) / सामर्थ्यम् (strength) / ततः (than that) / दुःखतरम् (more painful) / नु (indeed) / किम् (what)
2:36.meaning: Your enemies will mock your ability and say cruel things about you. For a respected person, that kind of public shame can feel worse than death.

2:37.shloka: हतो वा प्राप्स्यसि स्वर्गं जित्वा वा भोक्ष्यसे महीम् । तस्मादुत्तिष्ठ कौन्तेय युद्धाय कृतनिश्चयः ॥
2:37.transliteration: हतो (killed) / वा (or) / प्राप्स्यसि (you will attain) / स्वर्गं (heaven) / जित्वा (having won) / वा (or) / भोक्ष्यसे (you will enjoy) / महीम् (the earth) / तस्मात् (therefore) / उत्तिष्ठ (rise) / कौन्तेय (Kaunteya) / युद्धाय (for battle) / कृतनिश्चयः (with firm resolve)
2:37.meaning: Arjuna is told to stand up and fight without wavering. Whether he wins or dies, he must do what stands before him now.

2:38.shloka: सुखदुःखे समे कृत्वा लाभालाभौ जयाजयौ । ततो युद्धाय युज्यस्व नैवं पापमवाप्स्यसि ॥
2:38.transliteration: सुखदुःखे (pleasure and pain) / समे (equal) / कृत्वा (having made) / लाभालाभौ (gain and loss) / जयाजयौ (victory and defeat) / ततः (then) / युद्धाय (for battle) / युज्यस्व (be engaged) / न (not) / एवम् (thus) / पापम् (wrongdoing) / अवाप्स्यसि (you will obtain)
2:38.meaning: Stay steady through pleasure and pain, success and failure. Act from that balance, and your action will not bind you.

2:39.shloka: एषा तेऽभिहिता सांख्ये बुद्धिर्योगे त्विमां श्रृणु । बुद्ध्यायुक्तो यया पार्थ कर्मबन्धं प्रहास्यसि ॥
2:39.transliteration: एषा (this) / ते (to you) / अभिहिता (has been taught) / सांख्ये (in Sankhya) / बुद्धिः (wisdom) / योगे (in yoga) / तु (now) / इमाम् (this) / श्रृणु (hear) / बुद्ध्या (with understanding) / युक्तः (equipped) / यया (by which) / पार्थ (Arjuna) / कर्मबन्धम् (bondage of action) / प्रहास्यसि (you will cast off)
2:39.meaning: Krishna says the same wisdom has already been explained as knowledge, and now he will explain it as action. With this clear understanding, Arjuna can break free from the bondage created by action.

2:40.shloka: नेहाभिक्रमनाशोऽस्ति प्रत्यवायो न विद्यते । स्वल्पमप्यस्य धर्मस्य त्रायते महतो भयात् ॥
2:40.transliteration: न इह (not here/in this world) / अभिक्रम-नाशः (loss of beginning effort) / अस्ति (there is) / प्रत्यवायः (reverse result or harmful consequence) / न (not) / विद्यते (is found) / स्वल्पम् (a little) / अपि (even) / अस्य (of this) / धर्मस्य (of dharma) / त्रायते (protects) / महतः (from great) / भयात् (fear)
2:40.meaning: Even a small beginning in this discipline is never wasted, never backfires, and can protect you from deep fear.

2:41.shloka: व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिरेकेह कुरुनन्दन । बहुशाखा ह्यनन्ताश्च बुद्धयोऽव्यवसायिनाम् ॥
2:41.transliteration: व्यवसायात्मिका (resolute) / बुद्धिः (intellect) / एका (one) / इह (here) / कुरुनन्दन (O son of the Kurus) / बहुशाखाः (many-branched) / हि (indeed) / अनन्ताः (endless) / च (and) / बुद्धयः (intellects) / अव्यवसायिनाम् (of the irresolute)
2:41.meaning: A focused mind has one clear direction, while an unfocused mind keeps branching into endless doubts and options.

2:42.shloka: यामिमां पुष्पितां वाचं प्रवदन्त्यविपश्चितः । वेदवादरताः पार्थ नान्यदस्तीति वादिनः ॥
2:42.transliteration: याम् (which) / इमाम् (this) / पुष्पिताम् (flowery, ornamented) / वाचम् (speech) / प्रवदन्ति (they speak) / अविपश्चितः (the unwise) / वेदवादरताः (delighting in Vedic discourse) / पार्थ (O Partha) / न (not) / अन्यत् (anything else) / अस्ति (there is) / इति (thus) / वादिनः (those who say)
2:42.meaning: Ignorant people speak in attractive, polished language that promises pleasure and reward. They get stuck in ritual talk and claim there is nothing beyond it.

2:43.shloka: कामात्मानः स्वर्गपरा जन्मकर्मफलप्रदाम् । क्रियाविशेषबहुलां भोगैश्वर्यगतिं प्रति ॥
2:43.transliteration: कामात्मानः (those whose minds are full of desire) / स्वर्गपरा (with heaven as their highest aim) / जन्मकर्मफलप्रदाम् (giving the fruits of birth and action) / क्रियाविशेषबहुलाम् (full of many special rites) / भोगैश्वर्यगतिम् (a path to enjoyment and power) / प्रति (toward)
2:43.meaning: People driven by desire praise rituals that promise pleasure, status, and future rewards. Their words sound attractive, but they keep the mind chasing more rewards.

meanings.meaning: those whose minds are full of desire
with heaven as their highest aim
giving the fruits of birth and action
a path to enjoyment and power

2:44.shloka: भोगैश्वर्यप्रसक्तानां तयापहृतचेतसाम् । व्यवसायात्मिका बुद्धिः समाधौ न विधीयते ॥
2:44.transliteration: भोगैश्वर्यप्रसक्तानाम् (of those attached to enjoyment and power) / तया (by that) / अपहृतचेतसाम् (whose minds have been stolen) / व्यवसायात्मिका (one-pointed, resolute) / बुद्धिः (intellect) / समाधौ (in meditation / absorbed stillness) / न (not) / विधीयते (arises / is established)
2:44.meaning: People who are pulled toward pleasure and power lose clear judgment. Their mind cannot settle into steady focus on the supreme reality.

meanings.meaning: of those attached to enjoyment and power

2:45.shloka: त्रैगुण्यविषया वेदा निस्त्रैगुण्यो भवार्जुन । निर्द्वन्द्वो नित्यसत्त्वस्थो निर्योगक्षेम आत्मवान् ॥
2:45.transliteration: त्रैगुण्यविषया (concerned with the three qualities) / वेदा (the Vedas) / निस्त्रैगुण्यो (one beyond the three qualities) / भव (be) / अर्जुन (Arjuna) / निर्द्वन्द्वो (free from opposites) / नित्यसत्त्वस्थो (steadfast in the ever-real) / निर्योगक्षेमः (free from concern for acquisition and preservation) / आत्मवान् (established in the true self)
2:45.meaning: The sacred texts describe the world of changing forces, but Krishna tells Arjuna to rise above them. He should stop chasing comfort and security, and stay rooted in what is unchanging.

meanings.meaning: free from concern for acquisition and preservation

2:46.shloka: यावानर्थ उदपाने सर्वतः संप्लुतोदके । तावान्सर्वेषु वेदेषु ब्राह्मणस्य विजानतः ॥
2:46.transliteration: यावान् (as much) / अर्थः (use, purpose) / उदपाने (in a small water-source) / सर्वतः (from all sides) / संप्लुतोदके (in a fully flooded reservoir) / तावान् (that much) / सर्वेषु (in all) / वेदेषु (in the Vedas) / ब्राह्मणस्य (for the knower of Brahman) / विजानतः (for one who truly knows)
2:46.meaning: A person who truly understands the supreme reality needs the Vedas no more than a person beside a full reservoir needs a tiny puddle.

2:47.shloka: कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन । मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि ॥
2:47.transliteration: कर्मणि (in action) / एव (only) / अधिकारः (right) / ते (your) / मा (not) / फलेषु (in fruits) / कदाचन (at any time) / मा (not) / कर्मफलहेतुः (cause of the fruit of action) / भूः (become) / मा (not) / ते (your) / सङ्गः (attachment) / अस्तु (let there be) / अकर्मणि (in inaction)
2:47.meaning: You control your action, not what it produces. Do your duty without chasing the reward or getting attached to doing nothing.

meanings.meaning: cause of the fruit of action

2:48.shloka: योगस्थः कुरु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा धनञ्जय । सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः समो भूत्वा समत्वं योग उच्यते ॥
2:48.transliteration: योगस्थः (established in yoga) / कुरु (do) / कर्माणि (actions) / सङ्गं (attachment) / त्यक्त्वा (having abandoned) / धनञ्जय (O Dhananjaya) / सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः (in success and failure) / समः (equal) / भूत्वा (having become) / समत्वं (equanimity) / योगः (yoga) / उच्यते (is called)
2:48.meaning: Keep doing your duty without clinging to results. Stay steady whether things work out or not; that steady balance is yoga.

2:49.shloka: दूरेण ह्यवरं कर्म बुद्धियोगाद्धनञ्जय । बुद्धौ शरणमन्विच्छ कृपणाः फलहेतवः ॥
2:49.transliteration: दूरेण (far) / हि (indeed) / अवरम् (inferior) / कर्म (action) / बुद्धियोगात् (to the yoga of discernment) / धनञ्जय (O Dhananjaya) / बुद्धौ (in discernment) / शरणम् (refuge) / अन्विच्छ (seek) / कृपणाः (pitiable ones) / फलहेतवः (those who are motivated by results)
2:49.meaning: Action done for rewards is lower than action guided by clear discernment. Krishna tells Arjuna to rely on that steadiness instead of craving outcomes.

meanings.meaning: those who are motivated by results

2:50.shloka: बुद्धियुक्तो जहातीह उभे सुकृतदुष्कृते । तस्माद्योगाय युज्यस्व योगः कर्मसु कौशलम् ॥
2:50.transliteration: बुद्धियुक्तः (one united with discerning mind) / जहाति (casts off) / इह (here, in this life) / उभे (both) / सुकृतदुष्कृते (good deed and bad deed) / तस्मात् (therefore) / योगाय (for yoga) / युज्यस्व (be devoted) / योगः (yoga) / कर्मसु (in actions) / कौशलम् (skill, dexterity)
2:50.meaning: Clear discernment lets a person rise above both merit and fault. Krishna tells Arjuna to take up yoga, because true yoga is skill in action.

2:51.shloka: कर्मजं बुद्धियुक्ता हि फलं त्यक्त्वा मनीषिणः । जन्मबन्धविनिर्मुक्ताः पदं गच्छन्त्यनामयम् ॥
2:51.transliteration: कर्मजं (born of action) / बुद्धियुक्ताः (those united with discerning clarity) / हि (indeed) / फलम् (fruit) / त्यक्त्वा (having abandoned) / मनीषिणः (wise people) / जन्मबन्धविनिर्मुक्ताः (freed from the bondage of birth) / पदम् (state) / गच्छन्ति (they go) / अनामयम् (flawless, free from affliction)
2:51.meaning: Wise people let go of the results of their actions. That freedom breaks the chain that keeps them bound to repeated birth and suffering.

meanings.meaning: freed from the bondage of birth

2:52.shloka: यदा ते मोहकलिलं बुद्धिर्व्यतितरिष्यति । तदा गन्तासि निर्वेदं श्रोतव्यस्य श्रुतस्य च ॥
2:52.transliteration: यदा (when) / ते (your) / मोहकलिलं (the swamp of delusion) / बुद्धिः (intellect) / व्यतितरिष्यति (will cross over) / तदा (then) / गन्तासि (you will go) / निर्वेदं (to dispassion) / श्रोतव्यस्य (of what is to be heard) / श्रुतस्य (of what has been heard) / च (and)
2:52.meaning: When clear understanding rises above confusion, attachment to pleasures you have heard of or are still chasing naturally fades.

meanings.meaning: of what is to be heard

2:53.shloka: श्रुतिविप्रतिपन्ना ते यदा स्थास्यति निश्चला । समाधावचला बुद्धिस्तदा योगमवाप्स्यसि ॥
2:53.transliteration: श्रुतिविप्रतिपन्ना (confused by conflicting teachings) / ते (your) / यदा (when) / स्थास्यति (will remain) / निश्चला (steady, unmoving) / समाधौ (in complete stillness) / अचला (unmoving) / बुद्धिः (intellect) / तदा (then) / योगम् (yoga) / अवाप्स्यसि (you will attain)
2:53.meaning: When your mind is no longer shaken by conflicting ideas and becomes completely steady, you reach yoga.

2:54.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचस्थितप्रज्ञस्य का भाषा समाधिस्थस्य केशव । स्थितधीः किं प्रभाषेत किमासीत व्रजेत किम् ॥
2:54.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / स्थितप्रज्ञस्य (of one whose wisdom is steady) / का (what) / भाषा (mark or description) / समाधिस्थस्य (of one established in complete stillness) / केशव (O Kesava) / स्थितधीः (one of steady understanding) / किम् (how) / प्रभाषेत (would speak) / किम् (how) / आसीत (would sit) / व्रजेत (would walk) / किम् (what)
2:54.meaning: Arjuna asks how a person with steady wisdom behaves in daily life — how they speak, sit, and move.

meanings.meaning: of one whose wisdom is steady
of one established in complete stillness

2:55.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचप्रजहाति यदा कामान् सर्वान् पार्थ मनोगतान् । आत्मन्येवात्मना तुष्टः स्थितप्रज्ञस्तदोच्यते ॥
2:55.transliteration: श्री भगवानुवाच (the divine said) / प्रजहाति (gives up) / यदा (when) / कामान् (desires) / सर्वान् (all) / पार्थ (O Partha) / मनोगतान् (arising in the mind) / आत्मनि (in the true self) / एव (indeed) / आत्मना (by the true self) / तुष्टः (satisfied) / स्थितप्रज्ञः (steady in wisdom) / तदा (then) / उच्यते (is called)
2:55.meaning: A person becomes steady in wisdom when all mental desires are released and contentment comes from within, not from outside things.

2:56.transliteration: दुःखेषु (in sorrows) / अनुद्विग्न-मनाः (mind not agitated) / सुखेषु (in pleasures) / विगत-स्पृहः (free from craving) / वीत-राग-भय-क्रोधः (gone beyond attachment, fear, and anger) / स्थित-धीः (steady-minded) / मुनिः (sage) / उच्यते (is called)
2:56.meaning: A truly steady person stays calm in hardship, does not chase pleasure, and is free from attachment, fear, and anger.

meanings.meaning: gone beyond attachment, fear, and anger

2:57.shloka: यः सर्वत्रानभिस्नेहस्तत्तत्प्राप्य शुभाशुभम् । नाभिनन्दति न द्वेष्टि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
2:57.transliteration: यः (who) / सर्वत्र (everywhere) / अनभिस्नेहः (without clinging) / तत्-तत् (that-that) / प्राप्य (having obtained) / शुभ-अशुभम् (good and bad) / न (not) / अभिनन्दति (rejoices) / न (not) / द्वेष्टि (hates) / तस्य (of that one) / प्रज्ञा (wisdom) / प्रतिष्ठिता (firmly established)
2:57.meaning: A person with steady wisdom does not cling to anything. When good or bad events happen, they neither get thrilled nor upset.

2:58.shloka: यदा संहरते चायं कूर्मोऽङ्गानीव सर्वशः । इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
2:58.transliteration: यदा (when) / संहरते (withdraws) / च (and) / अयम् (this one) / कूर्मः (tortoise) / अङ्गानि (limbs) / इव (like) / सर्वशः (from all sides) / इन्द्रियाणि (the senses) / इन्द्रियार्थेभ्यः (from sense-objects) / तस्य (of that one) / प्रज्ञा (wisdom) / प्रतिष्ठिता (steadfast)
2:58.meaning: Wisdom becomes stable when the senses are pulled back from the things they chase, just as a tortoise draws in its limbs.

2:59.shloka: विषया विनिवर्तन्ते निराहारस्य देहिनः । रसवर्जं रसोऽप्यस्य परं दृष्ट्वा निवर्तते ॥
2:59.transliteration: विषयाः (objects of the senses) / विनिवर्तन्ते (turn away) / निराहारस्य (of the one without intake) / देहिनः (of the embodied one) / रसवर्जम् (except for taste) / रसः (taste) / अपि (even) / अस्य (of this one) / परम् (the supreme) / दृष्ट्वा (having seen) / निवर्तते (turns away)
2:59.meaning: Stopping the senses is not enough. The outer attraction may fade, but the inner taste remains until a higher reality is directly seen.

2:60.shloka: यततो ह्यपि कौन्तेय पुरुषस्य विपश्चितः । इन्द्रियाणि प्रमाथीनि हरन्ति प्रसभं मनः ॥
2:60.transliteration: यततः (for one striving) / हि (indeed) / अपि (even) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / पुरुषस्य (of a person) / विपश्चितः (of the wise) / इन्द्रियाणि (the senses) / प्रमाथीनि (restless, turbulent) / हरन्ति (carry away) / प्रसभं (forcibly) / मनः (the mind)
2:60.meaning: Even a thoughtful person who is trying hard can lose control when the senses become restless and drag the mind away.

2:61.shloka: तानि सर्वाणि संयम्य युक्त आसीत मत्परः । वशे हि यस्येन्द्रियाणि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
2:61.transliteration: तानि (those) / सर्वाणि (all) / संयम्य (having restrained) / युक्तः (disciplined, aligned) / आसीत (should sit) / मत्परः (devoted to Me) / वशे (under control) / हि (indeed) / यस्य (whose) / इन्द्रियाणि (senses) / तस्य (of that one) / प्रज्ञा (wisdom) / प्रतिष्ठिता (firmly established)
2:61.meaning: Hold the senses in check and stay focused on the divine. When the senses are controlled, clear wisdom becomes steady.

2:62.shloka: ध्यायतो विषयान्पुंसः सङ्गस्तेषूपजायते । सङ्गात् संजायते कामः कामात्क्रोधोऽभिजायते ॥
2:62.transliteration: ध्यायतः (while thinking of) / विषयान् (sense objects) / पुंसः (for a person) / सङ्गः (attachment) / तेषु (to them) / उपजायते (arises) / सङ्गात् (from attachment) / संजायते (is born) / कामः (desire) / कामात् (from desire) / क्रोधः (anger) / अभिजायते (arises strongly)
2:62.meaning: Fixating on pleasures or objects creates attachment. Attachment turns into wanting, and wanting turns into anger when blocked.

2:63.shloka: क्रोधाद्भवति संमोहः संमोहात्स्मृतिविभ्रमः । स्मृतिभ्रंशाद् बुद्धिनाशो बुद्धिनाशात्प्रणश्यति ॥
2:63.transliteration: क्रोधात् (from anger) / भवति (arises) / संमोहः (delusion) / संमोहात् (from delusion) / स्मृतिविभ्रमः (confusion of memory) / स्मृतिभ्रंशात् (from loss of memory) / बुद्धिनाशः (destruction of discernment) / बुद्धिनाशात् (from destruction of discernment) / प्रणश्यति (one is ruined)
2:63.meaning: Anger starts a chain reaction: confusion, broken memory, lost judgment, and finally self-destruction.

2:64.transliteration: राग-द्वेष-वियुक्तैः (free from attraction and aversion) / तु (but) / विषयान् (objects) / इन्द्रियैः (with the senses) / चरन् (moving about) / आत्म-वश्यैः (under the control of the inner self) / विधेय-आत्मा (disciplined person) / प्रसादम् (serenity) / अधिगच्छति (attains)
2:64.meaning: A person with controlled senses can engage with the world without craving or rejecting it, and that steadiness brings inner clarity.

meanings.meaning: under the control of the inner self

2:65.shloka: प्रसादे सर्वदुःखानां हानिरस्योपजायते । प्रसन्नचेतसो ह्याशु बुद्धिः पर्यवतिष्ठते ॥
2:65.transliteration: प्रसादे (in clarity/serenity) / सर्वदुःखानाम् (of all sorrows) / हानिः (ending) / अस्य (of this one) / उपजायते (arises) । / प्रसन्नचेतसः (of one with a clear mind) / हि (indeed) / आशु (quickly) / बुद्धिः (understanding) / पर्यवतिष्ठते (becomes firmly established)
2:65.meaning: When the mind becomes calm and clear, suffering drops away and understanding becomes steady very quickly.

meanings.meaning: of one with a clear mind

2:66.shloka: नास्ति बुद्धिरयुक्तस्य न चायुक्तस्य भावना । न चाभावयतः शान्तिरशान्तस्य कुतः सुखम् ॥
2:66.transliteration: नास्ति (there is not) / बुद्धिः (steady discernment) / अयुक्तस्य (of one who is unrestrained) / न (not) / च (and) / अयुक्तस्य (of one who is unrestrained) / भावना (steady purpose) / न (not) / च (and) / अभावयतः (of one who lacks inner cultivation) / शान्तिः (peace) / अशान्तस्य (of one without peace) / कुतः (how can there be) / सुखम् (happiness)
2:66.meaning: A person who cannot control the mind and senses lacks clear judgment, steady purpose, peace, and therefore real happiness.

meanings.meaning: of one who lacks inner cultivation

2:67.shloka: इन्द्रियाणां हि चरतां यन्मनोऽनुविधीयते । तदस्य हरति प्रज्ञां वायुर्नावमिवाम्भसि ॥
2:67.transliteration: इन्द्रियाणाम् (of the senses) / हि (indeed) / चरताम् (roaming) / यत् (which) / मनः (the mind) / अनुविधीयते (follows after) / तत् (that) / अस्य (of this one) / हरति (takes away) / प्रज्ञाम् (discernment) / वायुः (the wind) / नावम् इव (like a boat) / अम्भसि (on water)
2:67.meaning: If even one sense runs after its objects, it can pull the mind along and destroy clear judgment, like wind pushing a boat off course.

2:68.shloka: तस्माद्यस्य महाबाहो निगृहीतानि सर्वशः । इन्द्रियाणीन्द्रियार्थेभ्यस्तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता ॥
2:68.transliteration: तस्मात् (therefore) / यस्य (whose) / महाबाहो (O mighty-armed one) / निगृहीतानि (restrained) / सर्वशः (in every way) / इन्द्रियाणि (the senses) / इन्द्रियार्थेभ्यः (from sense-objects) / तस्य (of that one) / प्रज्ञा (wisdom) / प्रतिष्ठिता (is established)
2:68.meaning: A person who fully controls the senses and keeps them away from objects of desire develops steady understanding.

2:69.shloka: या निशा सर्वभूतानां तस्यां जागर्ति संयमी । यस्यां जाग्रति भूतानि सा निशा पश्यतो मुनेः ॥
2:69.transliteration: या (that) / निशा (night) / सर्वभूतानां (of all beings) / तस्याम् (in that) / जागर्ति (is awake) / संयमी (the self-controlled one) / यस्याम् (in which) / जाग्रति (are awake) / भूतानि (beings) / सा (that) / निशा (night) / पश्यतः (of the seer) / मुनेः (of the sage)
2:69.meaning: Ordinary people chase pleasure and possessions, but the disciplined seeker sees through that pull. What seems valuable to most becomes empty to the one who knows the real.

2:70.shloka: आपूर्यमाणमचलप्रतिष्ठंसमुद्रमापः प्रविशन्ति यद्वत् । तद्वत्कामा यं प्रविशन्ति सर्वेस शान्तिमाप्नोति न कामकामी ॥
2:70.transliteration: आपूर्यमाणम् (being filled) / अचल-प्रतिष्ठम् (firmly established, unmoving) / समुद्रम् (the sea) / आपः (waters) / प्रविशन्ति (enter) / यद्वत् (just as) / तद्वत् (so) / कामाः (desires) / यम् (whom) / प्रविशन्ति (enter) / सर्वे (all) / सः (that one) / शान्तिम् (peace) / आप्नोति (attains) / न (not) / काम-कामी (one who desires pleasures)
2:70.meaning: A person can meet desire without being shaken by it. Peace belongs to the one who stays steady, not to the one chasing pleasure.

2:71.shloka: विहाय कामान्यः सर्वान्पुमांश्चरति निःस्पृहः । निर्ममो निरहंकारः स शांतिमधिगच्छति ॥
2:71.transliteration: विहाय (giving up) / कामान् (desires) / यः (who) / सर्वान् (all) / पुमान् (person) / चरति (moves, acts) / निःस्पृहः (without craving) / निर्ममः (without possessiveness) / निरहंकारः (without ego) / सः (that one) / शांतिम् (peace) / अधिगच्छति (attains)
2:71.meaning: A person who acts after releasing desire, possessiveness, and ego finds peace.

2:72.shloka: एषा ब्राह्मी स्थितिः पार्थ नैनां प्राप्य विमुह्यति । स्थित्वाऽस्यामन्तकालेऽपि ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृच्छति ॥
2:72.transliteration: एषा (this) / ब्राह्मी (of Brahman) / स्थितिः (state) / पार्थ (Arjuna) / न (not) / एनाम् (this) / प्राप्य (having attained) / विमुह्यति (is deluded) / स्थित्वा (having remained established) / अस्याम् (in this) / अन्तकाले (at the end of life) / अपि (even) / ब्रह्मनिर्वाणम् (freedom in Brahman) / ऋच्छति (attains)
2:72.meaning: A person who reaches this steady inner state is no longer confused. Even at life’s end, that steadiness leads to freedom in the supreme reality.

3:1.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचज्यायसी चेत्कर्मणस्ते मता बुद्धिर्जनार्दन । तत्किं कर्मणि घोरे मां नियोजयसि केशव ॥
3:1.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / ज्यायसी (better, superior) / चेत् (if) / कर्मणः (than action) / ते (by you) / मता (considered) / बुद्धिः (understanding) / जनार्दन (O Janardana) / तत् (then) / किम् (why) / कर्मणि (in action) / घोरे (terrible, dreadful) / माम् (me) / नियोजयसि (you engage, you urge) / केशव (O Keshava)
3:1.meaning: Arjuna is confused. Krishna seems to praise understanding over action, yet still asks him to fight. He asks for one clear instruction that will lead him toward what is good.

3:2.shloka: व्यामिश्रेणेव वाक्येन बुद्धिं मोहयसीव मे । तदेकं वद निश्िचत्य येन श्रेयोऽहमाप्नुयाम् ॥
3:2.transliteration: व्यामिश्रेण (mixed) / इव (as if) / वाक्येन (by words) / बुद्धिम् (understanding) / मोहयसि (you confuse) / इव (as if) / मे (my) / तत् (that) / एकम् (one) / वद (speak) / निश्िचत्य (after deciding) / येन (by which) / श्रेयः (highest good) / अहम् (I) / आप्नुयाम् (may attain)
3:2.meaning: Arjuna says Krishna's advice feels mixed and confusing. He asks for one clear teaching that will lead him to the highest good.

3:3.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचलोकेऽस्मिन्द्विविधा निष्ठा पुरा प्रोक्ता मयानघ । ज्ञानयोगेन सांख्यानां कर्मयोगेन योगिनाम् ॥
3:3.transliteration: श्रीभगवान् (the divine) / उवाच (said) / लोकेऽस्मिन् (in this world) / द्विविधा (twofold) / निष्ठा (steadfast practice) / पुरा (formerly) / प्रोक्ता (taught) / मया (by me) / अनघ (sinless one) / ज्ञानयोगेन (through the yoga of knowledge) / सांख्यानाम् (of the contemplative knowers) / कर्मयोगेन (through the yoga of action) / योगिनाम् (of the yogis)
3:3.meaning: There are two valid ways to live steadily: one through understanding, one through action. Different temperaments need different disciplines.

3:4.shloka: न कर्मणामनारम्भान्नैष्कर्म्यं पुरुषोऽश्नुते । न च संन्यसनादेव सिद्धिं समधिगच्छति ॥
3:4.transliteration: न (not) / कर्मणाम् (of actions) / अनारम्भात् (from non-beginning) / नैष्कर्म्यम् (actionlessness) / पुरुषः (a person) / अश्नुते (attains) । न (not) / च (and) / संन्यसनात् (by renunciation) / एव (alone) / सिद्धिम् (perfection) / समधिगच्छति (reaches)
3:4.meaning: Freedom does not come from simply stopping action, and perfection does not come from merely giving things up. Real growth requires a deeper way of acting.

3:5.shloka: न हि कश्िचत्क्षणमपि जातु तिष्ठत्यकर्मकृत् । कार्यते ह्यवशः कर्म सर्वः प्रकृतिजैर्गुणैः ॥
3:5.transliteration: न हि (indeed not) / कश्चित् (anyone) / क्षणम् अपि (even for a moment) / जातु (ever) / तिष्ठति (remains) / अकर्मकृत् (one who does no action) / कार्यते (is made to act) / हि (indeed) / अवशः (helplessly) / कर्म (action) / सर्वः (everyone) / प्रकृतिजैः (born of nature) / गुणैः (by the qualities)
3:5.meaning: No one can stay inactive forever. The qualities of nature keep pushing every person into action.

3:6.shloka: कर्मेन्द्रियाणि संयम्य य आस्ते मनसा स्मरन् । इन्द्रियार्थान्विमूढात्मा मिथ्याचारः स उच्यते ॥
3:6.transliteration: कर्मेन्द्रियाणि (organs of action) / संयम्य (having restrained) / यः (who) / आस्ते (sits) / मनसा (with the mind) / स्मरन् (remembering, thinking of) / इन्द्रियार्थान् (sense objects) / विमूढात्मा (deluded one) / मिथ्याचारः (false actor, hypocrite) / सः (he) / उच्यते (is called)
3:6.meaning: Holding back your actions while the mind still chases desire is self-deception. Outer restraint means little without inner discipline.

3:7.shloka: यस्त्विन्द्रियाणि मनसा नियम्यारभतेऽर्जुन । कर्मेन्द्रियैः कर्मयोगमसक्तः स विशिष्यते ॥
3:7.transliteration: यः (who) / तु (indeed) / इन्द्रियाणि (the senses) / मनसा (with the mind) / नियम्य (having restrained) / आरभते (begins) / अर्जुन (Arjuna) / कर्मेन्द्रियैः (with the organs of action) / कर्मयोगम् (karma yoga) / असक्तः (unattached) / सः (he) / विशिष्यते (is श्रेष्ठ, excels)
3:7.meaning: The best way to act is to keep the senses under the mind's control and do your work without attachment.

3:8.shloka: नियतं कुरु कर्म त्वं कर्म ज्यायो ह्यकर्मणः । शरीरयात्रापि च ते न प्रसिद्ध्येदकर्मणः ॥
3:8.transliteration: नियतं (prescribed) / कुरु (do) / कर्म (action, duty) / त्वं (you) / कर्म (action) / ज्यायः (better) / हि (indeed) / अकर्मणः (than inaction) / शरीरयात्रा (bodily maintenance, livelihood) / अपि (also, even) / च (and) / ते (your) / न (not) / प्रसिद्ध्येत् (would be accomplished) / अकर्मणः (by inaction)
3:8.meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna to do the duty assigned to him. Action is better than sitting idle, and even basic life cannot continue through inaction.

3:9.shloka: यज्ञार्थात्कर्मणोऽन्यत्र लोकोऽयं कर्मबन्धनः । तदर्थं कर्म कौन्तेय मुक्तसंगः समाचर ॥
3:9.transliteration: यज्ञार्थात् (for the purpose of sacrifice) / कर्मणः (from action) / अन्यत्र (other than) / लोकः अयम् (this world) / कर्मबन्धनः (bound by action) / तत्-अर्थम् (for that purpose) / कर्म (action) / कौन्तेय (son of Kunti) / मुक्तसंगः (free from attachment) / समाचर (perform)
3:9.meaning: Work done only for personal gain creates bondage. Work done as an offering, without attachment, should be done instead.

3:10.shloka: सहयज्ञाः प्रजाः सृष्ट्वा पुरोवाच प्रजापतिः । अनेन प्रसविष्यध्वमेष वोऽस्त्विष्टकामधुक् ॥
3:10.transliteration: सहयज्ञाः (together with sacrifice) / प्रजाः (beings) / सृष्ट्वा (having created) / पुरा (in the beginning) / उवाच (said) / प्रजापतिः (Prajapati) / अनेन (by this) / प्रसविष्यध्वम् (you shall prosper) / एषः (this) / वः (for you) / अस्तु (may be) / इष्टकामधुक् (the wish-fulfilling cow)
3:10.meaning: At the start of creation, Prajapati set up a way of living where sacrifice and duty help all beings flourish. The message is to support life through shared responsibility.

3:11.shloka: देवान्भावयतानेन ते देवा भावयन्तु वः । परस्परं भावयन्तः श्रेयः परमवाप्स्यथ ॥
3:11.transliteration: देवान् (the radiant beings) / भावयत (nurture, support) / अनेन (through this) / ते (those) / देवा (the radiant beings) / भावयन्तु (may nurture, support) / वः (you) / परस्परम् (mutually, one another) / भावयन्तः (nurturing, supporting) / श्रेयः (the highest good) / परम् (supreme) / अवाप्स्यथ (you will attain)
3:11.meaning: Support the larger order through your actions, and that order will support you in return. Mutual support leads to the highest good.

3:12.shloka: इष्टान्भोगान्हि वो देवा दास्यन्ते यज्ञभाविताः । तैर्दत्तानप्रदायैभ्यो यो भुङ्क्ते स्तेन एव सः ॥
3:12.transliteration: इष्टान् (desired) / भोगान् (enjoyments) / हि (indeed) / वः (to you) / देवाः (radiant beings) / दास्यन्ते (will give) / यज्ञभाविताः (nourished by sacrifice) / तैः (by those) / दत्तान् (given) / अप्रदाय (without giving back) / एभ्यः (to these) / यः (who) / भुङ्क्ते (enjoys) / स्तेनः (thief) / एव (indeed) / सः (he)
3:12.meaning: The world keeps giving when you participate in shared responsibility. Taking benefits without giving back is treated as theft.

3:13.shloka: यज्ञशिष्टाशिनः सन्तो मुच्यन्ते सर्वकिल्बिषैः । भुञ्जते ते त्वघं पापा ये पचन्त्यात्मकारणात् ॥
3:13.transliteration: यज्ञशिष्टाशिनः (eating what remains after sacrifice) / सन्तः (the good, the noble) / मुच्यन्ते (are freed) / सर्वकिल्बिषैः (from all faults) / भुञ्जते (they eat/enjoy) / ते (they) / तु (but) / अघं (wrongdoing) / पापाः (the sinful) / ये (those who) / पचन्ति (cook) / आत्मकारणात् (for their own sake)
3:13.meaning: People who take only what remains after offering their work for the larger order are cleansed. People who act only for themselves end up consuming their own wrongdoing.

3:14.shloka: अन्नाद्भवन्ति भूतानि पर्जन्यादन्नसम्भवः । यज्ञाद्भवति पर्जन्यो यज्ञः कर्मसमुद्भवः ॥
3:14.transliteration: अन्नात् (from food) / भवन्ति (come into being) / भूतानि (beings) / पर्जन्यात् (from rain) / अन्नसम्भवः (food-born) / यज्ञात् (from sacrifice) / भवति (comes) / पर्जन्यः (rain) / यज्ञः (sacrifice) / कर्मसमुद्भवः (action-born)
3:14.meaning: Life depends on a chain: food comes from rain, rain comes from sacrifice, and sacrifice comes from action.

3:15.shloka: कर्म ब्रह्मोद्भवं विद्धि ब्रह्माक्षरसमुद्भवम् । तस्मात्सर्वगतं ब्रह्म नित्यं यज्ञे प्रतिष्ठितम् ॥
3:15.transliteration: कर्म (action) / ब्रह्मोद्भवं (born from Brahman) / विद्धि (know) / ब्रह्म (Brahman) / अक्षरसमुद्भवम् (arising from the imperishable) / तस्मात् (therefore) / सर्वगतम् (all-pervading) / ब्रह्म (Brahman) / नित्यम् (always) / यज्ञे (in sacrifice) / प्रतिष्ठितम् (established)
3:15.meaning: Action arises from sacred order, that order arises from the imperishable, and the all-pervading supreme reality is rooted in selfless offering.

3:16.shloka: एवं प्रवर्तितं चक्रं नानुवर्तयतीह यः । अघायुरिन्द्रियारामो मोघं पार्थ स जीवति ॥
3:16.transliteration: एवम् (thus) / प्रवर्तितम् (set in motion) / चक्रम् (cycle) / न (not) / अनुवर्तयति (follows) / इह (here) / यः (who) / अघायुः (one whose life is tainted by wrongdoing) / इन्द्रियारामः (delighting in the senses) / मोघम् (in vain) / पार्थ (O Partha) / सः (he) / जीवति (lives)
3:16.meaning: A person who ignores the world’s proper cycle of action and only chases pleasure ends up living a wasted life.

meanings.meaning: one whose life is tainted by wrongdoing

3:17.shloka: यस्त्वात्मरतिरेव स्यादात्मतृप्तश्च मानवः । आत्मन्येव च सन्तुष्टस्तस्य कार्यं न विद्यते ॥
3:17.transliteration: यः (who) / तु (indeed) / आत्म-रतिḥ (one who delights in the self) / एव (alone) / स्यात् (may be) / आत्म-तृप्तः (satisfied in the self) / च (and) / मानवः (person) / आत्मनि (in the self) / एव (alone) / च (and) / सन्तुष्टः (content) / तस्य (for that one) / कार्यम् (duty, task) / न (not) / विद्यते (exists)
3:17.meaning: A person who finds complete fulfillment in the true self does not act from inner lack. Nothing remains to be gained through duty or effort.

meanings.meaning: one who delights in the self

3:18.shloka: नैव तस्य कृतेनार्थो नाकृतेनेह कश्चन । न चास्य सर्वभूतेषु कश्िचदर्थव्यपाश्रयः ॥
3:18.transliteration: नैव (not at all) / तस्य (for that one) / कृतेन (by doing) / अर्थः (purpose, gain) / न (not) / अकृतेन (by not doing) / इह (here) / कश्चन (any) / न (not) / च (and) / अस्य (for this one) / सर्वभूतेषु (among all beings) / कश्चित् (any) / अर्थव्यपाश्रयः (basis for personal gain)
3:18.meaning: A person who has reached inner completeness no longer acts for personal gain. Neither action nor inaction adds anything to that person.

3:19.shloka: तस्मादसक्तः सततं कार्यं कर्म समाचर । असक्तो ह्याचरन्कर्म परमाप्नोति पूरुषः ॥
3:19.transliteration: तस्मात् (therefore) / असक्तः (without attachment) / सततम् (always) / कार्यम् (duty) / कर्म (action) / समाचर (perform fully) / असक्तः (without attachment) / हि (indeed) / आचरन् (acting) / कर्म (action) / परमम् (the supreme) / आप्नोति (reaches) / पूरुषः (a person)
3:19.meaning: Keep doing what must be done, but do it without clinging to results. That kind of action leads you to the highest good.

3:20.shloka: कर्मणैव हि संसिद्धिमास्थिता जनकादयः । लोकसंग्रहमेवापि संपश्यन्कर्तुमर्हसि ॥
3:20.transliteration: कर्मणा (through action) / एव (indeed) / हि (for) / संसिद्धिम् (perfection) / आस्थिता (having attained) / जनकादयः (Janaka and others) / लोकसंग्रहम् (the holding-together of the world) / एव (indeed) / अपि (also) / संपश्यन् (seeing) / कर्तुम् (to do) / अर्हसि (you are fit)
3:20.meaning: Great people like Janaka reached perfection by acting, not by withdrawing. You should also act for the good of the world.

3:21.shloka: यद्यदाचरति श्रेष्ठस्तत्तदेवेतरो जनः । स यत्प्रमाणं कुरुते लोकस्तदनुवर्तते ॥
3:21.transliteration: यद्यत् (whatever) / आचरति (does, practices) / श्रेष्ठः (the best person) / तत् तत् एव (that very same) / इतरः (other) / जनः (people) / सः (that person) / यत् (whatever) / प्रमाणं (standard, authority) / कुरुते (sets, makes) / लोकः (the world, people) / तत् (that) / अनुवर्तते (follows)
3:21.meaning: People copy the conduct of those they admire. A respected person's actions become the standard others imitate.

3:22.shloka: न मे पार्थास्ति कर्तव्यं त्रिषु लोकेषु किञ्चन । नानवाप्तमवाप्तव्यं वर्त एव च कर्मणि ॥
3:22.transliteration: न (not) / मे (for me) / पार्थ (O Partha) / अस्ति (there is) / कर्तव्यम् (to be done / duty) / त्रिषु (in the three) / लोकेषु (worlds) / किञ्चन (anything) / न (not) / अनवाप्तम् (not attained) / अवाप्तव्यम् (to be attained) / वर्ते (I remain / I engage) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / कर्मणि (in action)
3:22.meaning: Krishna says he has no unfinished duty and nothing left to gain, yet he still stays active.

3:23.shloka: यदि ह्यहं न वर्तेयं जातु कर्मण्यतन्द्रितः । मम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्याः पार्थ सर्वशः ॥
3:23.transliteration: यदि (if) / हि (indeed) / अहं (I) / न (not) / वर्तेयं (were to act) / जातु (ever) / कर्मणि (in action) / अतन्द्रितः (without negligence) / मम (my) / वर्त्मन् (path) / अनुवर्तन्ते (follow) / मनुष्याः (people) / पार्थ (Arjuna) / सर्वशः (in every way)
3:23.meaning: Krishna says that if he stopped doing his duty carefully, people would copy that neglect and society would suffer.

3:24.shloka: उत्सीदेयुरिमे लोका न कुर्यां कर्म चेदहम् । सङ्करस्य च कर्ता स्यामुपहन्यामिमाः प्रजाः ॥
3:24.transliteration: उत्सीदेयुः (would fall into ruin) / इमे (these) / लोकाः (worlds) / न (not) / कुर्याम् (I would do) / कर्म (action) / चेत् (if) / अहम् (I) / सङ्करस्य (of disorder / mixed confusion) / च (and) / कर्ता (maker) / स्याम् (I would become) / उपहन्याम् (I would destroy) / इमाः (these) / प्रजाः (people)
3:24.meaning: Krishna says that even he must keep acting, because if he stopped, society would fall into disorder and people would be harmed.

3:25.transliteration: सक्ताः (attached) / कर्मणि (in action) / अविद्वांसः (the ignorant) / यथा (as) / कुर्वन्ति (perform) / भारत (O Bharata) / कुर्यात् (should perform) / विद्वान् (the wise) / तथा (likewise) / असक्तः (without attachment) / चिकीर्षुः (desiring to do) / लोकसंग्रहम् (the welfare/order of the world)
3:25.meaning: A wise person should keep acting, but without clinging to the results. The point is to support the world, not feed ego.

3:26.shloka: न बुद्धिभेदं जनयेदज्ञानां कर्मसङ्गिनाम् । जोषयेत्सर्वकर्माणि विद्वान् युक्तः समाचरन् ॥
3:26.transliteration: न (not) / बुद्धिभेदं (confusion of understanding) / जनयेत् (should generate) / अज्ञानां (of the ignorant) / कर्मसङ्गिनाम् (of those attached to action) / जोषयेत् (should encourage) / सर्वकर्माणि (all actions) / विद्वान् (the wise one) / युक्तः (steadfast, disciplined) / समाचरन् (while acting properly)
3:26.meaning: A wise person should not confuse people who are still attached to action. Instead, by acting well, they should guide others into right action.

3:27.shloka: प्रकृतेः क्रियमाणानि गुणैः कर्माणि सर्वशः । अहङ्कारविमूढात्मा कर्ताऽहमिति मन्यते ॥
3:27.transliteration: प्रकृतेः (of nature) / क्रियमाणानि (being done) / गुणैः (by the qualities) / कर्माणि (actions) / सर्वशः (in every way) / अहङ्कार-विमूढ-आत्मा (the inner being deluded by ego) / कर्ता (doer) / अहम् (I) / इति (thus) / मन्यते (thinks)
3:27.meaning: Actions happen through the forces of nature, not through the false sense of 'I am the doer.' Ego creates the illusion of authorship.

meanings.meaning: the inner being deluded by ego

3:28.shloka: तत्त्ववित्तु महाबाहो गुणकर्मविभागयोः । गुणा गुणेषु वर्तन्त इति मत्वा न सज्जते ॥
3:28.transliteration: तत्त्ववित् (one who knows the truth) / तु (indeed) / महाबाहो (mighty-armed one) / गुणकर्मविभागयोः (of the division of qualities and actions) / गुणाः (the qualities) / गुणेषु (among the qualities) / वर्तन्ते (move/operate) / इति (thus) / मत्वा (having understood) / न (not) / सज्जते (becomes attached)
3:28.meaning: A person who understands how qualities and actions work sees that nature is doing the acting, not the separate ego. That understanding removes attachment.

meanings.meaning: of the division of qualities and actions

3:29.shloka: प्रकृतेर्गुणसम्मूढाः सज्जन्ते गुणकर्मसु । तानकृत्स्नविदो मन्दान्कृत्स्नविन्न विचालयेत् ॥
