3:29.transliteration: प्रकृतेः (of nature) / गुणसम्मूढाः (deluded by the qualities) / सज्जन्ते (they cling) / गुणकर्मसु (to actions of the qualities) / तान् (those) / अकृत्स्नविदः (those who do not know fully) / मन्दान् (the dull-minded) / कृत्स्नवित् (the one who knows fully) / न (not) / विचालयेत् (should unsettle)
3:29.meaning: People lost in the forces of nature become attached to action and its results. A person who understands more deeply should not shake them up unnecessarily.

meanings.meaning: those who do not know fully

3:30.shloka: मयि सर्वाणि कर्माणि संन्यस्याध्यात्मचेतसा । निराशीर्निर्ममो भूत्वा युध्यस्व विगतज्वरः ॥
3:30.transliteration: मयि (to me) / सर्वाणि (all) / कर्माणि (actions) / संन्यस्य (having renounced, having surrendered) / अध्यात्मचेतसा (with the inwardly aware mind) / निराशीः (without desire) / निर्ममः (without possessiveness) / भूत्वा (having become) / युध्यस्व (fight) / विगतज्वरः (free from fever, free from distress)
3:30.meaning: Give every action inwardly to the divine, then do your duty without craving results, without clinging, and without inner distress.

meanings.meaning: free from fever, free from distress

3:31.shloka: ये मे मतमिदं नित्यमनुतिष्ठन्ति मानवाः । श्रद्धावन्तोऽनसूयन्तो मुच्यन्ते तेऽपि कर्मभिः ॥
3:31.transliteration: ये (those) / मे (My) / मतम् (teaching) / इदम् (this) / नित्यम् (always) / अनुतिष्ठन्ति (follow) / मानवाः (people) / श्रद्धावन्तः (full of trust) / अनसूयन्तः (without fault-finding) / मुच्यन्ते (are freed) / ते (they) / अपि (also) / कर्मभिः (from actions)
3:31.meaning: People who follow Krishna's teaching with trust and without resentment or criticism are freed from the binding effects of action.

3:32.shloka: ये त्वेतदभ्यसूयन्तो नानुतिष्ठन्ति मे मतम् । सर्वज्ञानविमूढांस्तान्विद्धि नष्टानचेतसः ॥
3:32.transliteration: ये (those) / तु (but) / एतत् (this) / अभ्यसूयन्तः (finding fault with) / न (not) / अनुतिष्ठन्ति (follow) / मे (my) / मतम् (teaching) / सर्वज्ञानविमूढान् (deluded in all knowledge) / तान् (them) / विद्धि (know) / नष्टान् (ruined) / अचेतसः (without understanding)
3:32.meaning: People who reject this teaching out of fault-finding lose clear understanding and become confused. The problem is not the teaching; it is the closed mind that refuses to practise it.

3:33.shloka: सदृशं चेष्टते स्वस्याः प्रकृतेर्ज्ञानवानपि । प्रकृतिं यान्ति भूतानि निग्रहः किं करिष्यति ॥
3:33.transliteration: सदृशं (according to) / चेष्टते (acts) / स्वस्याः (of one's own) / प्रकृतेः (nature) / ज्ञानवान् अपि (even the wise) / प्रकृतिम् (nature) / यान्ति (go to) / भूतानि (all beings) / निग्रहः (restraint) / किम् (what) / करिष्यति (will do)
3:33.meaning: Even a wise person acts according to their own nature. Every living being follows its nature, so force alone cannot change that.

3:34.shloka: इन्द्रियस्येन्द्रियस्यार्थे रागद्वेषौ व्यवस्थितौ । तयोर्न वशमागच्छेत्तौ ह्यस्य परिपन्थिनौ ॥
3:34.transliteration: इन्द्रियस्य-इन्द्रियस्य-अर्थे (in each sense's object) / राग-द्वेषौ (attachment and aversion) / व्यवस्थितौ (are established) / तयोः (of those two) / न (not) / वशम् (under the power) / आगच्छेत् (one should come) / तौ (those two) / हि (indeed) / अस्य (for this one) / परिपन्थिनौ (the two enemies on the path)
3:34.meaning: Craving and dislike appear in every sense experience. Do not let either one control your choices, because both block the path forward.

meanings.meaning: the two enemies on the path

3:35.shloka: श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात् । स्वधर्मे निधनं श्रेयः परधर्मो भयावहः ॥
3:35.transliteration: श्रेयान् (better) / स्वधर्मः (one's own duty) / विगुणः (imperfect, lacking qualities) / परधर्मात् (than another's duty) / स्वनुष्ठितात् (well performed) / स्वधर्मे (in one's own duty) / निधनम् (death) / श्रेयः (better, more auspicious) / परधर्मः (another's duty) / भयावहः (fearful, dangerous)
3:35.meaning: Your own responsibility matters more than copying someone else's path. Even if you do it imperfectly, it is better than doing another person's role well.

3:36.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचअथ केन प्रयुक्तोऽयं पापं चरति पूरुषः । अनिच्छन्नपि वार्ष्णेय बलादिव नियोजितः ॥
3:36.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / अथ (then) / केन (by what) / प्रयुक्तः (impelled) / अयम् (this) / पापम् (wrongdoing) / चरति (does) / पूरुषः (person) / अनिच्छन् (not desiring) / अपि (even) / वार्ष्णेय (O descendant of the Vrishni clan) / बलात् (by force) / इव (as if) / नियोजितः (appointed / driven) ॥
3:36.meaning: Arjuna asks what makes a person do wrong even when they do not want to, as if some force is pushing them.

meanings.meaning: O descendant of the Vrishni clan

3:37.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचकाम एष क्रोध एष रजोगुणसमुद्भवः । महाशनो महापाप्मा विद्ध्येनमिह वैरिणम् ॥
3:37.transliteration: श्रीभगवानुवाच (the Lord said) / कामः (desire) / एषः (this) / क्रोधः (anger) / एषः (this) / रजोगुणसमुद्भवः (born from the quality of passion) / महाशनः (all-devouring) / महापाप्मा (greatly destructive) / विद्धि (know) / एनम् (this) / इह (here) / वैरिणम् (as an enemy)
3:37.meaning: Desire, born from restlessness, turns into anger and destroys clarity. Krishna says to recognize it as an enemy before it takes over.

meanings.meaning: born from the quality of passion

3:38.shloka: धूमेनाव्रियते वह्निर्यथाऽऽदर्शो मलेन च । यथोल्बेनावृतो गर्भस्तथा तेनेदमावृतम् ॥
3:38.transliteration: धूमेन (by smoke) / आव्रियते (is covered) / वह्निः (fire) / यथा (as) / आदर्शः (mirror) / मलेन (by dust) / च (and) / यथा (as) / उल्बेन (by a membrane/womb-covering) / आवृतः (covered) / गर्भः (womb/embryo) / तथा (so) / तेन (by that) / इदम् (this) / आवृतम् (is covered)
3:38.meaning: Desire hides clear seeing just as smoke hides fire, dust hides a mirror, and a womb-covering hides an embryo.

3:39.shloka: आवृतं ज्ञानमेतेन ज्ञानिनो नित्यवैरिणा । कामरूपेण कौन्तेय दुष्पूरेणानलेन च ॥
3:39.transliteration: आवृतम् (covered) / ज्ञानम् (wisdom) / एतेन (by this) / ज्ञानिनः (of the wise) / नित्य-वैरिणा (by the eternal enemy) / काम-रूपेण (in the form of desire) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / दुष्पूरेण (by the hard-to-satisfy) / अनलेन (by fire)
3:39.meaning: Desire hides clear understanding. It never feels satisfied, and it keeps working against the wise like an unquenchable fire.

3:40.shloka: इन्द्रियाणि मनो बुद्धिरस्याधिष्ठानमुच्यते । एतैर्विमोहयत्येष ज्ञानमावृत्य देहिनम् ॥
3:40.transliteration: इन्द्रियाणि (the senses) / मनः (the mind) / बुद्धिः (the discerning mind) / अस्य (of this) / अधिष्ठानम् (seat, basis) / उच्यते (is said to be) । / एतैः (through these) / विमोहयति (deludes) / एषः (this) / ज्ञानम् (understanding) / आवृत्य (covering) / देहिनम् (the embodied one)
3:40.meaning: Desire works through the senses, the restless mind, and the discerning mind. It clouds understanding and makes a person act blindly.

3:41.shloka: तस्मात्त्वमिन्द्रियाण्यादौ नियम्य भरतर्षभ । पाप्मानं प्रजहि ह्येनं ज्ञानविज्ञाननाशनम् ॥
3:41.transliteration: तस्मात् (therefore) / त्वम् (you) / इन्द्रियाणि (the senses) / आदौ (first) / नियम्य (having restrained) / भरतर्षभ (O best of the Bharatas) / पाप्मानम् (the sinful one) / प्रजहि (strike down) / हि (indeed) / एनम् (this) / ज्ञान-विज्ञान-नाशनम् (the destroyer of knowledge and wisdom)
3:41.meaning: Control the senses first, because unchecked craving destroys clear understanding. The real enemy is not outside you but the force that clouds judgment from within.

meanings.meaning: the destroyer of knowledge and wisdom

3:42.shloka: इन्द्रियाणि पराण्याहुरिन्द्रियेभ्यः परं मनः । मनसस्तु परा बुद्धिर्यो बुद्धेः परतस्तु सः ॥
3:42.transliteration: इन्द्रियाणि (the senses) / पराणि (higher) / आहुः (they say) / इन्द्रियेभ्यः (than the senses) / परम् (higher) / मनः (the mind) / मनसः (than the mind) / तु (but) / परा (higher) / बुद्धिः (the discerning mind) / यः (which) / बुद्धेः (than the discerning mind) / परतः (higher) / तु (but) / सः (that)
3:42.meaning: The senses are above the body, the mind is above the senses, and the discerning mind is above the mind. Beyond even the discerning mind stands desire, which must be mastered.

3:43.shloka: एवं बुद्धेः परं बुद्ध्वा संस्तभ्यात्मानमात्मना । जहि शत्रुं महाबाहो कामरूपं दुरासदम् ॥
3:43.transliteration: एवम् (thus) / बुद्धेः (of the intellect) / परम् (higher) / बुद्ध्वा (knowing) / संस्तभ्य (steadying) / आत्मानम् (the lower self) / आत्मना (by the higher self) / जहि (destroy) / शत्रुम् (enemy) / महाबाहो (mighty-armed one) / कामरूपम् (in the form of desire) / दुरासदम् (hard to conquer)
3:43.meaning: Recognize that desire is not the highest force in you. Use your deeper discernment to steady yourself and overcome it.

4:1.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचइमं विवस्वते योगं प्रोक्तवानहमव्ययम् । विवस्वान् मनवे प्राह मनुरिक्ष्वाकवेऽब्रवीत् ॥
4:1.transliteration: श्रीभगवानुवाच (the Lord said) / इमम् (this) / विवस्वते (to Vivasvan) / योगम् (yoga) / प्रोक्तवान् (taught) / अहम् (I) / अव्ययम् (imperishable) / विवस्वान् (Vivasvan) / मनवे (to Manu) / प्राह (said) / मनुः (Manu) / इक्ष्वाकवे (to Ikshvaku) / अब्रवीत् (spoke)
4:1.meaning: Krishna says he taught this timeless yoga to the sun-god, who passed it to Manu, then to Ikshvaku. The teaching is ancient and preserved through a lineage.

4:2.shloka: एवं परम्पराप्राप्तमिमं राजर्षयो विदुः । स कालेनेह महता योगो नष्टः परन्तप ॥
4:2.transliteration: एवम् (thus) / परम्परा-प्राप्तम् (received through succession) / इमम् (this) / राजर्षयः (royal sages) / विदुः (knew) / सः (that) / कालेन (by time) / इह (here) / महता (great) / योगः (yoga) / नष्टः (lost) / परन्तप (O scorcher of foes)
4:2.meaning: This ancient yoga was passed down from teacher to student and known by wise rulers, but over time it faded from the world.

4:3.shloka: स एवायं मया तेऽद्य योगः प्रोक्तः पुरातनः । भक्तोऽसि मे सखा चेति रहस्यं ह्येतदुत्तमम् ॥
4:3.transliteration: सः (that) / एव (indeed) / अयम् (this) / मया (by me) / ते (to you) / अद्य (today) / योगः (yoga) / प्रोक्तः (spoken) / पुरातनः (ancient) / भक्तः (devotee) / असि (you are) / मे (my) / सखा (friend) / च (and) / इति (thus) / रहस्यम् (secret) / हि (indeed) / एतत् (this) / उत्तमम् (supreme)
4:3.meaning: Krishna says he is teaching Arjuna an ancient teaching because Arjuna is both devoted and close to him. This teaching is a rare and highest secret.

4:4.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचअपरं भवतो जन्म परं जन्म विवस्वतः । कथमेतद्विजानीयां त्वमादौ प्रोक्तवानिति ॥
4:4.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / अपरम् (later/recent) / भवतः (your) / जन्म (birth) / परम् (earlier/ancient) / जन्म (birth) / विवस्वतः (of the Sun) / कथम् (how) / एतत् (this) / विजानीयाम् (may I understand) / त्वम् (you) / आदौ (at the beginning) / प्रोक्तवान् (had spoken) / इति (thus)
4:4.meaning: Arjuna doubts Krishna's claim. Krishna is younger than the Sun, so Arjuna cannot see how Krishna could have taught this yoga at the beginning of creation.

4:5.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचबहूनि मे व्यतीतानि जन्मानि तव चार्जुन । तान्यहं वेद सर्वाणि न त्वं वेत्थ परन्तप ॥
4:5.transliteration: श्री भगवानुवाच (the Blessed Lord said) / बहूनि (many) / मे (my) / व्यतीतानि (passed) / जन्मानि (births) / तव (your) / च (and) / अर्जुन (Arjuna) / तानि (those) / अहं (I) / वेद (know) / सर्वाणि (all) / न (not) / त्वं (you) / वेत्थ (know) / परन्तप (enemy-burner)
4:5.meaning: Krishna says that both of them have passed through many births, but only Krishna remembers them all. Arjuna does not remember his own past births.

4:6.shloka: अजोऽपि सन्नव्ययात्मा भूतानामीश्वरोऽपि सन् । प्रकृतिं स्वामधिष्ठाय संभवाम्यात्ममायया ॥
4:6.transliteration: अजः (unborn) / अपि (even though) / सन् (being) / अव्ययात्मा (of imperishable nature) / भूतानाम् (of beings) / ईश्वरः (lord) / अपि (even though) / सन् (being) / प्रकृतिम् (nature) / स्वाम् (own) / अधिष्ठाय (having taken control of) / संभवामि (I manifest) / आत्ममायया (by my own power of appearance)
4:6.meaning: Krishna says he is unborn and imperishable, yet still appears in the world by using his own power over nature.

meanings.meaning: by my own power of appearance

4:7.shloka: यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत । अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदाऽऽत्मानं सृजाम्यहम् ॥
4:7.transliteration: यदा यदा (whenever) / हि (indeed) / धर्मस्य (of dharma) / ग्लानिः (decline) / भवति (becomes) / भारत (O Bharata) / अभ्युत्थानम् (rise) / अधर्मस्य (of adharma) / तदा (then) / आत्मानम् (myself) / सृजामि (I create/manifest) / अहम् (I)
4:7.meaning: Whenever righteous order declines and wrongdoing rises, Krishna says he manifests in the world to restore balance.

4:8.shloka: परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम् । धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय संभवामि युगे युगे ॥
4:8.transliteration: परित्राणाय (for protection) / साधूनाम् (of the righteous) / विनाशाय (for destruction) / च (and) / दुष्कृताम् (of wrongdoers) / धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय (for the purpose of establishing dharma) / संभवामि (I appear) / युगे युगे (age after age)
4:8.meaning: Krishna says he appears again and again to protect the good, stop wrongdoing, and restore dharma.

meanings.meaning: for the purpose of establishing dharma

4:9.shloka: जन्म कर्म च मे दिव्यमेवं यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः । त्यक्त्वा देहं पुनर्जन्म नैति मामेति सोऽर्जुन ॥
4:9.transliteration: जन्म (birth) / कर्म (action) / च (and) / मे (my) / दिव्यम् (divine) / एवम् (thus) / यः (who) / वेत्ति (knows) / तत्त्वतः (in truth) / त्यक्त्वा (having given up) / देहम् (the body) / पुनर्जन्म (rebirth) / न (not) / एति (goes) / माम् (to me) / एति (goes) / सः (that one) / अर्जुन (Arjuna)
4:9.meaning: Krishna says his birth and actions are not ordinary. Whoever understands their divine nature reaches him and is not born again.

4:10.shloka: वीतरागभयक्रोधा मन्मया मामुपाश्रिताः । बहवो ज्ञानतपसा पूता मद्भावमागताः ॥
4:10.transliteration: वीतरागभयक्रोधाः (free from attachment, fear, and anger) / मन्मयाः (mind fixed in Me) / मामुपाश्रिताः (taking refuge in Me) / बहवः (many) / ज्ञानतपसा (by the austerity of knowledge) / पूताः (purified) / मद्भावम् (My being/state) / आगताः (have attained)
4:10.meaning: People who shed attachment, fear, and anger, and stay centered in the divine, become purified through knowledge and reach that divine state.

meanings.meaning: free from attachment, fear, and anger

4:11.shloka: ये यथा मां प्रपद्यन्ते तांस्तथैव भजाम्यहम् । मम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्याः पार्थ सर्वशः ॥
4:11.transliteration: ये (who) / यथा (in whatever way) / माम् (Me) / प्रपद्यन्ते (take refuge) / तान् (them) / तथा (in that way) / एव (indeed) / भजामि (I favor/respond to) / अहम् (I) / मम (My) / वर्त्म (path) / अनुवर्तन्ते (follow) / मनुष्याः (people) / पार्थ (O Arjuna) / सर्वशः (in every way)
4:11.meaning: People meet the divine according to their own approach, and the divine responds accordingly. Everyone ultimately follows that same higher path, even if they do it unconsciously.

4:12.shloka: काङ्क्षन्तः कर्मणां सिद्धिं यजन्त इह देवताः । क्षिप्रं हि मानुषे लोके सिद्धिर्भवति कर्मजा ॥
4:12.transliteration: काङ्क्षन्तः (desiring) / कर्मणाम् (of actions) / सिद्धिम् (success, accomplishment) / यजन्ते (worship) / इह (here) / देवताः (radiant beings) / क्षिप्रम् (quickly) / हि (indeed) / मानुषे (in human) / लोके (world) / सिद्धिः (success, accomplishment) / भवति (becomes) / कर्मजा (born of action)
4:12.meaning: People who want immediate success often turn to lesser powers for help, because in the human world action can produce quick results.

4:13.shloka: चातुर्वर्ण्यं मया सृष्टं गुणकर्मविभागशः । तस्य कर्तारमपि मां विद्ध्यकर्तारमव्ययम् ॥
4:13.transliteration: चातुर्वर्ण्यम् (the four social orders) / मया (by Me) / सृष्टम् (created) / गुणकर्मविभागशः (according to division of qualities and actions) / तस्य (of that) / कर्तारम् (maker) / अपि (even) / माम् (Me) / विद्धि (know) / अकर्तारम् (as non-doer) / अव्ययम् (unchanging)
4:13.meaning: The four social orders arise from qualities and actions, but Krishna says he remains the unchanging one who is not bound by creation.

meanings.meaning: according to division of qualities and actions

4:14.shloka: न मां कर्माणि लिम्पन्ति न मे कर्मफले स्पृहा । इति मां योऽभिजानाति कर्मभिर्न स बध्यते ॥
4:14.transliteration: न मां (not me) / कर्माणि (actions) / लिम्पन्ति (stain) / न (not) / मे (my) / कर्मफले (in the fruit of action) / स्पृहा (desire) । इति (thus) / माम् (me) / यः (who) / अभिजानाति (knows truly) / कर्मभिः (by actions) / न (not) / सः (he) / बध्यते (is bound)
4:14.meaning: Actions do not affect Krishna, because he has no desire for their results. Anyone who understands this deeply is also freed from being bound by action.

4:15.shloka: एवं ज्ञात्वा कृतं कर्म पूर्वैरपि मुमुक्षुभिः । कुरु कर्मैव तस्मात्त्वं पूर्वैः पूर्वतरं कृतम् ॥
4:15.transliteration: एवम् (thus) / ज्ञात्वा (having known) / कृतम् (done) / कर्म (action) / पूर्वैः (by the earlier ones) / अपि (also) / मुमुक्षुभिः (by seekers of freedom) / कुरु (do) / कर्म (action) / एव (indeed) / तस्मात् (therefore) / त्वम् (you) / पूर्वैः (by the earlier ones) / पूर्वतरम् (from ancient times) / कृतम् (done) /
4:15.meaning: People seeking freedom acted in this way before you. So you should do the same rightful work that has long been done.

4:16.shloka: किं कर्म किमकर्मेति कवयोऽप्यत्र मोहिताः । तत्ते कर्म प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वा मोक्ष्यसेऽशुभात् ॥
4:16.transliteration: किम् (what) / कर्म (action) / किम्-अकर्म (what [is] inaction) / इति (thus) / कवयः (the wise) / अपि (even) / अत्र (here) / मोहिताः (deluded) / तत् (that) / ते (to you) / कर्म (action) / प्रवक्ष्यामि (I will explain fully) / यत् (which) / ज्ञात्वा (having known) / मोक्ष्यसे (you will be freed) / अशुभात् (from the harmful)
4:16.meaning: Even intelligent people get confused about what action and inaction really mean. Krishna says he will explain it clearly so Arjuna can be freed from harmful bondage.

4:17.shloka: कर्मणो ह्यपि बोद्धव्यं बोद्धव्यं च विकर्मणः । अकर्मणश्च बोद्धव्यं गहना कर्मणो गतिः ॥
4:17.transliteration: कर्मणः (of action) / हि (indeed) / अपि (also) / बोद्धव्यम् (must be understood) / बोद्धव्यम् (must be understood) / च (and) / विकर्मणः (of forbidden action) / अकर्मणः (of inaction) / च (and) / बोद्धव्यम् (must be understood) / गहना (deep, hard to grasp) / कर्मणः (of action) / गतिः (movement, course)
4:17.meaning: You must understand action, wrong action, and inaction, because the way action works is subtle and difficult to see.

4:18.shloka: कर्मण्यकर्म यः पश्येदकर्मणि च कर्म यः । स बुद्धिमान् मनुष्येषु स युक्तः कृत्स्नकर्मकृत् ॥
4:18.transliteration: कर्मणि (in action) / अकर्म (inaction) / यः (who) / पश्येत् (sees) / अकर्मणि (in inaction) / च (and) / कर्म (action) / यः (who) / सः (that one) / बुद्धिमान् (wise) / मनुष्येषु (among people) / सः (that one) / युक्तः (disciplined, united) / कृत्स्नकर्मकृत् (one who does all action completely)
4:18.meaning: A truly wise person sees that outward action can be inwardly free, and apparent inaction can still hide attachment or effort.

meanings.meaning: one who does all action completely

4:19.shloka: यस्य सर्वे समारम्भाः कामसङ्कल्पवर्जिताः । ज्ञानाग्निदग्धकर्माणं तमाहुः पण्डितं बुधाः ॥
4:19.transliteration: यस्य (whose) / सर्वे (all) / समारम्भाः (undertakings, beginnings of action) / कामसङ्कल्पवर्जिताः (free from desire and intention) / ज्ञानाग्निदग्धकर्माणं (whose actions are burnt by the fire of knowledge) / तम् (that person) / आहुः (they call) / पण्डितं (learned, wise) / बुधाः (the wise)
4:19.meaning: A truly wise person starts actions without craving or mental scheming, and knowledge has already burned up the binding force of those actions.

meanings.meaning: whose actions are burnt by the fire of knowledge

4:20.shloka: त्यक्त्वा कर्मफलासङ्गं नित्यतृप्तो निराश्रयः । कर्मण्यभिप्रवृत्तोऽपि नैव किञ्चित्करोति सः ॥
4:20.transliteration: त्यक्त्वा (having abandoned) / कर्मफलासङ्गम् (attachment to the fruit of action) / नित्यतृप्तः (ever content) / निराश्रयः (without dependence) / कर्मणि (in action) / अभिप्रवृत्तः अपि (even while fully engaged) / न एव (indeed not) / किञ्चित् (anything) / करोति (does) / सः (he)
4:20.meaning: A person can stay fully active and still be inwardly free by dropping attachment to results and resting in inner contentment.

meanings.meaning: attachment to the fruit of action

4:21.shloka: निराशीर्यतचित्तात्मा त्यक्तसर्वपरिग्रहः । शारीरं केवलं कर्म कुर्वन्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम् ॥
4:21.transliteration: निराशीर् (without hope) / यतचित्तात्मा (one whose mind and inner being are controlled) / त्यक्तसर्वपरिग्रहः (one who has abandoned all possessions) / शारीरम् (bodily) / केवलम् (only) / कर्म (action) / कुर्वन् (doing) / न (not) / आप्नोति (attains) / किल्बिषम् (fault)
4:21.meaning: A person who has no craving, controls the body and mind, and lets go of possessions can act without being stained by wrongdoing.

meanings.meaning: one whose mind and inner being are controlled
one who has abandoned all possessions

4:22.shloka: यदृच्छालाभसन्तुष्टो द्वन्द्वातीतो विमत्सरः । समः सिद्धावसिद्धौ च कृत्वापि न निबध्यते ॥
4:22.transliteration: यदृच्छा-लाभ-सन्तुष्टः (content with spontaneous gain) / द्वन्द्व-अतीतः (beyond pairs of opposites) / विमत्सरः (free from envy) / समः (equal) / सिद्धि-असिद्धौ (in success and failure) / च (and) / कृत्वा अपि (even while doing) / न (not) / निबध्यते (is bound)
4:22.meaning: A person who is satisfied with whatever comes naturally, stays calm through ups and downs, and does not envy others remains free even while working.

4:23.shloka: गतसङ्गस्य मुक्तस्य ज्ञानावस्थितचेतसः । यज्ञायाचरतः कर्म समग्रं प्रविलीयते ॥
4:23.transliteration: गतसङ्गस्य (of one gone beyond attachment) / मुक्तस्य (of one freed) / ज्ञानावस्थितचेतसः (of one whose mind is established in knowledge) / यज्ञाय (for sacrifice) / आचरतः (practising) / कर्म (action) / समग्रम् (entire) / प्रविलीयते (melts away)
4:23.meaning: When attachment is gone and the mind rests in clear understanding, work done as an offering no longer binds a person.

meanings.meaning: of one whose mind is established in knowledge

4:24.shloka: ब्रह्मार्पणं ब्रह्महविर्ब्रह्माग्नौ ब्रह्मणा हुतम् । ब्रह्मैव तेन गन्तव्यं ब्रह्मकर्मसमाधिना ॥
4:24.transliteration: ब्रह्मार्पणं (Brahman as the offering) / ब्रह्महविः (Brahman as the oblation) / ब्रह्माग्नौ (in the fire of Brahman) / ब्रह्मणा (by Brahman) / हुतम् (offered) / ब्रह्म (Brahman) / एव (indeed) / तेन (by that one) / गन्तव्यम् (to be reached) / ब्रह्मकर्मसमाधिना (by one whose action is absorbed in Brahman)
4:24.meaning: When action is fully understood, the giver, the offering, the fire, and the result all belong to the same ultimate reality.

meanings.meaning: by one whose action is absorbed in Brahman

4:25.shloka: दैवमेवापरे यज्ञं योगिनः पर्युपासते । ब्रह्माग्नावपरे यज्ञं यज्ञेनैवोपजुह्वति ॥
4:25.transliteration: दैवमेव (to the divine alone) / अपरे (others) / यज्ञम् (sacrifice) / योगिनः (yogis) / पर्युपासते (worship) / ब्रह्माग्नौ (in the fire of Brahman) / अपरे (others) / यज्ञम् (sacrifice) / यज्ञेन (by sacrifice) / एव (indeed) / उपजुह्वति (offer)
4:25.meaning: Different yogis practice sacrifice in different ways. Some dedicate worship to the divine, while others make the act of sacrifice itself into an offering within Brahman.

4:26.shloka: श्रोत्रादीनीन्द्रियाण्यन्ये संयमाग्निषु जुह्वति । शब्दादीन्विषयानन्य इन्द्रियाग्निषु जुह्वति ॥
4:26.transliteration: श्रोत्रादीनि (hearing and the other senses) / इन्द्रियाणि (the senses) / अन्ये (some others) / संयमाग्निषु (into the fires of restraint) / जुह्वति (they offer) / शब्दादीन् (sound and the other objects) / विषयान् (sense-objects) / अन्ये (others) / इन्द्रियाग्निषु (into the fires of the senses) / जुह्वति (they offer)
4:26.meaning: Some seekers restrain the senses like a fire sacrifice. Others use the senses themselves to consume sense-objects, turning experience into a disciplined offering.

meanings.meaning: into the fires of the senses

4:27.shloka: सर्वाणीन्द्रियकर्माणि प्राणकर्माणि चापरे । आत्मसंयमयोगाग्नौ जुह्वति ज्ञानदीपिते ॥
4:27.transliteration: सर्वाणि (all) / इन्द्रियकर्माणि (activities of the senses) / प्राणकर्माणि (activities of the life-force) / च (and) / अपरे (others) / आत्मसंयमयोगाग्नौ (in the fire of the yoga of self-control) / जुह्वति (offer as an oblation) / ज्ञानदीपिते (kindled by knowledge)
4:27.meaning: Some seekers train themselves so deeply that every sense activity and every life-force activity is offered into the fire of self-control, which is lit by knowledge.

meanings.meaning: in the fire of the yoga of self-control

4:28.transliteration: द्रव्ययज्ञाः (wealth-offering sacrificers) / तपोयज्ञाः (austerity-offering sacrificers) / योगयज्ञाः (yoga-offering sacrificers) / तथा (likewise) / अपरे (others) / स्वाध्याय-ज्ञान-यज्ञाः (self-study and knowledge sacrificers) / च (and) / यतयः (disciplined seekers) / संशित-व्रताः (those with firm vows)
4:28.meaning: Different seekers turn different things into an offering: wealth, austerity, yoga practice, and study. All of them are disciplined forms of sacrifice.

4:29.shloka: अपाने जुह्वति प्राण प्राणेऽपानं तथाऽपरे । प्राणापानगती रुद्ध्वा प्राणायामपरायणाः ॥
4:29.transliteration: अपाने (into the outgoing breath) / जुह्वति (offer) / प्राणम् (the incoming breath) / प्राणे (into the incoming breath) / अपानम् (the outgoing breath) / तथाऽपरे (and others likewise) / प्राणापानगती (the movements of incoming and outgoing breaths) / रुद्ध्वा (having restrained) / प्राणायामपरायणाः (devoted to breath-control)
4:29.meaning: Some practitioners use breath-control as a kind of sacrifice. They regulate inhalation and exhalation, and others do the same with disciplined eating.

meanings.meaning: the movements of incoming and outgoing breaths

4:30.shloka: अपरे नियताहाराः प्राणान्प्राणेषु जुह्वति । सर्वेऽप्येते यज्ञविदो यज्ञक्षपितकल्मषाः ॥
4:30.transliteration: अपरे (others) / नियताहाराः (regulated in food) / प्राणान् (breaths) / प्राणेषु (into breaths) / जुह्वति (offer) / सर्वे (all) / अपि (indeed) / एते (these) / यज्ञविदः (knowers of sacrifice) / यज्ञक्षपितकल्मषाः (whose impurities are destroyed by sacrifice)
4:30.meaning: Some people discipline their breathing, and some control their eating and life-energy. All of them are sincere practitioners of sacrifice, and that practice cleanses them.

meanings.meaning: whose impurities are destroyed by sacrifice

4:31.shloka: यज्ञशिष्टामृतभुजो यान्ति ब्रह्म सनातनम् । नायं लोकोऽस्त्ययज्ञस्य कुतो़ऽन्यः कुरुसत्तम ॥
4:31.transliteration: यज्ञशिष्टामृतभुजो (those who eat the nectar left over from sacrifice) / यान्ति (go) / ब्रह्म (to the supreme reality) / सनातनम् (eternal) । / न (not) / अयम् (this) / लोकः (world) / अस्ति (is) / अयज्ञस्य (for one without sacrifice) / कुतः (how) / अन्यः (the other) / कुरुसत्तम (O best among the Kurus)
4:31.meaning: People who live by sacrifice and accept its blessed remainder move toward the eternal supreme reality. Without sacrifice, even this life loses its value, so the next one cannot be better.

meanings.meaning: those who eat the nectar left over from sacrifice

4:32.shloka: एवं बहुविधा यज्ञा वितता ब्रह्मणो मुखे । कर्मजान्विद्धि तान्सर्वानेवं ज्ञात्वा विमोक्ष्यसे ॥
4:32.transliteration: एवम् (in this way) / बहुविधाः (many kinds) / यज्ञाः (sacrifices) / वितताः (are विस्तृत / described at length) / ब्रह्मणः (of the sacred teaching) / मुखे (in the mouth) / कर्मजान् (born of action) / विद्धि (know) / तान् (them) / सर्वान् (all) / एवम् (thus) / ज्ञात्वा (having known) / विमोक्ष्यसे (you will be freed)
4:32.meaning: Many kinds of sacrifice are laid out in the sacred teaching. Understand that all of them arise from action, and that understanding frees you from action’s binding force.

4:33.shloka: श्रेयान्द्रव्यमयाद्यज्ञाज्ज्ञानयज्ञः परन्तप । सर्वं कर्माखिलं पार्थ ज्ञाने परिसमाप्यते ॥
4:33.transliteration: श्रेयान् (better) / द्रव्यमयात् (than material) / यज्ञात् (than sacrifice) / ज्ञानयज्ञः (knowledge-sacrifice) / परन्तप (O scorcher of foes) / सर्वम् (all) / कर्म (action) / अखिलम् (entire) / पार्थ (O Partha) / ज्ञाने (in knowledge) / परिसमाप्यते (is completed / ends)
4:33.meaning: Inner understanding is higher than outer offerings. Every action and every material effort finally leads toward knowledge.

4:34.shloka: तद्विद्धि प्रणिपातेन परिप्रश्नेन सेवया । उपदेक्ष्यन्ति ते ज्ञानं ज्ञानिनस्तत्त्वदर्शिनः ॥
4:34.transliteration: तत् (that) / विद्धि (know) / प्रणिपातेन (by prostration) / परिप्रश्नेन (by sincere questioning) / सेवया (by service) / उपदेक्ष्यन्ति (will teach) / ते (to you) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / ज्ञानिनः (the knowers) / तत्त्वदर्शिनः (who see reality)
4:34.meaning: Real understanding comes from approaching wise people with humility, honest questions, and service. Those who truly see reality can teach knowledge.

4:35.shloka: यज्ज्ञात्वा न पुनर्मोहमेवं यास्यसि पाण्डव । येन भूतान्यशेषेण द्रक्ष्यस्यात्मन्यथो मयि ॥
4:35.transliteration: यत् (which) / ज्ञात्वा (having known) / न (not) / पुनः (again) / मोहम् (delusion, confusion) / एवम् (thus) / यास्यसि (you will go into) / पाण्डव (O Pandava) / येन (by which) / भूतानि (beings) / अशेषेण (without remainder) / द्रक्ष्यसि (you will see) / आत्मनि (in the self) / अथो (then) / मयि (in me)
4:35.meaning: True understanding ends confusion. It lets you see every being as part of one reality, first within yourself and then in the supreme reality.

4:36.shloka: अपि चेदसि पापेभ्यः सर्वेभ्यः पापकृत्तमः । सर्वं ज्ञानप्लवेनैव वृजिनं सन्तरिष्यसि ॥
4:36.transliteration: अपि (even) / चेदसि (even if you are) / पापेभ्यः (than wrongdoers) / सर्वेभ्यः (all) / पापकृत्तमः (most sinful) / सर्वम् (all) / ज्ञानप्लवेन (by the raft of knowledge) / एव (alone) / वृजिनम् (wrongdoing / trouble) / सन्तरिष्यसि (you will cross over)
4:36.meaning: Even if someone has done terrible things, clear understanding can still carry them beyond the whole burden of wrongdoing.

4:37.shloka: यथैधांसि समिद्धोऽग्निर्भस्मसात्कुरुतेऽर्जुन । ज्ञानाग्निः सर्वकर्माणि भस्मसात्कुरुते तथा ॥
4:37.transliteration: यथा (just as) / एधांसि (fuel) / समिद्धः (blazing) / अग्निः (fire) / भस्मसात् (to ashes) / कुरुते (makes) / अर्जुन (Arjuna) / ज्ञान-अग्निः (fire of knowledge) / सर्व-कर्माणि (all actions) / भस्मसात् (to ashes) / कुरुते (makes) / तथा (so)
4:37.meaning: Clear understanding destroys the binding force of past actions, just as fire destroys fuel.

4:38.shloka: न हि ज्ञानेन सदृशं पवित्रमिह विद्यते । तत्स्वयं योगसंसिद्धः कालेनात्मनि विन्दति ॥
4:38.transliteration: न (not) / हि (indeed) / ज्ञानेन (by knowledge) / सदृशं (equal) / पवित्रम्इह (purifying here) / विद्यते (exists) / तत् (that) / स्वयम् (by oneself) / योगसंसिद्धः (one perfected in yoga) / कालेन (in time) / आत्मनि (in the self) / विन्दति (finds)
4:38.meaning: Clear understanding is the highest purifier. A person who has matured through yoga eventually discovers that truth within themselves.

4:39.shloka: श्रद्धावाँल्लभते ज्ञानं तत्परः संयतेन्द्रियः । ज्ञानं लब्ध्वा परां शान्तिमचिरेणाधिगच्छति ॥
4:39.transliteration: श्रद्धावान् (full of trust) / लभते (attains) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / तत्परः (devoted, intent) / संयतेन्द्रियः (with controlled senses) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / लब्ध्वा (having attained) / पराम् (supreme) / शान्तिम् (peace) / अचिरेण (quickly) / अधिगच्छति (reaches)
4:39.meaning: Trust, steady effort, and sense control bring knowledge. Once knowledge arrives, deep peace comes quickly.

4:40.shloka: अज्ञश्चाश्रद्दधानश्च संशयात्मा विनश्यति । नायं लोकोऽस्ति न परो न सुखं संशयात्मनः ॥
4:40.transliteration: अज्ञः (ignorant) / च (and) / अश्रद्दधानः (without trust) / च (and) / संशयात्मा (a doubting inner being) / विनश्यति (is ruined) / न (not) / अयम् (this) / लोकः (world) / अस्ति (exists) / न (not) / परः (other) / न (not) / सुखम् (happiness) / संशयात्मनः (of the doubting inner being)
4:40.meaning: A person who lacks clear understanding and trust, and keeps doubting, falls apart. Such a divided mind finds no real happiness here or beyond.

4:41.shloka: योगसंन्यस्तकर्माणं ज्ञानसंछिन्नसंशयम् । आत्मवन्तं न कर्माणि निबध्नन्ति धनञ्जय ॥
4:41.transliteration: योगसंन्यस्तकर्माणम् (one whose actions are renounced through yoga) / ज्ञानसंछिन्नसंशयम् (one whose doubts are cut away by knowledge) / आत्मवन्तम् (the one established in the true self) / न (not) / कर्माणि (actions) / निबध्नन्ति (bind) / धनञ्जय (O Dhananjaya)
4:41.meaning: A person rooted in yoga, free of doubt, and established in the true self is not trapped by actions. Action still happens, but it no longer leaves a binding mark.

meanings.meaning: one whose actions are renounced through yoga
one whose doubts are cut away by knowledge
the one established in the true self

4:42.shloka: तस्मादज्ञानसंभूतं हृत्स्थं ज्ञानासिनाऽऽत्मनः । छित्त्वैनं संशयं योगमातिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ भारत ॥
4:42.transliteration: तस्मात् (therefore) / अज्ञानसंभूतम् (born of ignorance) / हृत्स्थम् (situated in the heart) / ज्ञानासिना (with the sword of knowledge) / आत्मनः (of the self) / छित्त्वा (having cut) / एनम् (this) / संशयम् (doubt) / योगम् (in yoga) / आतिष्ठ (stand in) / उत्तिष्ठ (rise up) / भारत (O Bharata)
4:42.meaning: Knowledge should cut through inner doubt, and then you should stand steady and act. Krishna is telling Arjuna to stop wavering and rise for battle.

5:1.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचसंन्यासं कर्मणां कृष्ण पुनर्योगं च शंससि । यच्छ्रेय एतयोरेकं तन्मे ब्रूहि सुनिश्िचतम् ॥
5:1.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / संन्यासम् (renunciation) / कर्मणाम् (of actions) / कृष्ण (Krishna) / पुनः (again) / योगम् (yoga) / च (and) / शंससि (you praise) / यत् (which) / श्रेयः (better for welfare) / एतयोः (of these two) / एकम् (one) / तत् (that) / मे (to me) / ब्रूहि (tell) / सुनिश्चितम् (definitely)
5:1.meaning: Arjuna asks Krishna to choose between renouncing action and acting with discipline, and wants the one path that is clearly best.

5:2.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचसंन्यासः कर्मयोगश्च निःश्रेयसकरावुभौ । तयोस्तु कर्मसंन्यासात्कर्मयोगो विशिष्यते ॥
5:2.transliteration: संन्यासः (renunciation) / कर्मयोगः (karma yoga) / च (and) / निःश्रेयसकरौ (bringing the highest good) / उभौ (both) / तयोः (of the two) / तु (but) / कर्मसंन्यासात् (than renunciation of action) / कर्मयोगः (karma yoga) / विशिष्यते (is superior)
5:2.meaning: Both renunciation and karma yoga lead to the highest good, but Krishna says karma yoga is better than simply giving up action.

5:3.shloka: ज्ञेयः स नित्यसंन्यासी यो न द्वेष्टि न काङ्क्षति । निर्द्वन्द्वो हि महाबाहो सुखं बन्धात्प्रमुच्यते ॥
5:3.transliteration: ज्ञेयः (to be understood) / सः (that one) / नित्यसंन्यासी (always-renunciant) / यः (who) / न (not) / द्वेष्टि (hates) / न (not) / काङ्क्षति (desires) / निर्द्वन्द्वः (free from opposites) / हि (indeed) / महाबाहो (O mighty-armed one) / सुखम् (easily) / बन्धात् (from bondage) / प्रमुच्यते (is released)
5:3.meaning: A true renunciant is not someone who abandons action, but someone who drops hatred and craving. Freedom comes when inner opposites no longer pull the mind.

5:4.shloka: सांख्ययोगौ पृथग्बालाः प्रवदन्ति न पण्डिताः । एकमप्यास्थितः सम्यगुभयोर्विन्दते फलम् ॥
5:4.transliteration: सांख्ययोगौ (renunciation and yoga) / पृथक् (separate) / बालाः (the unwise) / प्रवदन्ति (declare) / न (not) / पण्डिताः (the wise) / एकम् अपि (even one) / आस्थितः (established) / सम्यक् (properly) / उभयोः (of both) / विन्दते (attains) / फलम् (the fruit)
5:4.meaning: People who do not understand think renunciation and disciplined action give different results. A wise person sees that either one, practiced well, leads to the same highest fulfillment.

5:5.shloka: यत्सांख्यैः प्राप्यते स्थानं तद्योगैरपि गम्यते । एकं सांख्यं च योगं च यः पश्यति स पश्यति ॥
5:5.transliteration: यत् (that which) / सांख्यैः (by the wise of analysis) / प्राप्यते (is attained) / स्थानं (state) / तत् (that) / योगैः (by yogis) / अपि (also) / गम्यते (is reached) / एकं (one) / सांख्यं (analysis) / च (and) / योगं (yoga) / च (and) / यः (who) / पश्यति (sees) / सः (that one) / पश्यति (sees rightly)
5:5.meaning: The wise path of understanding and the path of disciplined action lead to the same realization. Seeing them as separate is the mistake.

5:6.shloka: संन्यासस्तु महाबाहो दुःखमाप्तुमयोगतः । योगयुक्तो मुनिर्ब्रह्म नचिरेणाधिगच्छति ॥
5:6.transliteration: संन्यासः (renunciation) / तु (but) / महाबाहो (mighty-armed one) / दुःखम् (with difficulty) / आप्तुम् (to attain) / अयोगतः (without yoga) / योगयुक्तः (joined with yoga) / मुनिः (the contemplative sage) / ब्रह्म (the supreme reality) / नचिरेण (without delay) / अधिगच्छति (attains)
5:6.meaning: Letting go is not achieved by avoiding action. A steady, disciplined path of yoga leads the thoughtful seeker to the supreme reality quickly.

5:7.shloka: योगयुक्तो विशुद्धात्मा विजितात्मा जितेन्द्रियः । सर्वभूतात्मभूतात्मा कुर्वन्नपि न लिप्यते ॥
5:7.transliteration: योगयुक्तः (joined in yoga) / विशुद्धात्मा (pure inner being) / विजितात्मा (self-mastered) / जितेन्द्रियः (senses conquered) / सर्वभूतात्मभूतात्मा (whose self has become the self of all beings) / कुर्वन् अपि (even while acting) / न (not) / लिप्यते (is stained, clings)
5:7.meaning: A person grounded in yoga, with a pure mind and mastered senses, acts without being stained by action because they see the same self in all beings.

meanings.meaning: whose self has become the self of all beings

5:8.shloka: नैव किंचित्करोमीति युक्तो मन्येत तत्त्ववित् । पश्यन् श्रृणवन्स्पृशञ्जिघ्रन्नश्नन्गच्छन्स्वपन् श्वसन् ॥
5:8.transliteration: नैव (not at all) / किंचित् (anything) / करोमि (I do) / इति (thus) / युक्तः (the disciplined one) / मन्येत (should think) / तत्त्ववित् (the knower of reality) / पश्यन् (seeing) / श्रृणवन् (hearing) / स्पृशन् (touching) / जिघ्रन् (smelling) / अश्नन् (eating) / गच्छन् (walking) / स्वपन् (sleeping) / श्वसन् (breathing)
5:8.meaning: A person who knows reality sees the senses doing all the work and no longer claims personal doership.

5:9.transliteration: प्रलपन् (speaking) / विसृजन् (releasing) / गृह्णन् (taking) / उन्मिषन् (opening the eyes) / निमिषन् (closing the eyes) / अपि (even) / इन्द्रियाणि (the senses) / इन्द्रियार्थेषु (among sense-objects) / वर्तन्ते (move) / इति (thus) / धारयन् (holding in mind)
5:9.meaning: A person who knows the truth understands that actions are happening through the senses, not through a separate doer.

5:10.shloka: ब्रह्मण्याधाय कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा करोति यः । लिप्यते न स पापेन पद्मपत्रमिवाम्भसा ॥
5:10.transliteration: ब्रह्मणि (in Brahman) / आधाय (having placed) / कर्माणि (actions) / सङ्गम् (attachment) / त्यक्त्वा (having abandoned) / करोति (does) / यः (who) / लिप्यते (is stained) / न (not) / सः (he) / पापेन (by wrongdoing) / पद्मपत्रम् (lotus leaf) / इव (like) / अम्भसा (by water)
5:10.meaning: Do your actions without clinging to them, and they do not bind you. Like a lotus leaf in water, you remain untouched.

5:11.shloka: कायेन मनसा बुद्ध्या केवलैरिन्द्रियैरपि । योगिनः कर्म कुर्वन्ति सङ्गं त्यक्त्वाऽऽत्मशुद्धये ॥
5:11.transliteration: कायेन (by the body) / मनसा (by the mind) / बुद्ध्या (by the intellect) / केवलैः (by the merely) / इन्द्रियैः (by the senses) / अपि (also) / योगिनः (yogis) / कर्म (action) / कुर्वन्ति (perform) / सङ्गम् (attachment) / त्यक्त्वा (having given up) / आत्मशुद्धये (for inner purification)
5:11.meaning: A true practitioner acts with the body, mind, intellect, and senses, but without clinging to results. The purpose is inner cleansing, not possession.

5:12.shloka: युक्तः कर्मफलं त्यक्त्वा शान्तिमाप्नोति नैष्ठिकीम् । अयुक्तः कामकारेण फले सक्तो निबध्यते ॥
5:12.transliteration: युक्तः (disciplined one) / कर्मफलम् (fruit of action) / त्यक्त्वा (having given up) / शान्तिम् (peace) / आप्नोति (attains) / नैष्ठिकीम् (steady, abiding) । / अयुक्तः (undisciplined one) / कामकारेण (through desire) / फले (in the fruit) / सक्तः (attached) / निबध्यते (is bound)
5:12.meaning: Letting go of results brings steady peace. Wanting a specific reward keeps you trapped.

5:13.shloka: सर्वकर्माणि मनसा संन्यस्यास्ते सुखं वशी । नवद्वारे पुरे देही नैव कुर्वन्न कारयन् ॥
5:13.transliteration: सर्वकर्माणि (all actions) / मनसा (with the mind) / संन्यस्य (having renounced) / आस्ते (dwells) / सुखं (happily) / वशी (self-mastered) / नवद्वारे (with nine gates) / पुरे (in the city) / देही (the embodied one) / न (not) / एव (indeed) / कुर्वन् (doing) / न (not) / कारयन् (causing to do)
5:13.meaning: A self-mastered person can live peacefully in the body while inwardly letting go of all action. Such a person does not feel like the doer or the one making others act.

5:14.shloka: न कर्तृत्वं न कर्माणि लोकस्य सृजति प्रभुः । न कर्मफलसंयोगं स्वभावस्तु प्रवर्तते ॥
5:14.transliteration: न (not) / कर्तृत्वं (doership) / न (not) / कर्माणि (actions) / लोकस्य (of the world / for people) / सृजति (creates) / प्रभुः (the supreme being) / न (not) / कर्मफलसंयोगं (connection with action-result) / स्वभावः (nature) / तु (but) / प्रवर्तते (moves onward / operates)
5:14.meaning: The supreme being does not manufacture a separate doer, the actions, or the binding link between action and result. What actually operates is nature.

5:15.shloka: नादत्ते कस्यचित्पापं न चैव सुकृतं विभुः । अज्ञानेनावृतं ज्ञानं तेन मुह्यन्ति जन्तवः ॥
5:15.transliteration: नादत्ते (does not take) / कस्यचित् (of anyone) / पापम् (wrongdoing) / न (not) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / सुकृतम् (good deed) / विभुः (the all-pervading one) / अज्ञानेन (by ignorance) / आवृतम् (covered) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / तेन (by that) / मुह्यन्ति (are deluded) / जन्तवः (living beings)
5:15.meaning: The divine does not collect anyone's good or bad actions. People get confused because ignorance covers their understanding.

5:16.shloka: ज्ञानेन तु तदज्ञानं येषां नाशितमात्मनः । तेषामादित्यवज्ज्ञानं प्रकाशयति तत्परम् ॥
5:16.transliteration: ज्ञानेन (by knowledge) / तु (but) / तत्-अज्ञानम् (that ignorance) / येषाम् (of those whose) / नाशितम् (destroyed) / आत्मनः (of the true self) / तेषाम् (for them) / आदित्य-वत् (like the sun) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / प्रकाशयति (reveals) / तत्-परम् (the supreme reality)
5:16.meaning: When ignorance is removed by true understanding, that understanding itself reveals the highest reality, just as the sun reveals everything in daylight.

5:17.transliteration: तद्बुद्धयः (whose intellect is That) / तदात्मानः (whose mind is That) / तन्निष्ठाः (steadfast in That) / तत्परायणाः (devoted to That as the supreme goal) / गच्छन्ति (they go) / अपुनरावृत्तिम् (to no return) / ज्ञाननिर्धूतकल्मषाः (their impurities washed away by knowledge)
5:17.meaning: When a person's mind, intellect, and whole focus are fully aligned with the supreme reality, knowledge clears away impurity and they reach a state beyond coming back.

meanings.meaning: devoted to That as the supreme goal
their impurities washed away by knowledge

5:18.shloka: विद्याविनयसंपन्ने ब्राह्मणे गवि हस्तिनि । शुनि चैव श्वपाके च पण्डिताः समदर्शिनः ॥
5:18.transliteration: विद्याविनयसंपन्ने (endowed with learning and humility) / ब्राह्मणे (in a brahmin) / गवि (in a cow) / हस्तिनि (in an elephant) / शुनि (in a dog) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / श्वपाके (in an outcaste) / च (and) / पण्डिताः (the wise) / समदर्शिनः (equal-seeing)
5:18.meaning: A truly wise person does not rank living beings by status, species, or social position. The same reality is seen in all of them.

5:19.shloka: इहैव तैर्जितः सर्गो येषां साम्ये स्थितं मनः । निर्दोषं हि समं ब्रह्म तस्माद्ब्रह्मणि ते स्थिताः ॥
5:19.transliteration: इहैव (here itself) / तैः (by them) / जितः (conquered) / सर्गः (the created world / rebirth) / येषाम् (of whom) / साम्ये (in sameness) / स्थितम् (स्थित) / मनः (mind) / निर्दोषम् (flawless) / हि (indeed) / समम् (equal) / ब्रह्म (the supreme reality) / तस्मात् (therefore) / ब्रह्मणि (in the supreme reality) / ते (they) / स्थिताः (established)
5:19.meaning: A mind that stays even and unshaken has already overcome the cycle of reactivity. Because the supreme reality is free of flaw and division, such a person is established in it.

5:20.shloka: न प्रहृष्येत्प्रियं प्राप्य नोद्विजेत्प्राप्य चाप्रियम् । स्थिरबुद्धिरसम्मूढो ब्रह्मविद्ब्रह्मणि स्थितः ॥
5:20.transliteration: न (not) / प्रहृष्येत् (should rejoice) / प्रियं (the pleasant) / प्राप्य (having obtained) / न (not) / उद्विजेत् (should be disturbed) / प्राप्य (having obtained) / च (and) / अप्रियम् (the unpleasant) / स्थिरबुद्धिः (steady in understanding) / असम्मूढः (undeluded) / ब्रह्मविद् (knower of the supreme reality) / ब्रह्मणि (in the supreme reality) / स्थितः (established)
5:20.meaning: A person who stays calm in both good and bad situations has clear understanding and lives in the supreme reality.

5:21.shloka: बाह्यस्पर्शेष्वसक्तात्मा विन्दत्यात्मनि यत्सुखम् । स ब्रह्मयोगयुक्तात्मा सुखमक्षयमश्नुते ॥
5:21.transliteration: बाह्यस्पर्शेषु (in outer contacts) / असक्त-आत्मा (one whose inner being is not attached) / विन्दति (finds) / आत्मनि (in the true self) / यत् (which) / सुखम् (happiness) / सः (that one) / ब्रह्म-योग-युक्त-आत्मा (one whose inner being is joined in union with the supreme reality) / सुखम् (happiness) / अक्षयम् (imperishable) / अश्नुते (enjoys, attains)
5:21.meaning: Real happiness is found by turning away from outside pleasures and resting in the true self. That inner joy does not wear out.

meanings.meaning: one whose inner being is not attached
one whose inner being is joined in union with the supreme reality

5:22.shloka: ये हि संस्पर्शजा भोगा दुःखयोनय एव ते । आद्यन्तवन्तः कौन्तेय न तेषु रमते बुधः ॥
5:22.transliteration: ये (these) / हि (indeed) / संस्पर्शजा (born from contact) / भोगाः (pleasures) / दुःखयोनयः (sources of suffering) / एव (only) / ते (they) । / आद्यन्तवन्तः (having beginning and end) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / न (not) / तेषु (in them) / रमते (delights) / बुधः (the wise person)
5:22.meaning: Pleasures that come from the senses do not last and often lead to suffering. A clear-minded person does not get absorbed in them.

5:23.shloka: शक्नोतीहैव यः सोढुं प्राक्शरीरविमोक्षणात् । कामक्रोधोद्भवं वेगं स युक्तः स सुखी नरः ॥
5:23.transliteration: शक्नोति (is able) / इह एव (here itself) / यः (who) / सोढुम् (to endure) / प्राक्-शरीर-विमोक्षणात् (before the body’s release) / काम-क्रोध-उद्भवम् (born from desire and anger) / वेगम् (impulse/surge) / सः (that one) / युक्तः (disciplined, integrated) / सः (that one) / सुखी (happy) / नरः (person)
5:23.meaning: A person who can endure the rush of desire and anger before death is truly disciplined and truly happy.

5:24.shloka: योऽन्तःसुखोऽन्तरारामस्तथान्तर्ज्योतिरेव यः । स योगी ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं ब्रह्मभूतोऽधिगच्छति ॥
5:24.transliteration: यः (who) / अन्तःसुखः (one whose happiness is भीतर / within) / अन्तरारामः (one whose delight is within) / तथा (and) / अन्तर्ज्योतिः (inner light) / एव (alone) / यः (who) / सः (that one) / योगी (yogi) / ब्रह्मनिर्वाणम् (freedom in the supreme reality) / ब्रह्मभूतः (become the supreme reality) / अधिगच्छति (attains)
5:24.meaning: Real freedom comes to the person who finds joy, delight, and clear awareness within, rather than depending on outside things.

5:25.shloka: लभन्ते ब्रह्मनिर्वाणमृषयः क्षीणकल्मषाः । छिन्नद्वैधा यतात्मानः सर्वभूतहिते रताः ॥
5:25.transliteration: लभन्ते (attain) / ब्रह्मनिर्वाणम् (freedom in the supreme reality) / ऋषयः (sages) / क्षीणकल्मषाः (whose impurities are diminished) / छिन्नद्वैधाः (whose doubts are cut away) / यतात्मानः (self-controlled) / सर्वभूतहिते (in the welfare of all beings) / रताः (delighting, devoted)
5:25.meaning: People who have cleansed themselves, ended doubt, controlled their minds and senses, and care for the good of all beings reach freedom in the supreme reality.

meanings.meaning: in the welfare of all beings

5:26.shloka: कामक्रोधवियुक्तानां यतीनां यतचेतसाम् । अभितो ब्रह्मनिर्वाणं वर्तते विदितात्मनाम् ॥
5:26.transliteration: काम-क्रोध-वियुक्तानाम् (for those free from desire and anger) / यतीनाम् (for disciplined renouncers) / यत-चेतसाम् (for those with controlled minds) / अभितः (on both sides) / ब्रह्म-निर्वाणम् (freedom in the supreme reality) / वर्तते (is present) / विदित-आत्मनाम् (for those who know the true self)
5:26.meaning: When desire and anger no longer control the mind, and a person knows the true self, inner freedom is close whether alive or after death.

meanings.meaning: for those free from desire and anger
for those who know the true self

5:27.shloka: स्पर्शान्कृत्वा बहिर्बाह्यांश्चक्षुश्चैवान्तरे भ्रुवोः । प्राणापानौ समौ कृत्वा नासाभ्यन्तरचारिणौ ॥
5:27.transliteration: स्पर्शान् (outer contacts) / कृत्वा (having made) / बहिः (outside) / बाह्यान् (outer) / चक्षुः (the eyes) / च एव (and indeed) / अन्तरे (between) / भ्रुवोः (the eyebrows) / प्राणापानौ (the inward and outward breaths) / समौ (equal) / कृत्वा (having made) / नासाभ्यन्तरचारिणौ (moving within the nose)
5:27.meaning: Withdraw the senses from outer objects, steady the gaze, and balance the breath. This prepares the mind for deep inward focus.

5:28.shloka: यतेन्द्रियमनोबुद्धिर्मुनिर्मोक्षपरायणः । विगतेच्छाभयक्रोधो यः सदा मुक्त एव सः ॥
5:28.transliteration: यतेन्द्रियमनःबुद्धिः (with senses, mind, and intellect controlled) / मुनिः (the sage) / मोक्षपरायणः (having freedom as the highest aim) / विगतेच्छाभयक्रोधः (free from desire, fear, and anger) / यः (who) / सदा (always) / मुक्तः (free) / एव (indeed) / सः (that one)
5:28.meaning: A person who has mastered the senses, mind, and intellect, and has let go of desire, fear, and anger, lives in freedom already.

meanings.meaning: with senses, mind, and intellect controlled
having freedom as the highest aim
free from desire, fear, and anger

5:29.shloka: भोक्तारं यज्ञतपसां सर्वलोकमहेश्वरम् । सुहृदं सर्वभूतानां ज्ञात्वा मां शान्तिमृच्छति ॥
5:29.transliteration: भोक्तारम् (enjoyer) / यज्ञ-तपसाम् (of sacrifices and austerities) / सर्व-लोक-महेश्वरम् (great lord of all worlds) / सुहृदम् (friend) / सर्व-भूतानाम् (of all beings) / ज्ञात्वा (having known) / माम् (me) / शान्तिम् (peace) / ऋच्छति (attains)
5:29.meaning: Peace comes from knowing Krishna as the true receiver of sacrifice and effort, the ruler of all worlds, and the friend of every being.

6:1.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचअनाश्रितः कर्मफलं कार्यं कर्म करोति यः । स संन्यासी च योगी च न निरग्निर्न चाक्रियः ॥
6:1.transliteration: श्रीभगवानुवाच (the divine said) / अनाश्रितः (not taking refuge) / कर्मफलम् (fruit of action) / कार्यम् (what must be done) / कर्म (action) / करोति (does) / यः (who) / सः (that one) / संन्यासी (renunciate) / च (and) / योगी (yogi) / च (and) / न (not) / निरग्निः (without fire) / न (not) / च (and) / अक्रियः (one without action)
6:1.meaning: Real renunciation is not escaping action. It is doing what must be done without clinging to the result.

6:2.shloka: यं संन्यासमिति प्राहुर्योगं तं विद्धि पाण्डव । न ह्यसंन्यस्तसङ्कल्पो योगी भवति कश्चन ॥
6:2.transliteration: यं (what) / संन्यासम् (renunciation) / इति (thus) / प्राहुः (they call) / योगम् (yoga) / तम् (that) / विद्धि (know) / पाण्डव (Arjuna) / न (not) / हि (indeed) / असंन्यस्त (not given up) / सङ्कल्पः (intention) / योगी (yogi) / भवति (becomes) / कश्चन (anyone)
6:2.meaning: Real renunciation is not outer withdrawal. It is letting go of inner plans and fixed desires so the mind can truly enter yoga.

6:3.shloka: आरुरुक्षोर्मुनेर्योगं कर्म कारणमुच्यते । योगारूढस्य तस्यैव शमः कारणमुच्यते ॥
6:3.transliteration: आरुरुक्षोः (of one seeking to ascend) / मुनेः (of the sage) / योगम् (to yoga) / कर्म (action) / कारणम् (means/cause) / उच्यते (is said) / योगारूढस्य (of one established in yoga) / तस्य (of that one) / एव (indeed) / शमः (stillness) / कारणम् (means/cause) / उच्यते (is said)
6:3.meaning: Action helps a seeker rise into yoga. Once that steadiness is reached, calm stillness becomes the support.

6:4.shloka: यदा हि नेन्द्रियार्थेषु न कर्मस्वनुषज्जते । सर्वसङ्कल्पसंन्यासी योगारूढस्तदोच्यते ॥
6:4.transliteration: यदा (when) हि (indeed) न (not) इन्द्रियार्थेषु (in sense objects) न (not) कर्मसु (in actions) अनुषज्जते (clings) / सर्वसङ्कल्पसंन्यासी (renouncer of all intentions) / योगारूढः (established in yoga) तदा (then) उच्यते (is called)
6:4.meaning: A person is truly steady in yoga when neither pleasures nor actions hook the mind, and all intentions have been let go.

meanings.meaning: when) हि (indeed) न (not) इन्द्रियार्थेषु (in sense objects) न (not) कर्मसु (in actions) अनुषज्जते (clings
established in yoga) तदा (then) उच्यते (is called

6:5.shloka: उद्धरेदात्मनाऽऽत्मानं नात्मानमवसादयेत् । आत्मैव ह्यात्मनो बन्धुरात्मैव रिपुरात्मनः ॥
6:5.transliteration: उद्धरेत् (should lift up) / आत्मना (by the self) / आत्मानम् (the self) / न (not) / आत्मानम् (the self) / अवसादयेत् (should let fall) / आत्मा (the self) / एव (indeed) / हि (for) / आत्मनः (of the self) / बन्धुः (friend) / आत्मा (the self) / एव (indeed) / रिपुः (enemy) / आत्मनः (of the self)
6:5.meaning: Your own effort can raise you or ruin you. The same mind that helps you grow can also drag you down.

6:6.shloka: बन्धुरात्माऽऽत्मनस्तस्य येनात्मैवात्मना जितः । अनात्मनस्तु शत्रुत्वे वर्तेतात्मैव शत्रुवत् ॥
6:6.transliteration: बन्धुः (friend) / आत्मा (the true self) / आत्मनः (of the self) / तस्य (of that one) / येन (by whom) / आत्मा (the true self) / एव (indeed) / आत्मना (by the true self) / जितः (conquered) / अनात्मनः (of one not mastered) / तु (but) / शत्रुत्वे (in hostility) / वर्तेत (acts) / आत्मा (the true self) / एव (indeed) / शत्रुवत् (like an enemy)
6:6.meaning: When you master your restless impulses, your deepest nature supports you. If you do not, your own mind and habits work against you.

6:7.shloka: जितात्मनः प्रशान्तस्य परमात्मा समाहितः । शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु तथा मानापमानयोः ॥
6:7.transliteration: जितात्मनः (of one who has conquered the self) / प्रशान्तस्य (of the serene) / परमात्मा (the supreme reality) / समाहितः (is present, absorbed) / शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु (in cold and heat, pleasure and pain) / तथा (and also) / मानापमानयोः (in honor and dishonor)
6:7.meaning: A person who has mastered the restless mind stays steady through comfort and discomfort, praise and insult. In that steadiness, the supreme reality is immediately present.

meanings.meaning: of one who has conquered the self
in cold and heat, pleasure and pain

6:8.shloka: ज्ञानविज्ञानतृप्तात्मा कूटस्थो विजितेन्द्रियः । युक्त इत्युच्यते योगी समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चनः ॥
6:8.transliteration: ज्ञानविज्ञानतृप्तात्मा (one whose inner being is satisfied by knowledge and realization) / कूटस्थः (steady like a mountain; unchanging) / विजितेन्द्रियः (one whose senses are conquered) / युक्तः (joined; in union) / इति (thus) / उच्यते (is called) / योगी (the yogi) / समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चनः (equal-minded toward clay, stone, and gold)
6:8.meaning: A person who is fulfilled by clear understanding, steady in mind, and in control of the senses sees wealth and ordinary matter with the same calmness.

meanings.meaning: one whose inner being is satisfied by knowledge and realization
equal-minded toward clay, stone, and gold

6:9.transliteration: सुहृद् (well-wisher) / मित्र (friend) / अरि (enemy) / उदासीन (indifferent) / मध्यस्थ (mediator) / द्वेष्य (hateful) / बन्धुषु (among relatives) / साधुषु (among the virtuous) / अपि (also) / च (and) / पापेषु (among the sinful) / समबुद्धिः (equal understanding) / विशिष्यते (is श्रेष्ठ)
6:9.meaning: A calm, even-minded person does not get pulled by labels like friend, enemy, good, or bad. That balanced understanding is considered best.

6:10.shloka: योगी युञ्जीत सततमात्मानं रहसि स्थितः । एकाकी यतचित्तात्मा निराशीरपरिग्रहः ॥
6:10.transliteration: योगी (the yogi) / युञ्जीत (should unite) / सततम् (constantly) / आत्मानम् (the self/mind) / रहसि (in seclusion) / स्थितः (स्थित, established) / एकाकी (alone) / यतचित्तात्मा (one whose mind and inner being are restrained) / निराशीः (without desire) / अपरिग्रहः (without possessiveness)
6:10.meaning: A yogi should sit alone, keep the mind steadily focused on the divine, and let go of desire and possessiveness.

meanings.meaning: one whose mind and inner being are restrained

6:11.shloka: शुचौ देशे प्रतिष्ठाप्य स्थिरमासनमात्मनः । नात्युच्छ्रितं नातिनीचं चैलाजिनकुशोत्तरम् ॥
6:11.transliteration: शुचौ (on a clean) / देशे (place) / प्रतिष्ठाप्य (having placed) / स्थिरम् (firm) / आसनम् (seat) / आत्मनः (for oneself) / न (not) / अत्युच्छ्रितम् (too high) / न (not) / अति-नीचम् (too low) / चैल-आजिन-कुश-उत्तरम् (with cloth, deerskin, and kusha grass laid above)
6:11.meaning: Choose a clean, steady seat that is neither too high nor too low, and prepare it with cloth, deerskin, and kusha grass.

meanings.meaning: with cloth, deerskin, and kusha grass laid above

6:12.shloka: तत्रैकाग्रं मनः कृत्वा यतचित्तेन्द्रियक्रियः । उपविश्यासने युञ्ज्याद्योगमात्मविशुद्धये ॥
6:12.transliteration: तत्र (there) / एकाग्रम् (one-pointed) / मनः (mind) / कृत्वा (having made) / यतचित्तेन्द्रियक्रियः (with mind-and-sense activity restrained) / उपविश्य (having sat down) / आसने (on the seat) / युञ्ज्यात् (should practice) / योगम् (yoga) / आत्मविशुद्धये (for inner purification)
6:12.meaning: Sit steadily, gather the mind, restrain the senses, and practice yoga to purify the inner state.

6:13.shloka: समं कायशिरोग्रीवं धारयन्नचलं स्थिरः । संप्रेक्ष्य नासिकाग्रं स्वं दिशश्चानवलोकयन् ॥
6:13.transliteration: समम् (straight/even) / काय-शिरो-ग्रीवम् (body-head-neck) / धारयन् (holding) / अचलम् (motionless) / स्थिरः (steady) / संप्रेक्ष्य (gazing) / नासिका-अग्रम् (tip of the nose) / स्वम् (own) / दिशः (directions) / च (and) / अनवलोकयन् (not looking)
6:13.meaning: Sit with your body straight and still. Keep your gaze fixed and do not let the eyes wander.

6:14.shloka: प्रशान्तात्मा विगतभीर्ब्रह्मचारिव्रते स्थितः । मनः संयम्य मच्चित्तो युक्त आसीत मत्परः ॥
6:14.transliteration: प्रशान्तात्मा (one whose inner state is peaceful) / विगतभीः (free from fear) / ब्रह्मचारिव्रते (in the vow of celibacy) / स्थितः (established) / मनः (mind) / संयम्य (having restrained) / मच्चित्तः (with mind on Me) / युक्तः (yoked in yoga) / आसीत (should sit) / मत्परः (for whom I am the supreme aim)
6:14.meaning: A steady yogi keeps the mind under control, stays free from fear, and turns awareness toward Krishna while sitting in disciplined practice.

meanings.meaning: one whose inner state is peaceful
for whom I am the supreme aim

6:15.shloka: युञ्जन्नेवं सदाऽऽत्मानं योगी नियतमानसः । शान्तिं निर्वाणपरमां मत्संस्थामधिगच्छति ॥
6:15.transliteration: युञ्जन् (uniting) / एवम् (thus) / सदा (always) / आत्मानम् (the inner self) / योगी (the yogi) / नियत-मानसः (with a disciplined mind) / शान्तिम् (peace) / निर्वाण-परमाम् (supreme freedom) / मत्-संस्थाम् (rooted in Me) / अधिगच्छति (attains)
6:15.meaning: A steady yogi keeps the mind gathered and focused, and reaches the highest peace rooted in the divine presence.

6:16.shloka: नात्यश्नतस्तु योगोऽस्ति न चैकान्तमनश्नतः । न चातिस्वप्नशीलस्य जाग्रतो नैव चार्जुन ॥
6:16.transliteration: न अति-अश्नतः (for one who eats too much) / तु (indeed) / योगः (yoga) / अस्ति (is) / न (not) / च (and) / एकान्तम् (entirely) / अनश्नतः (for one who does not eat) / न (not) / च (and) / अति-स्वप्न-शीलस्य (for one who sleeps too much) / जाग्रतः (for one who stays awake) / न (not) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / अर्जुन (Arjuna)
6:16.meaning: Meditation does not work with extremes. Too much food, no food, too much sleep, or no sleep all disturb the practice.

meanings.meaning: for one who eats too much
for one who does not eat
for one who sleeps too much

6:17.shloka: युक्ताहारविहारस्य युक्तचेष्टस्य कर्मसु । युक्तस्वप्नावबोधस्य योगो भवति दुःखहा ॥
6:17.transliteration: युक्ताहारविहारस्य (of one with balanced eating and movement) / युक्तचेष्टस्य (of one with balanced effort) / कर्मसु (in actions) / युक्तस्वप्नावबोधस्य (of one with balanced sleep and waking) / योगः (yoga) / भवति (becomes) / दुःखहा (destroyer of suffering)
6:17.meaning: A steady practice works when daily life is balanced: eating, moving, working, sleeping, and waking all in proper measure.

meanings.meaning: of one with balanced eating and movement
of one with balanced sleep and waking

6:18.shloka: यदा विनियतं चित्तमात्मन्येवावतिष्ठते । निःस्पृहः सर्वकामेभ्यो युक्त इत्युच्यते तदा ॥
6:18.transliteration: यदा (when) / विनियतं (well-controlled) / चित्तम् (mind) / आत्मनि (in the true self) / एव (alone) / अवतिष्ठते (rests) / निःस्पृहः (free from craving) / सर्वकामेभ्यः (from all desires) / युक्तः (united, disciplined) / इति (thus) / उच्यते (is called) / तदा (then)
6:18.meaning: A person is truly steady in yoga when the mind settles into the true self and no longer chases every desire.

6:19.shloka: यथा दीपो निवातस्थो नेङ्गते सोपमा स्मृता । योगिनो यतचित्तस्य युञ्जतो योगमात्मनः ॥
6:19.transliteration: यथा (as) / दीपः (lamp) / निवातस्थः (placed in a windless place) / नेङ्गते (does not flicker) / सा (that) / उपमा (comparison) / स्मृता (is remembered) / योगिनः (of the yogi) / यतचित्तस्य (of the controlled mind) / युञ्जतः (practicing) / योगम् (yoga) / आत्मनः (of the true self)
6:19.meaning: A trained mind becomes steady and unmoving, like a lamp that does not flicker when there is no wind.

6:20.shloka: यत्रोपरमते चित्तं निरुद्धं योगसेवया । यत्र चैवात्मनाऽऽत्मानं पश्यन्नात्मनि तुष्यति ॥
6:20.transliteration: यत्र (where/in which state) / उपरमते (comes to rest) / चित्तम् (the mind) / निरुद्धम् (restrained) / योगसेवया (through the practice of yoga) / यत्र (where/in which state) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / आत्मना (by the true self) / आत्मानम् (the true self) / पश्यन् (seeing) / आत्मनि (in the true self) / तुष्यति (is content)
6:20.meaning: Through steady yoga practice, the mind becomes quiet. In that quiet, a person directly sees the true self and feels complete within it.

6:21.shloka: सुखमात्यन्तिकं यत्तद्बुद्धिग्राह्यमतीन्द्रियम् । वेत्ति यत्र न चैवायं स्थितश्चलति तत्त्वतः ॥
6:21.transliteration: सुखम् (joy) / अत्यन्तिकम् (boundless, ultimate) / यत् (which) / तत् (that) / बुद्धिग्राह्यम् (grasped by the discerning mind) / अतीन्द्रियम् (beyond the senses) / वेत्ति (knows) / यत्र (in which) / न (not) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / अयम् (this one) / स्थितः (established) / चलति (falls away, wavers) / तत्त्वतः (in truth)
6:21.meaning: Meditation reveals a deep joy that the senses cannot reach. Once someone is grounded in it, that person no longer wavers in truth.

6:22.shloka: यं लब्ध्वा चापरं लाभं मन्यते नाधिकं ततः । यस्मिन्स्थितो न दुःखेन गुरुणापि विचाल्यते ॥
6:22.transliteration: यम् (which) / लब्ध्वा (having attained) / च (and) / अपरम् (another) / लाभम् (gain) / मन्यते (one considers) / न (not) / अधिकम् (greater) / ततः (than that) / यस्मिन् (in which) / स्थितः (established) / न (not) / दुःखेन (by sorrow) / गुरुणा (heavy) / अपि (even) / विचाल्यते (is shaken)
6:22.meaning: A person who reaches this state sees nothing greater to attain and cannot be shaken even by severe suffering.

6:23.shloka: तं विद्याद् दुःखसंयोगवियोगं योगसंज्ञितम् । स निश्चयेन योक्तव्यो योगोऽनिर्विण्णचेतसा ॥
6:23.transliteration: तम् (that) / विद्यात् (one should know) / दुःखसंयोगवियोगम् (the separation from union with suffering) / योगसंज्ञितम् (called yoga) / सः (that) / निश्चयेन (with certainty) / योक्तव्यः (should be practiced) / योगः (yoga) / अनिर्विण्णचेतसा (with an undiscouraged mind)
6:23.meaning: True meditation is the ending of the mind’s bond with suffering. That practice should be continued steadily, without losing heart.

meanings.meaning: the separation from union with suffering

6:24.transliteration: सङ्कल्पप्रभवान् (born from resolve) / कामान् (desires) / त्यक्त्वा (having abandoned) / सर्वान् (all) / अशेषतः (completely) / मनसा (by the mind) / एव (alone) / इन्द्रियग्रामम् (the group of senses) / विनियम्य (restraining) / समन्ततः (on all sides)
6:24.meaning: Let go of every desire that rises from planning and craving. Use the mind to pull the senses back from every direction.

6:25.shloka: शनैः शनैरुपरमेद् बुद्ध्या धृतिगृहीतया । आत्मसंस्थं मनः कृत्वा न किञ्चिदपि चिन्तयेत् ॥
6:25.transliteration: शनैः शनैः (little by little) / उपरमेत् (should become quiet) / बुद्ध्या (by the discerning mind) / धृतिगृहीतया (held by firmness) / आत्मसंस्थम् (established in the true self) / मनः (the mind) / कृत्वा (having made) / न (not) / किञ्चित् अपि (anything at all) / चिन्तयेत् (should think)
6:25.meaning: The mind should be calmed gradually with firm understanding. Once it rests in the true self, it should stop chasing thoughts.

6:26.shloka: यतो यतो निश्चरति मनश्चञ्चलमस्थिरम् । ततस्ततो नियम्यैतदात्मन्येव वशं नयेत् ॥
6:26.transliteration: यतो यतः (wherever) / निश्चरति (wanders out) / मनः (the mind) / चञ्चलम् (restless) / अस्थिरम् (unsteady) / ततस्ततः (from there, from there) / नियम्य (restraining) / एतत् (this) / आत्मनि (in the true self) / एव (only) / वशम् (under control) / नयेत् (should bring)
6:26.meaning: Each time the mind runs off, bring it back again and again to the true self.

6:27.shloka: प्रशान्तमनसं ह्येनं योगिनं सुखमुत्तमम् । उपैति शान्तरजसं ब्रह्मभूतमकल्मषम् ॥
6:27.transliteration: प्रशान्तमनसम् (with a fully peaceful mind) / हि (indeed) / एनम् (this) / योगिनम् (the yogi) / सुखम् (joy) / उत्तमम् (highest) / उपैति (attains) / शान्तरजसम् (with passion stilled) / ब्रह्मभूतम् (become Brahman) / अकल्मषम् (spotless, free from impurity)
6:27.meaning: A person who has calmed the mind, quieted passion, and become free of inner impurity reaches the highest kind of joy.

6:28.shloka: युञ्जन्नेवं सदाऽऽत्मानं योगी विगतकल्मषः । सुखेन ब्रह्मसंस्पर्शमत्यन्तं सुखमश्नुते ॥
6:28.transliteration: युञ्जन् (uniting) / एवम् (thus) / सदा (always) / आत्मानम् (the self) / योगी (the yogi) / विगतकल्मषः (free from impurity) / सुखेन (with ease) / ब्रह्मसंस्पर्शम् (contact with Brahman) / अत्यन्तम् (limitless) / सुखम् (joy) / अश्नुते (attains)
6:28.meaning: A purified yogi keeps the mind steadily joined to the supreme reality and experiences deep, lasting joy.

6:29.shloka: सर्वभूतस्थमात्मानं सर्वभूतानि चात्मनि । ईक्षते योगयुक्तात्मा सर्वत्र समदर्शनः ॥
6:29.transliteration: सर्वभूतस्थम् (situated in all beings) / आत्मानम् (the true self) / सर्वभूतानि (all beings) / च (and) / आत्मनि (in the true self) / ईक्षते (sees) / योगयुक्तात्मा (the inner being united in yoga) / सर्वत्र (everywhere) / समदर्शनः (one of equal vision)
6:29.meaning: A person steady in meditation sees the same inner self present in every living being, and sees every living being within that same self.

meanings.meaning: the inner being united in yoga

6:30.shloka: यो मां पश्यति सर्वत्र सर्वं च मयि पश्यति । तस्याहं न प्रणश्यामि स च मे न प्रणश्यति ॥
6:30.transliteration: यो (who) / माम् (Me) / पश्यति (sees) / सर्वत्र (everywhere) / सर्वम् (all) / च (and) / मयि (in Me) / पश्यति (sees) / तस्य (to that one) / अहम् (I) / न (not) / प्रणश्यामि (am lost) / सः (that one) / च (and) / मे (to Me) / न (not) / प्रणश्यति (is lost)
6:30.meaning: Seeing the divine in every being creates a living connection that cannot be broken. The one who sees this way is never separated from Krishna, and Krishna is never separated from that person.

6:31.shloka: सर्वभूतस्थितं यो मां भजत्येकत्वमास्थितः । सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि स योगी मयि वर्तते ॥
6:31.transliteration: सर्वभूतस्थितम् (situated in all beings) / यः (who) / माम् (Me) / भजति (worships) / एकत्वम् (oneness) / आस्थितः (established) / सर्वथा (in every way) / वर्तमानः अपि (even while acting) / सः (that) / योगी (yogi) / मयि (in Me) / वर्तते (abides)
6:31.meaning: A person who sees the divine in every being and lives in that oneness remains rooted in the divine, even while going through ordinary activity.

6:32.shloka: आत्मौपम्येन सर्वत्र समं पश्यति योऽर्जुन । सुखं वा यदि वा दुःखं सः योगी परमो मतः ॥
6:32.transliteration: आत्मौपम्येन (by comparison with the self) / सर्वत्र (everywhere) / समम् (equally) / पश्यति (sees) / यः (who) / अर्जुन (Arjuna) / सुखम् (pleasure) / वा (or) / यदि (if) / वा (or) / दुःखम् (pain) / सः (that) / योगी (yogi) / परमः (highest) / मतः (is considered)
6:32.meaning: A true meditator sees every being with equal regard and does not get thrown off by pleasure or pain.

6:33.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचयोऽयं योगस्त्वया प्रोक्तः साम्येन मधुसूदन । एतस्याहं न पश्यामि चञ्चलत्वात् स्थितिं स्थिराम् ॥
6:33.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / यः (which) / अयम् (this) / योगः (yoga) / त्वया (by you) / प्रोक्तः (spoken) / साम्येन (with balance) / मधुसूदन (O Madhusudana) / एतस्य (of this) / अहम् (I) / न (not) / पश्यामि (see) / चञ्चलत्वात् (because of restlessness) / स्थितिम् (steady state) / स्थिराम् (firm)
6:33.meaning: Arjuna says the balanced state Krishna described sounds clear, but his restless mind cannot hold it steadily.

6:34.shloka: चञ्चलं हि मनः कृष्ण प्रमाथि बलवद्दृढम् । तस्याहं निग्रहं मन्ये वायोरिव सुदुष्करम् ॥
6:34.transliteration: चञ्चलम् (restless) / हि (indeed) / मनः (mind) / कृष्ण (O Krishna) / प्रमाथि (turbulent) / बलवत् (strong) / दृढम् (stubborn, firm) । / तस्य (of that) / अहम् (I) / निग्रहम् (control, restraint) / मन्ये (consider) / वायोः (of wind) / इव (like) / सुदुष्करम् (very difficult)
6:34.meaning: Arjuna admits that the mind is wild, forceful, and hard to restrain, almost like trying to catch the wind.

6:35.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचअसंशयं महाबाहो मनो दुर्निग्रहं चलं । अभ्यासेन तु कौन्तेय वैराग्येण च गृह्यते ॥
6:35.transliteration: असंशयम् (without doubt) / महाबाहो (mighty-armed one) / मनः (mind) / दुर्निग्रहम् (hard to restrain) / चलम् (restless) / अभ्यासेन (through practice) / तु (but) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / वैराग्येण (through detachment) / च (and) / गृह्यते (is mastered)
6:35.meaning: The mind really is restless and difficult to control, but steady practice and letting go of cravings can bring it under control.

6:36.shloka: असंयतात्मना योगो दुष्प्राप इति मे मतिः । वश्यात्मना तु यतता शक्योऽवाप्तुमुपायतः ॥
6:36.transliteration: असंयतात्मना (by one whose self is not controlled) / योगः (yoga) / दुष्प्रापः (hard to attain) / इति (thus) / मे (my) / मतिः (view) । / वश्यात्मना (by one whose self is under control) / तु (but) / यतता (by one striving) / शक्यः (possible) / अवाप्तुम् (to attain) / उपायतः (by right means)
6:36.meaning: A person ruled by an uncontrolled mind struggles to reach yoga. With self-control, steady effort, and the right method, yoga becomes possible.

meanings.meaning: by one whose self is not controlled
by one whose self is under control

6:37.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचअयतिः श्रद्धयोपेतो योगाच्चलितमानसः । अप्राप्य योगसंसिद्धिं कां गतिं कृष्ण गच्छति ॥
6:37.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / अयतिः (one without steady effort) / श्रद्धयोपेतः (endowed with faith) / योगात् (from yoga) / चलितमानसः (with a mind turned away) / अप्राप्य (without attaining) / योगसंसिद्धिम् (perfection in yoga) / कां गतिम् (what state) / कृष्ण (O Krishna) / गच्छति (does he go)
6:37.meaning: Arjuna asks what becomes of someone who begins with faith but loses steadiness before reaching full yogic success.

6:38.shloka: कच्चिन्नोभयविभ्रष्टश्छिन्नाभ्रमिव नश्यति । अप्रतिष्ठो महाबाहो विमूढो ब्रह्मणः पथि ॥
6:38.transliteration: कच्चित् (whether?) / नोभयविभ्रष्टः (fallen from both sides) / छिन्नाभ्रम् (torn cloud) / इव (like) / नश्यति (perishes) / अप्रतिष्ठः (without support) / महाबाहो (O mighty-armed one) / विमूढः (deluded) / ब्रह्मणः (of the supreme reality) / पथि (on the path)
6:38.meaning: Arjuna asks whether a person who fails in both worldly life and spiritual practice is lost completely, like a torn cloud blown apart.

6:39.shloka: एतन्मे संशयं कृष्ण छेत्तुमर्हस्यशेषतः । त्वदन्यः संशयस्यास्य छेत्ता न ह्युपपद्यते ॥
6:39.transliteration: एतत् (this) / मे (my) / संशयम् (doubt) / कृष्ण (O Krishna) / छेत्तुम् (to cut) / अर्हसि (you are able) / अशेषतः (completely) / त्वदन्यः (other than you) / संशयस्य (of the doubt) / अस्य (of this) / छेत्ता (cutter) / न (not) / हि (indeed) / उपपद्यते (is possible)
6:39.meaning: Arjuna asks Krishna to remove his doubt completely, saying no one else can resolve it.

6:40.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचपार्थ नैवेह नामुत्र विनाशस्तस्य विद्यते । नहि कल्याणकृत्कश्िचद्दुर्गतिं तात गच्छति ॥
6:40.transliteration: श्री भगवानुवाच (the Blessed One said) / पार्थ (O Partha) / नैव (not at all) / इह (here) / न (not) / अमुत्र (in the other world) / विनाशः (destruction) / तस्य (of him) / विद्यते (there is) / नहि (indeed not) / कल्याणकृत् (one who does good) / कश्चित् (anyone) / दुर्गतिम् (bad end) / तात (my dear) / गच्छति (goes)
6:40.meaning: A person who does good practice does not lose everything. Even if progress feels interrupted, that effort is never destroyed and never leads to a bad end.

6:41.shloka: प्राप्य पुण्यकृतां लोकानुषित्वा शाश्वतीः समाः । शुचीनां श्रीमतां गेहे योगभ्रष्टोऽभिजायते ॥
6:41.transliteration: प्राप्य (having attained) / पुण्यकृताम् (of those who have done good deeds) / लोकान् (worlds) / उषित्वा (having dwelt) / शाश्वतीः (lasting, long) / समाः (years) / शुचीनाम् (of the pure) / श्रीमताम् (of the prosperous) / गेहे (in the house) / योगभ्रष्टः (one fallen from yoga) / अभिजायते (is born again)
6:41.meaning: A person who slips from yoga does not lose everything. After enjoying the results of good deeds for a long time, that person is born again in a pure, prosperous family.

meanings.meaning: of those who have done good deeds

6:42.shloka: अथवा योगिनामेव कुले भवति धीमताम् । एतद्धि दुर्लभतरं लोके जन्म यदीदृशम् ॥
6:42.transliteration: अथवा (or) / योगिनाम् (of yogis) / एव (indeed) / कुले (in the family) / भवति (is born) / धीमताम् (of the wise) । / एतत् (this) / हि (indeed) / दुर्लभतरम् (more rare) / लोके (in the world) / जन्म (birth) / यत् (which) / ईदृशम् (of this kind)
6:42.meaning: A person who falls from yoga may be born into a family of wise practitioners. That kind of birth is very rare.

6:43.shloka: तत्र तं बुद्धिसंयोगं लभते पौर्वदेहिकम् । यतते च ततो भूयः संसिद्धौ कुरुनन्दन ॥
6:43.transliteration: तत्र (there) / तम् (that) / बुद्धिसंयोगम् (union with discernment) / लभते (he attains) / पौर्वदेहिकम् (belonging to the former body) / यतते (he strives) / च (and) / ततः (then) / भूयः (again) / संसिद्धौ (toward complete success) / कुरुनन्दन (O son of Kuru)
6:43.meaning: A person who falls from yoga does not lose everything. He regains the insight and practice from a former life and continues striving toward full success.

6:44.shloka: पूर्वाभ्यासेन तेनैव ह्रियते ह्यवशोऽपि सः । जिज्ञासुरपि योगस्य शब्दब्रह्मातिवर्तते ॥
6:44.transliteration: पूर्वाभ्यासेन (by earlier practice) / तेन (by that) / एव (indeed) / ह्रियते (is drawn away) / हि (certainly) / अवशः (against his will) / अपि (even) / सः (he) । / जिज्ञासुः (seeker) / अपि (even) / योगस्य (of yoga) / शब्दब्रह्म (sound-Brahman / ritual words) / अति-वर्तते (goes beyond) ॥
6:44.meaning: Past practice pulls a person toward yoga even when the person is not fully in control. A sincere seeker also rises beyond ritual words and outer religious forms.

6:45.shloka: प्रयत्नाद्यतमानस्तु योगी संशुद्धकिल्बिषः । अनेकजन्मसंसिद्धस्ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥
6:45.transliteration: प्रयत्नात् (by effort) / यतमानः (striving) / तु (but) / योगी (yogi) / संशुद्धकिल्बिषः (with impurities fully cleansed) / अनेकजन्मसंसिद्धः (perfected through many births) / ततः (then) / याति (goes) / पराम् (highest) / गतिम् (goal)
6:45.meaning: Persistent effort clears inner impurities, and over many lives of practice, the yogi reaches the highest goal.

6:46.shloka: तपस्विभ्योऽधिको योगी ज्ञानिभ्योऽपि मतोऽधिकः । कर्मिभ्यश्चाधिको योगी तस्माद्योगी भवार्जुन ॥
6:46.transliteration: तपस्विभ्योऽधिकः (greater than ascetics) / योगी (the yogi) / ज्ञानिभ्योऽपि (even than the wise) / मतोऽधिकः (is considered greater) / कर्मिभ्यः (than workers) / च (and) / अधिकः (greater) / योगी (the yogi) / तस्मात् (therefore) / योगी (a yogi) / भव (become) / अर्जुन (Arjuna)
6:46.meaning: Meditation and inner mastery are higher than harsh austerity, intellectual learning, or mere action. Krishna urges Arjuna to become a true yogi.

6:47.shloka: योगिनामपि सर्वेषां मद्गतेनान्तरात्मना । श्रद्धावान्भजते यो मां स मे युक्ततमो मतः ॥
6:47.transliteration: योगिनाम् (of yogis) / अपि (even) / सर्वेषाम् (of all) / मद्गतेन (with set in me) / अन्तरात्मना (with the inner self) / श्रद्धावान् (trustful) / भजते (worships) / यः (who) / माम् (me) / सः (that one) / युक्ततमः (most united) / मतः (is considered)
6:47.meaning: Among all kinds of yogis, the one whose inner being is fixed on Krishna and who worships him with trust is the highest.

7:1.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचमय्यासक्तमनाः पार्थ योगं युञ्जन्मदाश्रयः । असंशयं समग्रं मां यथा ज्ञास्यसि तच्छृणु ॥
7:1.transliteration: श्रीभगवान् (the Blessed Lord) / उवाच (said) / मयि (in Me) / आसक्त-मनाः (mind attached) / पार्थ (Arjuna) / योगम् (yoga) / युञ्जन् (practicing) / मद्-आश्रयः (taking refuge in Me) / असंशयम् (without doubt) / समग्रम् (completely) / माम् (Me) / यथा (how) / ज्ञास्यसि (you will know) / तत् (that) / शृणु (hear)
7:1.meaning: Keep your mind focused on Krishna, rely on him, and practice yoga. Then you will come to know him fully and without doubt.

7:2.shloka: ज्ञानं तेऽहं सविज्ञानमिदं वक्ष्याम्यशेषतः । यज्ज्ञात्वा नेह भूयोऽन्यज्ज्ञातव्यमवशिष्यते ॥
7:2.transliteration: ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / ते (to you) / अहम् (I) / सविज्ञानम् (with realization) / इदम् (this) / वक्ष्यामि (will speak) / अशेषतः (completely) / यत् (which) / ज्ञात्वा (having known) / न (not) / इह (here) / भूयः (again) / अन्यत् (other) / ज्ञातव्यम् (to be known) / अवशिष्यते (remains)
7:2.meaning: Krishna promises to explain both understanding and direct realization completely, so that after grasping it, nothing else needs to be learned about the matter.

7:3.shloka: मनुष्याणां सहस्रेषु कश्िचद्यतति सिद्धये । यततामपि सिद्धानां कश्िचन्मां वेत्ति तत्त्वतः ॥
7:3.transliteration: मनुष्याणाम् (of people) / सहस्रेषु (among thousands) / कश्चित् (some one) / यतति (strives) / सिद्धये (for perfection) / यतताम् अपि (even among those striving) / सिद्धानाम् (of the perfected) / कश्चित् (some one) / माम् (Me) / वेत्ति (knows) / तत्त्वतः (in truth)
7:3.meaning: Many people try to reach perfection, but very few reach true understanding of Krishna. Effort is common; real realization is rare.

7:4.shloka: भूमिरापोऽनलो वायुः खं मनो बुद्धिरेव च । अहङ्कार इतीयं मे भिन्ना प्रकृतिरष्टधा ॥
7:4.transliteration: भूमिः (earth) / आपः (water) / अनलः (fire) / वायुः (air) / खम् (ether) / मनः (mind) / बुद्धिः (intellect) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / अहङ्कारः (ego) / इति (thus) / इयम् (this) / मे (my) / भिन्ना (separate) / प्रकृतिः (nature) / अष्टधा (eightfold)
7:4.meaning: Krishna names eight parts of his lower nature: the five elements, mind, intellect, and ego. This is the material side of existence, not the highest reality.

7:5.shloka: अपरेयमितस्त्वन्यां प्रकृतिं विद्धि मे पराम् । जीवभूतां महाबाहो ययेदं धार्यते जगत् ॥
7:5.transliteration: अपरा (lower) / इयम् (this) / इतः (from this) / तु (but) / अन्याम् (another) / प्रकृतिम् (nature) / विद्धि (know) / मे (My) / पराम् (higher) / जीवभूताम् (become living) / महाबाहो (O mighty-armed one) / यया (by which) / इदम् (this) / धार्यते (is sustained) / जगत् (world)
7:5.meaning: There is a higher nature beyond matter and mind. That living power holds up the whole world.

7:6.shloka: एतद्योनीनि भूतानि सर्वाणीत्युपधारय । अहं कृत्स्नस्य जगतः प्रभवः प्रलयस्तथा ॥
7:6.transliteration: एतद्योनीनि (having these as their womb/source) / भूतानि (beings) / सर्वाणि (all) / इति (thus) / उपधारय (understand) / अहम् (I) / कृत्स्नस्य (of the entire) / जगतः (universe) / प्रभवः (source/origin) / प्रलयः (dissolution) / तथा (also)
7:6.meaning: All beings arise from these two natures. Krishna says he is the source and end of the whole universe.

7:7.shloka: मत्तः परतरं नान्यत्किञ्चिदस्ति धनञ्जय । मयि सर्वमिदं प्रोतं सूत्रे मणिगणा इव ॥
7:7.transliteration: मत्तः (than Me) / परतरं (higher) / न (not) / अन्यत् (other) / किञ्चिद् (anything) / अस्ति (exists) / धनञ्जय (O Dhananjaya) / मयि (in Me) / सर्वम् (all) / इदम् (this) / प्रोतम् (strung) / सूत्रे (on a thread) / मणिगणाः (clusters of gems) / इव (like)
7:7.meaning: Krishna says nothing is greater than him, and the whole world is held together within him like beads on a string.

7:8.shloka: रसोऽहमप्सु कौन्तेय प्रभास्मि शशिसूर्ययोः । प्रणवः सर्ववेदेषु शब्दः खे पौरुषं नृषु ॥
7:8.transliteration: रसः (taste) / अहम् (I am) / अप्सु (in waters) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / प्रभा (light) / अस्मि (am) / शशि-सूर्ययोः (of moon and sun) / प्रणवः (the sacred syllable Om) / सर्व-वेदेषु (in all the Vedas) / शब्दः (sound) / खे (in space) / पौरुषम् (valor / human strength) / नृषु (in human beings)
7:8.meaning: Krishna says he is the life and meaning present in ordinary things: taste in water, light in the sun and moon, Om in the Vedas, sound in space, and strength in people.

7:9.shloka: पुण्यो गन्धः पृथिव्यां च तेजश्चास्मि विभावसौ । जीवनं सर्वभूतेषु तपश्चास्मि तपस्विषु ॥
7:9.transliteration: पुण्यो (pure) / गन्धः (fragrance) / पृथिव्याम् (in the earth) / च (and) / तेजः (radiance) / च (and) / अस्मि (I am) / विभावसौ (in fire) / जीवनम् (life) / सर्वभूतेषु (in all beings) / तपः (austerity) / च (and) / अस्मि (I am) / तपस्विषु (in ascetics)
7:9.meaning: Krishna says he is present as the earth’s fragrance, fire’s brilliance, the life in every creature, and the discipline of seekers.
