7:10.shloka: बीजं मां सर्वभूतानां विद्धि पार्थ सनातनम् । बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम् ॥
7:10.transliteration: बीजम् (seed) / माम् (Me) / सर्वभूतानाम् (of all beings) / विद्धि (know) / पार्थ (O Partha) / सनातनम् (eternal) / बुद्धिः (intelligence) / बुद्धिमताम् (of the intelligent) / अस्मि (I am) / तेजः (brilliance) / तेजस्विनाम् (of the brilliant) / अहम् (I am)
7:10.meaning: Krishna says he is the original source of every living being, and that intelligence and brilliance in people come from him.

7:11.shloka: बलं बलवतामस्मि कामरागविवर्जितम् । धर्माविरुद्धो भूतेषु कामोऽस्मि भरतर्षभ ॥
7:11.transliteration: बलं (strength) / बलवताम् (of the strong) / अस्मि (I am) / कामरागविवर्जितम् (free from desire and attachment) । / धर्माविरुद्धः (not opposed to dharma) / भूतेषु (in beings) / कामः (desire) / अस्मि (I am) / भरतर्षभ (O best of the Bharatas)
7:11.meaning: True strength is free from craving, and desire is right only when it does not fight against dharma.

7:12.shloka: ये चैव सात्त्विका भावा राजसास्तामसाश्च ये । मत्त एवेति तान्विद्धि नत्वहं तेषु ते मयि ॥
7:12.transliteration: ये (which) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / सात्त्विकाः (sattvic) / भावाḥ (states of being) / राजसाः (rajasic) / तामसाः (tamasic) / च (and) / ये (which) / मत्तः (from Me) / एव (indeed) / इति (thus) / तान् (them) / विद्धि (know) / न (not) / तु (but) / अहम् (I) / तेषु (in them) / ते (they) / मयि (in Me)
7:12.meaning: Every mode of nature—clear, restless, or dull—comes from Krishna. But Krishna is beyond all of them and is not confined by any one quality.

7:13.shloka: त्रिभिर्गुणमयैर्भावैरेभिः सर्वमिदं जगत् । मोहितं नाभिजानाति मामेभ्यः परमव्ययम् ॥
7:13.transliteration: त्रिभिः (by three) / गुणमयैः (made of qualities) / भावैः (states) / एभिः (by these) / सर्वम् (all) / इदम् (this) / जगत् (world) / मोहितम् (deluded) / न (not) / अभिजानाति (knows) / माम् (me) / एभ्यः (from these) / परम् (beyond) / अव्ययम् (imperishable)
7:13.meaning: The whole world gets confused by the three qualities of nature and fails to recognize Krishna as the imperishable reality beyond them.

7:14.shloka: दैवी ह्येषा गुणमयी मम माया दुरत्यया । मामेव ये प्रपद्यन्ते मायामेतां तरन्ति ते ॥
7:14.transliteration: दैवी (divine) / हि (indeed) / एषा (this) / गुणमयी (made of the three qualities) / मम (my) / माया (illusion) / दुरत्यया (hard to cross) / माम् (Me) / एव (alone) / ये (those who) / प्रपद्यन्ते (take refuge) / मायाम् (this illusion) / एताम् (this) / तरन्ति (cross beyond) / ते (they)
7:14.meaning: The forces that shape ordinary life are hard to overcome on your own. Surrendering to Krishna is what carries a person beyond them.

7:15.shloka: न मां दुष्कृतिनो मूढाः प्रपद्यन्ते नराधमाः । माययापहृतज्ञाना आसुरं भावमाश्रिताः ॥
7:15.transliteration: न (not) / मां (to me) / दुष्कृतिनः (wrongdoers) / मूढाः (deluded) / प्रपद्यन्ते (take refuge) / नराधमाः (lowest among people) / मायया (by the veil) / अपहृत-ज्ञानाः (whose understanding has been stolen) / आसुरम् (shadowed) / भावम् (nature) / आश्रिताः (having taken refuge in)
7:15.meaning: People who are deeply confused, morally corrupt, and trapped by the veil do not turn toward the divine. They lean into a destructive nature instead.

7:16.shloka: चतुर्विधा भजन्ते मां जनाः सुकृतिनोऽर्जुन । आर्तो जिज्ञासुरर्थार्थी ज्ञानी च भरतर्षभ ॥
7:16.transliteration: चतुर्विधा (four kinds) / भजन्ते (worship) / माम् (Me) / जनाः (people) / सुकृतिनः (those with good deeds) / अर्जुन (Arjuna) / आर्तः (the distressed) / जिज्ञासुः (the seeker of knowledge) / अर्थार्थी (the seeker of wealth) / ज्ञानी (the wise one) / च (and) / भरतर्षभ (O best of the Bharatas)
7:16.meaning: People come to Krishna for different reasons: pain, curiosity, gain, or wisdom. All four are counted among the virtuous.

7:17.shloka: तेषां ज्ञानी नित्ययुक्त एकभक्ितर्विशिष्यते । प्रियो हि ज्ञानिनोऽत्यर्थमहं स च मम प्रियः ॥
7:17.transliteration: तेषाम् (among them) / ज्ञानी (the wise one) / नित्ययुक्तः (ever united) / एकभक्तिः (one-pointed in devotion) / विशिष्यते (is distinguished, is श्रेष्ठ) । / प्रियः (dear) / हि (indeed) / ज्ञानिनः (of the wise one) / अत्यर्थम् (greatly, exceedingly) / अहम् (I) / सः (that one) / च (and) / मम (to me) / प्रियः (dear)
7:17.meaning: Among the four kinds of devotees, the wise one who is constantly devoted and single-minded stands highest. Krishna says this devotee is very dear to him, and he is very dear to that devotee.

7:18.shloka: उदाराः सर्व एवैते ज्ञानी त्वात्मैव मे मतम् । आस्थितः स हि युक्तात्मा मामेवानुत्तमां गतिम् ॥
7:18.transliteration: उदाराः (noble) / सर्वे (all) / एव (indeed) / एते (these) / ज्ञानी (the wise one) / तु (but) / आत्मा (self) / एव (indeed) / मे (of Me) / मतम् (opinion) / आस्थितः (established) / सः (that one) / हि (indeed) / युक्तात्मा (one whose inner being is united) / माम् (Me) / एव (only) / अनुत्तमाम् (unsurpassed) / गतिम् (goal)
7:18.meaning: All four kinds of devotees are good, but the wise devotee is the closest to Krishna. Such a person is firmly united with him and sees him as the highest goal.

meanings.meaning: one whose inner being is united

7:19.shloka: बहूनां जन्मनामन्ते ज्ञानवान्मां प्रपद्यते । वासुदेवः सर्वमिति स महात्मा सुदुर्लभः ॥
7:19.transliteration: बहूनाम् (of many) / जन्मनाम् (births) / अन्ते (at the end) / ज्ञानवान् (the wise one) / माम् (to Me) / प्रपद्यते (surrenders) / वासुदेवः (Vasudeva) / सर्वम् (everything) / इति (thus) / सः (that) / महात्मा (great soul) / सुदुर्लभः (very rare)
7:19.meaning: Real understanding ripens over many lives. At its peak, a person sees that everything is the divine and naturally turns in surrender.

7:20.shloka: कामैस्तैस्तैर्हृतज्ञानाः प्रपद्यन्तेऽन्यदेवताः । तं तं नियममास्थाय प्रकृत्या नियताः स्वया ॥
7:20.transliteration: कामैः (by desires) / तैः तैः (by those various) / हृत-ज्ञानाः (whose understanding is taken away) / प्रपद्यन्ते (they take refuge) / अन्य-देवताः (other radiant beings) / तम् तम् (that each) / नियमम् (rule / discipline) / आस्थाय (having taken up) / प्रकृत्या (by nature) / नियताः (governed) / स्वया (by their own)
7:20.meaning: When desire overwhelms clear thinking, people begin seeking help from other powers and follow whatever practices fit their own nature.

7:21.shloka: यो यो यां यां तनुं भक्तः श्रद्धयार्चितुमिच्छति । तस्य तस्याचलां श्रद्धां तामेव विदधाम्यहम् ॥
7:21.transliteration: यो यो (whatever) / यां यां (whatever) / तनुं (form) / भक्तः (devotee) / श्रद्धया (with faith) / अर्चितुम् (to worship) / इच्छति (wants) / तस्य तस्य (for that very one) / अचलाम् (unshaken) / श्रद्धाम् (faith) / ताम् एव (that itself) / विदधामि (I make firm) / अहम् (I)
7:21.meaning: Krishna says he strengthens a devotee's faith in whatever form that devotee wishes to worship.

7:22.shloka: स तया श्रद्धया युक्तस्तस्याराधनमीहते । लभते च ततः कामान्मयैव विहितान् हि तान् ॥
7:22.transliteration: स (that person) / तया (with that) / श्रद्धया (faith) / युक्तः (endowed) / तस्य (of that) / आराधनम् (worship) / ईहते (seeks) / लभते (obtains) / च (and) / ततः (then) / कामान् (desires) / मया (by Me) / एव (alone) / विहितान् (ordained) / हि (indeed) / तान् (those)
7:22.meaning: A person with strong faith turns to that chosen deity and gets the desired result, but even that fulfillment is ultimately arranged by Krishna.

7:23.shloka: अन्तवत्तु फलं तेषां तद्भवत्यल्पमेधसाम् । देवान्देवयजो यान्ति मद्भक्ता यान्ति मामपि ॥
7:23.transliteration: अन्तवत्तु (finite indeed) / फलम् (fruit, result) / तेषाम् (of those) / तत् (that) / भवति (becomes) / अल्पमेधसाम् (of the little-wise) / देवान् (to the radiant beings) / देवयजः (worshippers of the radiant beings) / यान्ति (go) / मद्भक्ताः (my devotees) / यान्ति (go) / माम् (to me) / अपि (also)
7:23.meaning: Half-hearted understanding gives limited results. Those who worship lesser powers reach those powers, while Krishna's devotees reach Krishna.

7:24.shloka: अव्यक्तं व्यक्ितमापन्नं मन्यन्ते मामबुद्धयः । परं भावमजानन्तो ममाव्ययमनुत्तमम् ॥
7:24.transliteration: अव्यक्तम् (the unmanifest) / व्यक्तिम् (manifest form) / आपन्नम् (entered into) / मन्यन्ते (they think) / माम् (me) / अबुद्धयः (the unwise) / परम् (supreme) / भावम् (nature/state) / अजानन्तः (not knowing) / मम (my) / अव्ययम् (imperishable) / अनुत्तमम् (unsurpassed)
7:24.meaning: People without clear understanding think Krishna has taken on a limited human body. They do not recognize his imperishable, highest nature beyond what the senses can see.

7:25.shloka: नाहं प्रकाशः सर्वस्य योगमायासमावृतः । मूढोऽयं नाभिजानाति लोको मामजमव्ययम् ॥
7:25.transliteration: नाहम् (I am not) / प्रकाशः (manifest) / सर्वस्य (to everyone) / योगमायासमावृतः (covered by yoga-maya) । / मूढः (deluded) / अयम् (this) / न अभिजानाति (does not know) / लोकः (world) / माम् (me) / अजम् (unborn) / अव्ययम् (imperishable) ॥
7:25.meaning: Krishna says he is hidden from people who are confused. They do not recognize his unborn, unchanging nature because their minds are covered by the veil of appearance.

7:26.shloka: वेदाहं समतीतानि वर्तमानानि चार्जुन । भविष्याणि च भूतानि मां तु वेद न कश्चन ॥
7:26.transliteration: वेद (I know) / अहं (I) / समतीतानि (the past) / वर्तमानानि (the present) / च (and) / अर्जुन (Arjuna) / भविष्याणि (the future) / च (and) / भूतानि (beings) / माम् (me) / तु (but) / वेद (knows) / न (not) / कश्चन (anyone)
7:26.meaning: Krishna says he knows every being across past, present, and future, but no ordinary person truly knows him.

7:27.shloka: इच्छाद्वेषसमुत्थेन द्वन्द्वमोहेन भारत । सर्वभूतानि संमोहं सर्गे यान्ति परन्तप ॥
7:27.transliteration: इच्छा-द्वेष (desire and aversion) / समुत्थेन (arising from) / द्वन्द्व-मोहेन (by the delusion of opposites) / भारत (O Bharata) / सर्व-भूतानि (all beings) / संमोहं (delusion) / सर्गे (at birth / in creation) / यान्ति (go to) / परन्तप (O scorcher of foes)
7:27.meaning: Desire and aversion create confusion in living beings, and that confusion keeps them trapped in repeated cycles of ignorance and rebirth.

7:28.shloka: येषां त्वन्तगतं पापं जनानां पुण्यकर्मणाम् । ते द्वन्द्वमोहनिर्मुक्ता भजन्ते मां दृढव्रताः ॥
7:28.transliteration: येषाम् (of whom) / तु (but) / अन्तगतं (ended, destroyed) / पापं (wrongdoing) / जनानाम् (of people) / पुण्यकर्मणाम् (whose actions are meritorious) / ते (they) / द्वन्द्वमोहनिर्मुक्ताः (freed from the confusion of opposites) / भजन्ते (worship, serve) / माम् (me) / दृढव्रताः (firmly resolved)
7:28.meaning: People whose harmful impulses have been cleared and whose good actions have ripened are no longer torn by confusion. With steady commitment, they turn toward Krishna.

meanings.meaning: freed from the confusion of opposites

7:29.shloka: जरामरणमोक्षाय मामाश्रित्य यतन्ति ये । ते ब्रह्म तद्विदुः कृत्स्नमध्यात्मं कर्म चाखिलम् ॥
7:29.transliteration: जरा (old age) / मरण (death) / मोक्षाय (for release) / माम् (me) / आश्रित्य (having taken refuge) / यतन्ति (they strive) / ये (who) / ते (they) / ब्रह्म (Brahman) / तत् (that) / विदुः (know) / कृत्स्नम् (whole) / अध्यात्मम् (inner life) / कर्म (action) / च (and) / अखिलम् (entire)
7:29.meaning: Those who take refuge in Krishna and strive for freedom from aging and death come to know the supreme reality, the whole inner life, and all action.

7:30.shloka: साधिभूताधिदैवं मां साधियज्ञं च ये विदुः । प्रयाणकालेऽपि च मां ते विदुर्युक्तचेतसः ॥
7:30.transliteration: स-अधिभूत-आधिदैवम् (together with the physical realm and the radiant powers) / माम् (me) / स-अधियज्ञम् (together with the inner sacrifice) / च (and) / ये (those who) / विदुः (know) / प्रयाण-काले (at the time of departure/death) / अपि (even) / च (and) / माम् (me) / ते (they) / विदुः (know) / युक्त-चेतसः (those with a disciplined mind)
7:30.meaning: Those who understand Krishna as present in the physical world, the divine powers, and the ritual offering remember him even at death.

meanings.meaning: together with the physical realm and the radiant powers

8:1.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचकिं तद्ब्रह्म किमध्यात्मं किं कर्म पुरुषोत्तम । अधिभूतं च किं प्रोक्तमधिदैवं किमुच्यते ॥
8:1.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / किम् (what) / तत् (that) / ब्रह्म (Brahman) / किम् (what) / अध्यात्मम् (the self / inner principle) / किम् (what) / कर्म (action) / पुरुषोत्तम (O Supreme Person) / अधिभूतम् (the field of changing beings / material existence) / च (and) / किम् (what) / प्रोक्तम् (is said) / अधिदैवम् (the divine principle) / किम् (what) / उच्यते (is called)
8:1.meaning: Arjuna asks Krishna to explain the key terms of spiritual teaching: Brahman, the self, action, the changing world, and the divine principle.

8:2.shloka: अधियज्ञः कथं कोऽत्र देहेऽस्मिन्मधुसूदन । प्रयाणकाले च कथं ज्ञेयोऽसि नियतात्मभिः ॥
8:2.transliteration: अधियज्ञः (the lord of sacrifice) / कथम् (how) / कः (who) / अत्र (here) / देहे (in the body) / अस्मिन् (this) / मधुसूदन (Madhusudana) / प्रयाणकाले (at the time of departure) / च (and) / कथम् (how) / ज्ञेयः (to be known) / असि (you are) / नियतात्मभिः (by those with disciplined minds)
8:2.meaning: Arjuna asks who the inner sacrificer is, how that presence lives in the body, and how a disciplined person can know Krishna at death.

8:3.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचअक्षरं ब्रह्म परमं स्वभावोऽध्यात्ममुच्यते । भूतभावोद्भवकरो विसर्गः कर्मसंज्ञितः ॥
8:3.transliteration: अक्षरम् (imperishable) / ब्रह्म (supreme reality) / परमम् (highest) / स्वभावः (one's own nature) / अध्यात्मम् (inner self) / उच्यते (is called) / भूतभावोद्भवकरः (that which brings beings into existence) / विसर्गः (offering/releasing) / कर्मसंज्ञितः (called action)
8:3.meaning: The imperishable is the highest reality. Inner nature is called the inner self, and the act that brings beings into existence is called action.

meanings.meaning: that which brings beings into existence

8:4.shloka: अधिभूतं क्षरो भावः पुरुषश्चाधिदैवतम् । अधियज्ञोऽहमेवात्र देहे देहभृतां वर ॥
8:4.transliteration: अधिभूतं (the lower realm of embodied existence) / क्षरः (perishable) / भावः (state of being) / पुरुषः (cosmic person) / च (and) / अधिदैवतम् (the radiant being of the gods) / अधियज्ञः (inner witness of sacrifice) / अहम् (I) / एव (indeed) / अत्र (here) / देहे (in the body) / देहभृताम् (of embodied beings) / वर (O best)
8:4.meaning: The changing world is called the lower realm. The cosmic person is called the radiant being. In this body, Krishna says he himself is the inner witness of sacrifice.

meanings.meaning: the lower realm of embodied existence
the radiant being of the gods

8:5.shloka: अन्तकाले च मामेव स्मरन्मुक्त्वा कलेवरम् । यः प्रयाति स मद्भावं याति नास्त्यत्र संशयः ॥
8:5.transliteration: अन्तकाले (at the end of life) / च (and) / माम् (Me) / एव (indeed) / स्मरन् (remembering) / मुक्त्वा (having छोड़कर / leaving) / कलेवरम् (the body) / यः (who) / प्रयाति (goes forth) / सः (that one) / मद्भावम् (My state) / याति (attains) / न (not) / अस्ति (is) / अत्र (here) / संशयः (doubt)
8:5.meaning: A person who remembers Krishna at the moment of death and leaves the body reaches Krishna's state without doubt.

8:6.shloka: यं यं वापि स्मरन्भावं त्यजत्यन्ते कलेवरम् । तं तमेवैति कौन्तेय सदा तद्भावभावितः ॥
8:6.transliteration: यं यं (whatever) / वा अपि (also indeed) / स्मरन् (remembering) / भावम् (state of being) / त्यजति (abandons) / अन्ते (at the end) / कलेवरम् (the body) / तम् तम् एव (that very) / एति (attains) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / सदा (always) / तद्भावभावितः (formed by that state)
8:6.meaning: The final thought at death shapes where consciousness goes next. A person tends toward whatever state has been cultivated again and again.

8:7.shloka: तस्मात्सर्वेषु कालेषु मामनुस्मर युध्य च । मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्मामेवैष्यस्यसंशयम् ॥
8:7.transliteration: तस्मात् (therefore) / सर्वेषु कालेषु (at all times) / माम् (Me) / अनुस्मर (remember continuously) / युध्य (fight) / च (and) / मयि (in Me) / अर्पित (offered) / मनः (mind) / बुद्धिः (intellect) / माम् (Me) / एव (indeed) / एष्यसि (you will come) / असंशयम् (without doubt)
8:7.meaning: Keep the divine in mind all the time, do your duty, and place your thoughts and understanding there. Then you will surely reach the divine.

8:8.shloka: अभ्यासयोगयुक्तेन चेतसा नान्यगामिना । परमं पुरुषं दिव्यं याति पार्थानुचिन्तयन् ॥
8:8.transliteration: अभ्यासयोगयुक्तेन (joined with the yoga of practice) / चेतसा (with the mind) / नान्यगामिना (not going elsewhere) / परमं (supreme) / पुरुषं (person) / दिव्यं (divine) / याति (goes, reaches) / पार्थ (O Partha) / अनुचिन्तयन् (contemplating, remembering)
8:8.meaning: A mind trained by steady practice, and not wandering to other things, reaches the supreme divine being when life ends.

meanings.meaning: joined with the yoga of practice

8:9.shloka: कविं पुराणमनुशासितारमणोरणीयांसमनुस्मरेद्यः । सर्वस्य धातारमचिन्त्यरूपमादित्यवर्णं तमसः परस्तात् ॥
8:9.transliteration: कविम् (the wise one) / पुराणम् (the ancient one) / अनुशासितारम् (the ruler) / अणोः अणीयांसम् (subtler than the subtle) / अनुस्मरेत् (should remember) / यः (who) / सर्वस्य (of all) / धातारम् (supporter) / अचिन्त्यरूपम् (of inconceivable form) / आदित्यवर्णम् (sun-colored, radiant like the sun) / तमसः (than darkness) / परस्तात् (beyond)
8:9.meaning: Remember the supreme reality as wise, ancient, subtle, sustaining everything, beyond thought, radiant, and beyond darkness.

8:10.shloka: प्रयाणकाले मनसाऽचलेनभक्त्या युक्तो योगबलेन चैव । भ्रुवोर्मध्ये प्राणमावेश्य सम्यक्स तं परं पुरुषमुपैति दिव्यम् ॥
8:10.transliteration: प्रयाणकाले (at the time of departure) / मनसा (with the mind) / अचलेन (unmoving) / भक्त्या (with devotion) / युक्तः (joined) / योगबलेन (by the power of yoga) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / भ्रुवोः (of the two eyebrows) / मध्ये (in the middle) / प्राणम् (the life-force) / आवेश्य (placing) / सम्यक् (properly) / सः (that one) / तम् (that) / परम् (supreme) / पुरुषम् (person) / उपैति (reaches) / दिव्यम् (divine)
8:10.meaning: At death, a devoted person who keeps the mind steady and directs the life-force with yogic focus reaches the divine supreme person.

8:11.shloka: यदक्षरं वेदविदो वदन्तिविशन्ति यद्यतयो वीतरागाः । यदिच्छन्तो ब्रह्मचर्यं चरन्तितत्ते पदं संग्रहेण प्रवक्ष्ये ॥
8:11.transliteration: यत् (that which) / अक्षरम् (imperishable) / वेद-विदः (knowers of the Vedas) / वदन्ति (call) / विशन्ति (enter) / यत् (which) / यतयः (ascetics) / वीत-रागाः (free from desire) / यत् (which) / इच्छन्तः (seeking) / ब्रह्मचर्यम् (celibate discipline) / चरन्ति (practice) / तत् (that) / ते (to you) / पदम् (state) / संग्रहेण (briefly) / प्रवक्ष्ये (I will speak)
8:11.meaning: Krishna says he will briefly describe the imperishable state that Vedic knowers speak of, detached ascetics enter, and disciplined seekers approach through celibate practice.

8:12.shloka: सर्वद्वाराणि संयम्य मनो हृदि निरुध्य च । मूर्ध्न्याधायात्मनः प्राणमास्थितो योगधारणाम् ॥
8:12.transliteration: सर्वद्वाराणि (all doors) / संयम्य (having restrained) / मनः (mind) / हृदि (in the heart) / निरुध्य (having confined) / च (and) / मूर्ध्नि (in the head) / आधाय (having placed) / आत्मनः (of oneself) / प्राणम् (life-force) / आस्थितः (steadfast) / योगधारणाम् (yoga-discipline)
8:12.meaning: At the moment of death, the practitioner shuts the senses, steadies the mind in the heart, and places the life-force at the head through yoga practice.

8:13.shloka: ओमित्येकाक्षरं ब्रह्म व्याहरन्मामनुस्मरन् । यः प्रयाति त्यजन्देहं स याति परमां गतिम् ॥
8:13.transliteration: ॐ (Om) / इति (thus) / एकाक्षरम् (one-syllable) / ब्रह्म (Brahman) / व्याहरन् (uttering) / माम् (Me) / अनुस्मरन् (remembering repeatedly) / यः (who) / प्रयाति (departs) / त्यजन् (leaving) / देहम् (the body) / सः (he) / याति (goes) / परमाम् (supreme) / गतिम् (state, destination)
8:13.meaning: A person who leaves the body while remembering Om and Krishna reaches the highest destination.

8:14.shloka: अनन्यचेताः सततं यो मां स्मरति नित्यशः । तस्याहं सुलभः पार्थ नित्ययुक्तस्य योगिनः ॥
8:14.transliteration: अनन्यचेताः (one-pointed in mind) / सततम् (constantly) / यः (who) / माम् (me) / स्मरति (remembers) / नित्यशः (always) / तस्य (for that one) / अहम् (I) / सुलभः (easy to reach) / पार्थ (Arjuna) / नित्ययुक्तस्य (of the ever-joined) / योगिनः (of the yogi)
8:14.meaning: A mind that stays focused on Krishna and remembers him continually finds him easy to reach.

8:15.shloka: मामुपेत्य पुनर्जन्म दुःखालयमशाश्वतम् । नाप्नुवन्ति महात्मानः संसिद्धिं परमां गताः ॥
8:15.transliteration: माम् (me) / उपेत्य (having attained) / पुनर्जन्म (rebirth) / दुःखालयम् (abode of suffering) / अशाश्वतम् (impermanent) / नाप्नुवन्ति (do not attain) / महात्मानः (great souls) / संसिद्धिम् (perfect fulfillment) / परमाम् (supreme) / गताः (having reached)
8:15.meaning: Those who reach Krishna do not come back into the painful cycle of birth and death. They have reached the highest fulfillment.

8:16.shloka: आब्रह्मभुवनाल्लोकाः पुनरावर्तिनोऽर्जुन । मामुपेत्य तु कौन्तेय पुनर्जन्म न विद्यते ॥
8:16.transliteration: आब्रह्मभुवनात् (up to the world of Brahma) / लोकाः (worlds) / पुनरावर्तिनः (returning again) / अर्जुन (Arjuna) / माम् (me) / उपेत्य (having reached) / तु (but) / कौन्तेय (son of Kunti) / पुनर्जन्म (rebirth) / न (not) / विद्यते (exists)
8:16.meaning: Even the highest realms are temporary and lead back into rebirth. Only reaching Krishna ends that cycle completely.

meanings.meaning: up to the world of Brahma

8:17.shloka: सहस्रयुगपर्यन्तमहर्यद्ब्रह्मणो विदुः । रात्रिं युगसहस्रान्तां तेऽहोरात्रविदो जनाः ॥
8:17.transliteration: सहस्र-युग-पर्यन्तम् (lasting a thousand yugas) / अहः (day) / यत् (which) / ब्रह्मणः (of Brahma) / विदुः (they know) / रात्रिम् (night) / युग-सहस्र-अन्ताम् (ending in a thousand yugas) / ते (they) / अहः-रात्र-विदः (knowers of day and night) / जनाः (people)
8:17.meaning: Some people understand the vast scale of Brahma's day and night. They can see time on a cosmic scale, not just as ordinary human hours.

8:18.shloka: अव्यक्ताद्व्यक्तयः सर्वाः प्रभवन्त्यहरागमे । रात्र्यागमे प्रलीयन्ते तत्रैवाव्यक्तसंज्ञके ॥
8:18.transliteration: अव्यक्तात् (from the unmanifest) / व्यक्तयः (manifest beings) / सर्वाः (all) / प्रभवन्ति (arise) / अहरागमे (at the coming of day) / रात्र्यागमे (at the coming of night) / प्रलीयन्ते (dissolve) / तत्रैव (there alone) / अव्यक्तसंज्ञके (in the one called unmanifest)
8:18.meaning: All beings emerge from the unmanifest at the beginning of Brahma's day and return to it at the beginning of Brahma's night.

8:19.shloka: भूतग्रामः स एवायं भूत्वा भूत्वा प्रलीयते । रात्र्यागमेऽवशः पार्थ प्रभवत्यहरागमे ॥
8:19.transliteration: भूतग्रामः (group of beings) / सः (that same) / एव (indeed) / अयम् (this) / भूत्वा भूत्वा (having become again and again) / प्रलीयते (is dissolved) / रात्र्यागमे (at the coming of night) / अवशः (helplessly, under compulsion) / पार्थ (O Partha) / प्रभवति (arises) / अहरागमे (at the coming of day)
8:19.meaning: All living beings keep arising and dissolving again and again. They do so under the pull of nature, not by choice.

8:20.shloka: परस्तस्मात्तु भावोऽन्योऽव्यक्तोऽव्यक्तात्सनातनः । यः स सर्वेषु भूतेषु नश्यत्सु न विनश्यति ॥
8:20.transliteration: परस्तस्मात् (beyond that) / तु (but) / भावः (state/being) / अन्यः (another) / अव्यक्तः (unmanifest) / अव्यक्तात् (than the unmanifest) / सनातनः (eternal) / यः (which) / सः (that) / सर्वेषु (in all) / भूतेषु (beings) / नश्यत्सु (when perishing) / न (not) / विनश्यति (perishes)
8:20.meaning: There is something beyond the visible world that is eternal and cannot be destroyed, even when all living things disappear.

8:21.shloka: अव्यक्तोऽक्षर इत्युक्तस्तमाहुः परमां गतिम् । यं प्राप्य न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम ॥
8:21.transliteration: अव्यक्तः (unmanifest) / अक्षरः (imperishable) / इति (thus) / उक्तः (called) / तम् (that) / आहुः (they call) / परमाम् (highest) / गतिम् (goal) / यम् (which) / प्राप्य (having attained) / न (not) / निवर्तन्ते (return) / तत् (that) / धाम (abode) / परमम् (supreme) / मम (my)
8:21.meaning: The highest reality is beyond change and form. Whoever reaches it does not come back to the cycle of repeated return.

8:22.shloka: पुरुषः स परः पार्थ भक्त्या लभ्यस्त्वनन्यया । यस्यान्तःस्थानि भूतानि येन सर्वमिदं ततम् ॥
8:22.transliteration: पुरुषः (person) / सः (that) / परः (supreme) / पार्थ (O Partha) / भक्त्या (through devotion) / लभ्यः (is attainable) / तु (indeed) / अनन्यया (undivided) / यस्य (whose) / अन्तःस्थानि (situated within) / भूतानि (beings) / येन (by whom) / सर्वम् (all) / इदम् (this) / ततम् (pervaded)
8:22.meaning: The supreme person is reached through single-pointed devotion. That presence holds all beings within it and fills the whole world.

8:23.shloka: यत्र काले त्वनावृत्तिमावृत्तिं चैव योगिनः । प्रयाता यान्ति तं कालं वक्ष्यामि भरतर्षभ ॥
8:23.transliteration: यत्र (in which) / काले (time) / तु (indeed) / अनावृत्तिम् (non-return) / आवृत्तिम् (return) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / योगिनः (yogis) / प्रयाताः (having departed) / यान्ति (go) / तम् (that) / कालम् (time) / वक्ष्यामि (I will speak) / भरतर्षभ (O best of the Bharatas)
8:23.meaning: Krishna says he will explain which departing moments lead to no return and which lead back into return.

8:24.shloka: अग्निर्ज्योतिरहः शुक्लः षण्मासा उत्तरायणम् । तत्र प्रयाता गच्छन्ति ब्रह्म ब्रह्मविदो जनाः ॥
8:24.transliteration: अग्निः (fire) / ज्योतिः (light) / अहः (day) / शुक्लः (bright) / षण्मासा (six months) / उत्तरायणम् (northward course) / तत्र (there) / प्रयाताः (having departed) / गच्छन्ति (go) / ब्रह्म (the supreme reality) / ब्रह्मविदः (knowers of the supreme reality) / जनाः (people)
8:24.meaning: Those who leave the body during an auspicious path of light and know the supreme reality reach that supreme reality.

8:25.shloka: धूमो रात्रिस्तथा कृष्णः षण्मासा दक्षिणायनम् । तत्र चान्द्रमसं ज्योतिर्योगी प्राप्य निवर्तते ॥
8:25.transliteration: धूमः (smoke) / रात्रिः (night) / तथा (and) / कृष्णः (the dark fortnight) / षण्मासा (six months) / दक्षिणायनम् (the southern course) / तत्र (there) / चान्द्रमसम् (lunar) / ज्योतिः (light) / योगी (the yogi) / प्राप्य (having reached) / निवर्तते (returns)
8:25.meaning: A person who departs through this darker path reaches a lunar realm and returns again to mortal life.

8:26.shloka: शुक्लकृष्णे गती ह्येते जगतः शाश्वते मते । एकया यात्यनावृत्तिमन्ययाऽऽवर्तते पुनः ॥
8:26.transliteration: शुक्लकृष्णे (the bright and the dark) / गती (paths) / हि (indeed) / एते (these) / जगतः (of the world) / शाश्वते (eternal) / मते (are held to be) । / एकया (by one) / याति (goes) / अनावृत्तिम् (to non-return) / अन्यया (by the other) / आवर्तते (returns) / पुनः (again)
8:26.meaning: Two eternal paths exist. One leads beyond repeated return, and the other brings a person back again.

8:27.shloka: नैते सृती पार्थ जानन्योगी मुह्यति कश्चन । तस्मात्सर्वेषु कालेषु योगयुक्तो भवार्जुन ॥
8:27.transliteration: नैते (these not) / सृती (two paths) / पार्थ (O Partha) / जानन् (knowing) / योगी (the yogi) / मुह्यति (is deluded) / कश्चन (anyone) / तस्मात् (therefore) / सर्वेषु (in all) / कालेषु (times) / योगयुक्तः (joined in yoga) / भव (be) / अर्जुन (O Arjuna)
8:27.meaning: A person who understands these two routes after death is not confused. Krishna tells Arjuna to stay connected to yoga at all times.

8:28.shloka: वेदेषु यज्ञेषु तपःसु चैवदानेषु यत्पुण्यफलं प्रदिष्टम् । अत्येति तत्सर्वमिदं विदित्वायोगी परं स्थानमुपैति चाद्यम् ॥
8:28.transliteration: वेदेषु (in the Vedas) / यज्ञेषु (in sacrifices) / तपःसु (in austerities) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / दानेषु (in gifts) / यत् (which) / पुण्यफलम् (merit-fruit) / प्रदिष्टम् (is declared) / अत्येति (goes beyond) / तत् (that) / सर्वम् (all) / इदम् (this) / विदित्वा (having known) / योगी (the yogi) / परम् (supreme) / स्थानम् (abode) / उपैति (attains) / च (and) / आद्यम् (primordial)
8:28.meaning: Knowing the two paths frees a yogi from chasing ritual rewards. That understanding leads beyond all promised merit and into the highest state.

9:1.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचइदं तु ते गुह्यतमं प्रवक्ष्याम्यनसूयवे । ज्ञानं विज्ञानसहितं यज्ज्ञात्वा मोक्ष्यसेऽशुभात् ॥
9:1.transliteration: श्री भगवान् (the Blessed Lord) / उवाच (said) / इदम् (this) / तु (indeed) / ते (to you) / गुह्यतमम् (most secret) / प्रवक्ष्यामि (I will explain) / अनसूयवे (to one without envy) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / विज्ञानसहितम् (together with direct realization) / यत् (which) / ज्ञात्वा (having known) / मोक्ष्यसे (you will be freed) / अशुभात् (from what is harmful)
9:1.meaning: Krishna promises to share the deepest teaching with Arjuna because Arjuna is not hostile to it. Understanding this knowledge fully will free him from suffering and the cycle of rebirth.

9:2.shloka: राजविद्या राजगुह्यं पवित्रमिदमुत्तमम् । प्रत्यक्षावगमं धर्म्यं सुसुखं कर्तुमव्ययम् ॥
9:2.transliteration: राजविद्या (king of knowledge) / राजगुह्यं (king of secrets) / पवित्रम् (pure) / इदम् (this) / उत्तमम् (supreme) / प्रत्यक्ष-अवगमम् (directly knowable) / धर्म्यम् (aligned with dharma) / सुसुखम् (very easy) / कर्तुम् (to do) / अव्ययम् (imperishable)
9:2.meaning: This teaching is called the highest knowledge because it is pure, direct, righteous, easy to practice, and never lost.

9:3.shloka: अश्रद्दधानाः पुरुषा धर्मस्यास्य परन्तप । अप्राप्य मां निवर्तन्ते मृत्युसंसारवर्त्मनि ॥
9:3.transliteration: अश्रद्दधानाः (without trust) / पुरुषाः (people) / धर्मस्य (of dharma) / अस्य (of this) / परन्तप (O conqueror of foes) / अप्राप्य (not reaching) / माम् (Me) / निवर्तन्ते (return) / मृत्युसंसारवर्त्मनि (on the path of the cycle of death and rebirth)
9:3.meaning: People who lack trust in this sacred teaching do not reach Krishna and remain trapped in repeated birth and death.

meanings.meaning: on the path of the cycle of death and rebirth

9:4.shloka: मया ततमिदं सर्वं जगदव्यक्तमूर्तिना । मत्स्थानि सर्वभूतानि न चाहं तेष्ववस्थितः ॥
9:4.transliteration: मया (by me) / ततम् (pervaded) / इदम् (this) / सर्वम् (all) / जगत् (world) / अव्यक्त-मूर्तिना (by unmanifest form) / मत्-स्थानि (situated in me) / सर्व-भूतानि (all beings) / न (not) / च (and) / अहम् (I) / तेषु (in them) / अवस्थितः (situated)
9:4.meaning: Krishna says the whole universe is sustained by his unseen presence. Every being exists within him, yet he is not limited by any being.

9:5.shloka: न च मत्स्थानि भूतानि पश्य मे योगमैश्वरम् । भूतभृन्न च भूतस्थो ममात्मा भूतभावनः ॥
9:5.transliteration: न / च (not) / मत्स्थानि (situated in Me) / भूतानि (beings) / पश्य (behold) / मे (My) / योगम् (powerful union) / ऐश्वरम् (divine, sovereign) / भूतभृत् (sustainer of beings) / न (not) / च (and not) / भूतस्थः (situated in beings) / मम (My) / आत्मा (self) / भूतभावनः (the source and nourisher of beings)
9:5.meaning: Krishna says that all beings are sustained by him, yet he is not contained within them. He asks Arjuna to see this divine power.

meanings.meaning: the source and nourisher of beings

9:6.shloka: यथाऽऽकाशस्थितो नित्यं वायुः सर्वत्रगो महान् । तथा सर्वाणि भूतानि मत्स्थानीत्युपधारय ॥
9:6.transliteration: यथा (just as) / आकाश-स्थितः (स्थित in space) / नित्यम् (always) / वायुः (wind) / सर्वत्र-गः (moving everywhere) / महान् (great) / तथा (so) / सर्वाणि (all) / भूतानि (beings) / मत्-स्थानि (resting in Me) / इति (thus) / उपधारय (understand)
9:6.meaning: All living beings exist within Krishna just as wind moves within space. The verse asks Arjuna to hold this vision of divine presence everywhere.

9:7.shloka: सर्वभूतानि कौन्तेय प्रकृतिं यान्ति मामिकाम् । कल्पक्षये पुनस्तानि कल्पादौ विसृजाम्यहम् ॥
9:7.transliteration: सर्वभूतानि (all beings) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / प्रकृतिं (nature) / यान्ति (go to) / मामिकाम् (my) / कल्पक्षये (at the end of a cosmic cycle) / पुनः (again) / तानि (them) / कल्पादौ (at the beginning of a cosmic cycle) / विसृजामि (I send forth) / अहम् (I)
9:7.meaning: At the end of a vast cycle, every being dissolves back into Krishna's nature. When the next cycle begins, he brings them forth again.

meanings.meaning: at the end of a cosmic cycle
at the beginning of a cosmic cycle

9:8.shloka: प्रकृतिं स्वामवष्टभ्य विसृजामि पुनः पुनः । भूतग्राममिमं कृत्स्नमवशं प्रकृतेर्वशात् ॥
9:8.transliteration: प्रकृतिम् (nature) / स्वाम् (my own) / अवष्टभ्य (taking hold of) / विसृजामि (I create, send forth) / पुनः पुनः (again and again) / भूतग्रामम् (multitude of beings) / इमम् (this) / कृत्स्नम् (entire) / अवशम् (helpless, not independent) / प्रकृतेः (of nature) / वशात् (under the sway)
9:8.meaning: Krishna says he keeps bringing forth the whole world again and again through his own nature, while beings remain bound by nature’s power.

9:9.shloka: न च मां तानि कर्माणि निबध्नन्ति धनञ्जय । उदासीनवदासीनमसक्तं तेषु कर्मसु ॥
9:9.transliteration: न च (and not) / माम् (me) / तानि (those) / कर्माणि (actions) / निबध्नन्ति (bind) / धनञ्जय (Dhananjaya) / उदासीनवत् (like one indifferent) / आसीनम् (remaining) / असक्तम् (unattached) / तेषु (in those) / कर्मसु (actions)
9:9.meaning: Creation and world-order do not bind Krishna, because he remains unattached and unconcerned while they happen.

9:10.shloka: मयाऽध्यक्षेण प्रकृतिः सूयते सचराचरम् । हेतुनाऽनेन कौन्तेय जगद्विपरिवर्तते ॥
9:10.transliteration: मया (by me) / अध्यक्षेण (as overseer) / प्रकृतिः (nature) / सूयते (gives birth, brings forth) / सचराचरम् (the moving and the unmoving) / हेतुना (because, by reason) / अनेन (of this) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / जगत् (the world) / विपरिवर्तते (keeps turning, keeps changing)
9:10.meaning: All changing life unfolds under Krishna's supervision. The world keeps transforming because nature works through his oversight.

9:11.shloka: अवजानन्ति मां मूढा मानुषीं तनुमाश्रितम् । परं भावमजानन्तो मम भूतमहेश्वरम् ॥
9:11.transliteration: अवजानन्ति (despise) / माम् (Me) / मूढाः (the deluded) / मानुषीम् (human) / तनुम् (form) / आश्रितम् (having taken refuge in) / परम् (higher) / भावम् (nature) / अजानन्तः (not knowing) / मम (My) / भूतमहेश्वरम् (great Lord of beings)
9:11.meaning: People mock Krishna because they see only a human body and miss his higher nature as the lord of all beings.

9:12.shloka: मोघाशा मोघकर्माणो मोघज्ञाना विचेतसः । राक्षसीमासुरीं चैव प्रकृतिं मोहिनीं श्रिताः ॥
9:12.transliteration: मोघाशाः (wasted hopes) / मोघकर्माणः (wasted actions) / मोघज्ञाः (wasted understanding) / विचेतसः (confused-minded) / राक्षसीम् (demonic) / आसुरीम् (of ego and shadow) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / प्रकृतिम् (nature) / मोहिनीम् (deluding) / श्रिताः (having taken refuge in)
9:12.meaning: Confused people end up leaning on forces that distort their judgment. Their hopes, actions, and understanding do not bear fruit because they are guided by delusion.

9:13.shloka: महात्मानस्तु मां पार्थ दैवीं प्रकृतिमाश्रिताः । भजन्त्यनन्यमनसो ज्ञात्वा भूतादिमव्ययम् ॥
9:13.transliteration: महात्मानः (great-minded ones) / तु (but) / माम् (Me) / पार्थ (Arjuna) / दैवीं प्रकृतिम् (divine nature) / आश्रिताः (having taken refuge in) / भजन्ति (worship) / अनन्य-मनसः (with minds fixed on nothing else) / ज्ञात्वा (having known) / भूत-आदिम् (the source of beings) / अव्ययम् (imperishable)
9:13.meaning: Noble people rely on the divine nature, stay focused on Krishna alone, and worship him as the unborn, imperishable source of all beings.

meanings.meaning: with minds fixed on nothing else

9:14.shloka: सततं कीर्तयन्तो मां यतन्तश्च दृढव्रताः । नमस्यन्तश्च मां भक्त्या नित्ययुक्ता उपासते ॥
9:14.transliteration: सततं (continuously) / कीर्तयन्तः (chanting) / मां (Me) / यतन्तः (striving) / च (and) / दृढव्रताः (steadfast in vows) / नमस्यन्तः (bowing) / च (and) / मां (Me) / भक्त्या (with devotion) / नित्ययुक्ताः (always united) / उपासते (worship)
9:14.meaning: Devoted people keep chanting, bowing, and striving with steady commitment, always focused on Krishna.

9:15.shloka: ज्ञानयज्ञेन चाप्यन्ये यजन्तो मामुपासते । एकत्वेन पृथक्त्वेन बहुधा विश्वतोमुखम् ॥
9:15.transliteration: ज्ञानयज्ञेन (through the sacrifice of knowledge) / च (and) / अपि (also) / अन्ये (others) / यजन्तः (worshipping) / माम् (Me) / उपासते (they worship) / एकत्वेन (through oneness) / पृथक्त्वेन (through separateness) / बहुधा (in many ways) / विश्वतोमुखम् (the all-facing one)
9:15.meaning: Some people worship Krishna through understanding. They see him as one reality, as separate forms, or as the universal form facing everywhere.

9:16.shloka: अहं क्रतुरहं यज्ञः स्वधाऽहमहमौषधम् । मंत्रोऽहमहमेवाज्यमहमग्निरहं हुतम् ॥
9:16.transliteration: अहम् (I am) / क्रतुः (ritual sacrifice) / अहम् (I am) / यज्ञः (sacrifice) / स्वधा (offering to ancestors) / अहम् (I am) / अहम् (I am) / औषधम् (herb) / मंत्रः (mantra) / अहम् (I am) / अहम् (I am) / एव (indeed) / आज्यम् (ghee) / अहम् (I am) / अग्निः (fire) / अहम् (I am) / हुतम् (the offering cast into the fire)
9:16.meaning: Krishna says he is every part of sacred ritual: the offering, the chant, the fire, and the act itself. He is also the sacred sound, the Vedas, and the sustaining source of the world.

meanings.meaning: the offering cast into the fire

9:17.shloka: पिताऽहमस्य जगतो माता धाता पितामहः । वेद्यं पवित्रमोंकार ऋक् साम यजुरेव च ॥
9:17.transliteration: पिता (father) / अहम् (I am) / अस्य (of this) / जगतः (world) / माता (mother) / धाता (supporter) / पितामहः (grandfather) / वेद्यम् (to be known) / पवित्रम् (purifier / pure) / ओंकार (sacred syllable Om) / ऋक् (Rig) / साम (Sama) / यजुः (Yajur) / एव (indeed) / च (and)
9:17.meaning: Krishna says he is the world’s father, mother, supporter, and ancestor. He is also the sacred sound Om and the Vedas themselves.

9:18.shloka: गतिर्भर्ता प्रभुः साक्षी निवासः शरणं सुहृत् । प्रभवः प्रलयः स्थानं निधानं बीजमव्ययम् ॥
9:18.transliteration: गतिः (goal) / भर्ता (supporter) / प्रभुः (lord) / साक्षी (witness) / निवासः (dwelling) / शरणम् (refuge) / सुहृत् (well-wisher) / प्रभवः (source) / प्रलयः (dissolution) / स्थानम् (resting place) / निधानम् (treasure) / बीजम् (seed) / अव्ययम् (imperishable)
9:18.meaning: Krishna says he is everything that supports, witnesses, shelters, begins, ends, and sustains existence.

9:19.shloka: तपाम्यहमहं वर्षं निगृह्णाम्युत्सृजामि च । अमृतं चैव मृत्युश्च सदसच्चाहमर्जुन ॥
9:19.transliteration: तपामि (I heat) / अहम् (I) / अहम् (I) / वर्षम् (rain) / निगृह्णामि (I hold back) / उत्सृजामि (I release) / च (and) / अमृतम् (immortality) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / मृत्युः (death) / च (and) / सत् (being) / असत् (non-being) / च (and) / अहम् (I am) / अर्जुन (Arjuna)
9:19.meaning: Krishna says he is the force behind sunlight, rain, immortality, death, existence, and non-existence. Everything that sustains and changes the world moves through him.

9:20.shloka: त्रैविद्या मां सोमपाः पूतपापायज्ञैरिष्ट्वा स्वर्गतिं प्रार्थयन्ते । ते पुण्यमासाद्य सुरेन्द्रलोकमश्नन्ति दिव्यान्दिवि देवभोगान् ॥
9:20.transliteration: त्रैविद्या (knowers of the three Vedas) / मां (me) / सोमपाः (drinkers of soma) / पूतपापाः (purified from sin) / यज्ञैः (by sacrifices) / इष्ट्वा (having worshipped) / स्वर्गतिम् (heavenly attainment) / प्रार्थयन्ते (they pray for) / ते (they) / पुण्यम् (merit) / आसाद्य (having attained) / सुरेन्द्रलोकम् (realm of the lord of the radiant beings) / अश्नन्ति (they enjoy) / दिव्यान् (divine) / दिवि (in heaven) / देवभोगान् (pleasures of the radiant beings)
9:20.meaning: People who perform Vedic sacrifices and seek reward pray for heaven. They gain merit, reach the heavenly realm, and enjoy its pleasures for a time.

meanings.meaning: realm of the lord of the radiant beings

9:21.shloka: ते तं भुक्त्वा स्वर्गलोकं विशालंक्षीणे पुण्ये मर्त्यलोकं विशन्ति । एव त्रयीधर्ममनुप्रपन्नागतागतं कामकामा लभन्ते ॥
9:21.transliteration: ते (they) / तम् (that) / भुक्त्वा (having enjoyed) / स्वर्गलोकम् (heaven-world) / विशालम् (vast) / क्षीणे (having become exhausted) / पुण्ये (merit) / मर्त्यलोकम् (mortal world) / विशन्ति (enter) / एवम् (thus) / त्रयीधर्मम् (the three-Veda ritual dharma) / अनुप्रपन्नाः (having resorted to) / गतागतम् (coming and going) / कामकामा (desiring pleasures) / लभन्ते (obtain)
9:21.meaning: People who seek heavenly rewards through ritual enjoy those rewards for a while, but when their merit is used up, they return to ordinary life and the cycle continues.

9:22.shloka: अनन्याश्चिन्तयन्तो मां ये जनाः पर्युपासते । तेषां नित्याभियुक्तानां योगक्षेमं वहाम्यहम् ॥
9:22.transliteration: अनन्याः (with no other focus) / चिन्तयन्तः (thinking) / माम् (me) / ये (those who) / जनाः (people) / पर्युपासते (worship all around) / तेषाम् (for them) / नित्य-अभियुक्तानाम् (for those always joined) / योग-क्षेमम् (welfare and security) / वहामि (I carry) / अहम् (I)
9:22.meaning: People who focus on Krishna alone and worship him steadily do not have to carry every burden by themselves. He takes responsibility for their well-being and security.

9:23.shloka: येऽप्यन्यदेवता भक्ता यजन्ते श्रद्धयाऽन्विताः । तेऽपि मामेव कौन्तेय यजन्त्यविधिपूर्वकम् ॥
9:23.transliteration: येऽपि (even those who) / अन्यदेवता-भक्ताः (devotees of other radiant beings) / यजन्ते (worship) / श्रद्धया अन्विताः (endowed with trust) / तेऽपि (they also) / माम् एव (Me alone) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / यजन्ति (worship) / अविधिपूर्वकम् (in an improper way)
9:23.meaning: People who worship other radiant beings with trust are still, in a deeper sense, worshipping Krishna, but without the full understanding of how worship truly works.

9:24.shloka: अहं हि सर्वयज्ञानां भोक्ता च प्रभुरेव च । न तु मामभिजानन्ति तत्त्वेनातश्च्यवन्ति ते ॥
9:24.transliteration: अहम् (I) / हि (indeed) / सर्वयज्ञानाम् (of all sacrifices) / भोक्ता (the enjoyer) / च (and) / प्रभुः (the lord) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / न (not) / तु (but) / माम् (me) / अभिजानन्ति (they know) / तत्त्वेन (in truth) / अतः (therefore) / च्यवन्ति (fall away) / ते (they)
9:24.meaning: All sacrifices ultimately belong to Krishna, who receives them and rules over them. People fall when they do not know this truth clearly.

9:25.shloka: यान्ति देवव्रता देवान् पितृ़न्यान्ति पितृव्रताः । भूतानि यान्ति भूतेज्या यान्ति मद्याजिनोऽपि माम् ॥
9:25.transliteration: यान्ति (go/reach) / देवव्रताः (those devoted to radiant beings) / देवान् (the radiant beings) / पितॄन् (the ancestors) / यान्ति (go/reach) / पितृव्रताः (those devoted to the ancestors) / भूतानि (spirits/beings) / यान्ति (go/reach) / भूतेज्याः (those who worship spirits) / यान्ति (go/reach) / मद्याजिनः (those who worship me) / अपि (also) / माम् (me)
9:25.meaning: People reach the object they devote themselves to. Worship directed toward Krishna leads to Krishna; other devotions lead elsewhere.

9:26.shloka: पत्रं पुष्पं फलं तोयं यो मे भक्त्या प्रयच्छति । तदहं भक्त्युपहृतमश्नामि प्रयतात्मनः ॥
9:26.transliteration: पत्रम् (leaf) / पुष्पम् (flower) / फलम् (fruit) / तोयम् (water) / यः (who) / मे (to me) / भक्त्या (with devotion) / प्रयच्छति (offers) / तत् (that) / अहम् (I) / भक्त्युपहृतम् (devotion-offered) / अश्नामि (I accept / consume) / प्रयतात्मनः (of the pure-hearted person)
9:26.meaning: Even the smallest offering becomes meaningful when it is given with devotion and a sincere heart.

9:27.shloka: यत्करोषि यदश्नासि यज्जुहोषि ददासि यत् । यत्तपस्यसि कौन्तेय तत्कुरुष्व मदर्पणम् ॥
9:27.transliteration: यत्करोषि (whatever you do) / यदश्नासि (whatever you eat) / यज्जुहोषि (whatever you offer in sacrifice) / ददासि (you give) / यत् (whatever) / यत्तपस्यसि (whatever austerity you practice) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / तत्कुरुष्व (do that) / मदर्पणम् (as an offering to me)
9:27.meaning: Whatever you do in daily life can become an offering. Work, food, giving, sacrifice, and discipline all gain meaning when dedicated to the divine.

9:28.shloka: शुभाशुभफलैरेवं मोक्ष्यसे कर्मबन्धनैः । संन्यासयोगयुक्तात्मा विमुक्तो मामुपैष्यसि ॥
9:28.transliteration: शुभाशुभफलैः (by auspicious and inauspicious results) / एवम् (thus) / मोक्ष्यसे (you will be freed) / कर्मबन्धनैः (from the bonds of action) / संन्यासयोगयुक्तात्मा (one whose being is joined to renunciation-yoga) / विमुक्तः (released) / माम् (to me) / उपैष्यसि (you will come)
9:28.meaning: Offer every action and its results to the divine, and you become free from the bondage created by action.

meanings.meaning: one whose being is joined to renunciation-yoga

9:29.shloka: समोऽहं सर्वभूतेषु न मे द्वेष्योऽस्ति न प्रियः । ये भजन्ति तु मां भक्त्या मयि ते तेषु चाप्यहम् ॥
9:29.transliteration: समः (equal) / अहम् (I) / सर्वभूतेषु (toward all beings) / न (not) / मे (to me) / द्वेष्यः (hated one) / अस्ति (is) / न (not) / प्रियः (dear one) / ये (those who) / भजन्ति (worship) / तु (but) / माम् (me) / भक्त्या (with devotion) / मयि (in me) / ते (they) / तेषु (in them) / च (and) / अपि (also) / अहम् (I)
9:29.meaning: The divine is not partial or biased. Everyone is met equally, but loving devotion creates a living closeness between the divine and the devotee.

9:30.shloka: अपि चेत्सुदुराचारो भजते मामनन्यभाक् । साधुरेव स मन्तव्यः सम्यग्व्यवसितो हि सः ॥
9:30.transliteration: अपि (even) / चेत् (if) / सुदुराचारः (one of very bad conduct) / भजते (worships) / माम् (Me) / अनन्यभाक् (with undivided devotion) / साधुः (righteous) / एव (indeed) / सः (that person) / मन्तव्यः (to be regarded) / सम्यक् (properly) / व्यवसितः (resolved) / हि (indeed) / सः (that person)
9:30.meaning: Even someone with a very bad past is still worthy if that person turns to the divine with one-pointed devotion.

9:31.shloka: क्षिप्रं भवति धर्मात्मा शश्वच्छान्तिं निगच्छति । कौन्तेय प्रतिजानीहि न मे भक्तः प्रणश्यति ॥
9:31.transliteration: क्षिप्रम् (quickly) / भवति (becomes) / धर्मात्मा (righteous in nature) / शश्वत् (everlasting) / शान्तिम् (peace) / निगच्छति (attains) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / प्रतिजानीहि (declare boldly) / न (not) / मे (My) / भक्तः (devotee) / प्रणश्यति (perishes)
9:31.meaning: A devoted person quickly becomes upright and finds lasting peace. Krishna says his devotee will not come to ruin.

9:32.shloka: मां हि पार्थ व्यपाश्रित्य येऽपि स्युः पापयोनयः । स्त्रियो वैश्यास्तथा शूद्रास्तेऽपि यान्ति परां गतिम् ॥
9:32.transliteration: माम् (me) / हि (indeed) / पार्थ (O Partha) / व्यपाश्रित्य (having taken refuge) / ये (those who) / अपि (even) / स्युः (may be) / पापयोनयः (born in difficult circumstances) / स्त्रियः (women) / वैश्याः (merchants) / तथा (and) / शूद्राः (workers) / ते (they) / अपि (also) / यान्ति (go) / पराम् (highest) / गतिम् (state)
9:32.meaning: Anyone, regardless of birth or social position, can reach the highest state by taking refuge in Krishna.

9:33.shloka: किं पुनर्ब्राह्मणाः पुण्या भक्ता राजर्षयस्तथा । अनित्यमसुखं लोकमिमं प्राप्य भजस्व माम् ॥
9:33.transliteration: किं (what?) / पुनः (again) / ब्राह्मणाः (Brahmins) / पुण्याः (pure, meritorious) / भक्ताः (devotees) / राजर्षयः (royal sages) / तथा (also) / अनित्यम् (impermanent) / असुखम् (joyless) / लोकम् (world) / इमम् (this) / प्राप्य (having attained) / भजस्व (worship) / माम् (Me)
9:33.meaning: Even the most virtuous devotees and wise rulers can reach the highest state through devotion. Since this world is temporary and unsatisfying, turn toward Krishna.

9:34.shloka: मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु । मामेवैष्यसि युक्त्वैवमात्मानं मत्परायणः ॥
9:34.transliteration: मत्-मनाः (mind on me) / भव (become) / मत्-भक्तः (my devotee) / मत्-याजी (my worshipper) / माम् (to me) / नमस्कुरु (bow) / माम् (me) / एव (only) / एष्यसि (you will come) / युक्त्वा (having united) / एवम् (thus) / आत्मानम् (yourself) / मत्-परायणः (with me as your highest goal)
9:34.meaning: Krishna asks Arjuna to place his mind, devotion, worship, and respect entirely on him. He promises that this full orientation will lead Arjuna to him.

meanings.meaning: with me as your highest goal

10:1.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचभूय एव महाबाहो श्रृणु मे परमं वचः । यत्तेऽहं प्रीयमाणाय वक्ष्यामि हितकाम्यया ॥
10:1.transliteration: श्रीभगवानुवाच (the Blessed Lord said) / भूयः (again) / एव (indeed) / महाबाहो (O mighty-armed one) / श्रृणु (listen) / मे (to my) / परमम् (supreme) / वचः (word) / यत् (which) / ते (to you) / अहम् (I) / प्रीयमाणाय (to one who is dear) / वक्ष्यामि (will speak) / हितकाम्यया (with desire for your welfare)
10:1.meaning: Krishna asks Arjuna to listen again to a deeper teaching, spoken for Arjuna’s good because he is dear to him.

10:2.shloka: न मे विदुः सुरगणाः प्रभवं न महर्षयः । अहमादिर्हि देवानां महर्षीणां च सर्वशः ॥
10:2.transliteration: न (not) / मे (my) / विदुः (know) / सुरगणाः (hosts of devas) / प्रभवम् (origin) / न (not) / महर्षयः (great sages) / अहम (I) / आदिः (beginning) / हि (indeed) / देवानाम् (of devas) / महर्षीणाम् (of great sages) / च (and) / सर्वशः (in every way)
10:2.meaning: Even the devas and great sages do not know Krishna’s full source, because he is their own beginning.

10:3.shloka: यो मामजमनादिं च वेत्ति लोकमहेश्वरम् । असम्मूढः स मर्त्येषु सर्वपापैः प्रमुच्यते ॥
10:3.transliteration: यः (one who) / माम् (Me) / अजम् (unborn) / अनादिम् (beginningless) / च (and) / वेत्ति (knows) / लोकमहेश्वरम् (the great lord of all worlds) / असम्मूढः (undeluded) / सः (that one) / मर्त्येषु (among mortals) / सर्वपापैः (from all wrong deeds) / प्रमुच्यते (is freed)
10:3.meaning: Knowing Krishna as unborn, beginningless, and the ruler of all worlds removes confusion and frees a person from wrongdoing.

meanings.meaning: the great lord of all worlds

10:4.shloka: बुद्धिर्ज्ञानमसंमोहः क्षमा सत्यं दमः शमः । सुखं दुःखं भवोऽभावो भयं चाभयमेव च ॥
10:4.transliteration: बुद्धिः (understanding) / ज्ञानम् (wisdom) / असंमोहः (freedom from confusion) / क्षमा (forgiveness) / सत्यम् (truth) / दमः (self-restraint) / शमः (calmness) / सुखम् (pleasure) / दुःखम् (pain) / भवः (coming into being) / अभावः (ceasing to be) / भयम् (fear) / च (and) / अभयम् (fearlessness) / एव (indeed) / च (and)
10:4.meaning: Krishna says that every good and difficult human quality comes from him alone, including wisdom, restraint, joy, fear, courage, and even changes in existence itself.

10:5.shloka: अहिंसा समता तुष्टिस्तपो दानं यशोऽयशः । भवन्ति भावा भूतानां मत्त एव पृथग्विधाः ॥
10:5.transliteration: अहिंसा (nonviolence) / समता (equanimity) / तुष्टिः (contentment) / तपः (austerity) / दानम् (charity) / यशः (honor) / अयशः (dishonor) / भवन्ति (they become) / भावाḥ (states) / भूतानाम् (of beings) / मत् (from Me) / एव (only) / पृथग्विधाः (of different kinds)
10:5.meaning: Nonviolence, balance, contentment, discipline, generosity, honor, and dishonor all arise from Krishna alone in different beings.

10:6.shloka: महर्षयः सप्त पूर्वे चत्वारो मनवस्तथा । मद्भावा मानसा जाता येषां लोक इमाः प्रजाः ॥
10:6.transliteration: महर्षयः (great sages) / सप्त (seven) / पूर्वे (earlier) / चत्वारः (four) / मनवः (Manus) / तथा (and) / मद्भावाः (born from my being) / मानसा (from the mind) / जाताः (born) / येषाम् (from whom) / लोकाः (worlds) / इमाः (these) / प्रजाः (people)
10:6.meaning: Krishna says that the great sages, the ancient sages, and the Manus all arose from him, and all beings in these worlds descend through them.

10:7.shloka: एतां विभूतिं योगं च मम यो वेत्ति तत्त्वतः । सोऽविकम्पेन योगेन युज्यते नात्र संशयः ॥
10:7.transliteration: एताम् (this) / विभूतिम् (glory, manifestation) / योगम् (yoga, power of union) / च (and) / मम (my) / यः (who) / वेत्ति (knows) / तत्त्वतः (in truth) / सः (that one) / अविकम्पेन (unshaken) / योगेन (by yoga) / युज्यते (is joined) / न (not) / अत्र (here) / संशयः (doubt)
10:7.meaning: Knowing Krishna's divine manifestations deeply makes devotion steady and unshakable. Real understanding removes doubt and draws the mind into firm union.

10:8.shloka: अहं सर्वस्य प्रभवो मत्तः सर्वं प्रवर्तते । इति मत्वा भजन्ते मां बुधा भावसमन्विताः ॥
10:8.transliteration: अहम् (I am) / सर्वस्य (of all) / प्रभवः (the source) / मत्तः (from me) / सर्वम् (everything) / प्रवर्तते (proceeds, moves forth) / इति (thus) / मत्वा (having known) / भजन्ते (worship) / माम् (me) / बुधाः (the wise) / भावसमन्विताः (endowed with devotion, feeling, wholeheartedness)
10:8.meaning: Krishna says he is the source of everything, and wise people respond with devoted worship and full-hearted love.

10:9.shloka: मच्चित्ता मद्गतप्राणा बोधयन्तः परस्परम् । कथयन्तश्च मां नित्यं तुष्यन्ति च रमन्ति च ॥
10:9.transliteration: मच्चित्ता (with minds on Me) / मद्गतप्राणा (with lives directed toward Me) / बोधयन्तः (instructing, enlightening) / परस्परम् (one another) / कथयन्तः (speaking, telling) / च (and) / माम् (Me) / नित्यम् (always) / तुष्यन्ति (they are satisfied) / च (and) / रमन्ति (they delight) / च (and)
10:9.meaning: People devoted to Krishna keep their minds and lives focused on him. They talk about him together, and that shared remembrance brings them lasting joy.

10:10.shloka: तेषां सततयुक्तानां भजतां प्रीतिपूर्वकम् । ददामि बुद्धियोगं तं येन मामुपयान्ति ते ॥
10:10.transliteration: तेषां (to them) / सततयुक्तानां (of those always devoted) / भजतां (of those worshipping) / प्रीतिपूर्वकम् (with love) / ददामि (I give) / बुद्धियोगं (the yoga of discernment) / तं (that) / येन (by which) / माम् (Me) / उपयान्ति (attain) / ते (they)
10:10.meaning: Those who keep turning toward Krishna with steady love receive the discernment that leads them to him.

10:11.transliteration: तेषाम् (of them) / एव (indeed) / अनुकम्पा-अर्थम् (for compassion) / अहम् (I) / अज्ञान-जम् (born of ignorance) / तमः (darkness) / नाशयामि (I destroy) / आत्म-भाव-स्थः (abiding in their own being) / ज्ञान-दीपेन (with the lamp of knowledge) / भास्वता (shining)
10:11.meaning: Out of compassion, Krishna removes the darkness of ignorance from devoted people by giving them the light of understanding from within.

10:12.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचपरं ब्रह्म परं धाम पवित्रं परमं भवान् । पुरुषं शाश्वतं दिव्यमादिदेवमजं विभुम् ॥
10:12.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / परं (supreme) / ब्रह्म (supreme reality) / परं (highest) / धाम (abode/refuge) / पवित्रं (pure) / परमं (highest) / भवान् (you are) / पुरुषं (person) / शाश्वतं (eternal) / दिव्यम् (divine) / आदिदेवम् (first being) / अजम् (unborn) / विभुम् (all-pervading)
10:12.meaning: Arjuna recognises Krishna as the highest reality, the purest refuge, eternal, unborn, and present everywhere.

10:13.shloka: आहुस्त्वामृषयः सर्वे देवर्षिर्नारदस्तथा । असितो देवलो व्यासः स्वयं चैव ब्रवीषि मे ॥
10:13.transliteration: आहुः (they say) / त्वाम् (you) / ऋषयः (the sages) / सर्वे (all) / देवर्षिः (the divine sage) / नारदः (Narada) / तथा (and also) / असितः (Asita) / देवलः (Devala) / व्यासः (Vyasa) / स्वयम् (yourself) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / ब्रवीषि (you speak) / मे (to me)
10:13.meaning: Arjuna says that all the great sages, including Narada, Asita, Devala, and Vyasa, speak of Krishna this way, and Krishna himself says the same to him.

10:14.shloka: सर्वमेतदृतं मन्ये यन्मां वदसि केशव । न हि ते भगवन् व्यक्ितं विदुर्देवा न दानवाः ॥
10:14.transliteration: सर्वम् (all) / एतत् (this) / ऋतम् (true) / मन्ये (I consider) / यत् (what) / माम् (to me) / वदसि (you speak) / केशव (O Keshava) / न (not) / हि (indeed) / ते (your) / भगवन् (O Bhagavan) / व्यक्ितम् (manifested form) / विदुः (know) / देवाः (the devas) / न (not) / दानवाः (the asuras)
10:14.meaning: Arjuna accepts Krishna's words as true and says that even radiant beings and shadow beings do not understand Krishna's revealed form.

10:15.shloka: स्वयमेवात्मनाऽत्मानं वेत्थ त्वं पुरुषोत्तम । भूतभावन भूतेश देवदेव जगत्पते ॥
10:15.transliteration: स्वयमेव (by oneself alone) / आत्मना (by the self) / आत्मानम् (the self) / वेत्थ (you know) / त्वम् (you) / पुरुषोत्तम (O Supreme Person) / भूतभावन (O source of beings) / भूतेश (O lord of beings) / देवदेव (O divine of the divine) / जगत्पते (O ruler of the world)
10:15.meaning: Arjuna praises Krishna as the Supreme Person who alone fully knows his own nature and rules over all beings.

10:16.shloka: वक्तुमर्हस्यशेषेण दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः । याभिर्विभूतिभिर्लोकानिमांस्त्वं व्याप्य तिष्ठसि ॥
10:16.transliteration: वक्तुमर्हसि (you are able to speak) / अशेषेण (completely) / दिव्याः (divine) / हि (indeed) / आत्मविभूतयः (your own manifestations) / याभिः (by which) / विभूतिभिः (with manifestations) / लोकान् (the worlds) / इमान् (these) / त्वम् (you) / व्याप्य (pervading) / तिष्ठसि (you stand)
10:16.meaning: Arjuna says only Krishna can fully explain his own divine manifestations, because Krishna pervades and supports all the worlds through them.

10:17.shloka: कथं विद्यामहं योगिंस्त्वां सदा परिचिन्तयन् । केषु केषु च भावेषु चिन्त्योऽसि भगवन्मया ॥
10:17.transliteration: कथम् (how) / विद्याम् (may I know) / अहम् (I) / योगिन् (O Yogi) / त्वाम् (You) / सदा (always) / परिचिन्तयन् (contemplating fully) / केषु केषु (in which which) / च (and) / भावेषु (in forms / states) / चिन्त्यः (to be contemplated) / असि (You are) / भगवन् (O Blessed One) / मया (by me)
10:17.meaning: Arjuna asks Krishna how to keep remembering him and in what forms he should meditate on him.

10:18.shloka: विस्तरेणात्मनो योगं विभूतिं च जनार्दन । भूयः कथय तृप्तिर्हि श्रृण्वतो नास्ति मेऽमृतम् ॥
10:18.transliteration: विस्तरेण (in detail) / आत्मनः (of Your own) / योगम् (yogic power) / विभूतिम् (manifestations) / च (and) / जनार्दन (O Janardana) / भूयः (again) / कथय (tell) / तृप्तिः (satisfaction) / हि (indeed) / श्रृण्वतः (for the one hearing) / नास्ति (is not) / मे (my) / अमृतम् (nectar-like) / [वचनम्] (words)
10:18.meaning: Arjuna asks Krishna to keep speaking about his divine powers and forms, because hearing about them never feels complete.

10:19.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचहन्त ते कथयिष्यामि दिव्या ह्यात्मविभूतयः । प्राधान्यतः कुरुश्रेष्ठ नास्त्यन्तो विस्तरस्य मे ॥
10:19.transliteration: श्रीभगवानुवाच (the Blessed Lord said) / हन्त (indeed) / ते (to you) / कथयिष्यामि (I will tell) / दिव्याः (divine) / हि (indeed) / आत्मविभूतयः (my own manifestations) / प्राधान्यतः (chiefly / in the main) / कुरुश्रेष्ठ (O best of the Kurus) / नास्ति (there is not) / अन्तः (end) / विस्तरस्य (of the विस्तार / spread) / मे (of me)
10:19.meaning: Krishna agrees to describe his chief divine manifestations, but only in brief, because his full extent has no limit.

10:20.shloka: अहमात्मा गुडाकेश सर्वभूताशयस्थितः । अहमादिश्च मध्यं च भूतानामन्त एव च ॥
10:20.transliteration: अहम् (I am) / आत्मा (the Self) / गुडाकेश (O Gudakesha) / सर्वभूताशयस्थितः (standing in the heart of all beings) / अहम् (I am) / आदिः (the beginning) / च (and) / मध्यम् (the middle) / च (and) / भूतानाम् (of beings) / अन्तः (the end) / एव (indeed) / च (and)
10:20.meaning: Krishna says he lives within every being as the inner self and is present at every stage of existence: beginning, middle, and end.

meanings.meaning: standing in the heart of all beings

10:21.shloka: आदित्यानामहं विष्णुर्ज्योतिषां रविरंशुमान् । मरीचिर्मरुतामस्मि नक्षत्राणामहं शशी ॥
10:21.transliteration: आदित्यानाम् (among the sons of Aditi) / अहम् (I am) / विष्णुः (Vishnu) / ज्योतिषाम् (among radiant things) / रविः (the sun) / अंशुमान् (having rays) / मरीचिः (the brilliance) / मरुताम् (of the Maruts) / अस्मि (I am) / नक्षत्राणाम् (among stars) / अहम् (I am) / शशी (the moon)
10:21.meaning: Krishna says he appears as Vishnu among the sons of Aditi, as the sun among bright things, as the Maruts' brilliance, and as the moon among stars.

10:22.shloka: वेदानां सामवेदोऽस्मि देवानामस्मि वासवः । इन्द्रियाणां मनश्चास्मि भूतानामस्मि चेतना ॥
10:22.transliteration: वेदानाम् (among the Vedas) / सामवेदः (the Sama Veda) / अस्मि (I am) / देवानाम् (among the radiant beings) / अस्मि (I am) / वासवः (Indra) / इन्द्रियाणाम् (among the senses) / मनः (mind) / च (and) / अस्मि (I am) / भूतानाम् (among living beings) / अस्मि (I am) / चेतना (consciousness)
10:22.meaning: Krishna says he appears as the highest sacred chant, as Indra among radiant beings, as the mind among the senses, and as awareness in all living things.

10:23.shloka: रुद्राणां शङ्करश्चास्मि वित्तेशो यक्षरक्षसाम् । वसूनां पावकश्चास्मि मेरुः शिखरिणामहम् ॥
10:23.transliteration: रुद्राणाम् (among the Rudras) / शङ्करः (Shankara) / च (and) / अस्मि (I am) / वित्तेशः (Kubera, lord of wealth) / यक्षरक्षसाम् (of Yakshas and Rakshasas) / वसूनाम् (among the Vasus) / पावकः (fire) / च (and) / अस्मि (I am) / मेरुः (Meru) / शिखरिणाम् (of mountain peaks) / अहम् (I)
10:23.meaning: Krishna says he is present as the brightest or most powerful form within Rudras, Yakshas, Vasus, fire, and Mount Meru.

10:24.shloka: पुरोधसां च मुख्यं मां विद्धि पार्थ बृहस्पतिम् । सेनानीनामहं स्कन्दः सरसामस्मि सागरः ॥
10:24.transliteration: पुरोधसाम् (among priests) / च (and) / मुख्यम् (the chief) / माम् (Me) / विद्धि (know) / पार्थ (O Partha) / बृहस्पतिम् (Brihaspati) / सेनानीनाम् (among commanders) / अहम् (I) / स्कन्दः (Skanda) / सरसाम् (among waters) / अस्मि (am) / सागरः (the ocean)
10:24.meaning: Krishna says he is the highest among priests, the commander Skanda, and the ocean among all waters.

10:25.shloka: महर्षीणां भृगुरहं गिरामस्म्येकमक्षरम् । यज्ञानां जपयज्ञोऽस्मि स्थावराणां हिमालयः ॥
10:25.transliteration: महर्षीणाम् (among great sages) / भृगुः (Bhrigu) / अहम् (I am) / गिराम् (among words) / अस्मि (I am) / एकम् (one) / अक्षरम् (imperishable syllable) / यज्ञानाम् (among sacrifices) / जपयज्ञः (sacrifice of silent repetition) / अस्मि (I am) / स्थावराणाम् (among the steadfast) / हिमालयः (the Himalayas)
10:25.meaning: Krishna names his presence in sacred sages, sacred sound, silent repetition, and the Himalayas. He is showing that the divine can be found in revered forms across the world.

10:26.shloka: अश्वत्थः सर्ववृक्षाणां देवर्षीणां च नारदः । गन्धर्वाणां चित्ररथः सिद्धानां कपिलो मुनिः ॥
10:26.transliteration: अश्वत्थः (sacred fig tree) / सर्ववृक्षाणां (among all trees) / देवर्षीणां (among divine sages) / च (and) / नारदः (Narada) / गन्धर्वाणां (among celestial musicians) / चित्ररथः (Chitraratha) / सिद्धानां (among perfected beings) / कपिलः (Kapila) / मुनिः (sage)
10:26.meaning: Krishna names the most revered example in each group of trees, sages, musicians, and perfected beings, showing how his presence appears through the finest forms in creation.

10:27.shloka: उच्चैःश्रवसमश्वानां विद्धि माममृतोद्भवम् । ऐरावतं गजेन्द्राणां नराणां च नराधिपम् ॥
10:27.transliteration: उच्चैःश्रवसम् (Ucchaihshravas) / अश्वानाम् (among horses) / विद्धि (know) / माम् (me) / अमृतोद्भवम् (born from nectar) / ऐरावतम् (Airavata) / गजेन्द्राणाम् (among royal elephants) / नराणाम् (among humans) / च (and) / नराधिपम् (the king)
10:27.meaning: Krishna says to see his presence in the finest horse, the greatest elephant, and the human king.

10:28.shloka: आयुधानामहं वज्रं धेनूनामस्मि कामधुक् । प्रजनश्चास्मि कन्दर्पः सर्पाणामस्मि वासुकिः ॥
10:28.transliteration: आयुधानाम् (among weapons) / अहम् (I am) / वज्रम् (the thunderbolt) / धेनूनाम् (among cows) / अस्मि (I am) / कामधुक् (the wish-fulfilling cow) / प्रजनः (the cause of procreation) / च (and) / अस्मि (I am) / कन्दर्पः (Kandarpa, desire-born force) / सर्पाणाम् (among serpents) / अस्मि (I am) / वासुकिः (Vasuki)
10:28.meaning: Krishna names more of the finest powers and beings in the world as his expressions: thunderbolt, wish-fulfilling cow, the force behind procreation, and Vasuki among serpents.

10:29.shloka: अनन्तश्चास्मि नागानां वरुणो यादसामहम् । पितृ़णामर्यमा चास्मि यमः संयमतामहम् ॥
10:29.transliteration: अनन्तः (Ananta) / च (and) / अस्मि (I am) / नागानाम् (of serpents) / वरुणः (Varuna) / यादसाम् (of water beings) / अहम् (I) / पितृणाम् (of ancestors) / अर्यमा (Aryaman) / च (and) / अस्मि (I am) / यमः (Yama) / संयमताम् (of those who govern) / अहम् (I am)
10:29.meaning: Krishna says he is the highest presence among serpents, water beings, ancestors, and rulers. He is showing that his presence runs through every level of the world.

10:30.shloka: प्रह्लादश्चास्मि दैत्यानां कालः कलयतामहम् । मृगाणां च मृगेन्द्रोऽहं वैनतेयश्च पक्षिणाम् ॥
10:30.transliteration: प्रह्लादः (Prahlada) / च (and) / अस्मि (I am) / दैत्यानाम् (among the daityas) / कालः (Time) / कलयताम् (among those who count) / अहम् (I) / मृगाणाम् (among beasts) / च (and) / मृगेन्द्रः (lion) / अहम् (I) / वैनतेयः (Garuda) / च (and) / पक्षिणाम् (among birds)
10:30.meaning: Krishna says he is present as Prahlada among the daityas, as Time among those who count, as the lion among beasts, and as Garuda among birds.

10:31.shloka: पवनः पवतामस्मि रामः शस्त्रभृतामहम् । झषाणां मकरश्चास्मि स्रोतसामस्मि जाह्नवी ॥
10:31.transliteration: पवनः (the wind) / पवताम् (among purifiers) / अस्मि (I am) / रामः (Rama) / शस्त्रभृताम् (among weapon-bearers) / अहम् (I) / झषाणाम् (among aquatic beings) / मकरः (the crocodile) / च (and) / अस्मि (I am) / स्रोतसाम् (among streams) / अस्मि (I am) / जाह्नवी (the Ganga)
10:31.meaning: Krishna says he is the purifying wind, Rama among warriors, the crocodile among water beings, and the Ganga among rivers.

10:32.shloka: सर्गाणामादिरन्तश्च मध्यं चैवाहमर्जुन । अध्यात्मविद्या विद्यानां वादः प्रवदतामहम् ॥
10:32.transliteration: सर्गाणाम् (of creations) / आदिः (the beginning) / अन्तः (the end) / च (and) / मध्यं (the middle) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / अहम् (I) / अर्जुन (Arjuna) / अध्यात्मविद्या (knowledge of the true self) / विद्यानाम् (among knowledges) / वादः (reasoned debate) / प्रवदताम् (among those who speak) / अहम् (I)
10:32.meaning: Krishna says he is the beginning, middle, and end of all creation. He is also the deepest self-knowledge and the honest debate that seeks truth.

10:33.shloka: अक्षराणामकारोऽस्मि द्वन्द्वः सामासिकस्य च । अहमेवाक्षयः कालो धाताऽहं विश्वतोमुखः ॥
10:33.transliteration: अक्षराणाम् (among letters) / अकारः (the letter A) / अस्मि (I am) / द्वन्द्वः (dual compound) / सामासिकस्य (among compounds) / च (and) / अहम् (I) / एव (indeed) / अक्षयः (imperishable / endless) / कालः (time) / धाता (sustainer / creator) / अहम् (I am) / विश्वतोमुखः (having faces everywhere / all-facing)
10:33.meaning: Krishna says he is present in the first letter, in a basic kind of compound word, in endless time, and in the all-facing power that sustains the world.

10:34.shloka: मृत्युः सर्वहरश्चाहमुद्भवश्च भविष्यताम् । कीर्तिः श्रीर्वाक्च नारीणां स्मृतिर्मेधा धृतिः क्षमा ॥
10:34.transliteration: मृत्युः (death) / सर्वहरः (all-taking) / च (and) / अहम् (I am) / उद्भवः (origin) / च (and) / भविष्यताम् (of those yet to come) । कीर्तिः (fame) / श्रीः (splendour, fortune) / वाक् (speech) / च (and) / नारीणाम् (among women) / स्मृतिः (memory) / मेधा (intelligence) / धृतिः (steadfastness) / क्षमा (forgiveness)
10:34.meaning: Krishna says he is both the force that ends all lives and the source of all that is yet to come. He also shines as noble qualities in women: fame, beauty, speech, memory, intelligence, steadiness, and forgiveness.

meanings.meaning: of those yet to come) । कीर्तिः (fame

10:35.shloka: बृहत्साम तथा साम्नां गायत्री छन्दसामहम् । मासानां मार्गशीर्षोऽहमृतूनां कुसुमाकरः ॥
10:35.transliteration: बृहत्साम (the Brihat-Sama hymn) / तथा (and) / साम्नाम् (among hymns) / गायत्री (Gayatri) / छन्दसाम् (among meters) / अहम् (I am) / मासानाम् (among months) / मार्गशीर्षः (Margashirsha) / अहम् (I am) / ऋतूनाम् (among seasons) / कुसुमाकरः (spring)
10:35.meaning: Krishna says he is the finest sacred chant, the most revered meter, the best month, and the season of spring.

10:36.shloka: द्यूतं छलयतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम् । जयोऽस्मि व्यवसायोऽस्मि सत्त्वं सत्त्ववतामहम् ॥
10:36.transliteration: द्यूतम् (gambling) / छलयताम् (among deceivers) / अस्मि (I am) / तेजः (splendor) / तेजस्विनाम् (of the vigorous) / अहम् (I) / जयः (victory) / अस्मि (I am) / व्यवसायः (resolve) / अस्मि (I am) / सत्त्वम् (goodness) / सत्त्ववताम् (of the good) / अहम् (I am)
10:36.meaning: Krishna says he is present as the force behind victory, determination, and the goodness found in noble people.

10:37.shloka: वृष्णीनां वासुदेवोऽस्मि पाण्डवानां धनंजयः । मुनीनामप्यहं व्यासः कवीनामुशना कविः ॥
10:37.transliteration: वृष्णीनाम् (among the Vrishnis) / वासुदेवः (Vasudeva) / अस्मि (I am) / पाण्डवानाम् (among the Pandavas) / धनंजयः (Dhananjaya) / मुनीनाम् (among sages) / अपि (also) / अहम् (I) / व्यासः (Vyasa) / कवीनाम् (among poets) / उशना (Ushana) / कविः (poet)
10:37.meaning: Krishna says that the finest examples in different groups of people are his expressions. Greatness in warriors, sages, and poets all points back to him.

10:38.shloka: दण्डो दमयतामस्मि नीतिरस्मि जिगीषताम् । मौनं चैवास्मि गुह्यानां ज्ञानं ज्ञानवतामहम् ॥
10:38.transliteration: दण्डः (punishment/discipline) / दमयताम् (among those who restrain) / अस्मि (I am) / नीतिḥ (policy/right conduct) / अस्मि (I am) / जिगीषताम् (among those who seek victory) । / मौनम् (silence) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / अस्मि (I am) / गुह्यानाम् (among secrets) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / ज्ञानवताम् (among the wise) / अहम् (I am)
10:38.meaning: Krishna says he appears as rightful force, strategic conduct, silence, and true understanding wherever they are found.

10:39.shloka: यच्चापि सर्वभूतानां बीजं तदहमर्जुन । न तदस्ति विना यत्स्यान्मया भूतं चराचरम् ॥
10:39.transliteration: यच्चापि (and whatever also) / सर्वभूतानाम् (of all beings) / बीजम् (seed) / तत् (that) / अहम् (I am) / अर्जुन (Arjuna) / न (not) / तत् (that) / अस्ति (there is) / विना (without) / यत् (which) / स्यात् (would be) / मया (by me) / भूतम् (being) / चराचरम् (moving and unmoving)
10:39.meaning: Krishna says he is the source of every being. Nothing that moves or stands still can exist without him.

10:40.shloka: नान्तोऽस्ति मम दिव्यानां विभूतीनां परंतप । एष तूद्देशतः प्रोक्तो विभूतेर्विस्तरो मया ॥
10:40.transliteration: नान्तः (no end) / अस्ति (there is) / मम (of my) / दिव्यानां (of the divine) / विभूतीनां (manifestations) / परंतप (O scorcher of foes) / एषः (this) / तु (only) / उद्देशतः (in brief) / प्रोक्तः (spoken) / विभूतेः (of manifestations) / विस्तारः (expanse) / मया (by me)
10:40.meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna that his divine manifestations are limitless, and the examples already given are only a brief outline.

10:41.shloka: यद्यद्विभूतिमत्सत्त्वं श्रीमदूर्जितमेव वा । तत्तदेवावगच्छ त्वं मम तेजोंऽशसंभवम् ॥
10:41.transliteration: यद्यत् (whatever) / विभूतिमत् (endowed with splendour/power) / सत्त्वम् (being) / श्रीमत् (beautiful, glorious) / ऊर्जितम् (strong, mighty) / एव (indeed) / वा (or) / तत् तत् (that very) / एव (indeed) / अवगच्छ (know) / त्वम् (you) / मम (my) / तेजः-अंश-संभवम् (born from a portion of my radiance)
10:41.meaning: Anything that shines with beauty, strength, or greatness comes from Krishna's radiance. He asks Arjuna to recognize that source in every impressive thing.

meanings.meaning: born from a portion of my radiance

10:42.shloka: अथवा बहुनैतेन किं ज्ञातेन तवार्जुन । विष्टभ्याहमिदं कृत्स्नमेकांशेन स्थितो जगत् ॥
10:42.transliteration: अथवा (or) / बहुना (with much) / एतेन (by this) / किम् (what) / ज्ञातेन (by knowing) / तव (for you) / अर्जुन (Arjuna) / विष्टभ्य (pervading, sustaining) / अहम् (I) / इदम् (this) / कृत्स्नम् (whole) / एकांशेन (with one part) / स्थितः (established) / जगत् (universe)
10:42.meaning: Krishna says there is no need for endless detail: even a single part of his presence supports the entire universe.

11:1.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचमदनुग्रहाय परमं गुह्यमध्यात्मसंज्ञितम् । यत्त्वयोक्तं वचस्तेन मोहोऽयं विगतो मम ॥
11:1.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / मदनुग्रहाय (for my grace / for my benefit) / परमम् (supreme) / गुह्यम् (secret) / अध्यात्मसंज्ञितम् (called concerning the inner self) / यत् (which) / त्वया उक्तम् (spoken by you) / वचः (words) / तेन (by that) / मोहः अयम् (this delusion) / विगतः (has gone away) / मम (of mine)
11:1.meaning: Arjuna says Krishna’s hidden teaching has cleared away his confusion. He feels the words were spoken just for his own good.

11:2.shloka: भवाप्ययौ हि भूतानां श्रुतौ विस्तरशो मया । त्वत्तः कमलपत्राक्ष माहात्म्यमपि चाव्ययम् ॥
11:2.transliteration: भवाप्ययौ (arising and passing away) / हि (indeed) / भूतानाम् (of beings) / श्रुतौ (have been heard) / विस्तरशः (in detail) / मया (by me) / त्वत्तः (from You) / कमलपत्राक्ष (O lotus-eyed one) / माहात्म्यम् (majesty) / अपि (also) / च (and) / अव्ययम् (imperishable)
11:2.meaning: Arjuna says he has learned from Krishna about how all beings come and go, and about Krishna's imperishable greatness.

11:3.shloka: एवमेतद्यथात्थ त्वमात्मानं परमेश्वर । द्रष्टुमिच्छामि ते रूपमैश्वरं पुरुषोत्तम ॥
11:3.transliteration: एवम् (thus) / एतत् (this) / यथा (as) / आथ्थ (you have said) / त्वम् (you) / आत्मानम् (yourself) / परमेश्वर (O Supreme Lord) / द्रष्टुम् (to see) / इच्छामि (I wish) / ते (your) / रूपम् (form) / ऐश्वरम् (divine) / पुरुषोत्तम (O Supreme Person)
11:3.meaning: Arjuna accepts Krishna’s words as true, but asks to see Krishna’s divine form directly.

11:4.shloka: मन्यसे यदि तच्छक्यं मया द्रष्टुमिति प्रभो । योगेश्वर ततो मे त्वं दर्शयाऽत्मानमव्ययम् ॥
11:4.transliteration: मन्यसे (if you think) / यदि (if) / तत् (that) / शक्यम् (possible) / मया (by me) / द्रष्टुम् (to see) / इति (thus) / प्रभो (O Lord) / योगेश्वर (O master of yoga) / ततः (then) / मे (to me) / त्वम् (you) / दर्शय (show) / आत्मानम् (your self) / अव्ययम् (imperishable)
11:4.meaning: Arjuna asks Krishna to reveal his imperishable form if it can be seen by human eyes.

11:5.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचपश्य मे पार्थ रूपाणि शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः । नानाविधानि दिव्यानि नानावर्णाकृतीनि च ॥
11:5.transliteration: श्री भगवानुवाच (the Blessed Lord said) / पश्य (see) / मे (my) / पार्थ (Partha) / रूपाणि (forms) / शतशः (in hundreds) / अथ (and) / सहस्रशः (in thousands) / नानाविधानि (of many kinds) / दिव्यानि (divine) / नाना-वर्ण-आकृतीनि (of many colors and shapes) / च (and)
11:5.meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna to look at his countless divine forms, each with different colors and shapes.

11:6.transliteration: पश्य (see) / आदित्यान् (the Adityas) / वसून् (the Vasus) / रुद्रान् (the Rudras) / अश्विनौ (the two Ashvins) / मरुतः (the Maruts) / तथा (and) / बहूनि (many) / अदृष्टपूर्वाणि (never-seen-before) / पश्य (see) / आश्चर्याणि (wonders) / भारत (O Bharata)
11:6.meaning: Krishna invites Arjuna to see the cosmic form’s many divine beings and wonders, beyond anything he has seen before.

11:7.shloka: इहैकस्थं जगत्कृत्स्नं पश्याद्य सचराचरम् । मम देहे गुडाकेश यच्चान्यद्द्रष्टुमिच्छसि ॥
11:7.transliteration: इह (here) / एकस्थम् (placed in one) / जगत् (world) / कृत्स्नम् (entire) / पश्य (see) / अद्य (now) / सचराचरम् (with moving and unmoving) / मम (my) / देहे (in body) / गुडाकेश (Arjuna) / यत् (whatever) / च (and) / अन्यत् (other) / द्रष्टुम् (to see) / इच्छसि (you wish)
11:7.meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna that the whole universe, moving and still, is contained in his body. Arjuna can also look for anything else he wants to see.

11:8.shloka: न तु मां शक्यसे द्रष्टुमनेनैव स्वचक्षुषा । दिव्यं ददामि ते चक्षुः पश्य मे योगमैश्वरम् ॥
11:8.transliteration: न तु (but not) / मां (me) / शक्यसे (you are able) / द्रष्टुम् (to see) / अनेन (with this) / एव (only) / स्वचक्षुषा (with your own eyes) / दिव्यम् (divine) / ददामि (I give) / ते (to you) / चक्षुः (eyesight) / पश्य (see) / मे (my) / योगम् (power) / ऐश्वरम् (sovereign)
11:8.meaning: Ordinary sight is not enough to see Krishna's true form. He gives Arjuna a divine way of seeing so he can witness that supreme power.

11:9.shloka: सञ्जय उवाचएवमुक्त्वा ततो राजन्महायोगेश्वरो हरिः । दर्शयामास पार्थाय परमं रूपमैश्वरम् ॥
11:9.transliteration: सञ्जय उवाच (Sanjaya said) / एवम् (thus) / उक्त्वा (having spoken) / ततः (then) / राजन् (O King) / महायोगेश्वरः (the great lord of yoga) / हरिः (Hari) / दर्शयामास (revealed) / पार्थाय (to Arjuna, son of Pritha) / परमम् (supreme) / रूपम् (form) / ऐश्वरम् (divine, majestic)
11:9.meaning: Sanjaya tells the king that Krishna then showed Arjuna his supreme divine form.

11:10.transliteration: अनेक (many) / वक्त्र (mouths) / नयनम् (eyes) / अनेक (many) / अद्भुत-दर्शनम् (wonderful sights) / अनेक (many) / दिव्य-आभरणम् (divine ornaments) / दिव्य-अनेक-उद्यत-आयुधम् (divine, many, raised weapons)
11:10.meaning: The cosmic form appears with countless faces, eyes, ornaments, and divine weapons. It is a vast, overwhelming vision rather than a single human shape.

11:11.transliteration: दिव्य (divine) / माल्य (garlands) / अम्बर (garments) / धरम् (wearing) / दिव्य (divine) / गन्ध (fragrance) / अनुलेपनम् (anointing) / सर्व (all) / आश्चर्य (wonder) / मयम् (filled with) / देवम् (the radiant one) / अनन्तम् (endless) / विश्वतोमुखम् (facing everywhere)
11:11.meaning: Krishna's cosmic form is covered in divine garlands, clothes, and fragrance. It is endless, wondrous, and faces in every direction.

11:12.shloka: दिवि सूर्यसहस्रस्य भवेद्युगपदुत्थिता । यदि भाः सदृशी सा स्याद्भासस्तस्य महात्मनः ॥
11:12.transliteration: दिवि (in the sky) / सूर्यसहस्रस्य (of a thousand suns) / भवेत् (would be) / युगपत् (at once) / उत्थिता (risen) / यदि (if) / भाः (light) / सदृशी (equal) / सा (that) / स्यात् (would be) / भासः (radiance) / तस्य (of that) / महात्मनः (of the great being)
11:12.meaning: Arjuna says that even a thousand suns rising at once would not match the radiance of Krishna's cosmic form.

11:13.shloka: तत्रैकस्थं जगत्कृत्स्नं प्रविभक्तमनेकधा । अपश्यद्देवदेवस्य शरीरे पाण्डवस्तदा ॥
11:13.transliteration: तत्र (there) / एकस्थम् (in one place) / जगत्-कृत्स्नम् (the entire universe) / प्रविभक्तम् (divided) / अनेकधा (in many ways) / अपश्यत् (saw) / देवदेवस्य (of the Lord of the gods) / शरीरे (in the body) / पाण्डवः (the son of Pandu, Arjuna) / तदा (then)
11:13.meaning: Arjuna saw the whole universe inside Krishna's cosmic form, all held together in one body and yet appearing in countless forms.

meanings.meaning: of the Lord of the gods

11:14.shloka: ततः स विस्मयाविष्टो हृष्टरोमा धनञ्जयः । प्रणम्य शिरसा देवं कृताञ्जलिरभाषत ॥
11:14.transliteration: ततः (then) / सः (he) / विस्मय-आविष्टः (filled with wonder) / हृष्ट-रोमा (with hair standing on end) / धनञ्जयः (Arjuna) / प्रणम्य (having bowed) / शिरसा (with the head) / देवम् (to the divine) / कृताञ्जलिः (with folded hands) / अभाषत (spoke)
11:14.meaning: Arjuna is stunned by the cosmic form. He bows his head, joins his hands, and begins to speak.

11:15.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचपश्यामि देवांस्तव देव देहेसर्वांस्तथा भूतविशेषसङ्घान् । ब्रह्माणमीशं कमलासनस्थमृषींश्च सर्वानुरगांश्च दिव्यान् ॥
11:15.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / पश्यामि (I see) / देवान् (the gods) / तव (your) / देव (O divine one) / देहे (in the body) / सर्वान् (all) / तथा (and) / भूतविशेषसङ्घान् (groups of distinct beings) / ब्रह्माणम् (Brahma) / ईशम् (the lord) / कमलासनस्थम् (seated on the lotus seat) / ऋषीन् (sages) / च (and) / सर्वान् (all) / उरगान् (serpents) / च (and) / दिव्यान् (radiant / divine)
11:15.meaning: Arjuna sees the entire divine order inside Krishna's cosmic body: gods, beings of every kind, Brahma, Shiva, sages, and radiant serpents.

11:16.shloka: अनेकबाहूदरवक्त्रनेत्रंपश्यामि त्वां सर्वतोऽनन्तरूपम् । नान्तं न मध्यं न पुनस्तवादिंपश्यामि विश्वेश्वर विश्वरूप ॥
11:16.transliteration: अनेकबाहु (many-armed) / उदर (bellied) / वक्त्र (mouthed) / नेत्र (eyed) / पश्यामि (I see) / त्वाम् (You) / सर्वतः (everywhere) / अनन्तरूपम् (endless-formed) / न अन्तम् (no end) / न मध्यम् (no middle) / न पुनः तव आदिम् (nor again Your beginning) / पश्यामि (I see) / विश्वेश्वर (Lord of all) / विश्वरूप (cosmic form)
11:16.meaning: Arjuna sees Krishna’s cosmic form as limitless, with countless arms, mouths, and eyes. He cannot find its beginning, middle, or end.

11:17.shloka: किरीटिनं गदिनं चक्रिणं चतेजोराशिं सर्वतोदीप्तिमन्तम् । पश्यामि त्वां दुर्निरीक्ष्यं समन्ताद्दीप्तानलार्कद्युतिमप्रमेयम् ॥
11:17.transliteration: किरीटिनम् (crowned) / गदिनम् (mace-bearing) / चक्रिणम् (discus-bearing) / च (and) / तेजोराशिम् (a mass of radiance) / सर्वतोदीप्तिमन्तम् (shining on all sides) / पश्यामि (I see) / त्वाम् (you) / दुर्निरीक्ष्यम् (hard to behold) / समन्तात् (on every side) / दीप्तानलार्कद्युतिम् (with the brilliance of blazing fire and the sun) / अप्रमेयम् (immeasurable)
11:17.meaning: Arjuna sees Krishna in a radiant cosmic form, crowned and armed, blazing like fire and the sun, impossible to measure or fully look at.

meanings.meaning: with the brilliance of blazing fire and the sun

11:18.shloka: त्वमक्षरं परमं वेदितव्यंत्वमस्य विश्वस्य परं निधानम् । त्वमव्ययः शाश्वतधर्मगोप्तासनातनस्त्वं पुरुषो मतो मे ॥
11:18.transliteration: त्वम् (you) / अक्षरम् (imperishable) / परमम् (supreme) / वेदितव्यम् (to be known) / त्वम् (you) / अस्य (of this) / विश्वस्य (of the universe) / परम् (highest) / निधानम् (support, resting place) / त्वम् (you) / अव्ययः (unchanging) / शाश्वत-धर्म-गोप्ता (guardian of eternal dharma) / सनातनः (eternal) / त्वम् (you) / पुरुषः (person) / मतः (considered) / मे (by me)
11:18.meaning: Arjuna recognizes Krishna as the highest imperishable reality, the universe's support, the protector of eternal dharma, and the eternal person.

11:19.shloka: अनादिमध्यान्तमनन्तवीर्यमनन्तबाहुं शशिसूर्यनेत्रम् । पश्यामि त्वां दीप्तहुताशवक्त्रम्स्वतेजसा विश्वमिदं तपन्तम् ॥
11:19.transliteration: अनादि (without beginning) / मध्य (middle) / अन्तम् (end) / अनन्तवीर्यम् (of infinite power) / अनन्तबाहुम् (with infinite arms) / शशि-सूर्य-नेत्रम् (with moon-sun eyes) / पश्यामि (I see) / त्वाम् (you) / दीप्त-हुताश-वक्त्रम् (with blazing fire-mouths) / स्व-तेजसा (with own radiance) / विश्वम् (world) / इदम् (this) / तपन्तम् (scorching)
11:19.meaning: Arjuna sees Krishna as beyond beginning, middle, and end, with limitless power and terrifying cosmic features that burn the world with their own brilliance.
