11:20.shloka: द्यावापृथिव्योरिदमन्तरं हिव्याप्तं त्वयैकेन दिशश्च सर्वाः । दृष्ट्वाऽद्भुतं रूपमुग्रं तवेदंलोकत्रयं प्रव्यथितं महात्मन् ॥
11:20.transliteration: द्यावापृथिव्योः (of heaven and earth) / इदम् (this) / अन्तरम् (intermediate space) / हि (indeed) / व्याप्तम् (pervaded) / त्वया (by You) / एकेन (alone) / दिशः (directions) / च (and) / सर्वाः (all) / दृष्ट्वा (having seen) / अद्भुतम् (wondrous) / रूपम् (form) / उग्रम् (fierce) / तव (Your) / इदम् (this) / लोकत्रयम् (three worlds) / प्रव्यथितम् (shaken) / महात्मन् (O great one)
11:20.meaning: Arjuna sees that Krishna fills every direction and every space. The sight of Krishna's vast and fierce form makes all three worlds tremble.

11:21.shloka: अमी हि त्वां सुरसङ्घाः विशन्तिकेचिद्भीताः प्राञ्जलयो गृणन्ति । स्वस्तीत्युक्त्वा महर्षिसिद्धसङ्घाःस्तुवन्ति त्वां स्तुतिभिः पुष्कलाभिः ॥
11:21.transliteration: अमी (these) / हि (indeed) / त्वां (you) / सुरसङ्घाः (hosts of radiant beings) / विशन्ति (enter) / केचिद् (some) / भीताः (frightened) / प्राञ्जलयः (with joined palms) / गृणन्ति (sing/praise) / स्वस्ति (may it be well) / इति (thus) / उक्त्वा (having said) / महर्षि-सिद्ध-सङ्घाः (hosts of great seers and perfected beings) / स्तुवन्ति (praise) / त्वां (you) / स्तुतिभिः (with praises) / पुष्कलाभिः (abundant, excellent)
11:21.meaning: Even the radiant beings are drawn into Krishna’s cosmic form. Some are afraid and pray, while seers and perfected beings praise him with rich hymns.

meanings.meaning: hosts of great seers and perfected beings

11:22.shloka: रुद्रादित्या वसवो ये च साध्याविश्वेऽश्िवनौ मरुतश्चोष्मपाश्च । गन्धर्वयक्षासुरसिद्धसङ्घावीक्षन्ते त्वां विस्मिताश्चैव सर्वे ॥
11:22.transliteration: रुद्रादित्या (Rudras and Adityas) / वसवः (Vasus) / ये (who) / च (and) / साध्याः (Sadhyas) / विश्वे (the Visvedevas) / अश्विनौ (the two Ashvins) / मरुतः (Maruts) / च (and) / उष्मपाः (ancestors) / च (and) / गन्धर्व-यक्ष-असुर-सिद्ध-सङ्घाः (hosts of Gandharvas, Yakshas, Asuras, and Siddhas) / वीक्षन्ते (look upon) / त्वाम् (you) / विस्मिताः (astonished) / च एव (and indeed) / सर्वे (all)
11:22.meaning: Arjuna says that every class of celestial and powerful beings is astonished and looking at Krishna's cosmic form.

meanings.meaning: hosts of Gandharvas, Yakshas, Asuras, and Siddhas

11:23.shloka: रूपं महत्ते बहुवक्त्रनेत्रंमहाबाहो बहुबाहूरुपादम् । बहूदरं बहुदंष्ट्राकरालंदृष्ट्वा लोकाः प्रव्यथितास्तथाऽहम् ॥
11:23.transliteration: रूपम् (form) / महत् (great) / ते (your) / बहुवक्त्रनेत्रम् (with many mouths and eyes) / महाबाहो (O mighty-armed one) / बहुबाहूरुपादम् (with many arms, thighs, and feet) / बहूदरम् (with many bellies) / बहुदंष्ट्राकरालम् (terrible with many fangs) / दृष्ट्वा (having seen) / लोकाः (all beings) / प्रव्यथिताः (are shaken) / तथा (so) / अहम् (I) ॥
11:23.meaning: Arjuna sees Krishna’s cosmic form and feels overwhelmed. The sight shakes not only him, but all beings around him.

meanings.meaning: with many arms, thighs, and feet

11:24.shloka: नभःस्पृशं दीप्तमनेकवर्णंव्यात्ताननं दीप्तविशालनेत्रम् । दृष्ट्वा हि त्वां प्रव्यथितान्तरात्माधृतिं न विन्दामि शमं च विष्णो ॥
11:24.transliteration: नभःस्पृशम् (touching the sky) / दीप्तम् (blazing) / अनेकवर्णम् (of many colors) / व्यात्ताननम् (with spread-open mouths) / दीप्तविशालनेत्रम् (with blazing vast eyes) / दृष्ट्वा (having seen) / हि (indeed) / त्वाम् (you) / प्रव्यथितान्तरात्मा (with shaken inner being) / धृतिम् (steadiness) / न विन्दामि (I do not find) / शमम् (peace) / च (and) / विष्णो (O Vishnu)
11:24.meaning: Arjuna is overwhelmed by Krishna's cosmic form. The sight shakes his inner being so deeply that he cannot find calm or steadiness.

11:25.shloka: दंष्ट्राकरालानि च ते मुखानिदृष्ट्वैव कालानलसन्निभानि । दिशो न जाने न लभे च शर्मप्रसीद देवेश जगन्निवास ॥
11:25.transliteration: दंष्ट्राकरालानि (terrible with fangs) / च (and) / ते (your) / मुखानि (mouths) / दृष्ट्वा (having seen) / एव (indeed) / कालानलसन्निभानि (resembling the fire of destruction) / दिशः (directions) / न (not) / जाने (I know) / न (not) / लभे (I find) / च (and) / शर्म (peace) / प्रसीद (be gracious) / देवेश (Lord of the divine beings) / जगन्निवास (dwelling place of the world)
11:25.meaning: Arjuna is overwhelmed by Krishna's terrifying cosmic form. He cannot orient himself or find peace, and he begs Krishna to soften and show mercy.

11:26.shloka: अमी च त्वां धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्राःसर्वे सहैवावनिपालसङ्घैः । भीष्मो द्रोणः सूतपुत्रस्तथाऽसौसहास्मदीयैरपि योधमुख्यैः ॥
11:26.transliteration: अमी (these) / च (and) / त्वां (you) / धृतराष्ट्रस्य (of Dhritarashtra) / पुत्राः (sons) / सर्वे (all) / सह (together with) / एव (indeed) / अवनिपालसङ्घैः (with hosts of kings) / भीष्मः (Bhishma) / द्रोणः (Drona) / सूतपुत्रः (the charioteer's son) / तथा (and) / असौ (that one) / सह (together with) / अस्मदीयैः (with our own) / अपि (also) / योधमुख्यैः (with chief warriors)
11:26.meaning: Arjuna sees the great warriors and kings being pulled into Krishna's terrible cosmic mouth, where some are crushed between the teeth.

11:27.shloka: वक्त्राणि ते त्वरमाणा विशन्तिदंष्ट्राकरालानि भयानकानि । केचिद्विलग्ना दशनान्तरेषुसंदृश्यन्ते चूर्णितैरुत्तमाङ्गैः ॥
11:27.transliteration: वक्त्राणि (mouths) / ते (your) / त्वरमाणा (hastening) / विशन्ति (enter) / दंष्ट्राकरालानि (with fearsome teeth) / भयानकानि (terrible) / केचिद् (some) / विलग्नाः (stuck) / दशन-अन्तरेषु (between the teeth) / संदृश्यन्ते (are seen) / चूर्णितैः (crushed) / उत्तमाङ्गैः (with heads)
11:27.meaning: Arjuna sees warriors rushing into Krishna's terrible mouths. Some are trapped between the teeth, with their heads crushed.

11:28.shloka: यथा नदीनां बहवोऽम्बुवेगाःसमुद्रमेवाभिमुखाः द्रवन्ति । तथा तवामी नरलोकवीराविशन्ति वक्त्राण्यभिविज्वलन्ति ॥
11:28.transliteration: यथा (as) / नदीनाम् (of rivers) / बहवः (many) / अम्बुवेगाः (water-currents) / समुद्रम् (the sea) / एव (indeed) / अभिमुखाः (facing) / द्रवन्ति (run) / तथा (so) / तव (your) / अमी (these) / नरलोकवीराः (heroes of the human world) / विशन्ति (enter) / वक्त्राणि (mouths) / अभिविज्वलन्ति (blazing)
11:28.meaning: The great warriors are rushing into Krishna’s blazing mouths just as rivers rush toward the sea.

11:29.shloka: यथा प्रदीप्तं ज्वलनं पतङ्गाविशन्ति नाशाय समृद्धवेगाः । तथैव नाशाय विशन्ति लोकास्तवापि वक्त्राणि समृद्धवेगाः ॥
11:29.transliteration: यथा (just as) / प्रदीप्तम् (blazing) / ज्वलनम् (fire) / पतङ्गाः (moths) / विशन्ति (enter) / नाशाय (for destruction) / समृद्धवेगाः (with great speed) / तथा (so) / एव (indeed) / नाशाय (for destruction) / विशन्ति (enter) / लोकाः (people) / तव (your) / अपि (also) / वक्त्राणि (mouths) / समृद्धवेगाः (with great speed)
11:29.meaning: Arjuna sees people rushing helplessly into Krishna's blazing mouths, like moths flying into fire and destroying themselves.

11:30.shloka: लेलिह्यसे ग्रसमानः समन्ताल्लोकान्समग्रान्वदनैर्ज्वलद्भिः । तेजोभिरापूर्य जगत्समग्रंभासस्तवोग्राः प्रतपन्ति विष्णो ॥
11:30.transliteration: लेलिह्यसे (you lick up) / ग्रसमानः (devouring) / समन्तात् (from every side) / लोकान् (worlds) / समग्रान् (all) / वदनैः (with mouths) / ज्वलद्भिः (blazing) / तेजोभिः (with radiances) / आपूर्य (filling) / जगत् (the universe) / समग्रम् (entire) / भासः (light) / तव (your) / उग्राः (fierce) / प्रतपन्ति (burn) / विष्णो (O Vishnu)
11:30.meaning: Arjuna sees the cosmic form as all-consuming and unbearable. The blazing mouths and fierce radiance show that this vision is not gentle, but overwhelming and world-devouring.

11:31.shloka: आख्याहि मे को भवानुग्ररूपोनमोऽस्तु ते देववर प्रसीद । विज्ञातुमिच्छामि भवन्तमाद्यंन हि प्रजानामि तव प्रवृत्तिम् ॥
11:31.transliteration: आख्याहि (tell) / मे (to me) / कः (who) / भवान् (You are) / उग्र-रूपः (fierce-formed) / नमः अस्तु (salutations be) / ते (to You) / देव-वर (best of the radiant beings) / प्रसीद (be gracious) / विज्ञातुम् इच्छामि (I wish to know) / भवन्तम् (You) / आद्यम् (the primal one) / न हि (indeed not) / प्रजानामि (I know) / तव (Your) / प्रवृत्तिम् (purpose / action)
11:31.meaning: Arjuna bows in fear and asks Krishna to reveal who this terrible form is. He wants to understand the primal source behind the overwhelming vision.

11:32.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचकालोऽस्मि लोकक्षयकृत्प्रवृद्धोलोकान्समाहर्तुमिह प्रवृत्तः । ऋतेऽपि त्वां न भविष्यन्ति सर्वेयेऽवस्थिताः प्रत्यनीकेषु योधाः ॥
11:32.transliteration: श्री भगवानुवाच (the Blessed Lord said) / कालः (Time) / अस्मि (I am) / लोकक्षयकृत् (maker of the destruction of worlds) / प्रवृद्धः (grown mighty) / लोकान् (the worlds / beings) / समाहर्तुम् (to gather up / destroy) / इह (here) / प्रवृत्तः (set in motion / come forth) / ऋते (without) / अपि (even) / त्वाम् (you) / न (not) / भविष्यन्ति (will exist) / सर्वे (all) / ये (who) / अवस्थिताः (standing) / प्रत्यनीकेषु (in the opposing ranks) / योधाः (warriors)
11:32.meaning: Krishna reveals that the force of ending is already at work. Arjuna is not being asked to cause death; he is being asked to take part in a battle whose outcome is already decided.

meanings.meaning: maker of the destruction of worlds

11:33.shloka: तस्मात्त्वमुत्तिष्ठ यशो लभस्वजित्वा शत्रून् भुङ्क्ष्व राज्यं समृद्धम् । मयैवैते निहताः पूर्वमेवनिमित्तमात्रं भव सव्यसाचिन् ॥
11:33.transliteration: तस्मात् (therefore) / त्वम् (you) / उत्तिष्ठ (rise) / यशः (fame) / लभस्व (gain) / जित्वा (having conquered) / शत्रून् (enemies) / भुङ्क्ष्व (enjoy) / राज्यं (kingdom) / समृद्धम् (prosperous) / मया (by me) / एव (indeed) / एते (these) / निहताः (slain) / पूर्वम् (already) / एव (indeed) / निमित्तमात्रम् (mere instrument) / भव (become) / सव्यसाचिन् (Arjuna)
11:33.meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna to stand up, fight, and claim the kingdom, but without ego. The enemies are already defeated in the larger order; Arjuna must only act as the instrument.

11:34.shloka: द्रोणं च भीष्मं च जयद्रथं चकर्णं तथाऽन्यानपि योधवीरान् । मया हतांस्त्वं जहि मा व्यथिष्ठायुध्यस्व जेतासि रणे सपत्नान् ॥
11:34.transliteration: द्रोणं (Drona) / च (and) / भीष्मं (Bhishma) / च (and) / जयद्रथं (Jayadratha) / च (and) / कर्णं (Karna) / तथा (and) / अन्यान् (others) / अपि (also) / योधवीरान् (great warriors) / मया (by Me) / हतान् (slain) / त्वं (you) / जहि (slay) / मा (do not) / व्यथिष्ठाः (be distressed) / आयुध्यस्व (fight) / जेतासि (you will conquer) / रणे (in battle) / सपत्नान् (enemies)
11:34.meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna that the great warriors are already defeated in the larger order of events. Arjuna should stop trembling, keep fighting, and trust that victory is already assured.

11:35.shloka: सञ्जय उवाचएतच्छ्रुत्वा वचनं केशवस्यकृताञ्जलिर्वेपमानः किरीटी । नमस्कृत्वा भूय एवाह कृष्णंसगद्गदं भीतभीतः प्रणम्य ॥
11:35.transliteration: सञ्जय उवाच (Sanjaya said) / एतत् (this) / श्रुत्वा (having heard) / वचनम् (word/speech) / केशवस्य (of Keshava) / कृताञ्जलिः (with folded hands) / वेपमानः (trembling) / किरीटी (the crowned one, Arjuna) / नमस्कृत्वा (having bowed) / भूयः (again) / एव (indeed) / आह (said) / कृष्णम् (to Krishna) / सगद्गदम् (in a choked voice) / भीतभीतः (very frightened) / प्रणम्य (having prostrated)
11:35.meaning: Arjuna is overwhelmed by Krishna's cosmic revelation. He trembles, bows, and can only speak with fear.

11:36.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचस्थाने हृषीकेश तव प्रकीर्त्याजगत् प्रहृष्यत्यनुरज्यते च । रक्षांसि भीतानि दिशो द्रवन्तिसर्वे नमस्यन्ति च सिद्धसङ्घाः ॥
11:36.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / स्थाने (rightly; fittingly) / हृषीकेश (O Hrishikesha) / तव (Your) / प्रकीर्त्य (by glorifying; by chanting) / जगत् (the world) / प्रहृष्यति (rejoices greatly) / अनुरज्यते (becomes devoted; becomes attached in love) / च (and) / रक्षांसि (beings of darkness; hostile beings) / भीतानि (frightened) / दिशः (directions) / द्रवन्ति (flee) / सर्वे (all) / नमस्यन्ति (bow down) / च (and) / सिद्धसङ्घाः (assemblies of perfected beings)
11:36.meaning: Arjuna says that praising Krishna fills the world with joy and love, drives fearful forces away, and makes perfected beings bow in respect.

meanings.meaning: becomes devoted; becomes attached in love

11:37.shloka: कस्माच्च ते न नमेरन्महात्मन्गरीयसे ब्रह्मणोऽप्यादिकर्त्रे । अनन्त देवेश जगन्निवासत्वमक्षरं सदसत्तत्परं यत् ॥
11:37.transliteration: कस्मात् (why) / च (and) / ते (to you) / न (not) / नमेरन् (would bow) / महात्मन् (O great one) / गरीयसे (to the greater) / ब्रह्मणः (than Brahma) / अपि (even) / आदिकर्त्रे (to the first maker) / अनन्त (endless) / देवेश (Lord of radiant beings) / जगन्निवास (abode of the world) / त्वम् (you) / अक्षरम् (imperishable) / सत् (being) / असत् (non-being) / तत् परम् (beyond that) / यत् (which)
11:37.meaning: Arjuna asks why anyone would not bow to Krishna, who is greater than even Brahma and beyond all categories of existence and nonexistence.

11:38.shloka: त्वमादिदेवः पुरुषः पुराणस्त्वमस्य विश्वस्य परं निधानम् । वेत्तासि वेद्यं च परं च धामत्वया ततं विश्वमनन्तरूप ॥
11:38.transliteration: त्वम् (you are) / आदिदेवः (the first divine being) / पुरुषः (the supreme person) / पुराणः (ancient) / त्वम् (you are) / अस्य (of this) / विश्वस्य (of the universe) / परम् (highest) / निधानम् (refuge, resting place) / वेत्ता (knower) / असि (you are) / वेद्यम् (the knowable) / च (and) / परम् (highest) / च (and) / धाम (abode) / त्वया (by you) / तम् (pervaded) / विश्वम् (the universe) / अनन्तरूप (O infinite-formed one)
11:38.meaning: Arjuna sees Krishna as the first source, the ancient being, the highest refuge, and the one who pervades everything. Krishna is both the knower and the known.

11:39.shloka: वायुर्यमोऽग्निर्वरुणः शशाङ्कःप्रजापतिस्त्वं प्रपितामहश्च । नमो नमस्तेऽस्तु सहस्रकृत्वःपुनश्च भूयोऽपि नमो नमस्ते ॥
11:39.transliteration: वायुः (air) / यमः (Yama) / अग्निः (fire) / वरुणः (Varuna) / शशाङ्कः (moon) / प्रजापतिः (Prajapati) / त्वम् (you) / प्रपितामहः (great ancestor) / च (and) / नमः नमः ते (salutations, salutations to you) / अस्तु (may it be) / सहस्रकृत्वः (a thousand times) / पुनः (again) / च (and) / भूयः अपि (again and again) / नमः नमः ते (salutations, salutations to you)
11:39.meaning: Arjuna sees Krishna as the source behind all cosmic powers and offers repeated bowing. The response is not argument but reverence.

11:40.shloka: नमः पुरस्तादथ पृष्ठतस्तेनमोऽस्तु ते सर्वत एव सर्व । अनन्तवीर्यामितविक्रमस्त्वंसर्वं समाप्नोषि ततोऽसि सर्वः ॥
11:40.transliteration: नमः (salutation) / पुरस्तात् (from the front) / अथ (and) / पृष्ठतः (from behind) / ते (to you) / नमः अस्तु (let there be salutation) / ते (to you) / सर्वतः (from every side) / एव (indeed) / सर्व (all) / अनन्त-वीर्य (endless power) / अमित-विक्रम (immeasurable might) / त्वम् (you) / सर्वम् (all) / समाप्नोषि (pervade, encompass) / ततः (therefore) / असि (are) / सर्वः (all)
11:40.meaning: Arjuna bows to Krishna from every direction, recognizing that Krishna pervades everything and is beyond ordinary limits.

11:41.shloka: सखेति मत्वा प्रसभं यदुक्तंहे कृष्ण हे यादव हे सखेति । अजानता महिमानं तवेदंमया प्रमादात्प्रणयेन वापि ॥
11:41.transliteration: सखा-इति (as a friend) / मत्वा (thinking) / प्रसभम् (boldly, carelessly) / यत् (whatever) / उक्तम् (was said) / हे कृष्ण (O Krishna) / हे यादव (O Yadava) / हे सखः-इति (O friend) / अजानता (not knowing) / महिमानम् (greatness) / तव (your) / इदम् (this) / मया (by me) / प्रमादात् (through negligence) / प्रणयेन (through affection) / वा अपि (or even) / यत् (whatever) / अवहास-अर्थम् (for the sake of joking) / असत्कृतः (treated disrespectfully) / असि (you are) / विहार-शय्या-आसन-भोजनेषु (in recreation, sleep, seat, and भोजन) / एकः अथवा अपि (alone or even) / अच्युत (O infallible one) / तत् (that) / समक्षम (in the presence) / तत् क्षामये (that I ask forgiveness) / त्वाम् (from you) / अहम् (I) / अप्रमेयम् (immeasurable one)
11:41.meaning: Arjuna apologizes for treating Krishna casually as a friend, not knowing his true greatness. He asks forgiveness for every disrespectful word and act.

meanings.meaning: in recreation, sleep, seat, and भोजन

11:42.transliteration: यत् (whatever) च (and) अवहासार्थम् (for the sake of jest) असत्कृतः (shown disrespect) असि (you were) विहार-शय्या-आसन-भोजनेषु (in walking, resting, sitting, and eating) । एकः (alone) अथवा (or) अपि (also) अच्युत (O infallible one) तत्-समक्षम् (in that presence) तत् (that) क्षामये (I ask forgiveness) त्वाम् (you) अहम् (I) अप्रमेयम् (immeasurable one) ॥
11:42.meaning: Arjuna asks Krishna to forgive every careless or disrespectful thing he said or did, in private and in public.

11:43.shloka: पितासि लोकस्य चराचरस्यत्वमस्य पूज्यश्च गुरुर्गरीयान् । न त्वत्समोऽस्त्यभ्यधिकः कुतोऽन्योलोकत्रयेऽप्यप्रतिमप्रभाव ॥
11:43.transliteration: पिता (father) / असि (are) / लोकस्य (of the world) / चराचरस्य (of the moving and unmoving) / त्वम् (you) / अस्य (of this) / पूज्यः (worthy of worship) / च (and) / गुरुः (teacher) / गरीयान् (the greatest) / न (not) / त्वत्समः (equal to you) / अस्ति (there is) / अभ्यधिकः (greater) / कुतः (how) / अन्यः (another) / लोकत्रये (in the three worlds) / अपि (even) / अप्रतिमप्रभाव (of unmatched power)
11:43.meaning: Arjuna recognizes Krishna as the father, teacher, and highest object of reverence for all beings. No one in the three worlds is equal to him.

11:44.shloka: तस्मात्प्रणम्य प्रणिधाय कायंप्रसादये त्वामहमीशमीड्यम् । पितेव पुत्रस्य सखेव सख्युःप्रियः प्रियायार्हसि देव सोढुम् ॥
11:44.transliteration: तस्मात् (therefore) / प्रणम्य (bowing down) / प्रणिधाय (placing down) / कायम् (the body) / प्रसादये (I seek to please) / त्वाम् (You) / अहम् (I) / ईशम् (the Lord) / ईड्यम् (worthy of praise) / पितेव (like a father) / पुत्रस्य (of a son) / सखा इव (like a friend) / सख्युः (of a friend) / प्रियः (a beloved) / प्रियायाः (of a beloved) / अर्हसि (You should) / देव (O radiant one) / सोढुम् (to bear / forgive)
11:44.meaning: Arjuna bows fully to Krishna and asks for forgiveness, saying Krishna should bear his disrespect the way loved ones bear each other’s faults.

11:45.shloka: अदृष्टपूर्वं हृषितोऽस्मि दृष्ट्वाभयेन च प्रव्यथितं मनो मे । तदेव मे दर्शय देव रूपंप्रसीद देवेश जगन्निवास ॥
11:45.transliteration: अदृष्टपूर्वम् (never seen before) / हृषितः (delighted) / अस्मि (I am) / दृष्ट्वा (having seen) / अभयेन (with fearlessness) / च (and) / प्रव्यथितम् (disturbed) / मनः (mind) / मे (my) / तत् (that) / एव (only) / मे (to me) / दर्शय (show) / देव (O divine one) / रूपम् (form) / प्रसीद (be gracious) / देवेश (Lord of lords) / जगन्निवास (dwelling of the world)
11:45.meaning: Arjuna is overwhelmed by Krishna's cosmic form. He asks to see the familiar, gentle form again because the vast vision fills him with both awe and fear.

11:46.shloka: किरीटिनं गदिनं चक्रहस्तमिच्छामि त्वां द्रष्टुमहं तथैव । तेनैव रूपेण चतुर्भुजेनसहस्रबाहो भव विश्वमूर्ते ॥
11:46.transliteration: किरीटिनम् (crowned) / गदिनम् (with mace) / चक्रहस्तम् (with discus in hand) / इच्छामि (I want) / त्वाम् (you) / द्रष्टुम् (to see) / अहम् (I) / तथा एव (just so, exactly) / तेन एव (by that very) / रूपेण (form) / चतुर्भुजेन (with four arms) / सहस्रबाहो (O thousand-armed one) / भव (be) / विश्वमूर्ते (O universal form)
11:46.meaning: Arjuna asks Krishna to return to the familiar four-armed form with crown, mace, and discus. The overwhelming cosmic form has moved him, but he longs for a shape he can meet with devotion.

11:47.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचमया प्रसन्नेन तवार्जुनेदंरूपं परं दर्शितमात्मयोगात् । तेजोमयं विश्वमनन्तमाद्यंयन्मे त्वदन्येन न दृष्टपूर्वम् ॥
11:47.transliteration: श्रीभगवानुवाच (the Lord said) / मया (by me) / प्रसन्नेन (being pleased) / तव (to you) / अर्जुन (Arjuna) / इदम् (this) / रूपम् (form) / परम् (supreme) / दर्शितम् (shown) / आत्मयोगात् (through my own power) / तेजोमयम् (full of radiance) / विश्वम् (all) / अनन्तम् (endless) / आद्यम् (original) / यत् (which) / मे (of me) / त्वदन्येन (by anyone other than you) / न (not) / दृष्टपूर्वम् (seen before)
11:47.meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna that he has revealed this radiant, original, endless form through his own power, and no one else has seen it before.

11:48.shloka: न वेदयज्ञाध्ययनैर्न दानैर्न च क्रियाभिर्न तपोभिरुग्रैः । एवंरूपः शक्य अहं नृलोकेद्रष्टुं त्वदन्येन कुरुप्रवीर ॥
11:48.transliteration: न (not) / वेदयज्ञाध्ययनैः (by Veda-study and sacrifices) / न (not) / दानैः (by gifts) / न (not) / च (and) / क्रियाभिः (by actions) / न (not) / तपोभिः (by austerities) / उग्रैः (severe) / एवंरूपः (of this form) / शक्यः (possible) / अहम् (I) / नृलोके (in the human world) / द्रष्टुम् (to be seen) / त्वदन्येन (by anyone other than you) / कुरुप्रवीर (O best of the Kurus)
11:48.meaning: Krishna says his cosmic form cannot be seen through rituals, study, gifts, or harsh austerity. Only Arjuna, by divine grace, has that vision.

11:49.shloka: मा ते व्यथा मा च विमूढभावोदृष्ट्वा रूपं घोरमीदृङ्ममेदम् । व्यपेतभीः प्रीतमनाः पुनस्त्वंतदेव मे रूपमिदं प्रपश्य ॥
11:49.transliteration: मा (do not) / ते (your) / व्यथा (distress) / मा (do not) / च (and) / विमूढभावः (confused state) / दृष्ट्वा (having seen) / रूपम् (form) / घोरम् (fierce) / ईदृक् (such) / मम (my) / इदम् (this) / व्यपेतभीः (fear gone) / प्रीतमनाः (with pleased mind) / पुनः (again) / त्वम् (you) / तदेव (that very) / मे (my) / रूपम् (form) / इदम् (this) / प्रपश्य (look upon)
11:49.meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna not to panic after seeing the terrifying form. Fear and confusion should fall away, and Arjuna should look again with a calm, glad mind.

11:50.shloka: सञ्जय उवाचइत्यर्जुनं वासुदेवस्तथोक्त्वास्वकं रूपं दर्शयामास भूयः । आश्वासयामास च भीतमेनंभूत्वा पुनः सौम्यवपुर्महात्मा ॥
11:50.transliteration: सञ्जय उवाच (Sanjaya said) / इति (thus) / अर्जुनम् (to Arjuna) / वासुदेवः (Vasudeva) / तथा उक्त्वा (having spoken thus) / स्वकम् (his own) / रूपम् (form) / दर्शयामास (showed) / भूयः (again) / आश्वासयामास (reassured) / च (and) / भीतम् (the frightened one) / एनम् (him) / भूत्वा (having become) / पुनः (again) / सौम्य-वपुः (gentle-bodied / gentle form) / महात्मा (the great one)
11:50.meaning: After speaking to Arjuna, Krishna showed his gentler form again and calmed Arjuna’s fear.

11:51.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचदृष्ट्वेदं मानुषं रूपं तवसौम्यं जनार्दन । इदानीमस्मि संवृत्तः सचेताः प्रकृतिं गतः ॥
11:51.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / दृष्ट्वा (having seen) / इदम् (this) / मानुषम् (human) / रूपम् (form) / तव (your) / सौम्यम् (gentle) / जनार्दन (Janardana) / इदानीम् (now) / अस्मि (I am) / संवृत्तः (restored) / सचेताः (fully conscious, calm) / प्रकृतिम् (natural state) / गतः (gone to, returned)
11:51.meaning: Arjuna feels settled again after seeing Krishna’s gentle human form. The terrifying cosmic vision has passed, and his mind returns to normal.

11:52.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचसुदुर्दर्शमिदं रूपं दृष्टवानसि यन्मम । देवा अप्यस्य रूपस्य नित्यं दर्शनकाङ्क्षिणः ॥
11:52.transliteration: श्रीभगवानुवाच (the Lord said) / सुदुर्दर्शम् (very hard to behold) / इदम् (this) / रूपम् (form) / दृष्टवान् (seen) / असि (you have) / यत् (which) / मम (of mine) / देवाः (radiant beings) / अपि (even) / अस्य (of this) / रूपस्य (of form) / नित्यम् (always) / दर्शनकाङ्क्षिणः (desiring to see)
11:52.meaning: Krishna says Arjuna has seen a form that is extremely rare to behold. Even the radiant beings always long to see it.

11:53.shloka: नाहं वेदैर्न तपसा न दानेन न चेज्यया । शक्य एवंविधो द्रष्टुं दृष्टवानसि मां यथा ॥
11:53.transliteration: न अहं (not I) / वेदैः (by the Vedas) / न तपसा (not by austerity) / न दानेन (not by charity) / न च ईज्यया (and not by sacrifice) / शक्यः (possible) / एवम्-विधः (of this kind) / द्रष्टुम् (to be seen) / दृष्टवान् असि (you have seen) / माम् (me) / यथा (as)
11:53.meaning: Krishna says that this divine form cannot be fully reached through scripture, austerity, charity, or ritual alone. Arjuna has seen it because a deeper devotion has opened the way.

11:54.shloka: भक्त्या त्वनन्यया शक्यमहमेवंविधोऽर्जुन । ज्ञातुं दृष्टुं च तत्त्वेन प्रवेष्टुं च परंतप ॥
11:54.transliteration: भक्त्या (through devotion) / तु (but) / अनन्यया (undivided) / शक्यः (possible) / अहम् (I) / एवंविधः (of this kind) / अर्जुन (Arjuna) / ज्ञातुम् (to know) / द्रष्टुम् (to see) / च (and) / तत्त्वेन (in essence) / प्रवेष्टुम् (to enter) / च (and) / परंतप (O scorcher of foes)
11:54.meaning: Krishna says that his true form can be known, seen, and entered only through single-pointed devotion, not by ordinary effort alone.

11:55.shloka: मत्कर्मकृन्मत्परमो मद्भक्तः सङ्गवर्जितः । निर्वैरः सर्वभूतेषु यः स मामेति पाण्डव ॥
11:55.transliteration: मत्कर्मकृत् (doing action for Me) / मत्परमः (having Me as the highest) / मद्भक्तः (My devotee) / सङ्गवर्जितः (free from attachment) / निर्वैरः (without hostility) / सर्वभूतेषु (toward all beings) / यः (who) / सः (that one) / माम् (Me) / एति (comes) / पाण्डव (O Pandava)
11:55.meaning: The one who serves Krishna with full devotion, without attachment or hostility toward any being, reaches him.

12:1.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचएवं सततयुक्ता ये भक्तास्त्वां पर्युपासते । येचाप्यक्षरमव्यक्तं तेषां के योगवित्तमाः ॥
12:1.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / एवम् (thus) / सततयुक्ताः (constantly devoted) / ये (those who) / भक्ताः (devotees) / त्वाम् (You) / पर्युपासते (worship) / ये (those who) / च (and) / अपि (also) / अक्षरम् (the imperishable) / अव्यक्तम् (the unmanifest) / तेषाम् (of them) / के (which) / योगवित्तमाः (most accomplished in yoga)
12:1.meaning: Arjuna asks whether devoted worship of Krishna or worship of the imperishable, formless reality is the higher yoga.

12:2.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचमय्यावेश्य मनो ये मां नित्ययुक्ता उपासते । श्रद्धया परयोपेतास्ते मे युक्ततमा मताः ॥
12:2.transliteration: मयि (in me) / आवेश्य (placing) / मनः (mind) / ये (who) / माम् (me) / नित्ययुक्ताः (always united) / उपासते (worship) / श्रद्धया (with trust) / परया (highest) / उपेताः (endowed with) / ते (they) / मे (to me / in my view) / युक्ततमाः (most devotedly united) / मताः (are considered)
12:2.meaning: Krishna says that the people who keep their mind on him, worship him steadily, and approach him with the highest trust are the best yogis.

12:3.shloka: ये त्वक्षरमनिर्देश्यमव्यक्तं पर्युपासते । सर्वत्रगमचिन्त्यं च कूटस्थमचलं ध्रुवम् ॥
12:3.transliteration: ये (those who) / तु (but) / अक्षरम् (the imperishable) / अनिर्देश्यम् (the indefinable) / अव्यक्तम् (the unmanifest) / पर्युपासते (worship all around) / सर्वत्रगम् (all-pervading) / अचिन्त्यम् (unthinkable) / च (and) / कूटस्थम् (unchanging) / अचलम् (unmoving) / ध्रुवम् (eternal)
12:3.meaning: People who worship the imperishable, unmanifest reality by controlling their senses and seeing it everywhere ultimately reach Krishna.

12:4.shloka: संनियम्येन्द्रियग्रामं सर्वत्र समबुद्धयः । ते प्राप्नुवन्ति मामेव सर्वभूतहिते रताः ॥
12:4.transliteration: संनियम्य (having restrained) / इन्द्रियग्रामम् (the whole group of senses) / सर्वत्र (everywhere) / समबुद्धयः (those with equal understanding) / ते (they) / प्राप्नुवन्ति (attain) / माम् (me) / एव (alone) / सर्वभूतहिते (in the welfare of all beings) / रताः (delighting, devoted)
12:4.meaning: Control the senses, stay even-minded with everyone, and care for the good of all beings. That is the way to reach Krishna.

meanings.meaning: in the welfare of all beings

12:5.transliteration: क्लेशः (difficulty) / अधिकतरः (greater) / तेषाम् (for them) / अव्यक्तासक्तचेतसाम् (of those whose minds are attached to the unmanifest) । अव्यक्ता (unmanifest) / हि (indeed) / गतिः (path/goal) / दुःखम् (painful/difficult) / देहवद्भिः (by embodied beings) / अवाप्यते (is reached)
12:5.meaning: Holding the unmanifest as your focus is harder than it looks. For people still identified with the body, that subtle path is difficult to reach.

meanings.meaning: of those whose minds are attached to the unmanifest) । अव्यक्ता (unmanifest

12:6.shloka: ये तु सर्वाणि कर्माणि मयि संन्यस्य मत्पराः । अनन्येनैव योगेन मां ध्यायन्त उपासते ॥
12:6.transliteration: ये (those who) / तु (but) / सर्वाणि (all) / कर्माणि (actions) / मयि (in Me) / संन्यस्य (having renounced, offered) / मत्पराः (those devoted to Me as the highest) / अनन्येन (with no other) / एव (indeed) / योगेन (with yoga) / माम् (Me) / ध्यायन्तः (meditating) / उपासते (worship)
12:6.meaning: People who give all their actions to Krishna, make him their highest aim, and worship him with single-minded devotion are described here.

meanings.meaning: those devoted to Me as the highest

12:7.shloka: तेषामहं समुद्धर्ता मृत्युसंसारसागरात् । भवामि नचिरात्पार्थ मय्यावेशितचेतसाम् ॥
12:7.transliteration: तेषाम् (of those) / अहम् (I) / समुद्धर्ता (the rescuer) / मृत्यु-संसार-सागरात् (from the ocean of mortal existence) / भवामि (become) / नचिरात् (not after long) / पार्थ (O Partha) / मयि (in me) / आवेशित-चेतसाम् (of those whose minds are fixed)
12:7.meaning: Those who place their minds fully on Krishna are not left to struggle alone. He becomes the one who rescues them from the cycle of repeated death and return.

meanings.meaning: from the ocean of mortal existence
of those whose minds are fixed

12:8.shloka: मय्येव मन आधत्स्व मयि बुद्धिं निवेशय । निवसिष्यसि मय्येव अत ऊर्ध्वं न संशयः ॥
12:8.transliteration: मयि (in me) / एव (alone) / मनः (mind) / आधत्स्व (place) / मयि (in me) / बुद्धिम् (discerning mind) / निवेशय (fix) / निवसिष्यसि (you will dwell) / मयि (in me) / एव (alone) / अतः (therefore) / ऊर्ध्वम् (afterward) / न (not) / संशयः (doubt)
12:8.meaning: Keep your mind and discerning mind fixed on Krishna. Then you will abide in him, with no doubt about the result.

12:9.shloka: अथ चित्तं समाधातुं न शक्नोषि मयि स्थिरम् । अभ्यासयोगेन ततो मामिच्छाप्तुं धनञ्जय ॥
12:9.transliteration: अथ (then) / चित्तम् (mind) / समाधातुम् (to fix steadily) / न (not) / शक्नोषि (you are able) / मयि (in me) / स्थिरम् (steadily) / अभ्यासयोगेन (through practice yoga) / ततः (then) / माम् (me) / इच्छ (seek/desire) / आप्तुम् (to attain) / धनञ्जय (Dhananjaya)
12:9.meaning: If steady devotion feels too hard, begin with practice. Keep returning to the divine again and again until the mind learns to stay.

12:10.shloka: अभ्यासेऽप्यसमर्थोऽसि मत्कर्मपरमो भव । मदर्थमपि कर्माणि कुर्वन् सिद्धिमवाप्स्यसि ॥
12:10.transliteration: अभ्यासेऽपि (even in practice) / असमर्थः (unable) / असि (you are) / मत्कर्मपरः (devoted to My work) / भव (become) / मदर्थम् (for My sake) / अपि (even) / कर्माणि (actions) / कुर्वन् (doing) / सिद्धिम् (perfection) / अवाप्स्यसि (you will attain)
12:10.meaning: If steady practice is too hard, dedicate your actions to Krishna instead. Even ordinary work offered with devotion can lead to fulfillment.

12:11.shloka: अथैतदप्यशक्तोऽसि कर्तुं मद्योगमाश्रितः । सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं ततः कुरु यतात्मवान् ॥
12:11.transliteration: अथ (then) / एतत् अपि (this also) / अशक्तः असि (you are unable) / कर्तुम् (to do) / मद्योगम् आश्रितः (taking refuge in my yoga) / सर्व-कर्म-फल-त्यागम् (renunciation of all fruits of action) / ततः (then) / कुरु (do) / यत-आत्मवान् (self-controlled)
12:11.meaning: If even devotion-filled practice feels too hard, give up the need to own the results of your actions. Stay controlled and let the work be enough.

meanings.meaning: renunciation of all fruits of action

12:12.transliteration: श्रेयो (better) / हि (indeed) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / अभ्यासात् (than practice) / ज्ञानात् (than knowledge) / ध्यानम् (meditation) / विशिष्यते (is superior) / ध्यानात् (than meditation) / कर्मफलत्यागः (renunciation of the fruits of action) / त्यागात् (from renunciation) / शान्तिः (peace) / अनन्तरम् (immediately after)
12:12.meaning: Practice, study, and meditation all help, but letting go of personal reward is even higher. That release brings immediate calm.

meanings.meaning: renunciation of the fruits of action

12:13.shloka: अद्वेष्टा सर्वभूतानां मैत्रः करुण एव च । निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः समदुःखसुखः क्षमी ॥
12:13.transliteration: अद्वेष्टा (free from hatred) / सर्वभूतानां (of all beings) / मैत्रः (friendly) / करुणः (compassionate) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / निर्ममः (without possessiveness) / निरहङ्कारः (without ego) / समदुःखसुखः (equal in pain and pleasure) / क्षमी (forgiving)
12:13.meaning: A devoted person does not hate anyone, treats everyone as a friend, stays compassionate, and does not cling to possessions or ego. Such a person remains steady through joy and sorrow and forgives easily.

12:14.shloka: सन्तुष्टः सततं योगी यतात्मा दृढनिश्चयः । मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ॥
12:14.transliteration: सन्तुष्टः (content) / सततम् (always) / योगी (yogi) / यतात्मा (self-controlled) / दृढनिश्चयः (firm in resolve) / मयि (in Me) / अर्पित (offered) / मनः (mind) / बुद्धिः (discerning mind) / यः (who) / मत्-भक्तः (My devotee) / सः (that one) / मे (to Me) / प्रियः (dear)
12:14.meaning: A true devotee stays content, controls the body and mind, keeps firm resolve, and places both mind and discernment in Krishna.

12:15.shloka: यस्मान्नोद्विजते लोको लोकान्नोद्विजते च यः । हर्षामर्षभयोद्वेगैर्मुक्तो यः स च मे प्रियः ॥
12:15.transliteration: यस्मात् (from whom) / न उद्विजते (does not become agitated) / लोकः (the world) / लोकात् (from the world) / न उद्विजते (does not become agitated) / च (and) / यः (who) / हर्ष (delight) / अमर्ष (resentment) / भय (fear) / उद्वेगैः (with agitations) / मुक्तः (freed) / यः (who) / सः (he) / च (and) / मे (to me) / प्रियः (dear)
12:15.meaning: A beloved devotee does not unsettle others and is not easily unsettled by others. Freedom from excitement, envy, fear, and agitation is part of true devotion.

12:16.shloka: अनपेक्षः शुचिर्दक्ष उदासीनो गतव्यथः । सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ॥
12:16.transliteration: अनपेक्षः (without expectation) / शुचिः (pure) / दक्षः (skilled) / उदासीनः (detached) / गतव्यथः (free from distress) / सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी (one who has given up all undertakings) / यः (who) / मद्भक्तः (my devotee) / सः (that one) / मे (to me) / प्रियः (dear)
12:16.meaning: Krishna says his beloved devotee has no craving, stays inwardly clean, acts skillfully, remains detached, and drops anxious new beginnings.

meanings.meaning: one who has given up all undertakings

12:17.shloka: यो न हृष्यति न द्वेष्टि न शोचति न काङ्क्षति । शुभाशुभपरित्यागी भक्ितमान्यः स मे प्रियः ॥
12:17.transliteration: यः (who) / न (not) / हृष्यति (rejoices) / न (not) / द्वेष्टि (hates) / न (not) / शोचति (grieves) / न (not) / काङ्क्षति (craves) / शुभाशुभपरित्यागी (one who has abandoned good-and-bad attachment) / भक्तिमान् (devoted) / यः (one who) / सः (that one) / मे (to me) / प्रियः (dear)
12:17.meaning: A devoted person stays free from excitement, hatred, sorrow, and craving. They do not cling to outcomes or label actions as personally theirs.

meanings.meaning: one who has abandoned good-and-bad attachment

12:18.shloka: समः शत्रौ च मित्रे च तथा मानापमानयोः । शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु समः सङ्गविवर्जितः ॥
12:18.transliteration: समः (equal) / शत्रौ (to enemy) / च (and) / मित्रे (to friend) / च (and) / तथा (likewise) / मानापमानयोः (in honor and dishonor) / शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु (in cold and heat, pleasure and pain) / समः (equal) / सङ्गविवर्जितः (free from attachment)
12:18.meaning: A devoted person stays steady through praise and blame, comfort and discomfort, friendship and hostility, without clinging to either side.

meanings.meaning: in cold and heat, pleasure and pain

12:19.shloka: तुल्यनिन्दास्तुतिर्मौनी सन्तुष्टो येनकेनचित् । अनिकेतः स्थिरमतिर्भक्ितमान्मे प्रियो नरः ॥
12:19.transliteration: तुल्य (equal) / निन्दा (blame) / स्तुति (praise) / मौनी (silent) / सन्तुष्टः (content) / येनकेनचित् (by whatever means) / अनिकेतः (without a home) / स्थिरमतिः (steady-minded) / भक्तिमान् (devoted) / मे (to me) / प्रियः (dear) / नरः (person)
12:19.meaning: A devoted person stays steady through praise and blame, remains quiet, accepts whatever comes, feels no possessiveness about home, and keeps a firm mind.

12:20.shloka: ये तु धर्म्यामृतमिदं यथोक्तं पर्युपासते । श्रद्दधाना मत्परमा भक्तास्तेऽतीव मे प्रियाः ॥
12:20.transliteration: ये (those who) / तु (indeed) / धर्म्यामृतम् (nectar of righteous living) / इदम् (this) / यथा उक्तम् (as spoken) / पर्युपासते (diligently practice) / श्रद्दधानाः (with trust) / मत्परमाः (having Me as the highest) / भक्ताः (devotees) / ते (they) / अतीव (very) / मे (to Me) / प्रियाः (dear)
12:20.meaning: People who trust Krishna, hold him as their highest aim, and faithfully practice this teaching are very dear to him.

13:1.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचप्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव क्षेत्रं क्षेत्रज्ञमेव च । एतद्वेदितुमिच्छामि ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं च केशव ॥
13:1.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / प्रकृतिं (nature) / पुरुषं (the person) / च एव (and also) / क्षेत्रं (the field) / क्षेत्रज्ञम् (the knower of the field) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / एतत् (this) / वेदितुम् (to know) / इच्छामि (I wish) / ज्ञानम् (wisdom) / ज्ञेयम् (what is to be known) / च (and) / केशव (O Keshava)
13:1.meaning: Arjuna asks Krishna to explain nature, the body as the field, the knower of that field, wisdom, and the object of knowledge.

13:2.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचइदं शरीरं कौन्तेय क्षेत्रमित्यभिधीयते । एतद्यो वेत्ति तं प्राहुः क्षेत्रज्ञ इति तद्विदः ॥
13:2.transliteration: इदम् (this) / शरीरम् (body) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / क्षेत्रम् (field) / इति (thus) / अभिधीयते (is called) / एतत् (this) / यः (who) / वेत्ति (knows) / तम् (that one) / प्राहुः (they call) / क्षेत्रज्ञः (field-knower) / इति (thus) / तद्विदः (those who know this)
13:2.meaning: The body is the field where life unfolds. The one who knows the body is called the knower of the field.

13:3.shloka: क्षेत्रज्ञं चापि मां विद्धि सर्वक्षेत्रेषु भारत । क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोर्ज्ञानं यत्तज्ज्ञानं मतं मम ॥
13:3.transliteration: क्षेत्रज्ञम् (knower of the field) / च (and) / अपि (also) / माम् (Me) / विद्धि (know) / सर्वक्षेत्रेषु (in all fields) / भारत (Arjuna) / क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोः (of field and knower of the field) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / यत् (which) / तत् (that) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / मतम् (considered) / मम (My)
13:3.meaning: Know Krishna as the knower present in every field. Real knowledge is understanding both the field and the one who knows it.

meanings.meaning: of field and knower of the field

13:4.shloka: तत्क्षेत्रं यच्च यादृक् च यद्विकारि यतश्च यत् । स च यो यत्प्रभावश्च तत्समासेन मे श्रृणु ॥
13:4.transliteration: तत् (that) / क्षेत्रम् (the field) / यत् (which) / च (and) / यादृक् (what sort) / च (and) / यत्-विकारि (having what changes) / यतः (from where) / च (and) / यत् (what) / सः (that) / च (and) / यः (who) / यत्-प्रभावः (having what power) / च (and) / तत् (that) / समासेन (in brief) / मे (from me) / श्रृणु (hear)
13:4.meaning: Krishna is about to explain, in brief, what the field is like, how it changes, where it comes from, and what the knower of the field is like.

13:5.shloka: ऋषिभिर्बहुधा गीतं छन्दोभिर्विविधैः पृथक् । ब्रह्मसूत्रपदैश्चैव हेतुमद्भिर्विनिश्िचतैः ॥
13:5.transliteration: ऋषिभिः (by sages) / बहुधा (in many ways) / गीतम् (sung) / छन्दोभिः (by hymns or meters) / विविधैः (in varied forms) / पृथक् (separately) / ब्रह्मसूत्रपदैः (by Brahma Sutra verses) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / हेतुमद्भिः (by reasoned, cause-based) / विनिश्चितैः (by conclusive, well-determined [statements])
13:5.meaning: Sages, Vedic hymns, and the Brahma Sutras all point to the same understanding of the field and the knower.

13:6.shloka: महाभूतान्यहङ्कारो बुद्धिरव्यक्तमेव च । इन्द्रियाणि दशैकं च पञ्च चेन्द्रियगोचराः ॥
13:6.transliteration: महाभूतानि (the five great elements) / अहङ्कारः (ego) / बुद्धिः (intellect) / अव्यक्तम् (the unmanifest) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / इन्द्रियाणि (the senses) / दश (ten) / एकम् (one) / च (and) / पञ्च (five) / च (and) / इन्द्रियगोचराः (sense-objects)
13:6.meaning: The body-mind world is made of many parts: elements, ego, intellect, senses, mind, and sense-objects. Krishna is naming the field so Arjuna can see it clearly.

13:7.shloka: इच्छा द्वेषः सुखं दुःखं सङ्घातश्चेतनाधृतिः । एतत्क्षेत्रं समासेन सविकारमुदाहृतम् ॥
13:7.transliteration: इच्छा (desire) / द्वेषः (aversion) / सुखं (pleasure) / दुःखं (pain) / सङ्घातः (the body as a composite) / चेतना (consciousness) / धृतिः (steadiness) / एतत् (this) / क्षेत्रम् (field) / समासेन (briefly) / सविकारम् (with changes) / उदाहृतम् (is described)
13:7.meaning: The field includes the body, feelings, impulses, and the mind's steadiness. All of these are part of what changes, not the knower behind them.

13:8.transliteration: अमानित्वम् (absence of pride) / अदम्भित्वम् (absence of pretence) / अहिंसा (nonviolence) / क्षान्तिः (forgiveness) / आर्जवम् (simplicity) / आचार्य-उपासनम् (service to the teacher) / शौचम् (purity) / स्थैर्यम् (steadiness) / आत्म-विनिग्रहः (self-control)
13:8.meaning: A disciplined life is marked by humility, honesty, nonviolence, forgiveness, simplicity, respect for the teacher, cleanliness, steadiness, and self-mastery.

13:9.transliteration: इन्द्रियार्थेषु (in sense objects) / वैराग्यम् (dispassion) / अहङ्कारः (ego) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / जन्म (birth) / मृत्यु (death) / जरा (old age) / व्याधि (disease) / दुःख (suffering) / दोषानुदर्शनम् (seeing the fault repeatedly)
13:9.meaning: Let go of craving for sensory pleasures, drop ego, and keep seeing how birth, death, old age, and illness bring suffering.

13:10.transliteration: असक्ति (non-attachment) / अनभिष्वङ्गः (no clinging) / पुत्रदारगृहादिषु (in sons, wife, home, and such) / नित्यं (always) / च (and) / समचित्तत्वम् (evenness of mind) / इष्टानिष्टोपपत्तिषु (in the arrival of the desired and undesired)
13:10.meaning: Do not cling to family and possessions, and keep your mind steady whether things go your way or not.

meanings.meaning: in sons, wife, home, and such
in the arrival of the desired and undesired

13:11.transliteration: मयि (in me) / च (and) / अनन्ययोगेन (with undivided devotion) / भक्ितः (devotion) / अव्यभिचारिणी (unwavering) / विविक्तदेशसेवित्वम् (fondness for secluded places) / अरतिः (lack of delight) / जनसंसदि (in crowds)
13:11.meaning: Steady devotion to Krishna, a love that does not drift, grows with a taste for solitude and little interest in crowds.

13:12.transliteration: अध्यात्म-ज्ञान (knowledge of the inner reality) / नित्यत्वम् (constantness) / तत्त्व-ज्ञान-अर्थ-दर्शनम् (seeing the meaning of knowledge of reality) / एतत् (this) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / इति (thus) / प्रोक्तम् (is called) / अज्ञानम् (ignorance) / यत् (which) / अतः अन्यथा (other than this)
13:12.meaning: Real knowledge means staying aware of the inner reality and seeing the supreme reality everywhere. Anything that misses this is ignorance.

meanings.meaning: seeing the meaning of knowledge of reality

13:13.shloka: ज्ञेयं यत्तत्प्रवक्ष्यामि यज्ज्ञात्वाऽमृतमश्नुते । अनादिमत्परं ब्रह्म न सत्तन्नासदुच्यते ॥
13:13.transliteration: ज्ञेयम् (what is to be known) / यत् (which) / तत् (that) / प्रवक्ष्यामि (I will clearly tell) / यत् (which) / ज्ञात्वा (having known) / अमृतम् (freedom / immortality) / अश्नुते (attains) / अनादिमत् (beginningless) / परम् (supreme) / ब्रह्म (supreme reality) / न (not) / सत् (existent) / तत् (that) / न (not) / असत् (nonexistent) / उच्यते (is called)
13:13.meaning: Krishna says he will explain the highest reality that can be known. Knowing it brings freedom. It is beginningless, supreme, and beyond ordinary ideas of real and unreal.

13:14.shloka: सर्वतः पाणिपादं तत्सर्वतोऽक्षिशिरोमुखम् । सर्वतः श्रुतिमल्लोके सर्वमावृत्य तिष्ठति ॥
13:14.transliteration: सर्वतः (everywhere) / पाणिपादम् (hands and feet) / तत् (that) / सर्वतः (everywhere) / अक्षिशिरोमुखम् (eyes, heads, and faces) / सर्वतः (everywhere) / श्रुतिमत् (having ears) / लोके (in the world) / सर्वम् (all) / आवृत्य (covering) / तिष्ठति (stands, remains)
13:14.meaning: The supreme reality is not limited to one form or place. It is present everywhere, expressed through all beings and all senses.

13:15.shloka: सर्वेन्द्रियगुणाभासं सर्वेन्द्रियविवर्जितम् । असक्तं सर्वभृच्चैव निर्गुणं गुणभोक्तृ च ॥
13:15.transliteration: सर्वेन्द्रियगुणाभासम् (shining through the qualities of all senses) / सर्वेन्द्रियविवर्जितम् (devoid of all senses) / असक्तम् (unattached) / सर्वभृत् (supporter of all) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / निर्गुणम् (without qualities) / गुणभोक्तृ (enjoyer of qualities) / च (and)
13:15.meaning: The supreme being has no senses, yet makes all perception possible. It is free from attachment, sustains everything, stands beyond the three qualities, and still experiences them all.

meanings.meaning: shining through the qualities of all senses

13:16.shloka: बहिरन्तश्च भूतानामचरं चरमेव च । सूक्ष्मत्वात्तदविज्ञेयं दूरस्थं चान्तिके च तत् ॥
13:16.transliteration: बहिः (outside) / अन्तः (inside) / च (and) / भूतानाम् (of beings) / अचरम् (unmoving) / चरम् (moving) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / सूक्ष्मत्वात् (because of subtlety) / तत् (that) / अविज्ञेयम् (not knowable) / दूरस्थम् (far away) / च (and) / अन्तिके (near) / च (and) / तत् (that)
13:16.meaning: The supreme reality fills everything, inside and outside all beings. It is both moving and still, yet too subtle to grasp directly. It feels far away and also very near.

13:17.shloka: अविभक्तं च भूतेषु विभक्तमिव च स्थितम् । भूतभर्तृ च तज्ज्ञेयं ग्रसिष्णु प्रभविष्णु च ॥
13:17.transliteration: अविभक्तम् (undivided) / च (and) / भूतेषु (among beings) / विभक्तम् (divided) / इव (as if) / च (and) / स्थितम् (situated) / भूतभर्तृ (bearer of beings) / च (and) / तत् (that) / ज्ञेयम् (to be known) / ग्रसिष्णु (devouring) / प्रभविष्णु (originating) / च (and)
13:17.meaning: The supreme reality seems split into many beings, but it is actually one. It is the source, support, and end of all life.

13:18.shloka: ज्योतिषामपि तज्ज्योतिस्तमसः परमुच्यते । ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं ज्ञानगम्यं हृदि सर्वस्य विष्ठितम् ॥
13:18.transliteration: ज्योतिषाम् (of lights) / अपि (even) / तत् (that) / ज्योतिः (light) / तमसः (of darkness) / परम् (beyond) / उच्यते (is said) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / ज्ञेयम् (the knowable) / ज्ञानगम्यम् (reached through knowledge) / हृदि (in the heart) / सर्वस्य (of all) / विष्ठितम् (abiding)
13:18.meaning: The supreme reality is brighter than all light, beyond ignorance, and present in every heart. It is both what is known and the means of knowing it.

13:19.shloka: इति क्षेत्रं तथा ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं चोक्तं समासतः । मद्भक्त एतद्विज्ञाय मद्भावायोपपद्यते ॥
13:19.transliteration: इति (thus) / क्षेत्रम् (the field) / तथा (and also) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / ज्ञेयम् (the knowable) / च (and) / उक्तम् (has been spoken) / समासतः (briefly) / मद्भक्तः (my devotee) / एतत् (this) / विज्ञाय (having known) / मद्भावाय (to my state) / उपपद्यते (attains)
13:19.meaning: Krishna summarizes the teaching on the body, knowledge, and the object of knowledge. He says that a devotee who truly understands this reaches his own state.

13:20.shloka: प्रकृतिं पुरुषं चैव विद्ध्यनादी उभावपि । विकारांश्च गुणांश्चैव विद्धि प्रकृतिसंभवान् ॥
13:20.transliteration: प्रकृतिम् (Prakriti) / पुरुषम् (Purusha) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / विद्धि (know) / अनादी (beginningless) / उभौ (both) / अपि (also) / विकारान् (modifications) / च (and) / गुणान् (qualities) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / विद्धि (know) / प्रकृति-संभवान् (born from Prakriti)
13:20.meaning: Both nature and the conscious knower are without beginning. All changes and qualities come from nature, not from the knower.

13:21.shloka: कार्यकारणकर्तृत्वे हेतुः प्रकृतिरुच्यते । पुरुषः सुखदुःखानां भोक्तृत्वे हेतुरुच्यते ॥
13:21.transliteration: कार्यकारणकर्तृत्वे (in the doership of body, senses, and action) / हेतुः (cause) / प्रकृतिः (nature) / उच्यते (is said) । / पुरुषः (the inner being) / सुखदुःखानाम् (of pleasure and pain) / भोक्तृत्वे (in the state of experiencer) / हेतुः (cause) / उच्यते (is said)
13:21.meaning: The body, senses, and actions belong to nature. Pleasure and pain are experienced by the inner being. Krishna separates the changing process from the one who experiences it.

meanings.meaning: in the doership of body, senses, and action

13:22.shloka: पुरुषः प्रकृतिस्थो हि भुङ्क्ते प्रकृतिजान्गुणान् । कारणं गुणसङ्गोऽस्य सदसद्योनिजन्मसु ॥
13:22.transliteration: पुरुषः (the inner being) / प्रकृतिस्थः (situated in nature) / हि (indeed) / भुङ्क्ते (experiences) / प्रकृतिजान् (born of nature) / गुणान् (qualities) / कारणम् (cause) / गुणसङ्गः (attachment to qualities) / अस्य (of this) / सत्-असत् (good and bad) / योनि-जन्मसु (in womb-births)
13:22.meaning: The inner being, while tied to nature, experiences its changing qualities. Clinging to those qualities is what leads to repeated births in favorable and unfavorable forms.

13:23.shloka: उपद्रष्टाऽनुमन्ता च भर्ता भोक्ता महेश्वरः । परमात्मेति चाप्युक्तो देहेऽस्मिन्पुरुषः परः ॥
13:23.transliteration: उपद्रष्टा (witness) / अनुमन्ता (consenter) / च (and) / भर्ता (supporter) / भोक्ता (enjoyer) / महेश्वरः (great lord) / परमात्मा (supreme self) / इति (thus) / च (and) / अपि (also) / उक्तः (called) / देहे (in the body) / अस्मिन् (this) / पुरुषः (inner self) / परः (beyond)
13:23.meaning: The inner self seems to take on many roles because it is linked with the body, but in its true nature it remains beyond the body.

13:24.shloka: य एवं वेत्ति पुरुषं प्रकृतिं च गुणैःसह । सर्वथा वर्तमानोऽपि न स भूयोऽभिजायते ॥
13:24.transliteration: यः (who) / एवं (thus) / वेत्ति (knows) / पुरुषम् (the purusha) / प्रकृतिम् (prakriti) / च (and) / गुणैः सह (together with the qualities) / सर्वथा (in every way) / वर्तमानः अपि (even while acting) / न (not) / सः (that one) / भूयः (again) / अभिजायते (is born)
13:24.meaning: A person who clearly sees the true self, the body-nature, and the qualities that move them is no longer caught in rebirth, even while living normally.

13:25.shloka: ध्यानेनात्मनि पश्यन्ति केचिदात्मानमात्मना । अन्ये सांख्येन योगेन कर्मयोगेन चापरे ॥
13:25.transliteration: ध्यानेन (through meditation) / आत्मनि (in the self) / पश्यन्ति (they see) / केचित् (some) / आत्मानम् (the true self) / आत्मना (by the self) / अन्ये (others) / सांख्येन (through discernment) / योगेन (through yoga) / कर्मयोगेन (through the yoga of action) / च (and) / अपरे (others)
13:25.meaning: People realize the true self in different ways: through meditation, through clear discernment, or through the yoga of action.

13:26.shloka: अन्ये त्वेवमजानन्तः श्रुत्वाऽन्येभ्य उपासते । तेऽपि चातितरन्त्येव मृत्युं श्रुतिपरायणाः ॥
13:26.transliteration: अन्ये (others) / तु (but) / एवम् (thus) / अजानन्तः (not knowing) / श्रुत्वा (having heard) / अन्येभ्यः (from others) / उपासते (they worship) / ते (they) / अपि (also) / च (and) / अति-तरन्ति (cross beyond) / एव (indeed) / मृत्युम् (death) / श्रुति-परायणाः (devoted to hearing)
13:26.meaning: Some people do not understand these practices directly, but they listen to wise teachers and worship with trust. Even they can go beyond death through devoted listening.

13:27.transliteration: यावत् (whatever) / सञ्जायते (comes into being) / किञ्चित् (anything) / सत्त्वम् (being) / स्थावर-जङ्गमम् (moving and unmoving) / क्षेत्र-क्षेत्रज्ञ-संयोगात् (from the union of field and knower-of-field) / तत् (that) / विद्धि (know) / भरतर्षभ (O best among the Bharatas)
13:27.meaning: All living forms, moving or still, arise from the meeting of the field and the knower of the field.

meanings.meaning: from the union of field and knower-of-field

13:28.shloka: समं सर्वेषु भूतेषु तिष्ठन्तं परमेश्वरम् । विनश्यत्स्वविनश्यन्तं यः पश्यति स पश्यति ॥
13:28.transliteration: समम् (equally) / सर्वेषु (in all) / भूतेषु (beings) / तिष्ठन्तम् (standing) / परमेश्वरम् (the supreme being) / विनश्यत्सु (among the perishing) / अविनश्यन्तम् (imperishable) / यः (who) / पश्यति (sees) / सः (that one) / पश्यति (truly sees)
13:28.meaning: True seeing means recognizing one unchanging presence in every living being, even while bodies and forms keep changing and disappearing.

13:29.shloka: समं पश्यन्हि सर्वत्र समवस्थितमीश्वरम् । न हिनस्त्यात्मनाऽऽत्मानं ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥
13:29.transliteration: समम् (equally) / पश्यन् (seeing) / हि (indeed) / सर्वत्र (everywhere) / समवस्थितम् (equally situated) / ईश्वरम् (the divine ruler) / न (not) / हिनस्ति (hurts) / आत्मना (by the true self) / आत्मानम् (the true self) / ततः (therefore) / याति (goes) / पराम् (highest) / गतिम् (state, destination)
13:29.meaning: Seeing the same divine presence everywhere ends inner self-harm and leads to the highest state.

13:30.shloka: प्रकृत्यैव च कर्माणि क्रियमाणानि सर्वशः । यः पश्यति तथाऽऽत्मानमकर्तारं स पश्यति ॥
13:30.transliteration: प्रकृत्या (by nature) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / कर्माणि (actions) / क्रियमाणानि (being done) / सर्वशः (in every way) / यः (who) / पश्यति (sees) / तथा (thus) / आत्मानम् (the true self) / अकर्तारम् (as non-doer) / सः (that one) / पश्यति (sees)
13:30.meaning: All actions are carried out by nature, while the true self remains a non-doer. Clear seeing means noticing this distinction directly.

13:31.shloka: यदा भूतपृथग्भावमेकस्थमनुपश्यति । तत एव च विस्तारं ब्रह्म सम्पद्यते तदा ॥
13:31.transliteration: यदा (when) / भूतपृथग्भावम् (the separate states of beings) / एकस्थम् (standing in one place) / अनुपश्यति (one sees clearly) / ततः (then) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / विस्तारम् (expansion) / ब्रह्म (the supreme reality) / सम्पद्यते (attains) / तदा (at that time)
13:31.meaning: When a person sees the many forms of living beings as arising from one underlying reality, that person reaches that reality.

13:32.shloka: अनादित्वान्निर्गुणत्वात्परमात्मायमव्ययः । शरीरस्थोऽपि कौन्तेय न करोति न लिप्यते ॥
13:32.transliteration: अनादित्वात् (because of beginninglessness) / निर्गुणत्वात् (because of being beyond the gunas) / परमात्मा (supreme self) / अयम् (this) / अव्ययः (imperishable) । / शरीरस्थः (abiding in the body) / अपि (even though) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / न (not) / करोति (acts) / न (not) / लिप्यते (is stained)
13:32.meaning: The true self has no beginning, is beyond the three qualities of nature, and cannot be destroyed. Even while living in a body, it neither performs actions nor gets tainted by them.

meanings.meaning: because of being beyond the gunas

13:33.shloka: यथा सर्वगतं सौक्ष्म्यादाकाशं नोपलिप्यते । सर्वत्रावस्थितो देहे तथाऽऽत्मा नोपलिप्यते ॥
13:33.transliteration: यथा (just as) / सर्वगतं (all-pervading) / सौक्ष्म्यात् (because of subtlety) / आकाशं (space) / न (not) / उपलिप्यते (is tainted) / सर्वत्रावस्थितः (present everywhere) / देहे (in the body) / तथा (so) / आत्मा (the true self) / न (not) / उपलिप्यते (is tainted)
13:33.meaning: The true self is present in every body, but it is never stained or affected by the body it inhabits.

13:34.shloka: यथा प्रकाशयत्येकः कृत्स्नं लोकमिमं रविः । क्षेत्रं क्षेत्री तथा कृत्स्नं प्रकाशयति भारत ॥
13:34.transliteration: यथा (as) / प्रकाशयति (illuminates) / एकः (one) / कृत्स्नं (whole) / लोकम् (world) / इमम् (this) / रविः (sun) / क्षेत्रम् (field) / क्षेत्री (knower of the field) / तथा (so) / कृत्स्नम् (whole) / प्रकाशयति (illuminates) / भारत (O Bharata)
13:34.meaning: The one who knows the field makes the whole field known, just as one sun lights up the entire world.

13:35.shloka: क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञयोरेवमन्तरं ज्ञानचक्षुषा । भूतप्रकृतिमोक्षं च ये विदुर्यान्ति ते परम् ॥
13:35.transliteration: क्षेत्र-क्षेत्रज्ञयोः (of the field and the knower of the field) / एवम् (thus) / अन्तरम् (difference) / ज्ञानचक्षुषा (with the eye of knowledge) / भूतप्रकृतिमोक्षम् (release from material nature) / च (and) / ये (those who) / विदुः (know) / यान्ति (go) / ते (they) / परम् (to the supreme)
13:35.meaning: True seeing separates the changing field from the one who knows it. That clear discrimination frees a person from identification with nature and leads to the highest state.

meanings.meaning: of the field and the knower of the field

14:1.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचपरं भूयः प्रवक्ष्यामि ज्ञानानां ज्ञानमुत्तमम् । यज्ज्ञात्वा मुनयः सर्वे परां सिद्धिमितो गताः ॥
14:1.transliteration: श्री भगवानुवाच (the Blessed Lord said) / परम् (highest) / भूयः (again) / प्रवक्ष्यामि (I will declare) / ज्ञानानाम् (among knowledges) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / उत्तमम् (highest) / यत् (which) / ज्ञात्वा (having known) / मुनयः (sages) / सर्वे (all) / पराम् (supreme) / सिद्धिम् (perfection) / इतः (from here) / गताः (have gone)
14:1.meaning: Krishna says he will teach the highest kind of knowledge, the one that has taken wise seekers to the greatest fulfillment.

14:2.shloka: इदं ज्ञानमुपाश्रित्य मम साधर्म्यमागताः । सर्गेऽपि नोपजायन्ते प्रलये न व्यथन्ति च ॥
14:2.transliteration: इदम् (this) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / उपाश्रित्य (taking refuge in) / मम (my) / साधर्म्यम् (likeness) / आगताः (having attained) / सर्गे (at creation) / अपि (even) / न (not) / उपजायन्ते (are born) / प्रलये (at dissolution) / न (not) / व्यथन्ति (are troubled) / च (and)
14:2.meaning: With this knowledge, a person becomes like Krishna in nature. Such a person is no longer shaken by cosmic creation or destruction.

14:3.shloka: मम योनिर्महद्ब्रह्म तस्मिन् गर्भं दधाम्यहम् । संभवः सर्वभूतानां ततो भवति भारत ॥
14:3.transliteration: मम (my) / योनिः (womb, source) / महत्-ब्रह्म (great nature) / तस्मिन् (in that) / गर्भम् (seed, embryo) / दधामि (I place) / अहम् (I) / संभवः (coming into being, birth) / सर्व-भूतानाम् (of all beings) / ततः (from that) / भवति (comes to be) / भारत (O Bharata)
14:3.meaning: All living forms arise from the union of primal nature and the seed placed within it. Krishna is explaining how birth itself unfolds.

14:4.shloka: सर्वयोनिषु कौन्तेय मूर्तयः सम्भवन्ति याः । तासां ब्रह्म महद्योनिरहं बीजप्रदः पिता ॥
14:4.transliteration: सर्वयोनिषु (in all wombs/forms of birth) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / मूर्तयः (forms/bodies) / सम्भवन्ति (come into being) / याः (which) / तासाम् (of those) / ब्रह्म (material nature) / महत् (great) / योनिः (womb/source) / अहम् (I) / बीजप्रदः (seed-giving) / पिता (father)
14:4.meaning: All living forms come from the same source of nature, while Krishna says he is the seed-giving father behind their coming into being.

14:5.shloka: सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति गुणाः प्रकृतिसंभवाः । निबध्नन्ति महाबाहो देहे देहिनमव्ययम् ॥
14:5.transliteration: सत्त्वं (sattva) / रजः (rajas) / तमः (tamas) / इति (thus) / गुणाः (qualities) / प्रकृतिसंभवाः (born of nature) / निबध्नन्ति (bind) / महाबाहो (mighty-armed one) / देहे (in the body) / देहिनम् (the embodied one) / अव्ययम् (imperishable)
14:5.meaning: The three qualities born from nature keep the imperishable inner being tied to the body.

14:6.shloka: तत्र सत्त्वं निर्मलत्वात्प्रकाशकमनामयम् । सुखसङ्गेन बध्नाति ज्ञानसङ्गेन चानघ ॥
14:6.transliteration: तत्र (there, among them) / सत्त्वम् (sattva) / निर्मलत्वात् (because of purity) / प्रकाशकम् (luminous, illuminating) / अनामयम् (free from affliction) / सुखसङ्गेन (through attachment to happiness) / बध्नाति (binds) / ज्ञानसङ्गेन (through attachment to knowledge) / च (and) / अनघ (O sinless one)
14:6.meaning: Even purity can become a chain when you cling to comfort or to being wise. Sattva brings light, but attachment to that light still binds.

14:7.shloka: रजो रागात्मकं विद्धि तृष्णासङ्गसमुद्भवम् । तन्निबध्नाति कौन्तेय कर्मसङ्गेन देहिनम् ॥
14:7.transliteration: रजः (rajas) / रागात्मकम् (of the nature of passion) / विद्धि (know) / तृष्णा (craving) / सङ्ग (attachment) / समुद्भवम् (born from) / तत् (that) / निबध्नाति (binds) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / कर्मसङ्गेन (through attachment to action) / देहिनम् (the embodied being)
14:7.meaning: Restless desire and attachment create rajas. That force traps a person by making them cling to action and its rewards.

14:8.shloka: तमस्त्वज्ञानजं विद्धि मोहनं सर्वदेहिनाम् । प्रमादालस्यनिद्राभिस्तन्निबध्नाति भारत ॥
14:8.transliteration: तमः (darkness/inertia) / तु (indeed) / अज्ञानजम् (born of ignorance) / विद्धि (know) / मोहनम् (deluding) / सर्वदेहिनाम् (of all embodied beings) / प्रमाद-आलस्य-निद्राभिः (through heedlessness, laziness, and sleep) / तत् (that) / निबध्नाति (binds) / भारत (Arjuna)
14:8.meaning: Tamas is the force of confusion and inertia. It clouds judgment and traps people through carelessness, laziness, and sleepiness.

14:9.shloka: सत्त्वं सुखे सञ्जयति रजः कर्मणि भारत । ज्ञानमावृत्य तु तमः प्रमादे सञ्जयत्युत ॥
14:9.transliteration: सत्त्वम् (sattva) / सुखे (in pleasure) / सञ्जयति (binds) / रजः (rajas) / कर्मणि (in action) / भारत (O Bharata) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / आवृत्य (covering) / तु (but) / तमः (tamas) / प्रमादे (in negligence) / सञ्जयति (binds) / उत (also)
14:9.meaning: Three forces shape human behavior differently: one lures with pleasure, one drives constant activity, and one hides understanding while pulling a person into carelessness.

14:10.shloka: रजस्तमश्चाभिभूय सत्त्वं भवति भारत । रजः सत्त्वं तमश्चैव तमः सत्त्वं रजस्तथा ॥
14:10.transliteration: रजः (passion) / तमः (inertia) / च (and) / अभिभूय (having overpowered) / सत्त्वम् (purity, clarity) / भवति (becomes) / भारत (Arjuna) / रजः (passion) / सत्त्वम् (purity, clarity) / तमः (inertia) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / तमः (inertia) / सत्त्वम् (purity, clarity) / रजः (passion) / तथा (likewise)
14:10.meaning: The three qualities keep replacing one another. When one becomes stronger, the others are pushed down.

14:11.shloka: सर्वद्वारेषु देहेऽस्मिन्प्रकाश उपजायते । ज्ञानं यदा तदा विद्याद्विवृद्धं सत्त्वमित्युत ॥
14:11.transliteration: सर्वद्वारेषु (in all the gates) / देहेऽस्मिन् (in this body) / प्रकाशः (light) / उपजायते (arises) / ज्ञानम् (understanding) / यदा (when) / तदा (then) / विद्यात् (one should know) / विवृद्धम् (grown strong) / सत्त्वम् (sattva) / इति (thus) / उत (indeed)
14:11.meaning: When the senses and mind become bright, clear, and wise, that is a sign that sattva is increasing.

14:12.shloka: लोभः प्रवृत्तिरारम्भः कर्मणामशमः स्पृहा । रजस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे भरतर्षभ ॥
14:12.transliteration: लोभः (greed) / प्रवृत्तिः (activity, urge) / आरम्भः (beginning) / कर्मणाम् (of actions) / अशमः (restlessness, lack of peace) / स्पृहा (desire, craving) / रजसि (in rajas) / एतानि (these) / जायन्ते (are born) / विवृद्धे (when increased) / भरतर्षभ (O best of the Bharatas)
14:12.meaning: When rajas becomes strong, greed, restless activity, constant starting, and craving appear.

14:13.shloka: अप्रकाशोऽप्रवृत्तिश्च प्रमादो मोह एव च । तमस्येतानि जायन्ते विवृद्धे कुरुनन्दन ॥
14:13.transliteration: अप्रकाशः (absence of light) / अप्रवृत्तिः (absence of activity) / प्रमादः (negligence) / मोहः (delusion) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / तमसि (in tamas) / एतानि (these) / जायन्ते (are born) / विवृद्धे (when increased) / कुरुनन्दन (O descendant of Kuru)
14:13.meaning: When tamas becomes stronger, a person loses clarity, becomes inactive, starts neglecting things, and falls into confusion.

14:14.shloka: यदा सत्त्वे प्रवृद्धे तु प्रलयं याति देहभृत् । तदोत्तमविदां लोकानमलान्प्रतिपद्यते ॥
14:14.transliteration: यदा (when) / सत्त्वे (in sattva) / प्रवृद्धे (having become strong) / तु (indeed) / प्रलयम् (dissolution; death) / याति (goes) / देहभृत् (the embodied one) / तदा (then) / उत्तमविदाम् (of the knowers of the highest) / लोकान् (worlds) / अमलान् (pure, stainless) / प्रतिपद्यते (attains)
14:14.meaning: If a person dies while sattva is strongest, that being reaches pure realms associated with the wise.

meanings.meaning: of the knowers of the highest

14:15.shloka: रजसि प्रलयं गत्वा कर्मसङ्गिषु जायते । तथा प्रलीनस्तमसि मूढयोनिषु जायते ॥
14:15.transliteration: रजसि (in rajas) / प्रलयं गत्वा (having gone to dissolution, having died) / कर्मसङ्गिषु (among those attached to action) / जायते (is born) । / तथा (similarly) / प्रलीनः (having dissolved, having died) / तमसि (in tamas) / मूढयोनिषु (among deluded births/forms) / जायते (is born)
14:15.meaning: The state of mind at death shapes the kind of birth that follows. Restless desire leads to another life of attachment; dull confusion leads to a lower, more deluded condition.

meanings.meaning: having gone to dissolution, having died

14:16.shloka: कर्मणः सुकृतस्याहुः सात्त्विकं निर्मलं फलम् । रजसस्तु फलं दुःखमज्ञानं तमसः फलम् ॥
14:16.transliteration: कर्मणः (of action) / सुकृतस्य (of well-done, wholesome) / आहुः (they say) / सात्त्विकं (sattvic, pure) / निर्मलं (stainless, clear) / फलम् (fruit, result) / रजसः (of rajas) / तु (but) / फलम् (fruit, result) / दुःखम् (suffering) / अज्ञानम् (ignorance) / तमसः (of tamas) / फलम् (fruit, result)
14:16.meaning: Wholesome action leads to a clear, pure result. Restless action leads to suffering. Dark, confused action leads to ignorance and delusion.

14:17.shloka: सत्त्वात्सञ्जायते ज्ञानं रजसो लोभ एव च । प्रमादमोहौ तमसो भवतोऽज्ञानमेव च ॥
14:17.transliteration: सत्त्वात् (from sattva) / सञ्जायते (is born) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / रजसः (from rajas) / लोभः (greed) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / प्रमादमोहौ (negligence and delusion) / तमसः (from tamas) / भवन्ति (arise) / अज्ञानम् (ignorance) / एव (indeed) / च (and)
14:17.meaning: A clear, balanced nature gives rise to understanding. Restless nature produces greed. Heavy, dull nature produces carelessness, delusion, and ignorance.

14:18.shloka: ऊर्ध्वं गच्छन्ति सत्त्वस्था मध्ये तिष्ठन्ति राजसाः । जघन्यगुणवृत्तिस्था अधो गच्छन्ति तामसाः ॥
14:18.transliteration: ऊर्ध्वं (upward) / गच्छन्ति (go) / सत्त्वस्था (those established in sattva) / मध्ये (in the middle) / तिष्ठन्ति (remain) / राजसाः (those in rajas) / जघन्यगुणवृत्तिस्थाः (those established in the lowest quality’s movement) / अधः (downward) / गच्छन्ति (go) / तामसाः (those in tamas)
14:18.meaning: People shaped by clarity rise to higher states. People ruled by restlessness stay in the middle. People sunk in dullness fall into lower states.

meanings.meaning: those established in the lowest quality’s movement

14:19.shloka: नान्यं गुणेभ्यः कर्तारं यदा द्रष्टानुपश्यति । गुणेभ्यश्च परं वेत्ति मद्भावं सोऽधिगच्छति ॥
14:19.transliteration: नान्यम् (no other) / गुणेभ्यः (than the qualities) / कर्तारम् (doer) / यदा (when) / द्रष्टा (the seer) / अनुपश्यति (sees clearly) / गुणेभ्यः (than the qualities) / च (and) / परम् (beyond) / वेत्ति (knows) / मद्भावम् (my nature) / सः (that one) / अधिगच्छति (attains)
14:19.meaning: Clear seeing reveals that action belongs to the three qualities, not to a separate personal doer. When a person sees beyond them, they come to Krishna's nature.

14:20.transliteration: गुणान् (qualities) / एतान् (these) / अतीत्य (having gone beyond) / त्रीन् (three) / देही (the embodied one) / देहसमुद्भवान् (born from the body) / जन्म-मृत्यु-जरा-दुःखैः (by birth, death, old age, and sorrow) / विमुक्तः (freed) / अमृतम् (immortality) / अश्नुते (attains)
14:20.meaning: A person who rises above the three qualities that shape the body is no longer bound by birth, death, old age, or sorrow.

meanings.meaning: by birth, death, old age, and sorrow

14:21.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचकैर्लिंगैस्त्रीन्गुणानेतानतीतो भवति प्रभो । किमाचारः कथं चैतांस्त्रीन्गुणानतिवर्तते ॥
14:21.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / कैः लिंगैः (by what signs) / त्रीन् (three) / गुणान् (qualities) / एतान् (these) / अतीतः (gone beyond) / भवति (becomes) / प्रभो (O Lord) / किम् आचारः (what is the conduct) / कथम् च (and how) / एतान् (these) / त्रीन् गुणान् (three qualities) / अतिवर्तते (crosses beyond)
14:21.meaning: Arjuna asks how to recognize someone who has gone beyond the three qualities, how that person behaves, and how such freedom is reached.

14:22.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचप्रकाशं च प्रवृत्तिं च मोहमेव च पाण्डव । न द्वेष्टि सम्प्रवृत्तानि न निवृत्तानि काङ्क्षति ॥
14:22.transliteration: श्री भगवानुवाच (the Blessed One said) / प्रकाशं (light) / च (and) / प्रवृत्तिं (activity) / च (and) / मोहमेव (and delusion itself) / च (and) / पाण्डव (O Pandava) / न द्वेष्टि (does not hate) / सम्प्रवृत्तानि (when arisen) / न निवृत्तानि (when ceased) / काङ्क्षति (desire)
14:22.meaning: A person beyond the three gunas stays even-minded. When clarity, energy, or confusion appear, there is no hatred. When they fade, there is no craving.

14:23.shloka: उदासीनवदासीनो गुणैर्यो न विचाल्यते । गुणा वर्तन्त इत्येव योऽवतिष्ठति नेङ्गते ॥
14:23.transliteration: उदासीनवद् (like one indifferent) / आसीनः (seated, remaining) / गुणैः (by the qualities) / यः (who) / न (not) / विचाल्यते (is shaken) / गुणाः (the qualities) / वर्तन्ते (are acting) / इति (thus) / एव (only) / यः (who) / अवतिष्ठति (remains established) / न (not) / ईङ्गते (moves)
14:23.meaning: A wise person stays steady, not shaken by the three qualities, and sees that they are only moving among themselves.

14:24.transliteration: समदुःखसुखः (equal in sorrow and joy) / स्वस्थः (established in the true self) / समलोष्टाश्मकाञ्चनः (equal toward clod, stone, and gold) / तुल्यप्रियाप्रियः (equal toward the liked and the disliked) / धीरः (the steadfast one) / तुल्यनिन्दात्मसंस्तुतिः (equal toward blame and self-praise)
14:24.meaning: A truly steady person does not get shaken by pleasure, pain, praise, blame, wealth, or ordinary objects. They stay centered in themselves.

meanings.meaning: equal toward clod, stone, and gold
equal toward the liked and the disliked

14:25.shloka: मानापमानयोस्तुल्यस्तुल्यो मित्रारिपक्षयोः । सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी गुणातीतः स उच्यते ॥
14:25.transliteration: मानापमानयोः (in honor and dishonor) / तुल्यः (equal) / तुल्यः (equal) / मित्रारिपक्षयोः (toward friend and enemy sides) / सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी (one who has abandoned all beginnings of action) / गुणातीतः (beyond the gunas) / सः (that one) / उच्यते (is called)
14:25.meaning: A truly steady person stays the same in praise or insult, with friends or enemies, and no longer starts actions from personal craving. That person rises beyond the three gunas.

meanings.meaning: one who has abandoned all beginnings of action

14:26.shloka: मां च योऽव्यभिचारेण भक्ितयोगेन सेवते । स गुणान्समतीत्यैतान् ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ॥
14:26.transliteration: मां (Me) / च (and) / यः (who) / अव्यभिचारेण (with unwavering) / भक्ति-योगेन (through devotion-yoga) / सेवते (serves) / सः (that one) / गुणान् (the qualities) / समतीत्य (having fully transcended) / एतान् (these) / ब्रह्म-भूयाय (for becoming Brahman) / कल्पते (is fit)
14:26.meaning: Steady devotion to Krishna lifts a person beyond the three qualities of nature and makes that person fit for union with the supreme reality.

14:27.shloka: ब्रह्मणो हि प्रतिष्ठाऽहममृतस्याव्ययस्य च । शाश्वतस्य च धर्मस्य सुखस्यैकान्तिकस्य च ॥
14:27.transliteration: ब्रह्मणः (of Brahman) / हि (indeed) / प्रतिष्ठा (foundation, support) / अहम् (I am) / अमृतस्य (of immortality) / अव्ययस्य (of the imperishable) / च (and) / शाश्वतस्य (of the eternal) / च (and) / धर्मस्य (of dharma) / सुखस्य (of bliss) / ऐकान्तिकस्य (of exclusive, ultimate) / च (and)
14:27.meaning: Krishna says he is the support behind Brahman, immortality, the imperishable, eternal dharma, and ultimate bliss.

15:1.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचऊर्ध्वमूलमधःशाखमश्वत्थं प्राहुरव्ययम् । छन्दांसि यस्य पर्णानि यस्तं वेद स वेदवित् ॥
15:1.transliteration: श्रीभगवानुवाच (the Blessed Lord said) / ऊर्ध्वमूलम् (with roots above) / अधःशाखम् (with branches below) / अश्वत्थम् (the banyan-like world tree) / प्राहुः (they say) / अव्ययम् (imperishable) / छन्दांसि (the Vedas) / यस्य (whose) / पर्णानि (leaves) / यः (who) / तम् (that) / वेद (knows) / सः (he) / वेदवित् (knower of the Vedas)
15:1.meaning: The world is like an upside-down tree: its roots are above, its branches below, and the Vedas are its leaves. Truly understanding this structure means understanding the deepest teaching.

15:2.shloka: अधश्चोर्ध्वं प्रसृतास्तस्य शाखा     गुणप्रवृद्धा विषयप्रवालाः । अधश्च मूलान्यनुसन्ततानि     कर्मानुबन्धीनि मनुष्यलोके ॥
15:2.transliteration: अधः (below) / च (and) / ऊर्ध्वं (above) / प्रसृताः (spread out) / तस्य (of that) / शाखाः (branches) / गुणप्रवृद्धाः (grown by the qualities) / विषयप्रवालाः (with sense-objects as shoots) / अधः (below) / च (and) / मूलानि (roots) / अनुसन्ततानि (extended everywhere) / कर्मानुबन्धीनि (bound by action) / मनुष्यलोके (in the human world)
15:2.meaning: The world-tree spreads everywhere, fed by the three qualities and by desire for sense-pleasures. Its roots reach through human life through action and its consequences.

15:3.shloka: न रूपमस्येह तथोपलभ्यते    नान्तो न चादिर्न च संप्रतिष्ठा । अश्वत्थमेनं सुविरूढमूल    मसङ्गशस्त्रेण दृढेन छित्त्वा ॥
15:3.transliteration: न (not) / रूपम् (form) / अस्य (of this) / इह (here) / तथा (thus) / उपलभ्यते (is perceived) / न (not) / अन्तः (end) / न (not) / च (and) / आदिः (beginning) / न (not) / च (and) / संप्रतिष्ठा (fixed base) / अश्वत्थम् (ashvattha tree) / एनम् (this) / सु-विरूढ-मूलम् (deeply rooted) / असङ्ग-शस्त्रेण (with the weapon of detachment) / दृढेन (strong) / छित्त्वा (having cut)
15:3.meaning: The world-tree cannot be grasped as a normal thing, because it has no clear beginning, end, or stable base. Cut through it with firm detachment.

15:4.shloka: ततः पदं तत्परिमार्गितव्य    यस्मिन्गता न निवर्तन्ति भूयः । तमेव चाद्यं पुरुषं प्रपद्ये    यतः प्रवृत्तिः प्रसृता पुराणी ॥
15:4.transliteration: ततः (therefore) / पदम् (state) / तत् (that) / परिमार्गितव्यम् (should be sought) / यस्मिन् (in which) / गताः (gone) / न (not) / निवर्तन्ति (return) / भूयः (again) / तम् (that) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / आद्यम् (original) / पुरुषम् (Person) / प्रपद्ये (I take refuge) / यतः (from whom) / प्रवृत्तिः (emanation) / प्रसृता (spread forth) / पुराणी (ancient)
15:4.meaning: Beyond the changing world lies a supreme state where return stops. Krishna says that state should be sought, and he himself takes refuge in the original Person from whom creation unfolds.

15:5.shloka: निर्मानमोहा जितसङ्गदोषा    अध्यात्मनित्या विनिवृत्तकामाः । द्वन्द्वैर्विमुक्ताः सुखदुःखसंज्ञै    र्गच्छन्त्यमूढाः पदमव्ययं तत् ॥
15:5.transliteration: निर्मानमोहाः (free from pride and delusion) / जितसङ्गदोषाः (who have conquered the fault of attachment) / अध्यात्मनित्याः (ever devoted to the true self) / विनिवृत्तकामाः (whose desires have fully withdrawn) / द्वन्द्वैः (from the pairs of opposites) / विमुक्ताः (freed) / सुखदुःखसंज्ञैः (named pleasure and pain) / गच्छन्ति (they reach) / अमूढाः (the undeluded) / पदम् (state) / अव्ययम् (imperishable) / तत् (that)
15:5.meaning: Those who lose pride, cut attachment, stay rooted in the true self, and rise above pleasure and pain reach the imperishable state.

meanings.meaning: who have conquered the fault of attachment
ever devoted to the true self

15:6.shloka: न तद्भासयते सूर्यो न शशाङ्को न पावकः । यद्गत्वा न निवर्तन्ते तद्धाम परमं मम ॥
15:6.transliteration: न (not) / तत् (that) / भासयते (illumines) / सूर्यः (the sun) / न (not) / शशाङ्कः (the moon) / न (not) / पावकः (fire) / यत् (which) / गत्वा (having gone) / न (not) / निवर्तन्ते (they return) / तत् (that) / धाम (abode) / परमम् (supreme) / मम (Mine)
15:6.meaning: Krishna says his highest abode cannot be illuminated by ordinary light, and anyone who reaches it does not come back to the cycle of return.

15:7.shloka: ममैवांशो जीवलोके जीवभूतः सनातनः । मनःषष्ठानीन्द्रियाणि प्रकृतिस्थानि कर्षति ॥
15:7.transliteration: ममैव (my indeed) / अंशः (part) / जीवलोके (in the world of living beings) / जीवभूतः (become a living being) / सनातनः (eternal) / मनःषष्ठानि (mind as the sixth, together with the five) / इन्द्रियाणि (senses) / प्रकृतिस्थानि (situated in nature) / कर्षति (draws, pulls)
15:7.meaning: The living being is Krishna's eternal part, but it gets pulled by the mind and senses that belong to nature and mistakes them for itself.

meanings.meaning: in the world of living beings
mind as the sixth, together with the five

15:8.shloka: शरीरं यदवाप्नोति यच्चाप्युत्क्रामतीश्वरः । गृहीत्वैतानि संयाति वायुर्गन्धानिवाशयात् ॥
15:8.transliteration: शरीरम् (body) / यत् (which) / अवाप्नोति (attains) / यत् (which) / च अपि (and also) / उत्क्रामति (leaves) / ईश्वरः (the lord) / गृहीत्वा (having taken) / एतानि (these) / संयाति (goes forth) / वायुः (wind) / गन्धान् (fragrances) / इव (like) / आशयात् (from a source)
15:8.meaning: The embodied being leaves one body and enters another, carrying the mind and senses along, just as wind carries scent.

15:9.shloka: श्रोत्रं चक्षुः स्पर्शनं च रसनं घ्राणमेव च । अधिष्ठाय मनश्चायं विषयानुपसेवते ॥
15:9.transliteration: श्रोत्रम् (ear) / चक्षुः (eye) / स्पर्शनम् (touch) / रसनम् (taste) / घ्राणम् (smell) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / अधिष्ठाय (having taken hold of) / मनः (mind) / च (and) / अयम् (this) / विषयान् (objects) / उपसेवते (enjoys, experiences)
15:9.meaning: The inner being uses the mind as a base and experiences the world through the five senses.

15:10.shloka: उत्क्रामन्तं स्थितं वापि भुञ्जानं वा गुणान्वितम् । विमूढा नानुपश्यन्ति पश्यन्ति ज्ञानचक्षुषः ॥
15:10.transliteration: उत्क्रामन्तम् (departing) / स्थितम् (remaining) / वा (or) / अपि (also) / भुञ्जानम् (enjoying) / वा (or) / गुणान्वितम् (endowed with the qualities) / विमूढाः (the deluded) / न (not) / अनुपश्यन्ति (perceive) / पश्यन्ति (see) / ज्ञानचक्षुषः (those with the eye of knowledge)
15:10.meaning: Ordinary sight cannot grasp the true self moving through bodies and experiences. Only clear understanding sees what is really happening.

meanings.meaning: those with the eye of knowledge

15:11.shloka: यतन्तो योगिनश्चैनं पश्यन्त्यात्मन्यवस्थितम् । यतन्तोऽप्यकृतात्मानो नैनं पश्यन्त्यचेतसः ॥
15:11.transliteration: यतन्तः (striving) / योगिनः (yogis) / च (and) / एनम् (this) / पश्यन्ति (see) / आत्मनि (in the self) / अवस्थितम् (abiding) । / यतन्तः (striving) / अपि (even) / अकृतात्मानः (those without a purified inner being) / न (not) / एनम् (this) / पश्यन्ति (see) / अचेतसः (the thoughtless)
15:11.meaning: People who have disciplined and purified themselves can realize the supreme reality within. Effort without inner refinement is not enough.

meanings.meaning: those without a purified inner being

15:12.shloka: यदादित्यगतं तेजो जगद्भासयतेऽखिलम् । यच्चन्द्रमसि यच्चाग्नौ तत्तेजो विद्धि मामकम् ॥
15:12.transliteration: यत् (which) / आदित्य-गतं (gone into the sun) / तेजः (radiance) / जगत् (the world) / भासयते (illumines) / अखिलम् (entire) / यत् (which) / चन्द्रमसि (in the moon) / यत् (which) / अग्नौ (in fire) / तत् (that) / तेजः (radiance) / विद्धि (know) / मामकम् (Mine)
15:12.meaning: Krishna says that the light shining in the sun, the moon, and fire belongs to him. All visible brilliance is a sign of his presence.

15:13.shloka: गामाविश्य च भूतानि धारयाम्यहमोजसा । पुष्णामि चौषधीः सर्वाः सोमो भूत्वा रसात्मकः ॥
15:13.transliteration: गाम् (the earth) / आविश्य (entering) / च (and) / भूतानि (beings) / धारयामि (I sustain) / अहम् (I) / ओजसा (with power) / पुष्णामि (I nourish) / च (and) / औषधीः (plants, herbs) / सर्वाः (all) / सोमः (the moon) / भूत्वा (having become) / रसात्मकः (of life-giving essence)
15:13.meaning: Krishna says he enters the earth to support all living beings, and he becomes the moon that nourishes all plants and herbs.

15:14.shloka: अहं वैश्वानरो भूत्वा प्राणिनां देहमाश्रितः । प्राणापानसमायुक्तः पचाम्यन्नं चतुर्विधम् ॥
15:14.transliteration: अहम् (I) / वैश्वानरः (the digestive fire) / भूत्वा (having become) / प्राणिनाम् (of living beings) / देहम् (the body) / आश्रितः (dwelling in) / प्राण-अपान-समायुक्तः (joined with the inward and outward breaths) / पचामि (I digest) / अन्नम् (food) / चतुर्विधम् (of four kinds)
15:14.meaning: Krishna says he lives inside every body as the force that digests food, working through the breaths that sustain life.

meanings.meaning: joined with the inward and outward breaths

15:15.shloka: सर्वस्य चाहं हृदि सन्निविष्टो    मत्तः स्मृतिर्ज्ञानमपोहनं च । वेदैश्च सर्वैरहमेव वेद्यो    वेदान्तकृद्वेदविदेव चाहम् ॥
15:15.transliteration: सर्वस्य (of all) / च (and) / अहम् (I) / हृदि (in the heart) / सन्निविष्टः (dwelling) / मत्तः (from Me) / स्मृतिः (memory) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / अपोहनम् (removal) / च (and) / वेदैः (through the Vedas) / च (and) / सर्वैः (all) / अहम् (I) / एव (indeed) / वेद्यः (to be known) / वेदान्तकृत् (maker of Vedanta) / वेदविद् (knower of the Vedas) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / अहम् (I)
15:15.meaning: Krishna says He lives in every being’s heart. Memory, understanding, and the clearing of confusion come from Him. All the Vedas point to Him, and He is their final knower.

15:16.shloka: द्वाविमौ पुरुषौ लोके क्षरश्चाक्षर एव च । क्षरः सर्वाणि भूतानि कूटस्थोऽक्षर उच्यते ॥
15:16.transliteration: द्वौ (two) / इमौ (these) / पुरुषौ (beings) / लोके (in the world) / क्षरः (perishable) / च (and) / अक्षरः (imperishable) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / क्षरः (perishable) / सर्वाणि (all) / भूतानि (beings) / कूटस्थः (steadfast) / अक्षरः (imperishable) / उच्यते (is called)
15:16.meaning: There are two kinds of existence here: what changes and what does not. Bodies pass away, but the unchanging reality within remains.

15:17.shloka: उत्तमः पुरुषस्त्वन्यः परमात्मेत्युदाहृतः । यो लोकत्रयमाविश्य बिभर्त्यव्यय ईश्वरः ॥
15:17.transliteration: उत्तमः (supreme) / पुरुषः (person/self) / तु (indeed) / अन्यः (other) / परमात्मा (supreme self) / इति (thus) / उदाहृतः (is called) । / यः (who) / लोकत्रयम् (the three worlds) / आविश्य (entering) / बिभर्ति (sustains) / अव्ययः (imperishable) / ईश्वरः (ruler)
15:17.meaning: Beyond the changing world and even beyond the unchanging witness stands the highest reality, which enters all three worlds and sustains them.

15:18.shloka: यस्मात्क्षरमतीतोऽहमक्षरादपि चोत्तमः । अतोऽस्मि लोके वेदे च प्रथितः पुरुषोत्तमः ॥
