15:18.transliteration: यस्मात् (because) / क्षरम् (the perishable) / अतीतः (beyond) / अहम् (I) / अक्षरात् (than the imperishable) / अपि (also) / च (and) / उत्तमः (higher) / अतः (therefore) / अस्मि (I am) / लोके (in the world) / वेदे (in the Veda) / च (and) / प्रथितः (well known) / पुरुषोत्तमः (Purushottama)
15:18.meaning: Krishna says he is beyond both the changing world and the unchanging principle behind it. That is why he is called the Supreme Self.

15:19.shloka: यो मामेवमसम्मूढो जानाति पुरुषोत्तमम् । स सर्वविद्भजति मां सर्वभावेन भारत ॥
15:19.transliteration: यः (who) / माम् (Me) / एवम् (thus) / असम्मूढः (not deluded) / जानाति (knows) / पुरुषोत्तमम् (as the Supreme Self) / सः (that one) / सर्ववित् (all-knowing) / भजति (worships) / माम् (Me) / सर्वभावेन (with all being) / भारत (O Bharata)
15:19.meaning: Knowing Krishna as the Supreme Self removes confusion and leads to wholehearted devotion. Clear understanding and complete worship become one movement.

15:20.shloka: इति गुह्यतमं शास्त्रमिदमुक्तं मयाऽनघ । एतद्बुद्ध्वा बुद्धिमान्स्यात्कृतकृत्यश्च भारत ॥
15:20.transliteration: इति (thus) / गुह्यतमम् (most secret) / शास्त्रम् (teaching) / इदम् (this) / उक्तम् (spoken) / मया (by me) / अनघ (O sinless one) / एतत् (this) / बुद्ध्वा (having known) / बुद्धिमान् (wise) / स्यात् (becomes) / कृतकृत्यः (one who has done what must be done) / च (and) / भारत (O descendant of Bharata)
15:20.meaning: Krishna says he has shared the most hidden teaching. A person who truly understands it becomes wise and feels complete, with nothing essential left undone.

meanings.meaning: one who has done what must be done

16:1.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचअभयं सत्त्वसंशुद्धिः ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थितिः । दानं दमश्च यज्ञश्च स्वाध्यायस्तप आर्जवम् ॥
16:1.transliteration: अभयम् (fearlessness) / सत्त्वसंशुद्धिः (purity of being) / ज्ञानयोगव्यवस्थितिः (steadfastness in the yoga of knowledge) / दानम् (giving) / दमः (self-control) / च (and) / यज्ञः (sacrifice) / च (and) / स्वाध्यायः (study of sacred texts) / तपः (austerity) / आर्जवम् (straightforwardness)
16:1.meaning: A divine life begins with fearlessness, inner purity, self-control, generosity, sacrifice, study, disciplined effort, and honesty.

meanings.meaning: steadfastness in the yoga of knowledge

16:2.shloka: अहिंसा सत्यमक्रोधस्त्यागः शान्तिरपैशुनम् । दया भूतेष्वलोलुप्त्वं मार्दवं ह्रीरचापलम् ॥
16:2.transliteration: अहिंसा (nonviolence) / सत्य (truth) / अक्रोधः (freedom from anger) / त्यागः (renunciation) / शान्तिः (calm) / अपैशुनम् (absence of slander) / दया (compassion) / भूतेषु (toward beings) / अलोलुप्त्वम् (freedom from greed) / मार्दवम् (gentleness) / ह्रीः (modesty) / अचापलम् (absence of restlessness)
16:2.meaning: A divine life is built from nonviolence, honesty, calm, compassion, restraint, and modest conduct.

16:3.shloka: तेजः क्षमा धृतिः शौचमद्रोहो नातिमानिता । भवन्ति सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातस्य भारत ॥
16:3.transliteration: तेजः (splendor) / क्षमा (forgiveness) / धृतिः (steadiness) / शौचम् (purity) / अद्रोहः (absence of hostility) / नातिमानिता (lack of pride) / भवन्ति (become) / सम्पदम् (attainment) / दैवीम् (divine) / अभिजातस्य (of one born) / भारत (O Bharata)
16:3.meaning: A person with noble qualities shows strength, forgiveness, steadiness, purity, no hostility, and no pride.

16:4.shloka: दम्भो दर्पोऽभिमानश्च क्रोधः पारुष्यमेव च । अज्ञानं चाभिजातस्य पार्थ सम्पदमासुरीम् ॥
16:4.transliteration: दम्भः (hypocrisy) / दर्पः (arrogance) / अभिमानः (self-importance) / च (and) / क्रोधः (anger) / पारुष्यम् (harshness) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / अज्ञानम् (ignorance) / च (and) / अभिजातस्य (of one born) / पार्थ (O Partha) / सम्पदम् (qualities) / आसुरीम् (demonic)
16:4.meaning: False display, pride, self-importance, anger, harsh speech, and ignorance are signs of a destructive nature.

16:5.shloka: दैवी सम्पद्विमोक्षाय निबन्धायासुरी मता । मा शुचः सम्पदं दैवीमभिजातोऽसि पाण्डव ॥
16:5.transliteration: दैवी (divine) / सम्पत् (nature or wealth) / विमोक्षाय (for freedom) / निबन्धाय (for bondage) / आसुरी (demonic) / मता (is held to be) / मा (do not) / शुचः (grieve) / सम्पदम् (nature or wealth) / दैवीम् (divine) / अभिजातः (born with) / असि (you are) / पाण्डव (Pandava)
16:5.meaning: Qualities that uplift a person lead to freedom, while qualities rooted in ego create bondage. Krishna tells Arjuna not to despair, because Arjuna already has the qualities that lead upward.

16:6.shloka: द्वौ भूतसर्गौ लोकेऽस्मिन् दैव आसुर एव च । दैवो विस्तरशः प्रोक्त आसुरं पार्थ मे श्रृणु ॥
16:6.transliteration: द्वौ (two) / भूतसर्गौ (classes of beings) / लोकेऽस्मिन् (in this world) / दैवः (divine) / आसुरः (shadowed) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / दैवः (the divine) / विस्तरशः (in detail) / प्रोक्तः (has been spoken) / आसुरम् (the shadowed) / पार्थ (Arjuna) / मे (from me) / श्रृणु (listen)
16:6.meaning: Krishna says there are two broad kinds of human nature: one shaped by clarity and virtue, the other by ego and confusion. He has already explained the first and now turns to the second.

16:7.shloka: प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च जना न विदुरासुराः । न शौचं नापि चाचारो न सत्यं तेषु विद्यते ॥
16:7.transliteration: प्रवृत्तिं (what should be pursued) / च (and) / निवृत्तिं (what should be withdrawn from) / च (and) / जना (people) / न (not) / विदुः (know) / असुराः (those of shadowed nature) / न (not) / शौचं (purity) / न (not) / अपि (also) / च (and) / आचारः (noble conduct) / न (not) / सत्यं (truthfulness) / तेषु (in them) / विद्यते (exists)
16:7.meaning: A shadowed nature cannot tell right action from wrong withdrawal. It also lacks purity, good conduct, and honesty.

16:8.transliteration: असत्यम् (untrue) / अप्रतिष्ठम् (without foundation) / ते (they) / जगत् (world) / आहुः (say) / अनीश्वरम् (without a ruling lord) / अपरस्परसम्भूतम् (born without mutual cause) / किम् अन्यत् (what else) / कामहैतुकम् (caused by desire) ॥
16:8.meaning: Some people deny any higher order in the world. They reduce existence to chance, physical union, and desire alone.

16:9.shloka: एतां दृष्टिमवष्टभ्य नष्टात्मानोऽल्पबुद्धयः । प्रभवन्त्युग्रकर्माणः क्षयाय जगतोऽहिताः ॥
16:9.transliteration: एताम् (this) / दृष्टिम् (view) / अवष्टभ्य (holding) / नष्टात्मानः (those whose inner being is lost) / अल्पबुद्धयः (those of little discernment) / प्रभवन्ति (arise) / उग्रकर्माणः (fierce in action) / क्षयाय (for destruction) / जगतः (of the world) / अहिताः (harmful ones)
16:9.meaning: People who deny their true nature and cling to a false view become small-minded and destructive. Their energy turns toward harming the world instead of serving it.

meanings.meaning: those whose inner being is lost

16:10.transliteration: कामम् (desire) / आश्रित्य (taking refuge in) / दुष्पूरम् (hard to fill, never fully satisfied) / दम्भ-मान-मदान्विताः (endowed with hypocrisy, pride, and intoxication) / मोहात् (through delusion) / गृहीत्वा (having grasped) / असत्-ग्राहान् (false beliefs, false graspings) / प्रवर्तन्ते (they keep moving) / अशुचि-व्रताः (those with impure vows)
16:10.meaning: People trapped by endless wanting become arrogant, deluded, and stubborn. Their lives keep moving, but they keep holding on to false ideas and impure aims.

meanings.meaning: hard to fill, never fully satisfied
endowed with hypocrisy, pride, and intoxication

16:11.shloka: चिन्तामपरिमेयां च प्रलयान्तामुपाश्रिताः । कामोपभोगपरमा एतावदिति निश्िचताः ॥
16:11.transliteration: चिन्ताम् (worry) / अपरिमेयाम् (immeasurable) / च (and) / प्रलयान्ताम् (lasting until dissolution/death) / उपाश्रिताः (taking refuge in) / कामोपभोग-परमाः (for whom desire-enjoyment is the highest) / एतावत् (so much only) / इति (thus) / निश्चिताः (deciding firmly)
16:11.meaning: People trapped in desire live with endless worry, chase possessions and pleasure, and conclude that material life is all that exists.

meanings.meaning: for whom desire-enjoyment is the highest

16:12.transliteration: आशापाशशतैः (by hundreds of cords of hope) / बद्धाः (bound) / कामक्रोधपरायणाः (devoted to desire and anger) / ईहन्ते (they strive) / कामभोगार्थम् (for the sake of enjoyment of desire) / अन्यायेन (unjustly) / अर्थसञ्चयान् (accumulations of wealth)
16:12.meaning: Endless hopes trap people. Desire and anger drive them to gather wealth by unfair means just to feed more wanting.

meanings.meaning: by hundreds of cords of hope
for the sake of enjoyment of desire

16:13.shloka: इदमद्य मया लब्धमिमं प्राप्स्ये मनोरथम् । इदमस्तीदमपि मे भविष्यति पुनर्धनम् ॥
16:13.transliteration: इदम् (this) / अद्य (today) / मया (by me) / लब्धम् (gained) / इमम् (this) / प्राप्स्ये (I will obtain) / मनोरथम् (desired wish) / इदम् (this) / अस्ति (is) / इदम् (this) / अपि (also) / मे (of me) / भविष्यति (will be) / पुनः (again) / धनम् (wealth)
16:13.meaning: A deluded person keeps thinking, “I have gained this, I will get more, and this wealth will keep increasing.”

16:14.shloka: असौ मया हतः शत्रुर्हनिष्ये चापरानपि । ईश्वरोऽहमहं भोगी सिद्धोऽहं बलवान्सुखी ॥
16:14.transliteration: असौ (that) / मया (by me) / हतः (slain) / शत्रुः (enemy) / हनिष्ये (I will slay) / च (and) / अपरान् (others) / अपि (also) / ईश्वरः (lord) / अहम् (I) / अहम् (I) / भोगी (enjoyer) / सिद्धः (accomplished) / अहम् (I) / बलवान् (powerful) / सुखी (happy)
16:14.meaning: Pride speaks through the arrogant mind: it claims victory, power, pleasure, success, and happiness as its own.

16:15.shloka: आढ्योऽभिजनवानस्मि कोऽन्योऽस्ति सदृशो मया । यक्ष्ये दास्यामि मोदिष्य इत्यज्ञानविमोहिताः ॥
16:15.transliteration: आढ्यः (rich) / अभिजनवान् (well-born, surrounded by family and followers) / अस्मि (I am) / कः (who) / अन्यः (other) / अस्ति (is) / सदृशः (equal) / मया (to me) / यक्ष्ये (I will sacrifice) / दास्यामि (I will give) / मोदिष्ये (I will rejoice) / इति (thus) / अज्ञान-विमोहिताः (deluded by ignorance)
16:15.meaning: Arrogance makes a person feel rich, superior, and endlessly secure. That delusion then turns even sacrifice and giving into excuses for more enjoyment.

meanings.meaning: well-born, surrounded by family and followers

16:16.shloka: अनेकचित्तविभ्रान्ता मोहजालसमावृताः । प्रसक्ताः कामभोगेषु पतन्ति नरकेऽशुचौ ॥
16:16.transliteration: अनेकचित्तविभ्रान्ताः (confused by many minds/desires) / मोहजालसमावृताः (covered by the net of delusion) / प्रसक्ताः (attached) / कामभोगेषु (in pleasures of desire) / पतन्ति (fall) / नरके (into a lower realm) / अशुचौ (impure, miserable)
16:16.meaning: A mind pulled in many directions by desire gets caught in confusion and falls into misery through attachment to pleasure.

meanings.meaning: covered by the net of delusion

16:17.shloka: आत्मसम्भाविताः स्तब्धा धनमानमदान्विताः । यजन्ते नामयज्ञैस्ते दम्भेनाविधिपूर्वकम् ॥
16:17.transliteration: आत्मसम्भाविताः (self-important) / स्तब्धा (stubborn, rigid) / धनमानमदान्विताः (endowed with wealth-pride and honor-pride) / यजन्ते (they perform ritual) / नामयज्ञैः (with name-only sacrifices) / ते (they) / दम्भेन (with pretence) / अविधिपूर्वकम् (without proper method)
16:17.meaning: People swollen with pride, wealth, and status may still perform rituals, but only as show. The outer act remains, while the inner sincerity and right method are missing.

16:18.shloka: अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं च संश्रिताः । मामात्मपरदेहेषु प्रद्विषन्तोऽभ्यसूयकाः ॥
16:18.transliteration: अहङ्कारम् (ego) / बलम् (force) / दर्पम् (arrogance) / कामम् (desire) / क्रोधम् (anger) / च (and) / संश्रिताः (taking refuge in) / माम् (Me) / आत्मपरदेहेषु (in one's own and others' bodies) / प्रद्विषन्तः (hating) / अभ्यसूयकाः (fault-finding, envious)
16:18.meaning: People ruled by ego, force, arrogance, desire, and anger turn against the divine presence within themselves and others. They also habitually look for faults in what is good.

meanings.meaning: in one's own and others' bodies

16:19.shloka: तानहं द्विषतः क्रूरान्संसारेषु नराधमान् । क्षिपाम्यजस्रमशुभानासुरीष्वेव योनिषु ॥
16:19.transliteration: तान् (those) / अहम् (I) / द्विषतः (hateful) / क्रूरान् (cruel) / संसारेषु (in the cycles of worldly existence) / नराधमान् (lowest among men) / क्षिपामि (I cast) / अजस्रम् (again and again) / अशुभान् (inauspicious, impure) / आसुरीषु (into demonic) / एव (indeed) / योनिषु (wombs, births)
16:19.meaning: People who hate, act cruelly, and live in impurity fall into lower births again and again.

meanings.meaning: in the cycles of worldly existence

16:20.shloka: असुरीं योनिमापन्ना मूढा जन्मनि जन्मनि । मामप्राप्यैव कौन्तेय ततो यान्त्यधमां गतिम् ॥
16:20.transliteration: असुरीम् (shadowed) / योनिम् (birth) / आपन्नाः (having entered) / मूढाः (deluded ones) / जन्मनि जन्मनि (birth after birth) / माम् (me) / अप्राप्य (not reaching) / एव (indeed) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / ततः (then) / यान्ति (go) / अधमाम् (lower) / गतिम् (state)
16:20.meaning: People trapped in delusion keep taking shadowed births again and again. Because they never turn toward the divine, they sink into still lower states.

16:21.shloka: त्रिविधं नरकस्येदं द्वारं नाशनमात्मनः । कामः क्रोधस्तथा लोभस्तस्मादेतत्त्रयं त्यजेत् ॥
16:21.transliteration: त्रिविधम् (threefold) / नरकस्य (of ruin) / इदम् (this) / द्वारम् (gate) / नाशनम् (destroyer) / आत्मनः (of the self) / कामः (desire) / क्रोधः (anger) / तथा (and also) / लोभः (greed) / तस्मात् (therefore) / एतत् (these) / त्रयम् (three) / त्यजेत् (should abandon)
16:21.meaning: Desire, anger, and greed are the three forces that drag a person downward. Krishna says they must be given up.

16:22.shloka: एतैर्विमुक्तः कौन्तेय तमोद्वारैस्त्रिभिर्नरः । आचरत्यात्मनः श्रेयस्ततो याति परां गतिम् ॥
16:22.transliteration: एतैः (from these) / विमुक्तः (freed) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / तैः (by those) / त्रिभिः (three) / नरः (person) / आचरति (practices) / आत्मनः (of one's own) / श्रेयः (well-being) / ततः (then) / याति (goes) / पराम् (highest) / गतिम् (state)
16:22.meaning: When a person is free from desire, anger, and greed, they live in a way that supports their highest good and reach the highest state.

16:23.shloka: यः शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य वर्तते कामकारतः । न स सिद्धिमवाप्नोति न सुखं न परां गतिम् ॥
16:23.transliteration: यः (whoever) / शास्त्रविधिम् (scriptural rule) / उत्सृज्य (having set aside) / वर्तते (acts) / कामकारतः (under the pull of desire) / न (not) / सः (that person) / सिद्धिम् (perfection) / अवाप्नोति (attains) / न (not) / सुखम् (happiness) / न (not) / पराम् गतिम् (highest state) ॥
16:23.meaning: Acting only on impulse, while ignoring wise guidance, blocks real fulfilment, lasting happiness, and the highest good.

16:24.shloka: तस्माच्छास्त्रं प्रमाणं ते कार्याकार्यव्यवस्थितौ । ज्ञात्वा शास्त्रविधानोक्तं कर्म कर्तुमिहार्हसि ॥
16:24.transliteration: तस्मात् (therefore) / शास्त्रम् (scripture) / प्रमाणम् (authority) / ते (for you) / कार्य-अकार्य-व्यवस्थितौ (in the arrangement of what should be done and what should not be done) / ज्ञात्वा (having known) / शास्त्र-विधान-उक्तम् (spoken in scriptural rule) / कर्म (action / duty) / कर्तुम् (to do) / इह (here) / अर्हसि (you are fit / should)
16:24.meaning: Right and wrong action should be judged by scripture, not impulse. Knowing that guidance, a person should do the duty set before them.

meanings.meaning: in the arrangement of what should be done and what should not be done

17:1.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचये शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य यजन्ते श्रद्धयाऽन्विताः । तेषां निष्ठा तु का कृष्ण सत्त्वमाहो रजस्तमः ॥
17:1.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / ये (who) / शास्त्रविधिम् (scriptural rule) / उत्सृज्य (having set aside) / यजन्ते (worship) / श्रद्धया (with faith) / अन्विताः (endowed with) / तेषाम् (of them) / निष्ठा (standing / conviction) / तु (then) / का (what) / कृष्ण (O Krishna) / सत्त्वम् (sattva) / आहो (or) / रजः (rajas) / तमः (tamas)
17:1.meaning: Arjuna asks what kind of faith people have when they worship without following scripture. He wants to know whether their faith is pure, restless, or confused.

17:2.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचत्रिविधा भवति श्रद्धा देहिनां सा स्वभावजा । सात्त्विकी राजसी चैव तामसी चेति तां श्रृणु ॥
17:2.transliteration: त्रिविधा (threefold) / भवति (is) / श्रद्धा (faith) / देहिनां (of embodied beings) / सा (that) / स्वभावजा (born of nature) / सात्त्विकी (sattvic) / राजसी (rajasic) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / तामसी (tamasic) / चेति (thus) / तां (that) / श्रृणु (listen)
17:2.meaning: Faith is not all the same. It comes from a person's nature and appears in three forms: clear, restless, or dull.

17:3.shloka: सत्त्वानुरूपा सर्वस्य श्रद्धा भवति भारत । श्रद्धामयोऽयं पुरुषो यो यच्छ्रद्धः स एव सः ॥
17:3.transliteration: सत्त्वानुरूपा (according to inner nature) / सर्वस्य (of everyone) / श्रद्धा (trust before certainty) / भवति (becomes) / भारत (O Bharata) । / श्रद्धामयः (made of trust) / अयम् (this) / पुरुषः (person) / यः (who) / यत् (whatever) / श्रद्धः (trustful) / सः (that) / एव (indeed) / सः (he) ॥
17:3.meaning: A person's deepest trust reflects their inner makeup, and that trust shapes who they become.

17:4.shloka: यजन्ते सात्त्विका देवान्यक्षरक्षांसि राजसाः । प्रेतान्भूतगणांश्चान्ये यजन्ते तामसा जनाः ॥
17:4.transliteration: यजन्ते (worship) / सात्त्विकाः (sattvic) / देवान् (radiant beings) / यक्ष-रक्षांसि (yakshas and rakshasas) / राजसाः (rajasic) / प्रेतान् (spirits of the dead) / भूत-गणान् (hosts of beings) / च (and) / अन्ये (others) / यजन्ते (worship) / तामसाः (tamasic) / जनाः (people)
17:4.meaning: People worship different kinds of beings according to the quality that dominates their nature. Their faith shapes the form their devotion takes.

17:5.shloka: अशास्त्रविहितं घोरं तप्यन्ते ये तपो जनाः । दम्भाहङ्कारसंयुक्ताः कामरागबलान्विताः ॥
17:5.transliteration: अशास्त्रविहितम् (not laid down by scripture) / घोरम् (severe, terrible) / तप्यन्ते (they practice austerity) / ये (who) / तपः (austerity) / जनाः (people) / दम्भ-अहङ्कार-संयुक्ताः (joined with hypocrisy and ego) / काम-राग-बल-अन्विताः (endowed with desire, attachment, and forceful stubbornness)
17:5.meaning: Severe self-punishment without right guidance, mixed with pride and craving, does not make a person better.

meanings.meaning: endowed with desire, attachment, and forceful stubbornness

17:6.shloka: कर्षयन्तः शरीरस्थं भूतग्राममचेतसः । मां चैवान्तःशरीरस्थं तान्विद्ध्यासुरनिश्चयान् ॥
17:6.transliteration: कर्षयन्तः (wasting away) / शरीरस्थम् (dwelling in the body) / भूतग्रामम् (the group of elements / body) / अचेतसः (the unthinking) / माम् (Me) / च (and) / एव (also) / अन्तःशरीरस्थम् (dwelling within the body) / तान् (them) / विद्धि (know) / आसुरनिश्चयान् (having demonic resolve)
17:6.meaning: Blind, self-willed austerity can damage the body and ignore the divine presence within it. Such harsh practice is not purity; it comes from confused, destructive resolve.

17:7.shloka: आहारस्त्वपि सर्वस्य त्रिविधो भवति प्रियः । यज्ञस्तपस्तथा दानं तेषां भेदमिमं श्रृणु ॥
17:7.transliteration: आहारः (food) / तु (indeed) / अपि (also) / सर्वस्य (of everyone) / त्रिविधः (threefold) / भवति (is) / प्रियः (dear) / यज्ञः (sacrifice) / तपः (austerity) / तथा (and likewise) / दानम् (charity) / तेषाम् (of them) / भेदम् (difference) / इमम् (this) / श्रृणु (listen)
17:7.meaning: Food, sacrifice, charity, and austerity all come in three kinds. Krishna asks Arjuna to listen to the differences.

17:8.shloka: आयुःसत्त्वबलारोग्यसुखप्रीतिविवर्धनाः । रस्याः स्निग्धाः स्थिरा हृद्या आहाराः सात्त्विकप्रियाः ॥
17:8.transliteration: आयुः (life) / सत्त्व (purity) / बल (strength) / आरोग्य (health) / सुख (happiness) / प्रीतिविवर्धनाः (increasing cheerfulness) / रस्याः (tasty) / स्निग्धाः (smooth) / स्थिराः (firm) / हृद्या (pleasing to the heart) / आहाराः (foods) / सात्त्विकप्रियाः (dear to the sattvic person)
17:8.meaning: Pure, nourishing food supports long life, strength, health, calmness, and happiness. A clear-minded person naturally prefers what is wholesome and steadying.

17:9.transliteration: कटु (bitter) / अम्ल (sour) / लवण (salty) / अत्युष्ण (overly hot) / तीक्ष्ण (pungent) / रूक्ष (dry) / विदाहिनः (burning) / आहाराः (foods) / राजसस्य (of the passionate person) / इष्टाः (dear) / दुःख (pain) / शोक (grief) / आमय (illness) / प्रदाः (giving)
17:9.meaning: Foods that are too sharp, hot, dry, or harsh attract a restless, passionate nature, but they lead to pain, grief, and illness.

17:10.shloka: यातयामं गतरसं पूति पर्युषितं च यत् । उच्छिष्टमपि चामेध्यं भोजनं तामसप्रियम् ॥
17:10.transliteration: यातयामम् (half-cooked / stale) / गतरसम् (devoid of taste) / पूति (foul-smelling) / पर्युषितम् (stale / left overnight) / च (and) / यत् (which) / उच्छिष्टम् (leftover) / अपि (even) / च (and) / अमेध्यम् (impure) / भोजनम् (food) / तामसप्रियम् (dear to tamasic people)
17:10.meaning: People dominated by tamas are drawn to food that is stale, tasteless, foul-smelling, leftover, or impure.

17:11.shloka: अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्यज्ञो विधिदृष्टो य इज्यते । यष्टव्यमेवेति मनः समाधाय स सात्त्विकः ॥
17:11.transliteration: अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिः (by those without desire for reward) / यज्ञः (sacrifice) / विधिदृष्टः (seen by rule) / यः (which) / इज्यते (is performed) / यष्टव्यम् (must be offered) / एव (indeed) / इति (thus) / मनः (mind) / समाधाय (having settled) / सः (that) / सात्त्विकः (sattvic)
17:11.meaning: A sacrifice is pure when it is done because it should be done, not for reward.

meanings.meaning: by those without desire for reward

17:12.shloka: अभिसंधाय तु फलं दम्भार्थमपि चैव यत् । इज्यते भरतश्रेष्ठ तं यज्ञं विद्धि राजसम् ॥
17:12.transliteration: अभिसंधाय (with intention) / तु (but) / फलम् (fruit, result) / दम्भार्थम् (for display, for show) / अपि (also) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / यत् (which) / इज्यते (is performed as sacrifice) / भरतश्रेष्ठ (best of the Bharatas) / तम् (that) / यज्ञम् (sacrifice) / विद्धि (know) / राजसम् (rajasic)
17:12.meaning: A sacrifice done for personal gain or to look impressive is not pure. Its motive makes it rajasic, driven by restlessness and ego.

17:13.shloka: विधिहीनमसृष्टान्नं मन्त्रहीनमदक्षिणम् । श्रद्धाविरहितं यज्ञं तामसं परिचक्षते ॥
17:13.transliteration: विधिहीनम् (without rule) / असृष्टान्नम् (without offered food) / मन्त्रहीनम् (without mantra) / अदक्षिणम् (without fee) / श्रद्धाविरहितम् (without trust) / यज्ञम् (sacrifice) / तामसम् (dark) / परिचक्षते (they call)
17:13.meaning: A sacrifice done without proper guidance, offering, mantra, giving, or trust is called dark and lifeless.

17:14.shloka: देवद्विजगुरुप्राज्ञपूजनं शौचमार्जवम् । ब्रह्मचर्यमहिंसा च शारीरं तप उच्यते ॥
17:14.transliteration: देव (radiant beings) / द्विज (twice-born) / गुरु (teacher) / प्राज्ञ (wise) / पूजनम् (worship) / शौचम् (purity) / आर्जवम् (simplicity) / ब्रह्मचर्यम् (celibacy) / अहिंसा (non-violence) / च (and) / शारीरम् (bodily) / तपः (austerity) / उच्यते (is called)
17:14.meaning: True bodily discipline includes honoring wise teachers, staying clean, living simply, practicing self-restraint, and avoiding harm.

17:15.shloka: अनुद्वेगकरं वाक्यं सत्यं प्रियहितं च यत् । स्वाध्यायाभ्यसनं चैव वाङ्मयं तप उच्यते ॥
17:15.transliteration: अनुद्वेगकरं (not causing agitation) / वाक्यं (speech) / सत्यं (true) / प्रियहितं (pleasant and beneficial) / च (and) / यत् (which) / स्वाध्यायाभ्यसनं (study and practice) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / वाङ्मयं (of speech) / तपः (austerity) / उच्यते (is called)
17:15.meaning: Speech becomes a disciplined practice when it is calm, truthful, kind, helpful, and supported by study.

17:16.transliteration: मनःप्रसादः (cheerfulness of mind) / सौम्यत्वम् (gentleness) / मौनम् (silence) / आत्मविनिग्रहः (self-restraint) / भावसंशुद्धिः (purity of feeling) / इति (thus) / एतत् (this) / तपः (austerity) / मानसम् (mental) / उच्यते (is called)
17:16.meaning: Mental discipline means keeping the mind calm, gentle, quiet, controlled, and clean in its feelings.

17:17.shloka: श्रद्धया परया तप्तं तपस्तत्ित्रविधं नरैः । अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्युक्तैः सात्त्विकं परिचक्षते ॥
17:17.transliteration: श्रद्धया (with trust) / परया (supreme) / तप्तं (performed) / तपः (discipline) / तत् (that) / त्रिविधं (threefold) / नरैः (by people) / अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिः (by those not desiring fruit) / युक्तैः (by those joined) / सात्त्विकं (sattvic) / परिचक्षते (they call)
17:17.meaning: Body, speech, and mind are called pure discipline when practiced with trust and without wanting any reward.

17:18.shloka: सत्कारमानपूजार्थं तपो दम्भेन चैव यत् । क्रियते तदिह प्रोक्तं राजसं चलमध्रुवम् ॥
17:18.transliteration: सत्कारमानपूजार्थम् (for honor, respect, and worship) / तपः (austerity) / दम्भेन (with pretence, show) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / यत् (that which) / क्रियते (is done) / तत् (that) / इह (here, in this world) / प्रोक्तम् (is said) / राजसम् (rajasic) / चलम् (unstable) / अध्रुवम् (impermanent)
17:18.meaning: Practice done to impress others, gain respect, or look holy is unstable and short-lived. It may bring attention, but not lasting value.

17:19.shloka: मूढग्राहेणात्मनो यत्पीडया क्रियते तपः । परस्योत्सादनार्थं वा तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥
17:19.transliteration: मूढग्राहेण (with foolish stubbornness) / आत्मनः (of oneself) / यत् (which) / पीडया (by pain) / क्रियते (is done) / तपः (austerity) / परस्य (of others) / उत्सादनार्थम् (for harming) / वा (or) / तत् (that) / तामसम् (tamasic) / उदाहृतम् (is said)
17:19.meaning: Austerity becomes dark and mistaken when it is done through foolish self-torture or to hurt other people.

17:20.shloka: दातव्यमिति यद्दानं दीयतेऽनुपकारिणे । देशे काले च पात्रे च तद्दानं सात्त्विकं स्मृतम् ॥
17:20.transliteration: दातव्यम् (to be given) / इति (thus) / यत् (which) / दानम् (gift) / दीयते (is given) / अनुपकारिणे (to one who does not return a favor) / देशे (in place) / काले (in time) / च (and) / पात्रे (to a worthy recipient) / च (and) / तत् (that) / दानम् (gift) / सात्त्विकम् (sattvic) / स्मृतम् (is remembered as)
17:20.meaning: A gift is pure when it is given as a duty, to the right person, at the right time and place, without expecting anything back.

meanings.meaning: to one who does not return a favor

17:21.shloka: यत्तु प्रत्युपकारार्थं फलमुद्दिश्य वा पुनः । दीयते च परिक्लिष्टं तद्दानं राजसं स्मृतम् ॥
17:21.transliteration: यत् (which) / तु (but) / प्रत्युपकारार्थम् (for return-favor) / फलम् (fruit, result) / उद्दिश्य (aiming at) / वा (or) / पुनः (again) / दीयते (is given) / च (and) / परिक्लिष्टम् (with strain, grudgingly) / तत् (that) / दानम् (gift, charity) / राजसम् (rajasic) / स्मृतम् (is called)
17:21.meaning: A gift loses its purity when you give it to get something back or to force a result, especially if you give it unwillingly.

17:22.transliteration: अदेशकाले (at an improper place and time) / यत् (which) / दानम् (gift / charity) / अपात्रेभ्यः (to unworthy recipients) / च (and) / दीयते (is given) / असत्कृतम् (without respect) / अवज्ञातम् (with contempt) / तत् (that) / तामसम् (tamasic) / उदाहृतम् (is said to be)
17:22.meaning: A gift given at the wrong time and place, to the wrong person, without respect, is a low form of giving.

meanings.meaning: at an improper place and time

17:23.shloka: तत्सदिति निर्देशो ब्रह्मणस्त्रिविधः स्मृतः । ब्राह्मणास्तेन वेदाश्च यज्ञाश्च विहिताः पुरा ॥
17:23.transliteration: तत् (that) / सत् (being, real) / इति (thus) / निर्देशः (designation, name) / ब्रह्मणः (of Brahman, the supreme reality) / त्रिविधः (threefold) / स्मृतः (is remembered, is known) / ब्राह्मणाः (priests, sacred knowers) / तेन (by that) / वेदाः (the Vedas) / च (and) / यज्ञाः (sacrifices) / च (and) / विहिताः (were ordained, established) / पुरा (in the beginning, formerly)
17:23.meaning: The sacred syllables Om, Tat, and Sat point to the supreme reality. They also mark the origin of the Vedas, priests, and sacrifices.

17:24.shloka: तस्मादोमित्युदाहृत्य यज्ञदानतपःक्रियाः । प्रवर्तन्ते विधानोक्ताः सततं ब्रह्मवादिनाम् ॥
17:24.transliteration: तस्मात् (therefore) / ओम् इति (Om thus) / उदाहृत्य (having uttered) / यज्ञ (sacrifice) / दान (giving) / तपः-क्रियाः (austerity-acts) / प्रवर्तन्ते (are undertaken) / विधान-उक्ताः (prescribed by rule) / सततम् (always) / ब्रह्म-वादिनाम् (of those who speak of Brahman)
17:24.meaning: People who follow scripture begin sacrifice, giving, and austerity by saying Om.

meanings.meaning: of those who speak of Brahman

17:25.shloka: तदित्यनभिसन्धाय फलं यज्ञतपःक्रियाः । दानक्रियाश्च विविधाः क्रियन्ते मोक्षकाङ्क्षि ॥
17:25.transliteration: तत् (that) / इति (thus) / अनभिसन्धाय (without aiming at) / फलम् (fruit, result) / यज्ञ (sacrifice) / तपः (austerity) / क्रियाः (actions) / दान-क्रियाः (acts of giving) / च (and) / विविधाः (various) / क्रियन्ते (are done) / मोक्ष-काङ्क्षि (by those desiring freedom)
17:25.meaning: People seeking freedom do sacred actions, self-discipline, and giving without chasing personal reward. They treat the work itself as an offering.

17:26.shloka: सद्भावे साधुभावे च सदित्येतत्प्रयुज्यते । प्रशस्ते कर्मणि तथा सच्छब्दः पार्थ युज्यते ॥
17:26.transliteration: सत् (sat; the real / the good) / भावे (in existence / being) / साधुभावे (in noble being) / च (and) / सत् (sat) / इति (thus) / एतत् (this) / प्रयुज्यते (is used) / प्रशस्ते (praiseworthy) / कर्मणि (in action) / तथा (also) / सत्-शब्दः (the word sat) / पार्थ (O Partha) / युज्यते (is joined / applied)
17:26.meaning: The word sat is used for what truly exists, for noble character, and for good action.

17:27.shloka: यज्ञे तपसि दाने च स्थितिः सदिति चोच्यते । कर्म चैव तदर्थीयं सदित्येवाभिधीयते ॥
17:27.transliteration: यज्ञे (in sacrifice) / तपसि (in austerity) / दाने (in giving) / च (and) / स्थितिः (steadfastness) / सत् (sat) / इति (thus) / च (and) / उच्यते (is called) । कर्म (action) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / तत्-अर्थीयं (for that purpose) / सत् (sat) / इति (thus) / एव (indeed) / अभिधीयते (is called)
17:27.meaning: Steady dedication in sacrifice, disciplined effort, and giving is called sat. Any action done for that sacred purpose is also called sat.

17:28.shloka: अश्रद्धया हुतं दत्तं तपस्तप्तं कृतं च यत् । असदित्युच्यते पार्थ न च तत्प्रेत्य नो इह ॥
17:28.transliteration: अश्रद्धया (without trust) / हुतं (offered in sacrifice) / दत्तं (given) / तपःतप्तं (austerity performed) / कृतं (done) / च (and) / यत् (whatever) / असत् (unreal) / इति (thus) / उच्यते (is called) / पार्थ (O Partha) / न (not) / च (and) / तत् (that) / प्रेत्य (after death) / न (not) / इह (here)
17:28.meaning: Actions like sacrifice, charity, and austerity lose their value when done without trust. They produce no real result in this life or after death.

18:1.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचसंन्यासस्य महाबाहो तत्त्वमिच्छामि वेदितुम् । त्यागस्य च हृषीकेश पृथक्केशिनिषूदन ॥
18:1.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / संन्यासस्य (of renunciation) / महाबाहो (O mighty-armed one) / तत्त्वम् (the truth) / इच्छामि (I wish) / वेदितुम् (to know) / त्यागस्य (of relinquishment) / च (and) / हृषीकेश (O master of the senses) / पृथक् (separately) / केशिनिषूदन (O slayer of Kesi)
18:1.meaning: Arjuna asks Krishna to explain the difference between renunciation and relinquishment.

18:2.shloka: श्री भगवानुवाचकाम्यानां कर्मणां न्यासं संन्यासं कवयो विदुः । सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं प्राहुस्त्यागं विचक्षणाः ॥
18:2.transliteration: श्रीभगवानुवाच (the Blessed Lord said) / काम्यानां (of desire-born) / कर्मणां (actions) / न्यासं (giving up) / संन्यासं (renunciation) / कवयः (the wise) / विदुः (know) / सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं (giving up all fruits of action) / प्राहुः (they call) / त्यागं (renunciation) / विचक्षणाः (the discerning)
18:2.meaning: Wise people define renunciation in two ways: giving up actions driven by desire, and giving up attachment to all results of action.

meanings.meaning: giving up all fruits of action

18:3.shloka: त्याज्यं दोषवदित्येके कर्म प्राहुर्मनीषिणः । यज्ञदानतपःकर्म न त्याज्यमिति चापरे ॥
18:3.transliteration: त्याज्यम् (to be abandoned) / दोषवत् (as flawed) / इति (thus) / एके (some) / कर्म (action) / प्राहुः (say) / मनीषिणः (the wise) / यज्ञदानतपःकर्म (sacrifice-giving-austerity-actions) / न (not) / त्याज्यम् (to be abandoned) / इति (thus) / च (and) / अपरे (others)
18:3.meaning: Some teachers say action itself should be dropped because it seems flawed. Others say sacrifice, charity, and disciplined effort should never be dropped.

18:4.shloka: निश्चयं श्रृणु मे तत्र त्यागे भरतसत्तम । त्यागो हि पुरुषव्याघ्र त्रिविधः संप्रकीर्तितः ॥
18:4.transliteration: निश्चयं (settled view) / श्रृणु (hear) / मे (my) / तत्र (there) / त्यागे (on renunciation) / भरतसत्तम (best of the Bharatas) । / त्यागः (renunciation) / हि (indeed) / पुरुषव्याघ्र (tiger among men) / त्रिविधः (threefold) / संप्रकीर्तितः (is declared) ॥
18:4.meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna to listen carefully to his clear view on renunciation. He says renunciation has three forms, so the subject needs to be understood with precision.

18:5.shloka: यज्ञदानतपःकर्म न त्याज्यं कार्यमेव तत् । यज्ञो दानं तपश्चैव पावनानि मनीषिणाम् ॥
18:5.transliteration: यज्ञ (sacrifice) / दान (giving) / तपःकर्म (discipline-action) / न (not) / त्याज्यम् (to be abandoned) / कार्यम् (to be done) / एव (indeed) / तत् (that) / यज्ञः (sacrifice) / दानम् (giving) / तपः (disciplined effort) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / पावनानि (purifying) / मनीषिणाम् (of thoughtful people)
18:5.meaning: Sacrifice, giving, and disciplined effort should not be dropped. They are actions that purify a thoughtful person.

18:6.shloka: एतान्यपि तु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलानि च । कर्तव्यानीति मे पार्थ निश्िचतं मतमुत्तमम् ॥
18:6.transliteration: एतानि (these) / अपि (also) / तु (indeed) / कर्माणि (actions) / सङ्गं (attachment) / त्यक्त्वा (having given up) / फलानि (results) / च (and) / कर्तव्यानीति (ought to be done) / मे (my) / पार्थ (O Partha) / निश्चितं (settled) / मतम् (opinion/teaching) / उत्तमम् (best)
18:6.meaning: Even sacred actions and all other duties should be done without clinging to results. Krishna says this is the best and settled teaching.

18:7.shloka: नियतस्य तु संन्यासः कर्मणो नोपपद्यते । मोहात्तस्य परित्यागस्तामसः परिकीर्तितः ॥
18:7.transliteration: नियतस्य (of the prescribed) / तु (indeed) / संन्यासः (renunciation) / कर्मणः (of action) / न (not) / उपपद्यते (is उचित / is fitting) । / मोहात् (from confusion) / तस्य (of that) / परित्यागः (abandonment) / तामसः (tamasic) / परिकीर्तितः (is called)
18:7.meaning: You should not give up the work that is properly yours to do. If you drop it out of confusion, that is a dark and dull kind of renunciation.

18:8.shloka: दुःखमित्येव यत्कर्म कायक्लेशभयात्त्यजेत् । स कृत्वा राजसं त्यागं नैव त्यागफलं लभेत् ॥
18:8.transliteration: दुःखम् (suffering) / इति एव (just so) / यत् (which) / कर्म (action) / कायक्लेश-भयात् (from fear of bodily strain) / त्यजेत् (would abandon) / सः (that one) / कृत्वा (having done) / राजसम् (passionate) / त्यागम् (renunciation) / न (not) / एव (indeed) / त्याग-फलम् (fruit of renunciation) / लभेत् (would obtain)
18:8.meaning: Giving up your responsibilities because they feel hard is not true renunciation. It is a restless, fear-driven rejection of effort.

18:9.shloka: कार्यमित्येव यत्कर्म नियतं क्रियतेऽर्जुन । सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलं चैव स त्यागः सात्त्विको मतः ॥
18:9.transliteration: कार्यम् (to be done) / इति एव (thus only) / यत् (which) / कर्म (action) / नियतम् (fixed, prescribed) / क्रियते (is done) / अर्जुन (Arjuna) / सङ्गम् (attachment) / त्यक्त्वा (having given up) / फलम् (result, fruit) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / सः (that) / त्यागः (renunciation) / सात्त्विकः (sattvic) / मतः (is considered)
18:9.meaning: Fixed duty becomes pure renunciation when it is done without attachment to personal gain or to the result.

18:10.shloka: न द्वेष्ट्यकुशलं कर्म कुशले नानुषज्जते । त्यागी सत्त्वसमाविष्टो मेधावी छिन्नसंशयः ॥
18:10.transliteration: न द्वेष्टि (does not hate) / अकुशलं (unwholesome) / कर्म (action) / कुशले (in wholesome) / न (not) / अनुषज्जते (clings) / त्यागी (renunciate) / सत्त्वसमाविष्टः (filled with clarity) / मेधावी (wise) / छिन्नसंशयः (cut free of doubt)
18:10.meaning: A truly wise renunciate does not hate difficult actions and does not become attached to good ones. Such a person is steady, clear, and free of doubt.

18:11.shloka: न हि देहभृता शक्यं त्यक्तुं कर्माण्यशेषतः । यस्तु कर्मफलत्यागी स त्यागीत्यभिधीयते ॥
18:11.transliteration: न (not) / हि (indeed) / देहभृता (by one who bears a body) / शक्यम् (possible) / त्यक्तुम् (to renounce) / कर्माणि (actions) / अशेषतः (without remainder, completely) । / यः (who) / तु (but) / कर्मफलत्यागी (renouncer of the fruits of action) / सः (that one) / त्यागी (renunciant) / इति (thus) / अभिधीयते (is called) ॥
18:11.meaning: A person with a body cannot give up all action. Real renunciation means giving up attachment to the results, not abandoning work itself.

meanings.meaning: by one who bears a body
renouncer of the fruits of action

18:12.shloka: अनिष्टमिष्टं मिश्रं च त्रिविधं कर्मणः फलम् । भवत्यत्यागिनां प्रेत्य न तु संन्यासिनां क्वचित् ॥
18:12.transliteration: अनिष्टम् (unpleasant) / इष्टम् (pleasant) / मिश्रम् (mixed) / च (and) / त्रिविधम् (threefold) / कर्मणः (of action) / फलम् (fruit, result) / भवति (becomes, occurs) / अत्यागिनाम् (for those who do not renounce) / प्रेत्य (after death) / न तु (but not) / संन्यासिनाम् (for renouncers) / क्वचित् (anywhere, at any time)
18:12.meaning: If you keep wanting the result of your actions, those actions keep producing effects for you. If you let go of results, that chain ends.

meanings.meaning: for those who do not renounce

18:13.shloka: पञ्चैतानि महाबाहो कारणानि निबोध मे । सांख्ये कृतान्ते प्रोक्तानि सिद्धये सर्वकर्मणाम् ॥
18:13.transliteration: पञ्च (five) / एतानि (these) / महाबाहो (O mighty-armed one) / कारणानि (causes) / निबोध (understand) / मे (from Me) / सांख्ये (in Sankhya) / कृतान्ते (in the doctrine of conclusion) / प्रोक्तानि (are said) / सिद्धये (for accomplishment) / सर्वकर्मणाम् (of all actions)
18:13.meaning: Krishna asks Arjuna to understand the five causes that make any action possible. Human action is not as simple as one person deciding and doing everything alone.

18:14.shloka: अधिष्ठानं तथा कर्ता करणं च पृथग्विधम् । विविधाश्च पृथक्चेष्टा दैवं चैवात्र पञ्चमम् ॥
18:14.transliteration: अधिष्ठानम् (the basis) / तथा (and) / कर्ता (the doer) / करणम् (the instruments) / च (and) / पृथग्विधम् (of many kinds) / विविधाः (varied) / च (and) / पृथक् (separate) / चेष्टाः (efforts) / दैवम् (the unseen force) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / अत्र (here) / पञ्चमम् (the fifth)
18:14.meaning: Every action depends on several causes: the base, the doer, the tools, the different efforts, and a fifth factor beyond human control.

18:15.shloka: शरीरवाङ्मनोभिर्यत्कर्म प्रारभते नरः । न्याय्यं वा विपरीतं वा पञ्चैते तस्य हेतवः ॥
18:15.transliteration: शरीरवाङ्मनोभिः (through body, speech, and mind) / यत् (whatever) / कर्म (action) / प्रारभते (begins) / नरः (a person) / न्याय्यम् (right, proper) / वा (or) / विपरीतम् (wrong, contrary) / वा (or) / पञ्च (five) / एते (these) / तस्य (of that) / हेतवः (causes)
18:15.meaning: Any action a person starts through body, speech, or mind is driven by five causes, whether the action is proper or improper.

18:16.shloka: तत्रैवं सति कर्तारमात्मानं केवलं तु यः । पश्यत्यकृतबुद्धित्वान्न स पश्यति दुर्मतिः ॥
18:16.transliteration: तत्र (there) / एवम् (thus) / सति (being present) / कर्तारम् (the doer) / आत्मानम् (the self) / केवलम् (only) / तु (but) / यः (who) / पश्यति (sees) / अकृतबुद्धित्वात् (because of unformed understanding) / न (not) / सः (he) / पश्यति (sees) / दुर्मतिः (confused-minded one)
18:16.meaning: Action does not come from the pure self alone. A confused person wrongly thinks the self is the only doer and misses the real causes at work.

18:17.shloka: यस्य नाहंकृतो भावो बुद्धिर्यस्य न लिप्यते । हत्वापि स इमाँल्लोकान्न हन्ति न निबध्यते ॥
18:17.transliteration: यस्य (whose) / न (not) / अहंकृतः (made by ego; marked by separate self) / भावः (state) / बुद्धिः (understanding) / यस्य (whose) / न (not) / लिप्यते (is stained; clings) / हत्वा अपि (even after killing) / सः (that one) / इमान् लोकान् (these beings) / न (not) / हन्ति (kills) / न (not) / निबध्यते (is bound)
18:17.meaning: A person who has dropped ego and stays inwardly untouched is not truly the killer, and is not trapped by the act.

meanings.meaning: made by ego; marked by separate self

18:18.shloka: ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं परिज्ञाता त्रिविधा कर्मचोदना । करणं कर्म कर्तेति त्रिविधः कर्मसंग्रहः ॥
18:18.transliteration: ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / ज्ञेयम् (the object known) / परिज्ञाता (the knower) / त्रिविधा (threefold) / कर्मचोदना (impulse to action) / करणम् (instrument) / कर्म (action) / कर्ता (doer) / इति (thus) / त्रिविधः (threefold) / कर्मसंग्रहः (whole of action)
18:18.meaning: Action is built from three parts: the knower, what is known, and the knowing itself; and also from the tool, the act, and the doer.

18:19.shloka: ज्ञानं कर्म च कर्ता च त्रिधैव गुणभेदतः । प्रोच्यते गुणसंख्याने यथावच्छृणु तान्यपि ॥
18:19.transliteration: ज्ञानं (knowledge) / कर्म (action) / च (and) / कर्ता (doer) / च (and) / त्रिधा (threefold) / एव (indeed) / गुणभेदतः (according to the distinctions of the qualities) / प्रोच्यते (are said) / गुणसंख्याने (in the analysis of the qualities) / यथावत् (as they truly are) / शृणु (listen) / तानि (those) / अपि (also)
18:19.meaning: Knowledge, action, and the doer are each understood in three different ways according to the three qualities. Krishna asks Arjuna to listen carefully to this classification.

meanings.meaning: according to the distinctions of the qualities
in the analysis of the qualities

18:20.shloka: सर्वभूतेषु येनैकं भावमव्ययमीक्षते । अविभक्तं विभक्तेषु तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि सात्त्विकम् ॥
18:20.transliteration: सर्वभूतेषु (in all beings) / येन (by which) / एकम् (one) / भावम् (reality/state) / अव्ययम् (imperishable) / ईक्षते (sees) / अविभक्तम् (undivided) / विभक्तेषु (among the divided) / तत् (that) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / विद्धि (know) / सात्त्विकम् (sattvic)
18:20.meaning: Pure understanding sees one unchanging reality in all beings, even though they appear separate.

18:21.shloka: पृथक्त्वेन तु यज्ज्ञानं नानाभावान्पृथग्विधान् । वेत्ति सर्वेषु भूतेषु तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि राजसम् ॥
18:21.transliteration: पृथक्त्वेन (by separateness) / तु (but) / यत् (which) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / नानाभावान् (many states/traits) / पृथग्विधान् (separately formed) / वेत्ति (knows) / सर्वेषु भूतेषु (in all beings) / तत् (that) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / विद्धि (know) / राजसम् (rajasic)
18:21.meaning: Knowledge that only notices differences and treats every being as separate is restless and driven by ego.

18:22.shloka: यत्तु कृत्स्नवदेकस्मिन्कार्ये सक्तमहैतुकम् । अतत्त्वार्थवदल्पं च तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥
18:22.transliteration: यत् (which) / तु (but) / कृत्स्नवत् (as if complete) / एकस्मिन् (in one) / कार्ये (in body/action) / सक्तम् (attached) / अहैतुकम् (without reason) / अतत्त्वार्थवत् (without knowledge of reality) / अल्पम् (small/petty) / च (and) / तत् (that) / तामसम् (dark/tamas) / उदाहृतम् (is called)
18:22.meaning: A dark kind of understanding mistakes one limited thing for the whole, lacks clear reasoning, and stays small and confused.

18:23.shloka: नियतं सङ्गरहितमरागद्वेषतः कृतम् । अफलप्रेप्सुना कर्म यत्तत्सात्त्विकमुच्यते ॥
18:23.transliteration: नियतं (prescribed) / सङ्गरहितम् (free from attachment) / अरागद्वेषतः (without attraction and aversion) / कृतम् (done) / अफलप्रेप्सुना (by one not seeking fruit) / कर्म (action) / यत् (which) / तत् (that) / सात्त्विकम् (sattvic) / उच्यते (is called)
18:23.meaning: Action is pure when it follows duty, is free from attachment, and is done without craving the result.

18:24.shloka: यत्तु कामेप्सुना कर्म साहङ्कारेण वा पुनः । क्रियते बहुलायासं तद्राजसमुदाहृतम् ॥
18:24.transliteration: यत् (which) / तु (but) / कामेप्सुना (by one desiring pleasure) / कर्म (action) / साहङ्कारेण (with ego) / वा (or) / पुनः (again) / क्रियते (is done) / बहुलायासम् (with great effort) / तत् (that) / राजसम् (rajasic) / उदाहृतम् (is called)
18:24.meaning: Action driven by craving, ego, or strain belongs to restlessness, not clarity. It is active, but it is not clean.

18:25.shloka: अनुबन्धं क्षयं हिंसामनपेक्ष्य च पौरुषम् । मोहादारभ्यते कर्म यत्तत्तामसमुच्यते ॥
18:25.transliteration: अनुबन्धम् (consequence) / क्षयम् (loss) / हिंसाम् (harm) / अनपेक्ष्य (without considering) / च (and) / पौरुषम् (one’s own capacity) / मोहात् (from delusion) / आरभ्यते (is begun) / कर्म (action) / यत् (which) / तत् (that) / तामसम् (tamasic) / उच्यते (is called)
18:25.meaning: Action driven by confusion, without considering consequences, damage, or your real ability, is dark and unwise.

18:26.shloka: मुक्तसङ्गोऽनहंवादी धृत्युत्साहसमन्वितः । सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योर्निर्विकारः कर्ता सात्त्विक उच्यते ॥
18:26.transliteration: मुक्तसङ्गः (free from attachment) / अनहंवादी (not speaking 'I') / धृत्युत्साहसमन्वितः (endowed with steadiness and enthusiasm) / सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योः (in success and failure) / निर्विकारः (unchanged) / कर्ता (doer) / सात्त्विकः (sattvic) / उच्यते (is called)
18:26.meaning: A truly pure doer works without attachment, ego, or inner disturbance, and remains steady whether the result succeeds or fails.

18:27.shloka: रागी कर्मफलप्रेप्सुर्लुब्धो हिंसात्मकोऽशुचिः । हर्षशोकान्वितः कर्ता राजसः परिकीर्तितः ॥
18:27.transliteration: रागी (dominated by craving) / कर्मफलप्रेप्सुः (seeking the fruit of action) / लुब्धः (greedy) / हिंसात्मकः (of violent nature) / अशुचिः (impure) / हर्षशोकान्वितः (joined with joy and sorrow) / कर्ता (doer) / राजसः (passionate) / परिकीर्तितः (is called)
18:27.meaning: A person driven by craving, greed, and attachment to results acts in a restless, passionate way and stays pulled between pleasure and disappointment.

18:28.shloka: अयुक्तः प्राकृतः स्तब्धः शठो नैष्कृतिकोऽलसः । विषादी दीर्घसूत्री च कर्ता तामस उच्यते ॥
18:28.transliteration: अयुक्तः (undisciplined/careless) / प्राकृतः (crude/unenlightened) / स्तब्धः (stubborn/obstinate) / शठः (deceitful) / नैष्कृतिकः (malicious/ungrateful) / अलसः (lazy) / विषादी (gloomy) / दीर्घसूत्री (procrastinating) / च (and) / कर्ता (doer) / तामसः (tamasic) / उच्यते (is called)
18:28.meaning: A person who acts carelessly, lazily, stubbornly, and deceitfully is working from tamasic energy. That kind of action is dull, delayed, and harmful.

18:29.shloka: बुद्धेर्भेदं धृतेश्चैव गुणतस्त्रिविधं श्रृणु । प्रोच्यमानमशेषेण पृथक्त्वेन धनञ्जय ॥
18:29.transliteration: बुद्धेः (of understanding) / भेदम् (the distinctions) / धृतेः (of steadfastness) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / गुणतः (according to the qualities) / त्रिविधम् (threefold) / श्रृणु (hear) / प्रोच्यमानम् (being explained) / अशेषेण (completely) / पृथक्त्वेन (separately) / धनञ्जय (O Dhananjaya)
18:29.meaning: Krishna asks Arjuna to listen to the three kinds of understanding and steadfastness, explained separately according to the three qualities.

18:30.shloka: प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च कार्याकार्ये भयाभये । बन्धं मोक्षं च या वेत्ति बुद्धिः सा पार्थ सात्त्विकी ॥
18:30.transliteration: प्रवृत्तिम् (forward action) / च (and) / निवृत्तिम् (withdrawal) / च (and) / कार्याकार्ये (what should be done and what should not be done) / भयाभये (fear and fearlessness) / बन्धम् (bondage) / मोक्षम् (freedom) / च (and) / या (which) / वेत्ति (knows) / बुद्धिः (intellect) / सा (that) / पार्थ (O Partha) / सात्त्विकी (sattvic)
18:30.meaning: A clear mind can tell the difference between action and withdrawal, right and wrong action, fear and fearlessness, bondage and freedom.

meanings.meaning: what should be done and what should not be done

18:31.shloka: यया धर्ममधर्मं च कार्यं चाकार्यमेव च । अयथावत्प्रजानाति बुद्धिः सा पार्थ राजसी ॥
18:31.transliteration: यया (by which) / धर्मम् (right action) / अधर्मम् (wrong action) / च (and) / कार्यम् (what should be done) / च (and) / अकार्यम् (what should not be done) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / अयथावत् (not properly) / प्रजानाति (understands) / बुद्धिः (intellect) / सा (that) / पार्थ (Arjuna) / राजसी (rajasic)
18:31.meaning: A restless, rajasic mind misreads right action and wrong action. It cannot clearly tell what should be done and what should be avoided.

18:32.shloka: अधर्मं धर्ममिति या मन्यते तमसाऽऽवृता । सर्वार्थान्विपरीतांश्च बुद्धिः सा पार्थ तामसी ॥
18:32.transliteration: अधर्मम् (wrongdoing) / धर्मम् इति (as right) / या (which) / मन्यते (thinks) / तमसा (by darkness) / आवृता (covered) / सर्वार्थान् (all meanings / all aims) / विपरीतान् (reversed) / च (and) / बुद्धिः (intelligence) / सा (that) / पार्थ (O Partha) / तामसी (dark, inert)
18:32.meaning: A mind clouded by darkness mistakes wrongdoing for righteousness and gets its judgment completely reversed.

18:33.shloka: धृत्या यया धारयते मनःप्राणेन्द्रियक्रियाः । योगेनाव्यभिचारिण्या धृतिः सा पार्थ सात्त्विकी ॥
18:33.transliteration: धृत्या (by steadfastness) / यया (by which) / धारयते (one holds steady) / मनःप्राणेन्द्रियक्रियाः (the activities of mind, life-force, and senses) / योगेन (through yoga) / अव्यभिचारिण्या (unwavering) / धृतिः (steadfastness) / सा (that) / पार्थ (O Partha) / सात्त्विकी (sattvic)
18:33.meaning: True steadfastness is the power to keep the mind, life-force, and senses steady through yoga, without wavering.

meanings.meaning: the activities of mind, life-force, and senses

18:34.shloka: यया तु धर्मकामार्थान् धृत्या धारयतेऽर्जुन । प्रसङ्गेन फलाकाङ्क्षी धृतिः सा पार्थ राजसी ॥
18:34.transliteration: यया (by which) / तु (but) / धर्मकामार्थान् (duty, desire, and wealth) / धृत्या (by steadiness) / धारयते (one holds) / अर्जुन (Arjuna) / प्रसङ्गेन (through attachment) / फलाकाङ्क्षी (one desiring results) / धृतिः (steadiness) / सा (that) / पार्थ (Partha) / राजसी (passionate)
18:34.meaning: A person can appear steady and disciplined, yet still be driven by attachment to duty, pleasure, and wealth, along with desire for results. That kind of firmness is restless, not pure.

18:35.shloka: यया स्वप्नं भयं शोकं विषादं मदमेव च । न विमुञ्चति दुर्मेधा धृतिः सा पार्थ तामसी ॥
18:35.transliteration: यया (by which) / स्वप्नम् (sleep) / भयम् (fear) / शोकम् (grief) / विषादम् (sorrow) / मदम् (pride) / एव (even) / च (and) / न विमुञ्चति (does not let go) / दुर्मेधाः (the dull-minded one) / धृतिः (steadfastness) / सा (that) / पार्थ (Arjuna) / तामसी (tamasic)
18:35.meaning: Some people hold on to sleepiness, fear, grief, despair, and pride instead of releasing them. That kind of stubbornness is dull and tamasic.

18:36.shloka: सुखं त्विदानीं त्रिविधं श्रृणु मे भरतर्षभ । अभ्यासाद्रमते यत्र दुःखान्तं च निगच्छति ॥
18:36.transliteration: सुखम् (happiness) / तु (indeed) / इदानीम् (now) / त्रिविधम् (threefold) / श्रृणु (hear) / मे (from me) / भरतर्षभ (O best of the Bharatas) / अभ्यासात् (from practice) / रमते (finds delight) / यत्र (where) / दुःख-अन्तम् (end of sorrow) / च (and) / निगच्छति (attains)
18:36.meaning: Krishna describes a higher kind of happiness: it grows through practice, brings lasting relief, and feels difficult at first but sweet in the end.

18:37.shloka: यत्तदग्रे विषमिव परिणामेऽमृतोपमम् । तत्सुखं सात्त्विकं प्रोक्तमात्मबुद्धिप्रसादजम् ॥
18:37.transliteration: यत् (which) / तत् (that) / अग्रे (at first) / विष-इव (like poison) / परिणामे (in the end) / अमृत-उपमम् (like nectar) / तत् (that) / सुखम् (happiness) / सात्त्विकम् (sattvic) / प्रोक्तम् (is said) / आत्म-बुद्धि-प्रसाद-जम् (born from the peace of self-understanding)
18:37.meaning: A calm, clear happiness may feel difficult at first, but it ends in deep relief. It comes from a mind that sees clearly and settles inwardly.

meanings.meaning: born from the peace of self-understanding

18:38.shloka: विषयेन्द्रियसंयोगाद्यत्तदग्रेऽमृतोपमम् । परिणामे विषमिव तत्सुखं राजसं स्मृतम् ॥
18:38.transliteration: विषयेन्द्रियसंयोगात् (from contact of senses and objects) / यत् (which) / तत् (that) / अग्रे (at first) / अमृतोपमम् (like nectar) / परिणामे (in the end) / विषमिव (like poison) / तत् (that) / सुखम् (pleasure) / राजसम् (rajasic) / स्मृतम् (is said to be)
18:38.meaning: Pleasures that come from sensory contact can feel wonderful at first, but they often leave pain, restlessness, or disappointment afterward.

meanings.meaning: from contact of senses and objects

18:39.shloka: यदग्रे चानुबन्धे च सुखं मोहनमात्मनः । निद्रालस्यप्रमादोत्थं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥
18:39.transliteration: यत् (which) / अग्रे (at first) / च (and) / अनुबन्धे (in the end) / च (and) / सुखम् (pleasure) / मोहनम् (deluding) / आत्मनः (of the self) / निद्रा-आलस्य-प्रमाद-उत्थम् (born of sleep, laziness, and negligence) / तत् (that) / तामसम् (tamasic) / उदाहृतम् (is called)
18:39.meaning: Pleasure that comes from sleep, laziness, and carelessness feels good only briefly. It dulls the mind and leaves a person more confused.

meanings.meaning: born of sleep, laziness, and negligence

18:40.shloka: न तदस्ति पृथिव्यां वा दिवि देवेषु वा पुनः । सत्त्वं प्रकृतिजैर्मुक्तं यदेभिः स्यात्ित्रभिर्गुणैः ॥
18:40.transliteration: न (not) / तत् (that) / अस्ति (there is) / पृथिव्याम् (on earth) / वा (or) / दिवि (in heaven) / देवेषु (among the radiant beings) / वा (or) / पुनः (again) / सत्त्वम् (being) / प्रकृतिजैः (born of nature) / मुक्तम् (freed) / यत् (which) / एभिः (from these) / स्यात् (may be) / त्रिभिः (three) / गुणैः (qualities)
18:40.meaning: Everything in the world is shaped by the three qualities of nature. Nothing in the material order is completely free from them.

18:41.shloka: ब्राह्मणक्षत्रियविशां शूद्राणां च परंतप । कर्माणि प्रविभक्तानि स्वभावप्रभवैर्गुणैः ॥
18:41.transliteration: ब्राह्मण-क्षत्रिय-विशां (of priests, warriors, merchants) / शूद्राणां (of workers) / च (and) / परंतप (O scorcher of foes) / कर्माणि (duties / actions) / प्रविभक्तानि (are divided) / स्वभाव-प्रभवैः (born of one’s nature) / गुणैः (by the qualities)
18:41.meaning: Different kinds of work belong to different people according to their nature and qualities.

18:42.shloka: शमो दमस्तपः शौचं क्षान्तिरार्जवमेव च । ज्ञानं विज्ञानमास्तिक्यं ब्रह्मकर्म स्वभावजम् ॥
18:42.transliteration: शमः (control of the mind) / दमः (control of the senses) / तपः (austerity) / शौचम् (purity) / क्षान्तिः (forgiveness) / आर्जवम् (straightforwardness) / एव (indeed) / च (and) / ज्ञानम् (knowledge) / विज्ञानम् (realized understanding) / आस्तिक्यम् (faith) / ब्रह्मकर्म (brahmin duty) / स्वभावजम् (born of nature)
18:42.meaning: A person's natural work can include self-control, purity, forgiveness, honesty, knowledge, and trust in the supreme reality. These qualities belong to a brahmin by nature.

18:43.shloka: शौर्यं तेजो धृतिर्दाक्ष्यं युद्धे चाप्यपलायनम् । दानमीश्वरभावश्च क्षात्रं कर्म स्वभावजम् ॥
18:43.transliteration: शौर्यम् (valor) / तेजः (radiance) / धृतिः (firmness) / दाक्ष्यम् (skillfulness) / युद्धे (in battle) / च (and) / अपि (also) / अपलायनम् (not fleeing) / दानम् (generosity) / ईश्वरभावः (leadership / lordliness) / च (and) / क्षात्रम् (of a warrior) / कर्म (duty) / स्वभावजम् (born of nature)
18:43.meaning: A warrior’s natural duties include courage, steadiness, skill, generosity, and refusing to run from battle.

18:44.shloka: कृषिगौरक्ष्यवाणिज्यं वैश्यकर्म स्वभावजम् । परिचर्यात्मकं कर्म शूद्रस्यापि स्वभावजम् ॥
18:44.transliteration: कृषि (farming) / गौरक्ष्य (cow-protection) / वाणिज्य (trade) / वैश्यकर्म (the work of the merchant class) / स्वभावजम् (born of nature) / परिचर्यात्मकम् (service-oriented) / कर्म (work) / शूद्रस्य (of the worker class) / अपि (also) / स्वभावजम् (born of nature)
18:44.meaning: Different kinds of work suit different people. Farming, cattle care, trade, and service are described as natural duties for two social groups.

meanings.meaning: the work of the merchant class

18:45.shloka: स्वे स्वे कर्मण्यभिरतः संसिद्धिं लभते नरः । स्वकर्मनिरतः सिद्धिं यथा विन्दति तच्छृणु ॥
18:45.transliteration: स्वे स्वे (in each one's own) / कर्मणि (in work) / अभिरतः (delighting in, devoted) / संसिद्धिम् (perfection, fulfillment) / लभते (attains) / नरः (a person) । / स्वकर्मनिरतः (fixed in one's own work) / सिद्धिम् (perfection) / यथा (how) / विन्दति (attains) / तत् (that) / शृणु (hear)
18:45.meaning: A person reaches fulfillment by staying devoted to their own duty, rather than chasing someone else’s role.

18:46.shloka: यतः प्रवृत्तिर्भूतानां येन सर्वमिदं ततम् । स्वकर्मणा तमभ्यर्च्य सिद्धिं विन्दति मानवः ॥
18:46.transliteration: यतः (from whom) / प्रवृत्तिः (arising) / भूतानाम् (of beings) / येन (by whom) / सर्वम् इदम् (all this) / ततम् (is pervaded) / स्वकर्मणा (by one's own work) / तम् (that) / अभ्यर्च्य (having worshipped) / सिद्धिम् (perfection) / विन्दति (attains) / मानवः (a person)
18:46.meaning: A person reaches fulfillment by offering their own work to the source of all beings and all existence.

18:47.shloka: श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात् । स्वभावनियतं कर्म कुर्वन्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम् ॥
18:47.transliteration: श्रेयान् (better) / स्वधर्मः (one's own duty) / विगुणः (without qualities, imperfect) / परधर्मात् (than another's duty) / स्वनुष्ठितात् (well performed) / स्वभावनियतं (fixed by one's own nature) / कर्म (action, work) / कुर्वन् (doing) / न (not) / आप्नोति (attains) / किल्बिषम् (wrongdoing, fault)
18:47.meaning: It is better to do your own duty, even imperfectly, than to do someone else's duty well. Acting according to your nature does not lead you into wrongdoing.

18:48.shloka: सहजं कर्म कौन्तेय सदोषमपि न त्यजेत् । सर्वारम्भा हि दोषेण धूमेनाग्निरिवावृताः ॥
18:48.transliteration: सहजम् (natural) / कर्म (action, duty) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / सदोषम् (with fault) / अपि (even) / न (not) / त्यजेत् (should abandon) / सर्वारम्भाः (all undertakings) / हि (indeed) / दोषेण (by fault/defect) / धूमेन (by smoke) / अग्निः (fire) / इव (like) / आवृताः (covered)
18:48.meaning: Do not give up your natural duty just because it is imperfect. Every action has some flaw in it.

18:49.shloka: असक्तबुद्धिः सर्वत्र जितात्मा विगतस्पृहः । नैष्कर्म्यसिद्धिं परमां संन्यासेनाधिगच्छति ॥
18:49.transliteration: असक्तबुद्धिः (detached-minded) / सर्वत्र (everywhere) / जितात्मा (one who has conquered the self) / विगतस्पृहः (free from craving) / नैष्कर्म्यसिद्धिं (perfection of non-action) / परमाम् (highest) / संन्यासेन (through renunciation) / अधिगच्छति (attains)
18:49.meaning: A person who is detached everywhere, self-controlled, and free from craving reaches the highest state of actionlessness through renunciation.

meanings.meaning: one who has conquered the self

18:50.shloka: सिद्धिं प्राप्तो यथा ब्रह्म तथाप्नोति निबोध मे । समासेनैव कौन्तेय निष्ठा ज्ञानस्य या परा ॥
18:50.transliteration: सिद्धिं प्राप्तः (having attained perfection) / यथा (how) / ब्रह्म (the supreme reality) / तथा (so) / आप्नोति (attains) / निबोध (listen carefully) / मे (from me) / समासेन (briefly) / एव (indeed) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / निष्ठा (culmination / steadfastness) / ज्ञानस्य (of knowledge) / या (which) / परा (supreme)
18:50.meaning: Krishna asks Arjuna to listen briefly as he explains how a perfected seeker reaches the supreme reality, the highest point of knowledge.

18:51.shloka: बुद्ध्या विशुद्धया युक्तो धृत्याऽऽत्मानं नियम्य च । शब्दादीन् विषयांस्त्यक्त्वा रागद्वेषौ व्युदस्य च ॥
18:51.transliteration: बुद्ध्या (with understanding) / विशुद्धया (purified) / युक्तः (endowed) / धृत्या (with firmness) / आत्मानम् (the self) / नियम्य (having restrained) / च (and) / शब्दादीन् (sound and so on) / विषयान् (sense-objects) / त्यक्त्वा (having abandoned) / राग-द्वेषौ (attraction and aversion) / व्युदस्य (having cast away) / च (and)
18:51.meaning: A person with clear understanding steadies the mind, restrains the senses, gives up sense pleasures, and drops attraction and aversion.

18:52.shloka: विविक्तसेवी लघ्वाशी यतवाक्कायमानसः । ध्यानयोगपरो नित्यं वैराग्यं समुपाश्रितः ॥
18:52.transliteration: विविक्तसेवी (dwelling in solitude) / लघ्वाशी (eating lightly) / यतवाक्कायमानसः (one whose speech, body, and mind are restrained) / ध्यानयोगपरो (devoted to meditation-yoga) / नित्यं (always) / वैराग्यं (non-attachment) / समुपाश्रितः (taking refuge in)
18:52.meaning: A serious seeker lives simply, keeps the senses under control, and stays devoted to meditation with non-attachment.

meanings.meaning: one whose speech, body, and mind are restrained

18:53.shloka: अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं परिग्रहम् । विमुच्य निर्ममः शान्तो ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ॥
18:53.transliteration: अहङ्कारम् (ego) / बलम् (force) / दर्पम् (pride) / कामम् (desire) / क्रोधम् (anger) / परिग्रहम् (possessiveness) / विमुच्य (having released) / निर्ममः (without mine-ness) / शान्तः (peaceful) / ब्रह्मभूयाय (for becoming the supreme reality) / कल्पते (is fit)
18:53.meaning: Let go of ego, pride, craving, anger, and possessiveness. When the sense of “mine” falls away, the mind becomes peaceful and ready for the supreme reality.

18:54.shloka: ब्रह्मभूतः प्रसन्नात्मा न शोचति न काङ्क्षति । समः सर्वेषु भूतेषु मद्भक्तिं लभते पराम् ॥
18:54.transliteration: ब्रह्मभूतः (having become Brahman) / प्रसन्नात्मा (serene-minded) / न (not) / शोचति (grieves) / न (not) / काङ्क्षति (desires) / समः (equal) / सर्वेषु (in all) / भूतेषु (beings) / मद्भक्तिम् (devotion to Me) / लभते (attains) / पराम् (supreme)
18:54.meaning: A person who has reached a steady, clear state no longer grieves or chases after things. Seeing all beings equally, that person reaches the highest devotion to Krishna.

18:55.shloka: भक्त्या मामभिजानाति यावान्यश्चास्मि तत्त्वतः । ततो मां तत्त्वतो ज्ञात्वा विशते तदनन्तरम् ॥
18:55.transliteration: भक्त्या (through devoted love) / माम् (Me) / अभिजानाति (knows truly) / यावान् (as much as) / यः (who) / च (and) / अस्मि (I am) / तत्त्वतः (in truth) / ततः (then) / माम् (Me) / तत्त्वतः (in truth) / ज्ञात्वा (having known) / विशते (enters) / तत्-अनन्तरम् (immediately after)
18:55.meaning: Devoted love lets a person know Krishna truly, beyond surface ideas. That true knowing leads straight into closeness with him.

18:56.shloka: सर्वकर्माण्यपि सदा कुर्वाणो मद्व्यपाश्रयः । मत्प्रसादादवाप्नोति शाश्वतं पदमव्ययम् ॥
18:56.transliteration: सर्वकर्माणि (all actions) / अपि (even) / सदा (always) / कुर्वाणः (doing) / मद्व्यपाश्रयः (taking refuge in me) / मत्प्रसादात् (by my grace) / अवाप्नोति (attains) / शाश्वतम् (eternal) / पदम् (state) / अव्ययम् (imperishable)
18:56.meaning: Keep doing all your work, but rely on Krishna rather than your own control. That surrender leads to an eternal, unchanging state.

18:57.shloka: चेतसा सर्वकर्माणि मयि संन्यस्य मत्परः । बुद्धियोगमुपाश्रित्य मच्चित्तः सततं भव ॥
18:57.transliteration: चेतसा (with the mind) / सर्वकर्माणि (all actions) / मयि (in Me) / संन्यस्य (having renounced, having placed aside) / मत्परः (devoted to Me, having Me as the highest) / बुद्धियोगम् (the discipline of discernment) / उपाश्रित्य (taking refuge in) / मच्चित्तः (with mind fixed on Me) / सततं (constantly) / भव (be)
18:57.meaning: Give every action to Krishna, rely on clear understanding, and keep your mind steadily on the divine.

meanings.meaning: devoted to Me, having Me as the highest

18:58.shloka: मच्चित्तः सर्वदुर्गाणि मत्प्रसादात्तरिष्यसि । अथ चेत्त्वमहङ्कारान्न श्रोष्यसि विनङ्क्ष्यसि ॥
18:58.transliteration: मच्चित्तः (with mind fixed on me) / सर्वदुर्गाणि (all difficulties) / मत्प्रसादात् (by my grace) / तरिष्यसि (you will cross over) / अथ (but) / चेत् (if) / त्वम् (you) / अहङ्कारात् (from ego) / न (not) / श्रोष्यसि (you will listen) / विनङ्क्ष्यसि (you will perish/fall)
18:58.meaning: Keep your attention fixed on the divine, and you can cross every obstacle through grace. Refuse that guidance out of ego, and you will fall.

18:59.shloka: यदहङ्कारमाश्रित्य न योत्स्य इति मन्यसे । मिथ्यैष व्यवसायस्ते प्रकृतिस्त्वां नियोक्ष्यति ॥
18:59.transliteration: यत् (which) / अहङ्कारम् (ego) / आश्रित्य (taking refuge in) / न (not) / योत्स्ये (I will fight) इति (thus) / मन्यसे (you think) / मिथ्या (false) / एषः (this) / व्यवसायः (resolve) / ते (your) / प्रकृतिः (nature) / त्वाम् (you) / नियोक्ष्यति (will impel)
18:59.meaning: Refusing your duty out of pride will not hold. Your deeper nature will still push you toward the action that fits you.

18:60.shloka: स्वभावजेन कौन्तेय निबद्धः स्वेन कर्मणा । कर्तुं नेच्छसि यन्मोहात्करिष्यस्यवशोऽपि तत् ॥
18:60.transliteration: स्वभावजेन (born of one's own nature) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / निबद्धः (bound) / स्वेन (by one's own) / कर्मणा (by action) / कर्तुं (to do) / न इच्छसि (you do not wish) / यत् (that which) / मोहात् (from confusion) / करिष्यसि (you will do) / अवशः अपि (even unwillingly) / तत् (that)
18:60.meaning: Your own nature pushes you toward certain actions. Confusion may make you resist them, but you may still end up doing them anyway.

18:61.shloka: ईश्वरः सर्वभूतानां हृद्देशेऽर्जुन तिष्ठति । भ्रामयन्सर्वभूतानि यन्त्रारूढानि मायया ॥
18:61.transliteration: ईश्वरः (the Lord) / सर्वभूतानाम् (of all beings) / हृद्देशे (in the heart-place) / अर्जुन (Arjuna) / तिष्ठति (dwells) / भ्रामयन् (causing to revolve) / सर्वभूतानि (all beings) / यन्त्रारूढानि (mounted on a machine) / मायया (by maya)
18:61.meaning: The divine lives in every being’s heart and moves all beings through maya, as if they were riding a body-machine and turning according to their nature.

18:62.shloka: तमेव शरणं गच्छ सर्वभावेन भारत । तत्प्रसादात्परां शान्तिं स्थानं प्राप्स्यसि शाश्वतम् ॥
18:62.transliteration: तमेव (to that alone) / शरणं (refuge) / गच्छ (go) / सर्वभावेन (with all your being) / भारत (O descendant of Bharata) / तत्प्रसादात् (by that grace) / परां (supreme) / शान्तिं (peace) / स्थानं (abode) / प्राप्स्यसि (you will attain) / शाश्वतम् (eternal)
18:62.meaning: Give yourself entirely to the divine, and you will receive deep peace and the eternal home beyond change.

18:63.shloka: इति ते ज्ञानमाख्यातं गुह्याद्गुह्यतरं मया । विमृश्यैतदशेषेण यथेच्छसि तथा कुरु ॥
18:63.transliteration: इति (thus) / ते (to you) / ज्ञानम् (wisdom) / आख्यातम् (has been explained) / गुह्यात् (than the secret) / गुह्यतरम् (more secret) / मया (by me) / विमृश्य (having reflected upon) / एतत् (this) / अशेषेण (completely) / यथा (as) / इच्छसि (you wish) / तथा (so) / कुरु (do)
18:63.meaning: Krishna says he has given Arjuna the deepest teaching. Arjuna should think it through completely and then decide what to do.

18:64.shloka: सर्वगुह्यतमं भूयः श्रृणु मे परमं वचः । इष्टोऽसि मे दृढमिति ततो वक्ष्यामि ते हितम् ॥
18:64.transliteration: सर्वगुह्यतमं (most secret) / भूयः (again) / श्रृणु (listen) / मे (my) / परमं (highest) / वचः (word) / इष्टः असि (you are dear) / मे (to me) / दृढम् (truly) / इति (thus) / ततः (therefore) / वक्ष्यामि (I will speak) / ते (to you) / हितम् (what is good)
18:64.meaning: Krishna asks Arjuna to hear one more secret teaching. Because Arjuna is dear to him, Krishna will speak only what is truly beneficial.

18:65.shloka: मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु । मामेवैष्यसि सत्यं ते प्रतिजाने प्रियोऽसि मे ॥
18:65.transliteration: मन्मना (with mind on me) / भव (become) / मद्भक्तः (my devotee) / मद्याजी (my worshipper) / माम् (to me) / नमस्कुरु (bow) / माम् (me) / एव (only) / एष्यसि (you will come) / सत्यम् (truth) / ते (to you) / प्रतिजाने (I promise) / प्रियः (dear) / असि (you are) / मे (to me)
18:65.meaning: Krishna asks Arjuna to keep his mind on him, love him, worship him, and show reverence. He promises that this wholehearted devotion leads to union with him.

18:66.shloka: सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज । अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः ॥
18:66.transliteration: सर्वधर्मान् (all duties) / परित्यज्य (having abandoned) / माम् (to Me) / एकम् (alone) / शरणम् (refuge) / व्रज (go) / अहम् (I) / त्वा (you) / सर्वपापेभ्यः (from all wrongdoing) / मोक्षयिष्यामि (will free) / मा (do not) / शुचः (grieve)
18:66.meaning: Let go of every lesser support and place yourself fully in Krishna's care. He promises freedom from all wrongdoing and asks Arjuna not to grieve.

18:67.shloka: इदं ते नातपस्काय नाभक्ताय कदाचन । न चाशुश्रूषवे वाच्यं न च मां योऽभ्यसूयति ॥
18:67.transliteration: इदम् (this) / ते (to you) / न (not) / अतपस्काय (to one without discipline) / न (not) / अभक्ताय (to one without devotion) / कदाचन (ever) / न (not) / च (and) / अशुश्रूषवे (to one unwilling to listen) / वाच्यम् (to be spoken) / न (not) / च (and) / माम् (me) / यः (who) / अभ्यसूयति (finds fault with)
18:67.meaning: Keep this deepest teaching away from people who are unreceptive, undisciplined, or hostile to Krishna.

18:68.shloka: य इमं परमं गुह्यं मद्भक्तेष्वभिधास्यति । भक्ितं मयि परां कृत्वा मामेवैष्यत्यसंशयः ॥
18:68.transliteration: यः (whoever) / इमम् (this) / परमम् (supreme) / गुह्यम् (secret) / मद्भक्तेषु (among my devotees) / अभिधास्यति (will speak, will declare) / भक्तिम् (devotion) / मयि (in me) / पराम् (highest) / कृत्वा (having made) / माम् (me) / एव (indeed) / एष्यति (will come, will attain) / असंशयः (without doubt)
18:68.meaning: Anyone who shares this deepest teaching with devoted love will reach Krishna without doubt.

18:69.shloka: न च तस्मान्मनुष्येषु कश्िचन्मे प्रियकृत्तमः । भविता न च मे तस्मादन्यः प्रियतरो भुवि ॥
18:69.transliteration: न च (and not) / तस्मात् (than him) / मनुष्येषु (among human beings) / कश्चित् (anyone) / मे (to me) / प्रिय-कृत्तमः (most dear-doing) / भविता (will become) / न च (and not) / मे (to me) / तस्मात् (than him) / अन्यः (another) / प्रियतरो (dearer) / भुवि (on earth)
18:69.meaning: Anyone who passes on this sacred teaching becomes especially dear to Krishna. No one on earth can surpass that devotion.

18:70.shloka: अध्येष्यते च य इमं धर्म्यं संवादमावयोः । ज्ञानयज्ञेन तेनाहमिष्टः स्यामिति मे मतिः ॥
18:70.transliteration: अध्येष्यते (will study) / च (also) / यः (whoever) / इमम् (this) / धर्म्यम् (sacred, righteous) / संवादम् (dialogue) / आवयोः (of us two) / ज्ञानयज्ञेन (through the sacrifice of knowledge) / तेन (by that one) / अहम् (I) / इष्टः (worshipped) / स्याम् (may be) / इति (thus) / मे (my) / मतिः (view)
18:70.meaning: Reading and studying Krishna and Arjuna’s sacred dialogue is itself a form of worship through knowledge.

18:71.shloka: श्रद्धावाननसूयश्च श्रृणुयादपि यो नरः । सोऽपि मुक्तः शुभाँल्लोकान्प्राप्नुयात्पुण्यकर्मणाम् ॥
18:71.transliteration: श्रद्धावान् (trustful) / अनसूयः (without fault-finding) / च (and) / श्रृणुयात् (may listen) / अपि (even) / यः (who) / नरः (person) / सः (that one) / अपि (even) / मुक्तः (freed) / शुभान् (auspicious) / लोकान् (realms) / प्राप्नुयात् (may attain) / पुण्यकर्मणाम् (of those with noble actions)
18:71.meaning: A person who listens with trust and without criticism is lifted free and reaches a better state earned by good action.

18:72.shloka: कच्चिदेतच्छ्रुतं पार्थ त्वयैकाग्रेण चेतसा । कच्चिदज्ञानसंमोहः प्रनष्टस्ते धनञ्जय ॥
18:72.transliteration: कच्चिद् (whether) / एतत् (this) / श्रुतम् (heard) / पार्थ (O Partha) / त्वया (by you) / एकाग्रेण (with one-pointed) / चेतसा (mind) / कच्चिद् (whether) / अज्ञान-संमोहः (delusion born of ignorance) / प्रनष्टः (destroyed) / ते (your) / धनञ्जय (O Dhananjaya)
18:72.meaning: Krishna asks whether Arjuna has listened carefully and whether his confusion has been cleared away.

18:73.shloka: अर्जुन उवाचनष्टो मोहः स्मृतिर्लब्धा त्वत्प्रसादान्मयाच्युत । स्थितोऽस्मि गतसन्देहः करिष्ये वचनं तव ॥
18:73.transliteration: अर्जुन उवाच (Arjuna said) / नष्टः (destroyed) / मोहः (delusion) / स्मृतिः (memory) / लब्धा (obtained) / त्वत्-प्रसादात् (by your grace) / मया (by me) / अच्युत (O Achyuta) / स्थितः अस्मि (I am standing) / गत-सन्देहः (without doubt) / करिष्ये (I will do) / वचनम् (word) / तव (your)
18:73.meaning: Arjuna says Krishna's grace has removed his confusion. His doubt is gone, and he is ready to follow Krishna's instruction.

18:74.shloka: सञ्जय उवाचइत्यहं वासुदेवस्य पार्थस्य च महात्मनः । संवादमिममश्रौषमद्भुतं रोमहर्षणम् ॥
18:74.transliteration: सञ्जय उवाच (Sanjaya said) / इति (thus) / अहम् (I) / वासुदेवस्य (of Vasudeva) / पार्थस्य (of Partha) / च (and) / महात्मनः (of the great-souled one) / संवादम् (dialogue) / इमम् (this) / अश्रौषम् (I heard) / अद्भुतम् (wonderful) / रोमहर्षणम् (hair-raising)
18:74.meaning: Sanjaya closes by saying he heard this astonishing exchange between Krishna and Arjuna.

18:75.transliteration: व्यासप्रसादात् (by Vyasa's grace) / श्रुतवान् (I heard) / एतत् (this) / गुहम् (secret) / अहम् (I) / परम् (supreme) / योगम् (yoga) / योगेश्वरात् (from the Lord of yoga) / कृष्णात् (from Krishna) / साक्षात् (directly) / कथयतः (speaking) / स्वयम् (himself)
18:75.meaning: Sanjaya says he heard the highest secret teaching directly from Krishna, by Vyasa's grace.

18:76.shloka: राजन्संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य संवादमिममद्भुतम् । केशवार्जुनयोः पुण्यं हृष्यामि च मुहुर्मुहुः ॥
18:76.transliteration: राजन् (O King) / संस्मृत्य (remembering again and again) / संस्मृत्य (remembering again and again) / संवादम् (dialogue) / इमम् (this) / अद्भुतम् (wonderful, astonishing) / केशव-अर्जुनयोः (of Krishna and Arjuna) / पुण्यम् (holy, purifying) / हृष्यामि (I rejoice) / च (and) / मुहुः मुहुः (again and again)
18:76.meaning: Sanjaya tells Dhritarashtra that he keeps remembering Krishna and Arjuna's sacred, astonishing conversation and feels joy each time.

18:77.shloka: तच्च संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य रूपमत्यद्भुतं हरेः । विस्मयो मे महान् राजन् हृष्यामि च पुनः पुनः ॥
18:77.transliteration: तत् (that) / च (and) / संस्मृत्य (remembering) / संस्मृत्य (remembering again and again) / रूपम् (form) / अति-अद्भुतम् (extremely wondrous) / हरेः (of Krishna) / विस्मयः (wonder) / मे (my) / महान् (great) / राजन् (O king) / हृष्यामि (I rejoice) / च (and) / पुनः पुनः (again and again)
18:77.meaning: Sanjaya says that remembering Krishna's astonishing cosmic form fills him with wonder and repeated joy.

18:78.shloka: यत्र योगेश्वरः कृष्णो यत्र पार्थो धनुर्धरः । तत्र श्रीर्विजयो भूतिर्ध्रुवा नीतिर्मतिर्मम ॥
18:78.transliteration: यत्र (where) / योगेश्वरः (master of yoga) / कृष्णः (Krishna) / यत्र (where) / पार्थः (Arjuna, son of Pritha) / धनुर्धरः (bow-bearer) / तत्र (there) / श्रीः (prosperity, grace) / विजयः (victory) / भूतिः (glory, power) / ध्रुवा (firm, steadfast) / नीतिः (right order, justice) / मम (my)
18:78.meaning: Sanjaya says that wherever Krishna and Arjuna are together, success, prosperity, and right order are assured.
